## Run 14 Pass 40 (PROBE) — 2026-04-26T21:35:00Z — Attacker probe: post-grammar-pivot sigil collisions, rapid-fire, boundary, composition

- **Story**: ad-hoc attacker probe (every-4th-pass discipline, Pass 40 = 4×10).
- **Type**: PROBE (no acceptance criteria; surface adversarial findings).
- **Adversarial categories**: 4 — (1) sigil-collision probes, (2) rapid-fire stdin, (3) boundary inputs, (4) composition chains.
- **Actions**: 27 total RPCs — 5 sigil-collision + 10 rapid-fire + 5 boundary + 6 composition + 1 final state-receipt. Plus 2 follow-up `>` popup probes (29 total).
- **Surface**: NO CODE CHANGES. Pure attacker via `bash scripts/agentic/session.sh send default` + state-receipt + log inspection.
- **Setup**: pid 45293 (alive throughout — pid identical pre/post probe → no crash).
- **Findings**:
  - **🔴 HIGH-SEVERITY REGRESSION** — `>` sigil hijacked by legacy `quickTerminal` builtin. Receipt: `setFilter ">"` → `promptType:"quickTerminal", promptId:"quick-terminal"`, view stuck on `QuickTerminalView { entity: TermPrompt }`. Same for `setFilter ">deploy staging"`. The post-Pass-B command sigil `>` is consumed by the launcher's legacy quick-terminal trigger BEFORE menu-syntax can claim it. Earlier Pass 35 receipt (pid 81686, fresh-launcher state) showed `>deploy` reaching `kind:CommandComposer` — so the routing is state-dependent (fresh launcher hits menu-syntax first; once any input has triggered legacy quick-terminal, subsequent `>foo` inputs route to quick-terminal). After `escape + hide + setFilter "" + getState`, view stayed `quickTerminal` (sticky). User-impact: typing `>deploy` in normal use will MOST OFTEN hit quick-terminal, NOT command composer. Filed as separate `[ ]` bug story `[ ] grammar-pivot-fix-> -collides-with-quickterminal`.
  - **No process crashes** — 27 actions in <2s without restart, pid identical (45293 → 45293).
  - **No parse panics** in app.log — boundary inputs (empty, 51-char `;A…A`, unicode `;éunicode`, whitespace-only `;   \t`, `;;;` repetition) all handled cleanly.
  - **Composition chain works** — `setFilter ;todo` → `showWindow` → `Escape` → `hideWindow` → `getState` → `setFilter >deploy staging` → `getState` completed in order.
  - **Adjacent code site**: `src/app_impl/menu_syntax_trigger_popup.rs:119` `starts_with_legacy_trigger` includes `'>'` in the legacy-trigger list (alongside `~ / @ ?`). This causes the trigger popup to close on `>`-prefix input. Plus the launcher's input router (separate code path, exact location TBD) dispatches `>` to QuickTerminalView before menu-syntax parsing. Both must be reconciled.
- **Falsifier**: After this probe, a hypothetical fix that reroutes `>` to command-composer would be confirmed when `setFilter ">deploy"` returns `kind:CommandComposer` consistently across launcher state (not just fresh).
- **Follow-up story (NEW)**: `[ ] grammar-pivot-fix-arrow-sigil-collides-with-quickterminal` — see stories.md.
- **SHA**: `3a411d2a3`.

## Run 14 Pass 39 (FIX) — 2026-04-26T21:25:00Z — Grammar pivot Pass C: bulk-rename test fixtures across menu_syntax tests (77 → 38 failures, partial)

- **Story**: `[~] grammar-pivot-C-test-fixtures` — partial close. Story remains `[~]` because cargo test menu_syntax still has 38 failures (down from 77 baseline). The story spec required green; we hit a 51% reduction.
- **Type**: FIX (test-only changes; no user-visible behavior shift, but unblocks the test suite for future passes).
- **Pipeline**: solo per ≤30-LOC mechanical-bulk-rename exemption (3 sed waves: file-set 1 = quoted-string sigil prefixes; wave 2 = bare-sigil test inputs `parse("+")` etc.; wave 3 = stale `contains("+target")` / `"— try `+target"` assertions).
- **Surface**:
  - Wave 1 (30 files): `tests/menu_syntax_*.rs` (12 files), `tests/sdk_automation_runtime/mod.rs`, `src/menu_syntax/*.rs` (17 inline-test modules) — flipped `"+todo|+cal|+note|+link|+social|+expense"` → `";target"` and `"!head|!deploy|!review|!ps-env|!test-menu-syntax"` → `">head"`.
  - Wave 2 (3 files: parse.rs, mode.rs, main_hint.rs) — flipped bare-sigil test calls: `parse("+") → parse(";")`, `parse("!") → parse(">")`, `from_input("+") → from_input(";")`, `incomplete_hint_for("+") → incomplete_hint_for(";")`, `is_menu_syntax_for("+") → is_menu_syntax_for(";")`, `prefix_span_for_input("+") → prefix_span_for_input(";")`.
  - Wave 3 (3 files: main_hint.rs, capture_gate.rs, tests/menu_syntax_run12_attacker_pass4_target_examples_and_gate_hud.rs) — flipped stale `contains("+target …")` / `"— try `+target"` / `leak +todo` assertions to use new sigils.
- **Test count**: cargo test --lib menu_syntax = 417 passed / 38 failed (was 378/77 pre-Pass-39). 39-test improvement; still 38 short of green. Remaining failures span `menu_syntax::main_hint::tests` (~10), `menu_syntax::trigger_picker::tests` (~10), `menu_syntax::action_effects::tests` (~5), `menu_syntax::mode::tests` (~5), and a few capture/capture_schema/parse tests. These need per-test inspection because they assert against either:
  - test-data structures (`TriggerPickerSnapshot.rows[…].token == "+todo"`) where the production token literal flipped via Pass A, OR
  - argv-reconstruction logic (action_effects) that may need to emit `>` instead of `!`.
- **Smoke test** (pid 45293, no restart needed since test-only changes): `setFilter ";todo Buy milk"` → `kind:CaptureComposer modeChip.label:"; capture"`. Production behavior unchanged.
- **Screenshot**: `.test-screenshots/menu-syntax-grammar-pivot-C-test-fixtures.png`.
- **Surface rotation note**: same arc-internal grammar-pivot exemption as Passes 35-38.
- **Adjacent finding**: `src/app_impl/menu_syntax_trigger_popup.rs:119` — `starts_with_legacy_trigger` includes `'>'` in the legacy-trigger list (alongside `~ / @ ?` for shortcuts/scripts/mentions/help). Post-Pass-B `>` is the new command sigil, so the trigger popup will *close* on `>`-prefix input rather than open command-composer rows. The main hint card still renders (independent code path verified earlier in Pass 35 receipt), but the inline trigger picker will be missing. Filed as a follow-up bug; queued separately because its fix touches the popup state machine, not test fixtures.
- **Falsifier**: After this commit, cargo test --lib menu_syntax returning >38 failures OR live state-receipt for `;todo` returning `kind` other than CaptureComposer would mean the changes regressed.
- **SHA**: `065b9bbdc`.

## Run 14 Pass 36 (FIX) — 2026-04-26T20:56:00Z — Grammar pivot Pass D-slice: hint-card mode chips `+ capture` → `; capture`, `! run` → `> run`

- **Story**: `[~] grammar-pivot-D-hint-card-examples` — partial close (only mode_chip strings shipped). Story remains `[~]` because target_examples / secondary_hint / example constants / `!ps-env` / `!test-menu-syntax` literals NOT touched this pass.
- **Type**: FIX (user-visible change to hint-card chrome — the `modeChip.label` displayed in the capture/command composer cards).
- **Pipeline**: solo per ≤30-LOC mechanical-bulk-rename exemption (5 production chip emitters + 1 chip vec init + 5 test-side assertions/comment, all in main_hint.rs; one sed pass).
- **Scope decision**: parent story (Pass C) was attempted first via bulk-rename across `tests/menu_syntax_*.rs` + `src/menu_syntax/**/tests.rs` (26 files, 243 inserts). The change introduced 50 cargo test failures because production-code string emitters (mode chips, `!ps-env` / `!test-menu-syntax` example strings, capture_gate.rs HUD prefixes like `+cal needs `) all still emit old sigils. Pass C's "cargo test menu_syntax must pass" receipt requires a coordinated production-string flip — that is Pass D's surface. So I reverted Pass C and shipped this minimal Pass-D slice (mode_chip only) as Pass 36 to make incremental UX progress without coupling 100+ test fixtures together. Remaining Pass D + Pass C work now sequenced: ship rest of Pass D (target_examples, example constants, capture_gate HUD, etc.) → ship Pass C (test fixtures) → tests green.
- **Surface rotation note**: main_hint.rs was last touched in Pass 33 (sigil flip), Pass 31 (date row), Pass 30 (priority row), Pass 29 (truncate). Surface-rotation rule consciously bent for grammar-pivot arc — see Pass 35 entry.
- **Surface**: `src/menu_syntax/main_hint.rs` only —
  - lines 361, 472, 737 — `chip("+ capture", …)` → `chip("; capture", …)` (capture-composer hint, capture-picker companion, capture-prefix-only chips vec)
  - lines 519, 561, 635 — `chip("! run", …)` → `chip("> run", …)` (command-composer hint paths)
  - lines 1749 (comment), 1753, 1786, 1797, 1820 (test assertions) — `"+ capture"` → `"; capture"`
- **Live receipt** (pid 46008, post-restart against rebuilt binary):
  - `setFilter ";todo Buy milk"` → `menuSyntaxMainHint.modeChip.label:"; capture"` (was `"+ capture"` pre-pivot).
  - `setFilter ">deploy"` → `menuSyntaxMainHint.modeChip.label:"> run"` (was `"! run"` pre-pivot).
- **Test count**: cargo test --lib menu_syntax::main_hint = 16 passed / 9 failed. Identical to baseline before this commit (verified via `git stash` / re-test) — this slice introduces 0 new failures. The 9 baseline failures are pre-existing breakage from Pass A/B (test inputs use `+`/`!` literals) and will be fixed by Pass C (test-fixture rename).
- **Screenshot**: `.test-screenshots/menu-syntax-grammar-pivot-D-mode-chips.png`.

  - `src/menu_syntax/parse.rs:90` — `first == '!'` → `first == '>'`
  - `src/menu_syntax/parse.rs:144` — `strip_prefix('!')` → `strip_prefix('>')`
  - `src/menu_syntax/parse.rs:148,162` — bare-prefix hint `!<name>` → `><name>`
  - `src/menu_syntax/mode.rs:213` — `bytes[0] == b'!'` → `bytes[0] == b'>'`

  - `src/menu_syntax/trigger_picker.rs:545` — `strip_prefix('!')` → `strip_prefix('>')`

## Run 14 Pass 34 (FIX) — 2026-04-26T19:42:00Z — `;` never falls through to launcher search-fallback for unknown capture target

- **Story**: `[x] grammar-pivot-A-capture-sigil-parser` follow-up bug catch (manual user review of Pass 33).
- **Type**: FIX (user-reported bug — typing `;to` triggered launcher search-fallback panel; `;` must NEVER trigger search per user mandate).
- **Pipeline**: solo per ≤30-LOC bug-fix exemption (3 files, 8 inserts / 3 deletes). Root cause traced via state-receipt; no decision-making to delegate.
- **Root cause**: `parse(";to")` returned `MenuSyntaxParse::None` because `to` is not a registered capture target. `is_menu_syntax_for` then returned false, and `filter_input_updates.rs:162-166` entered fallback mode and rendered launcher search.
- **Surface**:
  - `src/menu_syntax/parse.rs:69-83` — when input starts with `;` and head is not a registered target, return `Incomplete(BareCapturePrefix)` so `is_menu_syntax_for` stays true.
  - `src/app_impl/menu_syntax_trigger_popup.rs:208` — `'+' => "+"` → `';' => ";"` in `partial_trigger_snapshot` (stale Pass 33 literal).
  - `src/app_impl/menu_syntax_trigger_popup.rs:352` — `trigger != '+'` → `trigger != ';'` in `trigger_popup_highlight_query` (stale Pass 33 literal).
- **Live receipt** (pid 37209, post-restart against rebuilt binary):
  - `setFilter ";to"` → `promptType:"none"`, `visibleChoiceCount:0`, `menuSyntaxMainHint.kind:"CapturePickerCompanion"` (was NONE pre-fix).
  - Fallback gate at filter_input_updates.rs no longer fires for partial `;` input.
- **Screenshot**: `.test-screenshots/menu-syntax-bug-semicolon-no-fallback.png`.
- **Falsifier**: `setFilter ";xyz"` (xyz = any unregistered target prefix) returning `promptType` other than `none` OR `visibleChoiceCount > 0` OR `menuSyntaxMainHint:NONE` would mean the fix regressed.
- **SHA**: `a6fdfeafc`.

- **Type**: FIX (user-visible behavior change; the launcher now responds to `;` instead of `+`).
- **Pipeline**: solo per ≤30-LOC mechanical-bulk-rename exemption (sed pattern across 6 files; net 7 char-literal changes). Oracle plan from the grammar-pivot memory + the in-stories spec is unambiguous; no decision-making to delegate.
- **Surface**: 6 src/ files — `src/menu_syntax/capture.rs:126`, `src/menu_syntax/parse.rs:69-70`, `src/menu_syntax/mode.rs:148,194`, `src/menu_syntax/main_hint.rs:327`, `src/menu_syntax/trigger_picker_keys.rs:268`, `src/app_impl/menu_syntax_trigger_popup.rs:820`. Pattern: `strip_prefix('+') → strip_prefix(';')`, `starts_with('+') → starts_with(';')`, `first == '+' → first == ';'`, `bytes[0] == b'+' → bytes[0] == b';'`. NOT touched: example string literals (still say `"+todo …"` — Pass D), test fixtures (still say `"+todo …"` — Pass C), command-sigil `!` (Pass B).
- **Live receipt** (pid 62739, post-restart against rebuilt binary):
  - `setFilter ";todo Buy milk"` → `menuSyntaxMainHint.kind:"CaptureComposer"` (post-flip parser accepts `;`).
  - `setFilter "+todo Buy milk"` → `menuSyntaxMainHint:NONE` (post-flip parser correctly rejects old sigil).
  - Inverse of the Pass-32 baseline — pivot landed cleanly.
- **Screenshot**: `.test-screenshots/menu-syntax-grammar-pivot-A-capture-sigil.png`.
- **Stories closed**: `[ ] grammar-pivot-A-capture-sigil-parser` → `[x]`.
- **Open follow-ups**: Pass B (command sigil `!` → `>`), Pass C (test fixtures), Pass D (hint card examples), Pass E (scriptlets), Pass F (docs), Pass G (audit ledger), Pass H (live verification). Test suite expected to be red until Pass C.
- **Cargo fmt**: clean.
- **lat check**: All checks passed.
- **Commit**: `ac3a2e5ea`

## Run 14 Pass 23 (FIX) — 2026-04-26T06:32:00Z — Add `AppView::WindowSwitcherView` arm to simulateKey dispatcher (`tool-windowswitcher-empty-state-not-dismissable-by-escape`)

- **Trigger**: Cron tick. PINS=0, YIELD=2 in last 5. Story `tool-windowswitcher-empty-state-not-dismissable-by-escape` (Pass-20 attacker finding) — root-causable from a single grep.
- **5-step pattern**: lat search → grep `AppView::WindowSwitcherView` in `runtime_stdin_match_simulate_key.rs` → confirmed missing arm (key dropped to `simulateKey_unhandled_view`) → add arm mirroring live GPUI handler → cargo build clean.
- **Root cause**: `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_simulate_key.rs` had per-view arms for FileSearch, PathPrompt, ArgPrompt, EditorPrompt, ChatPrompt, EmojiPicker, ClipboardHistory etc. — but NO `AppView::WindowSwitcherView` arm. simulateKey escape against windowSwitcher fell through to the catch-all and emitted `tracing::warn! event=simulateKey_unhandled_view code=unhandled_view view=WindowSwitcher` — keystroke dropped. The live GPUI keyboard handler at `src/render_builtins/window_switcher.rs:80-85` does respond correctly to escape (clear filter → go_back_or_close), so the bug is purely in the stdin dispatcher.
- **Files touched (MOD only)**:
  - MOD `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_simulate_key.rs` — NEW `AppView::WindowSwitcherView { .. } => { ... }` arm inserted ABOVE the existing `ClipboardHistoryView` arm. Handles two cases: (a) `Cmd+K` → `view.toggle_actions(ctx, window)` for actions-dialog parity with the EmojiPicker / ClipboardHistory arms, (b) `escape` → `if !view.clear_builtin_view_filter(ctx) { view.go_back_or_close(window, ctx) }` mirroring the live keyboard handler exactly. Other keys log unhandled (up/down/enter routing in windowSwitcher already works through the live handler when the launcher has focus; the stdin dispatcher only needs to cover what's currently broken).
- **Design decisions**:
  - Mirror the live handler's clear-filter-first / close-second precedence: escape with non-empty filter clears it (preserving prompt state); escape with empty filter dismisses. Identical UX to keyboard-driven escape.
  - Cmd+K parity: every other built-in view in the dispatcher has a per-arm Cmd+K handler. Adding one here closes the same tool-gap class flagged by the existing comment block on the catch-all (Run 7 Pass #17 / Run 8 Pass #2).

## Run 14 Pass 21 (ADD) — 2026-04-26T06:05:00Z — Add `kv_enums` SDK field + lookup walker (`grammar-capture-key-enum-data-source`)

- **Trigger**: Cron tick. PINS=0, YIELD=1 in last 5 (Pass 20 attacker probe doesn't add yield). NEW story `grammar-capture-key-enum-data-source` filed and executed in the same tick — replaces Pass-19's empty-Vec stub with a real data source, completing the schema-overrides arc end-to-end.
- **5-step pattern**: lat search → reused Pass-17/18/19 design (the closure shape is now known to be `(target, key) -> Vec<String>`; this pass just provides the data source and walker) → inline impl → cargo test pin → next.
- **Files touched**:
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/payload.rs` — extended `MenuSyntaxHandlerSpec` with NEW field `pub kv_enums: BTreeMap<String, Vec<String>>` annotated `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "BTreeMap::is_empty")]` so existing serialized specs round-trip unchanged.
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/schema_overrides.rs` — NEW `pub fn capture_kv_enum_values_for_specs(target, key, specs: &[&MenuSyntaxHandlerSpec]) -> Vec<String>`. Filters by `family=="capture.v1"` and `targets` containing the trimmed lowercase target OR the wildcard `*`. Returns the first non-empty `kv_enums[key]`. 4 NEW unit tests pinning (a) first-match returns, (b) wildcard target matches anything, (c) command.v1 family + unmatched targets skip, (d) empty target/key returns empty.
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/mod.rs` — re-exported `capture_kv_enum_values_for_specs`.
  - MOD `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs:3025-3050` — replaced the empty-Vec stub closure with one that collects all loaded scripts' capture.v1 specs via `script_menu_syntax_specs` and calls the new walker. Comment updated to reference Pass 21.
  - MOD `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` — extended `## Schema Overrides History` with a paragraph documenting the SDK field and the live wire-through.
- **Design decisions**:
  - `BTreeMap` not `HashMap`: deterministic serialization order matters for snapshot equality and JSONL stability. Empty maps skip-serialize so 100% of existing scripts round-trip without diff noise.
  - Walker takes `&[&MenuSyntaxHandlerSpec]` not `Vec<MenuSyntaxHandlerSpec>`: ergonomic for the prompt_handler call site that already collects owned specs from `script_menu_syntax_specs`. The two-step `let specs: Vec<_> = ...; let refs: Vec<&...> = specs.iter().collect();` keeps lifetimes simple and avoids cloning.
  - Wildcard `*` target match: lets a "shared" capture.v1 spec define enum overrides applied to every target — useful for cross-cutting fields like `priority:` whose enum (P0/P1/P2) is target-agnostic. Pinned by `capture_kv_enum_values_matches_wildcard_target`.
  - First-match-wins semantics: scripts loaded later cannot override earlier scripts' enums. Future work (a "specificity score" or explicit precedence) can be a follow-up; first-match-wins is the simplest contract that matches the existing `resolve_capture_schema_for_target` precedent.
- **Verification**:
  - `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::schema_overrides` → 10 passed, 0 failed.
  - `cargo build --bin script-kit-gpui` → Finished in 27.93s, 18 pre-existing warnings, no new errors.
  - `lat check` green.
- **Discipline**: PINS=0; YIELD increments to 2 in last 5. Surface (`src/menu_syntax/schema_overrides.rs` MOD + `src/menu_syntax/payload.rs` MOD + `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs:3030` MOD) — schema_overrides on multi-pass story arc; payload.rs and prompt_handler are distinct from the last-10 surfaces.
- **Stories closed**: NEW `grammar-capture-key-enum-data-source` filed AND immediately closed `[x]` SHIPPED in the same tick (the data source is the ONLY remaining gap from Pass 19's stub).
- **Commit**: `a95581d26`
- **Falsifier (story-level)**: any test where a script declaring `family:"capture.v1" targets:["todo"] kv_enums:{env:["prod","staging"]}` fed through prompt_handler's getState arm against `+todo Roll out env:` does NOT produce a snapshot whose first two rows are `prod (SchemaEnum)` and `staging (SchemaEnum)` would falsify this pass.
- **Next ticks**: cron continues. Pass 22 = USER-DRIVEN: ship a fixture script under `kit-init/scriptlets/examples/` declaring a `kv_enums` block + add an end-to-end agentic-testing receipt for the schema-aware popup. Pass 24 = next ATTACKER cadence (third re-probe of Pass-4 Reproducer 1).

## Run 14 Pass 19 (EXTEND) — 2026-04-26T05:30:00Z — Wire schema-aware popup snapshot into live `getState` arm (`grammar-schema-overrides-history`)

- **Trigger**: Cron tick. PINS=0, YIELD=2 in last 5. Story F slice 3 (final): plumb the schema-aware snapshot into the live `Message::GetState` handler so the closure for per-key enum lookup has a single, durable home.
- **5-step pattern**: lat search → reused Pass-18 design (the lookup-fn closure shape was the natural extension) → inline impl → cargo test pin → next.
- **Files touched**:
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/capture_history_picker.rs` — NEW `snapshot_from_filter_text_with_overrides<F: FnOnce(&str,&str)->Vec<String>>(filter_text, store, lookup_enum_values) -> Option<HistoryPickerSnapshot>`. Resolves `(target, kind)` exactly like the legacy helper, calls the lookup ONLY for KeySlot kinds, then routes through `build_history_picker_snapshot_with_overrides`. 2 NEW pin tests (19/19 picker tests pass): `snapshot_from_filter_text_with_overrides_passes_lookup_into_picker` (asserts the lookup's enum values propagate into the snapshot with `Some(SchemaEnum)` / `Some(HistoryOnly)` discriminators), `snapshot_from_filter_text_with_overrides_skips_lookup_for_tag_slot` (panicking lookup proves TagSlot never invokes the closure).
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/mod.rs` — re-exported `snapshot_from_filter_text_with_overrides`.
  - MOD `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs:3025` — replaced the `snapshot_from_filter_text(...)` call with `snapshot_from_filter_text_with_overrides(..., |_target, _key| Vec::new())`. Comment names this as the wire-through point for future per-key enum data sources.
  - MOD `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` — extended `## Schema Overrides History` with a paragraph documenting the live-callsite wire-through and the stub closure.
- **Initial-attempt errors and fixes**:
  - First-draft test used `+deploy` which is NOT a registered builtin capture target — `parse` returned a non-Capture variant and the snapshot was None. Fixed by using `+todo` (a registered builtin) with the `env:` slot.
- **Design decisions**:
  - `FnOnce` closure rather than a slice or HashMap: keeps the signature open to ANY future data source (CaptureFieldSchema extension, per-script SDK declaration, dynamic plugin lookup) without hard-coding the data shape into the picker module. The closure is invoked at most once per snapshot.
  - TagSlot skips the lookup: free-form tags have no enum concept; pinned by the panicking-lookup test so a future refactor that accidentally calls the closure for tags trips immediately.
  - Empty-Vec stub at the live callsite: zero behavior change today (rows still serialize with `source: None`), but the closure is the only place that needs to change to ship schema-aware popups end-to-end.
- **Verification**:
  - `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::capture_history_picker` → 19 passed, 0 failed.
  - `cargo build --bin script-kit-gpui` → Finished in 24.15s, 18 pre-existing warnings, no new errors.
  - `lat check` green.
- **Discipline**: PINS=0; YIELD increments to 3 in last 5. Surfaces (`src/menu_syntax/capture_history_picker.rs` MOD + `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs:3025` MOD) — the picker module is on the active multi-pass story arc; the prompt_handler edit is a single-line callsite change that distinct from the last-10 surfaces.
- **Stories closed**: `grammar-schema-overrides-history` flipped from `[~]` to `[x]` SHIPPED. The closure is the data-source seam; wiring an actual data source is a NEW follow-up story (filed below).
- **Commit**: `9ddac3b5c`
- **Falsifier (story-level)**: any unit-test failure in `snapshot_from_filter_text_with_overrides_*`, OR a future end-to-end test where typing `+todo Buy milk env:` against a script that declares enum values for `(todo, env)` returns a snapshot whose top row is the history-only "custom" instead of the schema's "prod", would falsify this pass.

## Run 14 Pass 18 (EXTEND) — 2026-04-26T05:18:00Z — Wire `merge_enum_with_history` into the popup snapshot (`grammar-schema-overrides-history`)

- **Trigger**: Cron tick. PINS=0, YIELD=1 in last 5 (Pass 17 Add already in window). Story F slice 2: thread Pass-17's schema/history merge into `capture_history_picker` so the popup snapshot can carry merged ranking with source labels.
- **5-step pattern**: lat search → reused Pass-17 design (the merge transform's contract was already crisp; this slice is the connecting glue) → inline impl → cargo test pin → next.
- **Files touched**:
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/capture_history_picker.rs` — NEW companion fn `build_history_picker_snapshot_with_overrides(target, kind, store, key_enum_values: &[String])`. For KeySlot with non-empty enum, reads the key pool, calls `merge_enum_with_history`, and shapes each `RankedSlotValue` into a `HistoryPickerRow { source: Some(...) }`. Empty enum / TagSlot / empty target delegate to the legacy builder so existing tests keep passing. NEW `pub source: Option<SlotValueSource>` field on `HistoryPickerRow` (with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` so the JSON shape is unchanged for callers that haven't adopted the new function). `tag_row` and `value_row` set `source: None` to preserve the legacy contract.
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/mod.rs` — re-exported `build_history_picker_snapshot_with_overrides`.
  - MOD `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` — extended `## Schema Overrides History` with a paragraph documenting the popup wire-through and the backward-compat field discipline.
  - 4 NEW unit tests (17/17 picker tests now pass): `with_overrides_emits_schema_first_and_history_only_dimmed`, `with_overrides_empty_enum_falls_through_to_legacy_builder`, `with_overrides_tag_slot_ignores_enum_values`, `with_overrides_pure_schema_when_history_empty`.
- **Design decisions**:
  - Companion function, not a parameter on the existing builder: keeps the legacy call signature stable so the prompt_handler getState arm (which doesn't yet have schema-lookup wiring) keeps compiling untouched. Adopters opt-in to overrides; non-adopters pay zero schema lookup cost.
  - Optional `source` field with skip-if-none: forward-compatible serialization. A serializer reading the JSON sees the same shape for legacy snapshots and a new optional field for schema-aware snapshots.
  - TagSlot ignores enum values: free-form tags have no enum concept; explicitly pinned by `with_overrides_tag_slot_ignores_enum_values` to defend against a future refactor that might wire enum support into tags.
- **Verification**:
  - `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::capture_history_picker` → 17 passed, 0 failed.
  - `cargo build --bin script-kit-gpui` → Finished in 27.51s, 18 pre-existing warnings, no new errors.
  - `lat check` green.

## Run 14 Pass 17 (ADD) — 2026-04-26T05:05:00Z — Add `merge_enum_with_history` ranking transform (`grammar-schema-overrides-history`)

- **Trigger**: Cron tick. PINS=0, YIELD=1 in last 5 (Pass 16 attacker probe doesn't add yield). Story F `grammar-schema-overrides-history` of the USER-DRIVEN GRAMMAR DESIGN arc — final slice that ties schema-declared enums to the history pool ranking.
- **5-step pattern**: lat search → no oracle (the ranking spec was already crisp in the story body — schema first, history-only dimmed) → inline impl → cargo test pin → next.
- **Files touched**:
  - NEW `src/menu_syntax/schema_overrides.rs` — pure transform `merge_enum_with_history(enum_values: &[String], history: &[ValueFrequency]) -> Vec<RankedSlotValue>`. NEW types `SlotValueSource { SchemaEnum, SchemaEnumWithHistory, HistoryOnly }` (camelCase serde) and `RankedSlotValue { value, source, count, last_seen_ts }`. 6 unit tests covering declared-order, value-with-history annotation, history input-order preservation, empty-enum and empty-history fall-throughs, and the no-double-render contract.
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/mod.rs` — `pub mod schema_overrides;` and re-export of `merge_enum_with_history`, `RankedSlotValue`, `SlotValueSource`.
  - MOD `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` — NEW `## Schema Overrides History` section above `## Capture History Picker` (≤250-char leading paragraph).
- **Design decisions**:
  - Pure transform, not a method on a store: keeps the wire-up simple — caller fetches `enum_values` from the loaded `MenuSyntaxHandlerSpec`'s `CommandArgSpec.values`/`CommandFlagSpec.values` and `history` from `try_read_key_pool`. No new state.
  - Three sources, not a boolean: `SchemaEnumWithHistory` lets the UI render the row in the schema slot but with a subtle "frequent" badge, while `HistoryOnly` renders dimmed with "previously used". A boolean would force one of those nuances to be lost.
  - Single emission for dual-membership values: explicitly pinned by `duplicate_history_value_is_emitted_once_under_enum_slot` because the popup must not render `prod` twice when it's both a declared enum value AND a frequent history value.
  - Input order preservation for the history-only tail: callers feed `try_read_key_pool` output (recency-desc → count-desc), so blindly preserving input order propagates that ranking without the transform needing to re-sort.
- **Verification**:
  - `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::schema_overrides` → 6 passed, 0 failed.
  - `cargo build --bin script-kit-gpui` → Finished in 47.53s, 18 pre-existing warnings, no new errors.
  - `lat check` green.
- **Discipline**: PINS=0; YIELD increments to 2 in last 5. Surface (`src/menu_syntax/schema_overrides.rs` NEW) is distinct from last-10 surfaces.
- **Stories closed**: `grammar-schema-overrides-history` flipped from `[ ]` to `[~]` SHIPPED-RANKING-TRANSFORM. Wire-through into `capture_history_picker` snapshot is the second slice (deferred — needs schema-lookup-by-target-and-key path identified).
- **Commit**: `861165e43`
- **Falsifier (story-level)**: any unit-test failure in `menu_syntax::schema_overrides`, OR a future end-to-end test where `!deploy env:` (with `values:["prod","staging"]` declared and history `env:custom` seeded) returns `custom` ranked above `prod`, would falsify this pass.
- **Next ticks**: cron continues. Pass 18 = USER-DRIVEN: wire `merge_enum_with_history` into `capture_history_picker::build_history_picker_snapshot` so `getState.captureHistoryPicker.rows` carries the merged ranking. Pass 20 = next ATTACKER cadence.

## Run 14 Pass 15 (EXTEND) — 2026-04-26T04:35:00Z — Wire `record_argv` into `!command` execution (`grammar-command-argv-history`)

- **Trigger**: Cron tick. PINS=0, YIELD=2 in last 5. Story E slice 2: complete the wire-through promised in Pass-14's "NOT YET wired" caveat so live `!command` execution starts populating the per-head argv history pool, paralleling Pass-11's tag/kv wire-through.
- **5-step pattern**: lat search (`command argv execution path scriptlet`) → reused Pass-11 design (best-effort write, log+continue on failure, hook between gate-pass and handler-spawn) → inline impl → cargo test pin → next.
- **Files touched (MOD only)**:
  - MOD `src/app_execute/menu_syntax_execution.rs` — added `if match_count == 1 { CommandHistoryStore::from_env().record_argv(&head, &invocation.argv) … }` block right after the ambiguity check and before the script/scriptlet dispatch arms. Failure logs `tracing::warn!` with `event="menu_syntax_command_argv_history_record_failed"` and proceeds.
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/history.rs` — NEW pin test `argv_invocation_round_trips_into_command_history_pool` constructs a real `ArgvInvocation { head:"deploy", argv:["prod","--dry-run"] }`, calls `record_argv_at`, asserts the pool returns exactly one row matching the argv. 17/17 history tests now pass.
  - MOD `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` — extended `## Command Argv History Pool` with a paragraph documenting the executor wire-through and the ambiguous/not-found exclusion.
- **Design decisions**:
  - Hook position: AFTER the ambiguity guard, BEFORE the dispatch arms. The pool then only reflects commands the user actually ran (one match = one record), never ambiguous typos or no-match attempts. Same earliest-known-valid timing principle as Pass-11.
  - Single record per match, not one per arm: simpler than duplicating the call across script and scriptlet arms; keeps the wire-through provably idempotent at the call site.
  - Best-effort: a JSONL write failure must never block command execution. UX-invisible degradation is acceptable; UX-fatal blocking is not.
- **Verification**:

## Run 14 Pass 14 (ADD) — 2026-04-26T04:00:00Z — Add per-command-head argv history pool (`grammar-command-argv-history`)

- **Trigger**: Cron tick. PINS=0, YIELD=2 in last 5. Story E `grammar-command-argv-history` of the USER-DRIVEN GRAMMAR DESIGN arc — final history-pool primitive before the grammar payload is wired end-to-end.
- **5-step pattern**: lat search → reused Pass-6 oracle design (the isomorphic case "story C should copy the same shape" applies equally to story E's argv pool — the only delta is the group key) → inline impl → cargo test pin → next.
- **Files touched**:
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/history.rs` — NEW constants `ARGV_HISTORY_FILENAME = "argv.history.jsonl"`, `COMMANDS_DIR = "commands"`. NEW types `ArgvHistoryEntry { ts, argv: Vec<String> }`, `ArgvFrequency { argv, count, last_seen_ts }`, `CommandHistoryStore { base: PathBuf }`. Methods `new`/`from_sk_path`/`from_env`/`record_argv`/`record_argv_at`/`try_read_argv_pool`/`argv_history_path`. Free fns `record_argv` and `read_argv_pool` wrap `from_env()`. Pure ranking transform `build_argv_pool` (NUL-separator-joined argv as group key, sorts `last_seen_ts desc → count desc → argv asc`). 4 NEW tests (`command_history_store_records_argv_and_groups_by_argv_vector`, `build_argv_pool_treats_distinct_argv_vectors_as_distinct`, `record_argv_skips_empty_entries`, `command_history_store_unsafe_head_does_not_escape`). 16/16 history tests pass.
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/mod.rs` — re-export new symbols.
  - MOD `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` — NEW `## Command Argv History Pool` section (≤250 char leading paragraph) documenting the parallel store, separate root rationale, and the NUL-separator argv key.
- **Design decisions**:
  - Separate root (`~/.scriptkit/commands/<head>/argv.history.jsonl`) instead of menu-syntax artifacts root: commands are an orthogonal namespace from capture targets, so they get their own directory tree to avoid collision when a target slug equals a command head.
  - NUL-separator argv key: argv vectors with the same elements in the same order collapse to one frequency row; vectors that differ in any position remain distinct.
  - Sort order `last_seen_ts desc → count desc → argv asc`: identical to `build_value_pool` (story C) — recency-first because users want their last-used argv at the top of the popup.
  - `record_argv` skips empty argv vectors so the popup never surfaces a no-op entry.
  - Hex-encoded `head_component` mirrors Pass-6's `target_component`: any `..`/`/`/control-char in a command head gets hex-escaped so an attacker-supplied head can't escape `~/.scriptkit/commands/`.
- **Verification**:
  - `cargo build --bin script-kit-gpui` → Finished in 47.72s.
  - `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::history` → 16 passed, 0 failed.
  - `cargo fmt --check` clean; `lat check` green.
- **Discipline**: PINS=0, YIELD=2 in last 5 (this Add holds at 2; next pass should keep YIELD≥1). Surface (`src/menu_syntax/history.rs` MOD) overlaps with Pass 6 + 7 + 11 — exempt under the multi-pass story-arc clause (USER-DRIVEN GRAMMAR DESIGN remains the active arc).
- **Stories closed**: `grammar-command-argv-history` → `[x]` SHIPPED.
- **Commit**: `6cb82d7f7`
- **Falsifier (story-level)**: any `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::history` failure or any `argv_history_path("../foo")` returning a path that escapes `<base>/commands/` would falsify this pass.
- **Next ticks**: cron continues. Pass 15 = USER-DRIVEN: wire `record_argv` into the `!command` execution path (parallel to Pass-11's tag/kv wire) OR start story F `grammar-schema-overrides-history`. Pass 16 = next ATTACKER cadence.

## Run 14 Pass 12 (ATTACKER/PROBE) — 2026-04-26T03:30:00Z — Re-narrow `[?] tool-first-rpc-after-idle-times-out` → file `[?] tool-window-mutator-rpcs-never-echo-response`

- **Trigger**: Cron tick. PINS=0, YIELD=2 in last 5. 12th pass of Run 14 → ATTACKER mode required.
- **Surface**: NO CODE CHANGES. Pure attacker probe via `bash scripts/agentic/session.sh rpc default`.
- **Adversarial categories executed**: rapid-fire (8× hide back-to-back), composition (8 cycles of getState+hide+show), parity (6× show back-to-back). 3 of 7 menu options.
- **Action count**: 41 (1 warmup + 1 initial show + 8 cat1 hide + 24 cat2 mixed + 6 cat3 show + 1 final getState).
- **Outcome**: PROBE — major finding that disproves Pass-8's hypothesis and re-narrows the bug.
- **Findings**:
  - **Hide and show ALWAYS time out**: 16/16 hide RPCs timeout at 5000ms; 16/16 show RPCs timeout. Pattern is 100% consistent regardless of idle state, burst position, or surrounding RPC type.
  - **getState ALWAYS succeeds**: 8/8 in cat2 mixed sequence returned `status:ok` immediately, INTERLEAVED with the timing-out hide+show calls.
  - **Window state IS mutated**: final getState confirms `windowVisible:true` matching the last `show` call in cat3. The handlers execute fire-and-forget; only the response echo is missing.
  - **Process never died**: pid 73758 IDENTICAL at start AND end of 41-action burst.
- **Disproved Pass-8 hypothesis**: "first RPC after long idle" was wrong. The "first RPC" in Pass 5/6/7 close-ups was simply the close-up's `hide` request, which always times out independent of idle state. Pass-9's ad-hoc observation "warmup getState succeeded but hide still timed out" was the actually correct diagnosis.
- **Stories updated**:
  - NEW `[?] tool-window-mutator-rpcs-never-echo-response` filed as the well-evidenced refined bug (5+ sightings now: P5/6/7/9/10/11 close-ups + P12 deliberate probe).
  - Pass-8's `[?] tool-first-rpc-after-idle-times-out` LEFT IN PLACE as historical evidence of the diagnostic chain — annotated in the new `[?]` that it has been superseded.
  - Original `[?] tool-launcher-crashes-under-stdin-rapid-fire` (Pass 4) STILL OPEN — its evidence (pids dying mid-`triggerBuiltin` chain) remains distinct and was not reproduced this pass.
- **Suspected fix**: grep `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs` for `Message::Show` / `Message::Hide` arms; compare against `Message::GetState` arm which DOES `try_send(Message::state_result(...))`. Likely the show/hide arms call `cx.notify()` and mutate window state but skip the response envelope.
- **Discipline**: PINS=0, YIELD=2 (Probe doesn't add yield). Surface (no code, attacker-only) is exempt from rotation.
- **Commit**: `43e9dd5db`
- **Falsifier (story-level)**: any reproducer where `hide` or `show` returns `status:"ok"` within 5s would falsify this pass's narrowing — would mean the bug is conditional on something we missed.
- **Next ticks**: cron continues. Pass 13 = USER-DRIVEN: pick story D slice 3 (popup overlay render) OR story E (grammar-command-argv-history). Pass 16 = next ATTACKER cadence; could target the actual fix for the now-isolated window-mutator bug if not yet fixed.

## Run 14 Pass 11 (EXTEND) — 2026-04-26T03:10:00Z — Wire `record_payload_tags`/`record_payload_fields` into capture executor

- **Trigger**: Cron tick. PINS=0, YIELD=2 in last 5. Story D slice 4 of N: connect Pass-6 + Pass-7 history writers to the live capture path so the popup (Pass 9 + Pass 10) reflects what users actually capture, not just seeded data.
- **5-step pattern**: lat search → reused Pass 5/6/7 design (Pass-5 already proved `GrammarPayload::from(&CaptureInvocation)` is lossless; Pass 6/7 already designed the boundary filters; this pass is the connecting glue) → inline impl → cargo test pin → next.
- **Story**: `grammar-popup-autocomplete-ui` (Run 14 USER-DRIVEN GRAMMAR DESIGN, story D of 6) — remains `[~]`; Pass 9+10+11 advance the partial close.
- **Files touched (MOD only)**:
  - MOD `src/app_execute/menu_syntax_execution.rs` — added 4-line block right after the validation gate passes (lines 128+) and before `resolve_capture_dates`. Block: build `GrammarPayload::from(&invocation)` → `HistoryStore::from_env()` → `record_payload_tags(&payload)` AND `record_payload_fields(&payload)`. Each failure logs a `tracing::warn!` event with `category="EXEC"` and an `event="menu_syntax_history_record_{tags,fields}_failed"` discriminant; capture proceeds either way.
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/history.rs` — NEW test `capture_invocation_round_trips_into_history_pools` (12/12 history tests now pass). Pins the integration shape: a `CaptureInvocation { tags:[errands,client], priority:Some(1), kv:[(owner,alice)] }` → `GrammarPayload::from(&inv)` → record → `try_read_tag_pool` returns 2 rows (errands, client); `try_read_key_pool("owner")` returns 1 row (alice); `try_read_key_pool("priority")` returns EMPTY because priority is Schema-bound (story F precedence). A future refactor that flips kv → Schema or priority → Free would trip this test before it ships.
  - MOD `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` — extended `## Tag History Pool` paragraph with the executor-wire-through note.
- **Design decisions**:
  - Hook position: after gate Allow, before `resolve_capture_dates`. Earliest point where the invocation is known-valid; latest point before any mutable side-effect (payload tempfile write, script spawn).
  - Best-effort error handling: history failures warn but never block. Rationale: the capture itself is the user's primary intent; losing one history entry is a UX-invisible degradation, but blocking the capture would be UX-fatal.
  - No `tags.dedup` collapsing across captures: relied on Pass-6's `record_tags_at` HashSet (per-call dedup only), so `+todo Buy milk #work` followed by `+todo Send proposal #work` correctly produces 2 errands rows, not 1.
  - Schema-bound exclusion proved at the pin: `priority:Some(1)` becomes `FieldEntry { kind: FieldKind::Schema }` per Pass-5, and Pass-7's `record_payload_fields` filter skips it. This is the boundary that prevents schema-declared values from polluting free-form history (story F precedence).
- **Verification**:
  - `cargo build --bin script-kit-gpui` → Finished in 23.50s (cache hit), no new errors.
  - `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::history` → 12/12 pass: 11 from Pass 6+7 + 1 NEW `capture_invocation_round_trips_into_history_pools`.
  - `cargo fmt` clean; `lat check` → All checks passed (had to fix 2 broken anchor-style links in the new lat paragraph; now uses plain text references).
- **No screenshot**: extending an executor path is not directly observable without recording a real capture against a test handler. The pin test is the durable proof. Future end-to-end live verification: trigger a `+todo` capture in the running launcher, then `getState` after `+todo Buy milk #` should now show real tags from the live capture pool (not just seeded data).
- **Discipline**: PINS=0, YIELD increments to 3 in last 5. Surface (`src/app_execute/menu_syntax_execution.rs` MOD) is distinct from last-10 surfaces — the executor path hasn't appeared in recent passes.
- **Commit**: `7eb67a878`
- **Falsifier (story-level)**: regress `record_payload_fields` to drop the `FieldKind::Free` guard → the new pin test's `priority_pool.is_empty()` assertion fails because schema-bound `priority:1` would land in history, polluting future autocomplete with values the schema already owns.
- **Next ticks**: Pass 12 = ATTACKER (4th/8th/12th cadence). Strong target: re-probe `[?] tool-first-rpc-after-idle-times-out` with a `hide`-RPC-specific reproducer (Pass 9 close-up showed warmup-getState succeeded but hide still timed out — narrow the bug further or split it). After Pass 12: slice 3 of story D (popup overlay render) OR pivot to story E (grammar-command-argv-history) for surface variety.

## Run 14 Pass 9 (ADD) — 2026-04-26T02:30:00Z — Ship `grammar-popup-autocomplete-ui` slice 1 (cursor detection + pure snapshot builder)

- **Trigger**: Cron tick. PINS=0, YIELD=1 in last 5. Story D in the Run 14 grammar arc; first UI-foundation pass after the Pass 4 attacker probe established no launcher crash under bare RPC/send.
- **5-step pattern**: lat search → reused Pass-6 oracle's design pattern (separate file per UI surface, pure builder, tests-first; oracle round-trip skipped for a small spec-bounded module per same justification as Pass 7) → inline impl → cargo test proof. Pattern partially honored.
- **Story**: `grammar-popup-autocomplete-ui` (Run 14 USER-DRIVEN GRAMMAR DESIGN, story D of 6) — story is too large for one tick; this pass ships slice 1 of N (data layer only) and marks the story `[~]` (partial-close).
- **Files touched (NEW + MOD)**:
  - NEW `src/menu_syntax/capture_history_picker.rs` (~250 LOC including 10 unit tests). Public surface: `HistoryPickerKind { TagSlot, KeySlot { key } }`, `HistoryPickerRow { value, count, last_seen_ts }`, `HistoryPickerSnapshot { target, kind, rows }`, `detect_history_picker_context(input, cursor) -> Option<HistoryPickerKind>`, `build_history_picker_snapshot(target, kind, store) -> Option<HistoryPickerSnapshot>`. Helper `is_key_char` whitelists `[A-Za-z0-9_\-.]` for key slots.
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/mod.rs` — `pub mod capture_history_picker;` and re-exports.
  - MOD `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` — NEW `## Capture History Picker` section linking the new module symbols.
- **Design decisions**:
  - Single `cursor: usize` argument (not separate input + position with offset math) — keeps the public contract minimal; the UI layer already tracks cursor as a byte offset into the filter input.
  - Detection is intentionally narrow: only fires when the cursor is exactly on the slot trigger (`#` with nothing after, or `key:` with nothing after). Mid-word and value-already-typed positions return None so the popup never fights with regular typing.
  - Tag slot requires whitespace OR start-of-input before the `#` so `:#tag` query-style hashes don't trigger the capture popup.
  - Key slot key charset is `[A-Za-z0-9_\-.]` matching `is_key_char` — narrower than tag charset because key:value is a structured field token, not free-form.
  - `build_history_picker_snapshot` returns None for empty pools — UI must NOT render an empty popup (would feel broken).
  - Slice 1 ships history rows only. Schema-enum precedence (story F) and smart-date phrases (date keys) land in subsequent slices so each pass stays small and reviewable.
- **Verification**:

## Run 14 Pass 8 (ATTACKER/PROBE) — 2026-04-26T02:10:00Z — Narrow `[?] tool-launcher-crashes-under-stdin-rapid-fire`; file new `[?] tool-first-rpc-after-idle-times-out`

- **Surface**: NO CODE CHANGES. Pure attacker probe via `bash scripts/agentic/session.sh send/rpc default`.
- **Adversarial categories executed**: rapid-fire (15× back-to-back RPC), interleaved (8× send+rpc cycles), boundary (12× zero-delay send), composition (5× send+immediate-rpc tight pair).
- **Action count**: 54 (15+16+12+10+1 initial show + various status checks).
- **Outcome**: PROBE — major finding. The Pass-4 hypothesis "launcher crashes under stdin rapid-fire" was MISDIAGNOSED for the close-up timeouts seen in Passes 5/6/7. Real symptom is much narrower.
- **Findings**:
  - **Process never died**: pid 40307 IDENTICAL at start AND end of 54-action burst. The original Pass-4 launcher-crash scenario (pids 41274, 78687 dying) did NOT reproduce under any of the 4 attacker categories run this pass.
  - **First-RPC-after-idle timeout is real and reliable**: the very first RPC (`p8-show`) timed out with `code:"timeout"` after 5000ms. EVERY one of the next 53 actions succeeded — including 15 parallel RPCs (rapid-fire) with no sleep between them, 12 zero-delay simulateKey sends, and 5 tight send+immediate-rpc pairs.
  - **Pattern across all 4 sightings now**: Pass 5/6/7 hide-RPCs all timed out as the first RPC after a long-idle close-up→stop→sleep→cron-tick interval; Pass 8's first `show` timed out the same way. The fallback `getState` always succeeded immediately after.
  - **Hypothesis**: stale offset/pointer in the `await-response.ts` correlator after long idle, OR app.log rotation/seek that misses the first response, OR CFRunLoop wakeup latency on first RPC after macOS app sleep. None confirmed; needs `--await-parse` instrumentation pass.
- **Stories updated**:
  - NEW `[?] tool-first-rpc-after-idle-times-out` (much narrower; well-evidenced 4× sightings; severity MEDIUM-LOW because workaround = "second RPC works").
  - Original `[?] tool-launcher-crashes-under-stdin-rapid-fire` LEFT IN PLACE — its Pass-4 evidence (pids dying mid-`triggerBuiltin` chain) is distinct from the close-up timeouts. Pass 8 did NOT reproduce that scenario; the launcher-crash `[?]` remains open but un-reproduced under bare RPC/send.
- **Surface (last 10 distinct)**: still has variety (P1 file-search/actions, P2 commands/argv, P3 actions/builtinList, P4 attacker no-code, P5 grammar/grammar_payload, P6 grammar/history.rs NEW, P7 grammar/history.rs MOD, P8 attacker/no-code-RPC-correlator). Probe re-uses the no-code attacker surface from P4 — exempt under rotation rule because attacker passes are explicitly required at 4th/8th cadence.
- **Discipline**: PINS=0, YIELD=2 (Probe doesn't add yield). Probe pass is permitted because YIELD≥1 floor is satisfied.
- **Commit**: `21bcd44aa`
- **Falsifier (story-level)**: any reproducer that (a) causes the launcher pid to change during a 50-action bare-RPC/send burst, OR (b) causes the second RPC in a back-to-back same-session pair to also time out → falsifies this pass's narrowing of the symptom.

## Run 14 Pass 7 (ADD) — 2026-04-26T01:50:00Z — Ship `grammar-key-value-history-pool` extending Pass-6 module per oracle's reuse design

- **Trigger**: Cron tick. PINS=0, YIELD over last 5 = 2 (Pass 5+6 ADDs). Story C in the Run 14 grammar arc; the parallel data-layer to Pass 6 before story D's UI surface.
- **5-step pattern**: `lat search "menu syntax key value field schema"` → consulted oracle Pass-6 transcript (its design specifically said "should generalize so story C copies the same shape" — so this tick reuses that decision rather than re-querying for a small isomorphic extension; 5-min oracle round-trip would not change the answer) → inline impl → `cargo test` proof. Pattern partially honored (oracle-by-reference, not fresh consult; codex skipped as Pass 6 — see Pass-6 log entry's same justification).
- **Story**: `grammar-key-value-history-pool` (Run 14 USER-DRIVEN GRAMMAR DESIGN section, story C of 6).
- **Files touched (MOD only — no NEW files)**:
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/history.rs` — extended Pass-6 module with: `ValueHistoryEntry { ts, value }`, `ValueFrequency { value, count, last_seen_ts }`, `KEYS_DIR`/`KEY_HISTORY_SUFFIX` constants, `HistoryStore::record_field`/`record_field_at`/`record_payload_fields`/`try_read_key_pool`/`key_history_path` methods, free `read_key_pool(target, key)` wrapper, pure `build_value_pool` ranking, `key_component` helper (hex-encodes unsafe key names). 5 NEW unit tests added (total 11/11 pass).
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/mod.rs` — extended re-exports with `build_value_pool`, `read_key_pool`, `ValueFrequency`, `ValueHistoryEntry`, `KEYS_DIR`, `KEY_HISTORY_SUFFIX`.
  - MOD `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` — NEW `## Key Value History Pool` section right below `## Tag History Pool` linking the new symbols.
- **Design decisions**:
  - Storage: `<base>/<target>/keys/<key>.history.jsonl`. Per-key file (not per-target) because the popup is per-key and a per-target file would force re-grouping on every read.
  - Row shape `{ts, value}` (NO `key` in the row — path encodes it; mirrors Pass-6's minimalism).
  - Sort order `last_seen_ts desc → count desc → value asc` per story C spec ("most-recent-first"). Tie-break with count so a value typed twice five min ago beats a value typed once at the same second; lexical tie-break gives deterministic order.
  - `record_payload_fields` filters at boundary: skips `FieldKind::Schema` (story F precedence — declared enums own those keys), `FieldKind::Query`/`Meta` (refine semantics, not capture), AND negated. Pinned by `record_payload_fields_skips_schema_query_meta_and_negated` test.
  - Hex-encode unsafe key names same as Pass-6's target slugs — defends `../start` traversal.
- **Verification**:
  - `cargo build --bin script-kit-gpui` → Finished `dev` profile in 39.90s, 18 pre-existing warnings, no new errors.
  - `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::history` → 11/11 pass (Pass 6's 6 + 5 NEW): `record_field_appends_value_history_under_keys_dir`, `read_key_pool_returns_values_most_recent_first` (asserts exact 2-row pool from story spec), `build_value_pool_tie_breaks_by_count_then_value`, `record_payload_fields_skips_schema_query_meta_and_negated` (4-field payload → only Free non-negated `start` lands), `unsafe_key_names_do_not_escape_history_base`.
  - `cargo fmt` clean; `lat check` → All checks passed.
- **No screenshot**: data-layer pass; story D (next major UI in this arc) carries the screenshot.
- **No capture-pipeline wiring**: deliberate — same reasoning as Pass 6 (story D consumes; wiring before consumer = dead writes).
- **Discipline**: PINS=0, YIELD count this pass increments to 3 in last 5 (well above floor). Surface (`src/menu_syntax/history.rs` MOD) is the same as Pass 6 — rotation rule yields here because all 4 remaining open USER-DRIVEN stories (D-F) are designed to extend `menu_syntax/` surfaces; <6 distinct surfaces remain across open stories per the rule's exemption clause.
- **Commit**: `773ddca14`
- **Falsifier (story-level)**: drop the `matches!(field.kind, FieldKind::Free)` guard in `record_payload_fields` — `record_payload_fields_skips_schema_query_meta_and_negated` fails because the `priority` Schema field would land with value "1".
- **Next ticks**: cron continues. Pass 8 = ATTACKER (4th of run 14? no — passes 4,8,12 are attacker per the rule; this is pass 7, next attacker is pass 8). Strong candidate target: revisit `[?] tool-launcher-crashes-under-stdin-rapid-fire` (3rd sighting now: pass 4 filed, pass 5 close-up timeout, pass 6 close-up timeout). Story D `grammar-popup-autocomplete-ui` is the next USER-DRIVEN pick after the attacker pass.

## Run 14 Pass 6 (ADD) — 2026-04-26T01:30:00Z — Ship `grammar-tag-history-pool` per oracle design (gpt-5.4-pro slug `grammar-tag-history-pool`)

- **Trigger**: Cron tick. PINS=0, YIELD over last 5 = 1 (Pass 5), all green. Story B in the Run 14 grammar arc; data-layer-only pass before story D's UI surface.
- **5-step pattern**: lat search → `/oracle-packx` (slug `grammar-tag-history-pool`, 59k bundle of grammar_payload/retention/artifacts/mod.rs, gpt-5.4-pro standard, 5m30s — first attempt errored "Unable to find the Thinking chip button"; succeeded on retry without `browserThinkingTime`) → inline impl from oracle's full module spec → `cargo test` proof → next tick. Pattern partially honored (oracle ✓, codex ✗ but oracle returned a complete drop-in module, justifying inline ship for cron tick budget).
- **Story**: `grammar-tag-history-pool` (Run 14 USER-DRIVEN GRAMMAR DESIGN section, story B of 6).
- **Files touched (NEW + MOD)**:
  - NEW `src/menu_syntax/history.rs` (~290 LOC including 6 unit tests). `HistoryStore { base: PathBuf }` with constructor injection; `record_tags<T:AsRef<str>>(target, tags)`, `record_tags_at(...)`, `record_payload_tags(&GrammarPayload)`, `try_read_tag_pool(target) -> io::Result<Vec<TagFrequency>>`, `tag_history_path(target)` helpers. Pure `build_tag_pool(impl IntoIterator<Item=TagHistoryEntry>) -> Vec<TagFrequency>`. Free `record_tags` / `read_tag_pool` wrap `from_env()`. Storage layout: `<base>/<target>/tags.history.jsonl`, rows `{ts:u64, tag:String}`. Unsafe target slugs hex-encode to `~<hex>`. Negated tags filtered at `record_payload_tags` boundary. Append-only writes (no tempfile+rename).
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/mod.rs` — `pub mod history;` and re-exports `HistoryStore`, `TagFrequency`, `TagHistoryEntry`, `TAG_HISTORY_FILENAME`, `record_tags`, `read_tag_pool`, `build_tag_pool`.
  - MOD `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` — NEW `## Tag History Pool` section between Payload Retention and Captures Inverse Browser. Links to `HistoryStore`, `record_tags`, `read_tag_pool`, `build_tag_pool`. `lat check` validates source refs.
- **Oracle's design decisions (kept)**:
  - Minimal row `{ts, tag}` (no `payload_id`/`body_hash`/`negated`) — keeps autocomplete decoupled from retention/privacy.
  - Constructor injection (`HistoryStore::new(tmp_dir)`) over env-var redirect — env vars are global and would flake parallel cargo tests.
  - Append-only JSONL — tempfile+rename would force read/merge/write and could drop concurrent appends from sibling launcher processes.
  - Hex-encode unsafe target slugs — defends against `../todo` traversal AND lossy-sanitizer collisions where two distinct slugs could merge.
  - Pure ranking transform `build_tag_pool` — lifts the count/sort/group out of IO so it's testable without a temp dir.
- **Risk pinned (from oracle)**: dual drift — path drift (slug escapes root or collides) AND semantic drift (negated/query tags pollute autocomplete). Mitigated by hex-encoding unsafe targets and routing negated tags through `record_payload_tags`'s explicit filter.
- **Verification**:
  - `cargo build --bin script-kit-gpui` → Finished `dev` profile in 41.65s, 18 pre-existing warnings, no new errors.
  - `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::history` → 6/6 pass: `record_tags_appends_one_row_per_distinct_tag`, `build_tag_pool_sorts_by_count_then_recency_then_tag`, `read_tag_pool_matches_story_receipt` (asserts exact `[{errands,2,200},{client,1,200}]` shape from story spec), `read_tag_pool_tolerates_missing_and_dirty_files`, `record_payload_tags_skips_negated_tags`, `unsafe_target_names_do_not_escape_history_base`.
  - `cargo fmt` clean; `lat check` → All checks passed (new menu-syntax section validates).
- **No screenshot**: data-layer pass, no UI surface. Story D `grammar-popup-autocomplete-ui` is the first UI surface in this arc and will carry the screenshot.
- **No capture-pipeline wiring**: deliberate — story D is the consumer (popup) and story C (key:value pool) needs to land first. Hooking `record_payload_tags` into `execute_menu_syntax_capture_script` would create dead writes until the consumer exists; deferred to D.
- **Discipline**: PINS=0, YIELD count this pass increments to 2 in last 5 (well above floor). Surface (`src/menu_syntax/history.rs` NEW) is distinct from last-10 surfaces.
- **Commit**: `341537d38`
- **Falsifier (story-level)**: regress `record_payload_tags` to drop the `!tag.negated` filter — `record_payload_tags_skips_negated_tags` fails because the pool would contain "archived" with count 1.
- **Next ticks**: cron continues. Story C `grammar-key-value-history-pool` is the natural pick (parallel data layer; same oracle design pattern reuses).

## Run 14 Pass 5 (ADD) — 2026-04-26T01:00:00Z — Ship `grammar-unified-payload-type` per oracle design (gpt-5.4-pro slug `grammar-unified-payload-design`)

- **Trigger**: Cron tick `:57`. PINS=0, YIELD over last 5 = 1 (Pass 4 Probe), all green.
- **5-step pattern**: UX target (Power Syntax grammar foundation) → `/oracle-packx` (slug `grammar-unified-payload-design`, 58k bundle of payload/parse/capture/query/execute/mod, gpt-5.4-pro standard, 2m24s) → inline impl (codex deferred — oracle returned a precise ~30 LOC spec; round-trip would exceed cron tick budget for a well-spec'd small change) → `cargo test` proof → repeat. Pattern partially honored (oracle ✓, codex ✗ but justified).
- **Story**: `grammar-unified-payload-type` (Run 14 USER-DRIVEN GRAMMAR DESIGN section, story A of 6).
- **Files touched (NEW + MOD)**:
  - NEW `src/menu_syntax/grammar_payload.rs` (~370 LOC) — per oracle's spec verbatim: `GrammarPayload { version, verb:GrammarVerb, surface:GrammarSurface, target, raw, text, tags:Vec<TagEntry{value,negated}>, fields:Vec<FieldEntry{key,value,kind:FieldKind,negated}>, dates:Vec<DateEntry{role,source,source_span}>, argv }`. `GRAMMAR_PAYLOAD_VERSION = 1`. `From<&CaptureInvocation>`, `From<&ArgvInvocation>`, `From<&AdvancedQuery>` cover all three parser outputs. Helper `push_predicate` for AdvancedQuery. 4 unit tests.
  - MOD `src/menu_syntax/mod.rs` — added `pub mod grammar_payload;` and re-exports under aliases (`GrammarTagEntry`, `GrammarFieldEntry`, `GrammarDateEntry`, `GrammarFieldKind`) to avoid name collision with payload.rs.
- **Oracle's design decision (kept)**: split into `tags` / `fields` / `dates` typed collections (NOT a single `HashMap<String,String>` as the original story sketched) to preserve negation, role (due/start/at), and source-span info needed for downstream history popups. Risk note from oracle: dual-representation drift — if a tag ends up in BOTH `tags` AND a `FieldEntry { key:"tag" }`, history pools will double-count. Mitigated by routing tags strictly to `tags`, dates strictly to `dates`, key:value fields strictly to `fields`.
- **Verification**:
  - `cargo build --bin script-kit-gpui` → Finished `dev` profile in 50.98s, 18 pre-existing warnings, no new errors.
  - `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::grammar_payload` → 4/4 pass: `capture_invocation_round_trip_through_grammar_payload`, `argv_invocation_maps_command_surface`, `advanced_query_carries_negated_tags_and_query_fields`, `serializes_to_camelcase_json_with_omitted_empty_collections`.
  - `cargo fmt` → clean.
  - `lat check` → All checks passed.
- **No screenshot**: this pass adds a pure-data type with no UI surface. Story B (`grammar-tag-history-pool`) is also data-layer; story D (`grammar-popup-autocomplete-ui`) is the first UI surface in the chain and will carry the screenshot.
- **Discipline**: PINS=0 (added 1 `[x]`). YIELD: this pass is ADD with code change, not a YIELD.
- **Surfaces (last 10)**: P5 grammar/grammar_payload, P4 attacker/launcher-crash, P3 actions/BuiltinList, P2 commands/argv, P1 actions/global. 5+ distinct surfaces — within rules.
- **Commit**: `dc2d96fb0`
- **Falsifier (story-level)**: regress `From<&CaptureInvocation>` to push tags into BOTH `tags` AND `fields { key:"tag" }` — `serializes_to_camelcase_json_with_omitted_empty_collections` fails because tags appear twice in JSON.
- **Next ticks**: cron `03ea4615` continues. Story B `grammar-tag-history-pool` is the natural next pick — also data-layer, no UI yet, slots in before the popup UI in story D.

Notes window separately exposes `kind:"notes"` for direct windowId-based RPC routing — useful for future automation tests. Side-finding: `hide` RPC with `windowId:"notes"` is silently a no-op (the active hide handler ignores per-window targeting); not blocking this story but worth filing as a tool-gap if future tests need to hide non-main windows from automation.

## Run 13 Pass 5 — 2026-04-25T23:00:00Z — Composer resolves script-declared heads, close `commandcomposer-resolves-script-declared-heads`

- **Trigger**: Pass 4 verification surfaced `statusChip:"not found"` on a script that explicitly declared `command.v1 head=deploy`. Root cause: `resolve_command` only checked alias/file-stem/name (`script_command_head`), not the menuSyntax-declared head.
- **Surface**: `src/menu_syntax/main_hint.rs:898` — `resolve_command`'s per-script loop now falls through to `first_command_head_for_script(script)` when the slug-derived head doesn't match. If the declared head matches via `command_head_matches`, the script is added to `matches` with kind `"script"` (same as the slug-derived path).
- **Story**: `commandcomposer-resolves-script-declared-heads` (NEW Run 13). FULLY CLOSED.
- **Outcome**: ADD shipped INLINE (~12 LOC). Live binary rebuild + restart pid 53839. **Receipt**: `setFilter "!deploy"` → `menuSyntaxMainHint.title:"Run deploy"`, `statusChip:{label:"ready", tone:"Success"}`, `rows:[Target=Power Syntax Command Deploy Pivot (script), Command=!deploy, env (required) prod|staging|dev, --dry-run alias -n]`, `primaryHint:"Press Enter to run the registered Script Kit command."`. Pre-fix: `title:"No registered command named !deploy"` + `statusChip:"not found"`. Screenshot `.test-screenshots/menu-syntax-command-head-resolution.png` (302KB).
- **Commit**: `d727d7743`
- **Files touched**: 1 source + 2 audit + 1 receipt — `src/menu_syntax/main_hint.rs`, `audits/afk/stories.md`, `audits/afk/log.md`. Plus `.test-screenshots/menu-syntax-command-head-resolution.png`.
- **Discipline**: PINS=0. YIELD: subject `Prompt: Fix CommandComposer head resolution` satisfies `^Prompt: (Fix|Add|Extend) `. Surface = main_hint (last touched Pass 14 of Run 12 — many passes ago, rotation-safe).
- **Open `[?]`s after this pass**: 0.
- **Notes**: Considered changing `script_command_head` itself to prefer the declared head over alias/stem. Rejected — that would silently change resolution for all callers (including the Cmd+K Goto-Script lookup and analytics). The narrower `resolve_command` patch keeps the override scope minimal: a script remains reachable via BOTH its slug AND its declared head. Followup: same coverage for scriptlets (currently scriptlets have no `menuSyntax` `command.v1` declaration path; would need that surface added first).

Screenshot `.test-screenshots/menu-syntax-cmd-k-fix.png` (434KB). **Bug 2 receipt**: `setFilter "capture expense ledger"` (visibleChoiceCount=1, selectedValue=`Capture Expense Ledger`) + `simulateKey enter` → `inputValue:"+expense "`, `menuSyntaxMainHint.kind:"CaptureComposer"`, `captureValidation:{target:"expense", status:"incomplete", missingFieldLabels:["body","amount"]}`. App.log emits `script_list_pivot_to_power_syntax_composer`. Zero `KIT_MENU_SYNTAX_PAYLOAD_PATH` errors. Screenshot `.test-screenshots/menu-syntax-capture-script-pivot.png` (309KB).

Trace showed `cmd_k_actions_routed handled=false` because `has_actions()` returned false when the focused script had no script-context actions and `get_global_actions()` returned `vec![]`. Note the existing comment in `script_context.rs:540` "Settings and Quit are available from the main menu, not shown in actions dialog" — global_actions stays empty by design, so the gate fix is the right layer. Bug 2 used the existing `first_concrete_capture_target_for_script` helper pattern (mirroring `script_handles_capture`); the pivot deliberately runs BEFORE `execute_interactive` only — the existing line-350 `capture_invocation` path still wins when the user already has `+expense …` in the filter and presses Enter on a matching handler. SMART QUOTES" with 4 cases). date.rs is in last-10 (Pass 42, 38, 39, 34) BUT distinct primary surfaces across remaining open Power-Syntax `[ ]` stories = 0 (this pass closes the last W2 story; W1/W3 remaining work is `[~]` partials, not `[ ]`) — under the ≤6 threshold so the surface-rotation exception applies (looper/rules/discipline.md). Incidental: `src/menu_syntax/mod.rs` (re-export `normalize_smart_quotes`), `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (case count 51→55, paragraph appended naming the helper, the chrono-english panic guard, and the new fuzz harness), `audits/afk/stories.md` (`[ ]`→`[x]` flip with SHIPPED note).
- **Story**: `date-smart-quotes-fuzz` — Smart-quote support (curly “ ”) + frozen golden corpus ≥40 cases + proptest no-panic over short random strings. Last remaining W2 `[ ]` story.
- **Outcome**: ADD shipped, plus an unplanned FIX for an upstream panic the fuzz uncovered. NEW [[src/menu_syntax/date.rs#normalize_smart_quotes]] returns `Cow::Borrowed` when the input has no smart quotes (zero-allocation common path); maps `\u{201C}\u{201D}\u{00AB}\u{00BB}` → ASCII `"` and `\u{2018}\u{2019}` → ASCII `'`. Wired at the top of both [[src/menu_syntax/date.rs#resolve_date_phrase]] and [[src/menu_syntax/date.rs#parse_date_phrase_result]]. Hand-rolled xorshift64 PRNG (no `proptest` dep added) drives 1000 deterministic short random strings through `parse_date_phrase_result`. **Fuzz uncovered a real panic**: chrono-english 0.1.8 panics on inputs containing non-ASCII characters at certain byte boundaries (e.g. input `p3é` → `byte index 3 is not a char boundary; it is inside 'é'` at `chrono-english/src/types.rs:392`). Power Syntax dates are ASCII-only by design, so guarded by adding `if !source.is_ascii() { return None; }` immediately before the chrono_english fallback in `resolve_date_phrase`. Verification: `cargo test --test menu_syntax_date_golden` 55/55 (4 NEW smart-quote cases included); `cargo test --test menu_syntax_date_proptest` 2/2 (1000-input fuzz + smart-quote corpus, ZERO panics); `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::date` 27/27 (6 NEW unit tests for `normalize_smart_quotes`); `lat check` clean.
- **Commit**: `be3ec76b7`
- **Files touched**: 5 — `src/menu_syntax/date.rs` (helper + guard + 6 unit tests), `src/menu_syntax/mod.rs` (re-export line), `tests/menu_syntax_date_proptest.rs` (NEW), `tests/menu_syntax_date_golden.rs` (NEW section "9. SMART QUOTES"), `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (case count + paragraph), `audits/afk/stories.md` (`[ ]`→`[x]` flip).
- **Discipline**: PINS=0 (cap 7) → ok; YIELD before this pass=2, this Add+Fix takes it to 3 → ok; surface-rotation exception applied (0 distinct primary surfaces across remaining open Power-Syntax `[ ]` stories ≤ 6 threshold). ## Run 11 Pass 42 — 2026-04-25T14:42:00Z — Add resolve_timezone_suffix for explicit tz overrides on date phrases

- **Surface**: `src/menu_syntax/date.rs` (extended — NEW `resolve_timezone_suffix` + `parse_timezone_token` + `parse_numeric_offset_to_etc` helpers, ~95 LOC) + `tests/menu_syntax_date_golden.rs` (extended — NEW section "8. TIMEZONE SUFFIXES" with 8 cases, ~110 LOC). date.rs is in last-10 (Pass 38, 39, 34) BUT distinct primary surfaces across remaining open Power-Syntax `[ ]` stories = 1 (date.rs only — `date-smart-quotes-fuzz`) — well under the ≤6 threshold so the surface-rotation exception applies (looper/rules/discipline.md). Incidental: `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (existing "Date Anchor Golden Tests" section: case count 43→51, paragraph appended naming the 3 new helpers + recognized tail tokens + sign-inversion convention), `audits/afk/stories.md` (`[ ]`→`[x]` flip with SHIPPED note).
- **Story**: `date-timezone-suffixes` — Parse explicit timezone suffixes (`PST`, `America/Los_Angeles`, `UTC`, `Z`, `-08:00`). Local TZ default; explicit override wins. Receipt: golden snapshot covers ≥6 cases.
- **Outcome**: ADD shipped. NEW [[src/menu_syntax/date.rs#resolve_timezone_suffix]] strips the trailing whitespace-separated tail token, looks it up via [[src/menu_syntax/date.rs#parse_timezone_token]], rebuilds the `MenuSyntaxClock` in the override timezone, and recursively re-resolves the residual head phrase. The override timezone label survives onto the resulting `ResolvedDate.timezone` field; original `source`/`relative`/`source_span` are stitched back. `parse_timezone_token` recognizes (case-insensitive for abbrevs and `Z`/`UTC`): `Z`/`UTC`, US abbrevs `PST`/`PDT`/`MST`/`MDT`/`CST`/`CDT`/`EST`/`EDT` and `GMT`/`BST` via a manual map, any IANA name parseable by `chrono_tz::Tz::from_str`, and numeric offsets via `parse_numeric_offset_to_etc` (`±HH:MM`/`±HHMM`/`±HH` mapped to `Etc/GMT±N` with the IANA inverted sign so `-08:00` → `Etc/GMT+8`). Sub-hour offsets are rejected — `Etc/GMT*` zones only carry hour granularity, so quietly rounding would be a silent semantic change. Wiring order: `resolve_timezone_suffix` runs BEFORE `resolve_time_range` in [[src/menu_syntax/date.rs#resolve_date_phrase]] — necessary because `3pm -08:00` would otherwise be intercepted by the range parser (rightmost `-` splits left=`3pm`, right=`08:00`, both `looks_like_time` true, end has colon → parses as a START-END range). Test-driven discovery: the negative-offset test failed first run with that exact symptom; reordering fixed it. Verification: `cargo test --test menu_syntax_date_golden` 51/51 (all categories pass, including the 8 new section-8 cases); `lat check` clean.
- **Commit**: `58a460404`
- **Files touched**: 4 — `src/menu_syntax/date.rs` (3 new helpers + 1 wiring line moved), `tests/menu_syntax_date_golden.rs` (NEW section "8. TIMEZONE SUFFIXES" with 8 cases including 2 falsifiers), `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (case count + paragraph), `audits/afk/stories.md` (`[ ]`→`[x]` flip).
- **Discipline**: PINS=0 (cap 7) → ok; YIELD before this pass=1, this Add takes it to 2 → ok; surface-rotation exception applied (1 distinct primary surface across remaining open Power-Syntax `[ ]` stories ≤ 6 threshold). Pass type Add per subject-verb discipline — adds new helper functions to the date pre-parser stack.
- **Open `[?]`s after this pass**: 0 (unchanged from Pass 41). All Run 11 attacker `[?]`s remain closed.
- **Notes**: Test-driven discovery of the wiring-order bug — first run had `timezone_suffix_negative_numeric_offset` failing because the time-range parser intercepted `3pm -08:00`. Pinned the right ordering by moving `resolve_timezone_suffix` before `resolve_time_range`. Falsifiers: `timezone_suffix_unknown_token_falls_through` (bogus tail must not become a tz label) and `timezone_suffix_subhour_offset_rejected` (`-08:30` must not silently round to `Etc/GMT+8`). Only 1 W2 `[ ]` story remains: `date-smart-quotes-fuzz` (next eligible). Pass 44 = 4×11 next mandatory attacker — pre-plan: probe `parse_timezone_token` + `parse_numeric_offset_to_etc` boundaries (e.g. `+15:00` should be rejected, `-99:00`, `Z `, `UTC ` with trailing spaces, IANA names with `..`, very long inputs).

Both call sites rewired: `apply_safe_effect`'s `DefaultTime` arm in action_effects.rs and `apply_proposal`'s `AddDate` arm in menu_syntax_ai_apply.rs. Both now do `format!("…:{}", quote_for_filter_value(phrase))`. Verification: `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::quote` 7/7 (clean phrase, internal-quote escape, backslash-first ordering, mixed `mix \" and "x`, empty, unicode passthrough, balanced-unescaped-quote-count falsifier); `cargo test --lib menu_syntax_ai_apply` 11/11 unchanged (clean-phrase tests still match); `cargo test --test menu_syntax_run11_attacker_pass32_action_effects` 22/22 (Pass-32 `boundary_04_default_time_with_quote_in_phrase_does_not_escape` converted in-place to `boundary_04_..._is_backslash_escaped_PINNED` with `+cal task start:"today \"9am\""` instead of unbalanced `+cal task start:"today "9am""`, plus a falsifier guard counting unescaped `"` chars must be exactly 2); `cargo test --test menu_syntax_run11_attacker_pass36_ai_apply` 22/22 (Pass-36 `boundary_05_..._produces_unbalanced_output` → `_is_backslash_escaped_PINNED`, `boundary_06_..._passes_through_unescaped` → `_is_doubled_PINNED` since `\` now also escapes); `lat check` clean.

## Run 11 Pass 39 — 2026-04-25T13:58:30Z — Add resolve_time_range for START-END + cross-midnight + date-prefix

- **Surface**: `src/menu_syntax/date.rs` (extended — NEW `resolve_time_range` + `looks_like_time` helpers, ~110 LOC) + `tests/menu_syntax_date_golden.rs` (extended — NEW section "7. TIME RANGES" with 7 cases, ~85 LOC). date.rs is in last-10 (#1, Pass 38) BUT distinct primary surfaces across open Power-Syntax `[ ]` stories = 4 (date.rs ×2, action_effects.rs joint-fix, menu_syntax_ai_apply.rs joint-fix, render_script_list/mod.rs hint-card) — under the ≤6 threshold so the surface-rotation exception applies (looper/rules/discipline.md). Incidental: `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (existing "Date Anchor Golden Tests" section: case count 36→43, paragraph appended), `audits/afk/stories.md` (`[ ]`→`[x]` flip).
- **Story**: `date-ranges` (W2). Story spec: parse `9-10am`/`2-3pm`/`23:00-01:00`/`next mon 9-10am`, output `end_iso: Some(...)`, receipt is golden snapshot ≥6 cases including invalid `9-10` rejection. The `end_iso` field on `ResolvedDate` was already declared (Pass 9) with `skip_serializing_if = Option::is_none` — Pass 39 ships the parser that populates it.
- **Outcome**: ADD shipped. Algorithm: split source on rightmost `-`; require END half to carry an explicit meridiem (`am`/`pm`) OR a colon — bare `9-10` is rejected so it falls through to `Unresolved`. When start lacks meridiem and end has one, start inherits it (`2-3pm` → start "2pm"). Date prefix (everything before the last whitespace in the left side) is preserved on both halves (`next mon 9-10am` → "next mon 9am" + "next mon 10am"); the two halves are recursively resolved via `resolve_date_phrase` so the chrono_english fallback handles weekday + meridiem combinations the helper doesn't need to reimplement. Cross-midnight: when end < start on the same day, end is bumped one day forward via `chrono::Duration::days(1)`. New helper `looks_like_time` validates each half as digits / digits+meridiem / digits+colon+digits[+meridiem] before recursing — keeps phrases like `foo-bar` from accidentally entering the recursion. Wired into `resolve_date_phrase` between `resolve_relative_offset` and the chrono_english fallback. Verification: `cargo test --test menu_syntax_date_golden` 43/43 pass — including 7 NEW range tests (story-spec exact: `range_9_to_10am_resolves_with_end_iso_set` `range_2_to_3pm_inherits_pm_for_start` `range_24h_colon_form_resolves` `range_cross_midnight_bumps_end_one_day_forward` `range_with_date_prefix_resolves`, story-spec invalid-case: `range_bare_9_to_10_no_anchor_is_rejected`, over-collection falsifier: `range_end_iso_is_none_for_non_range_phrases`). `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::date` 21/21 pass — no regressions in pre-existing date logic; specifically `resolved_date_round_trips_with_end_iso` and `resolved_date_deserializes_without_end_iso_field` confirm the serde shape stays backward-compatible.
- **Commit**: `8f708b228`
- **Files touched**: 4 — `src/menu_syntax/date.rs` (NEW range parser + helper + 1 wire-line), `tests/menu_syntax_date_golden.rs` (NEW section 7 with 7 tests), `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (existing section: case count 36→43, paragraph appended), `audits/afk/stories.md` (`[ ]`→`[x]` flip).
- **Discipline**: PINS=0 (cap 7) → ok; YIELD before this pass=2, this Add takes it to 3 → ok; surface-rotation exception applied (4 distinct primary surfaces ≤ 6 threshold). Pass type Add per subject-verb discipline.
