Both share root cause; joint fix awaits surface aging — action_effects.rs is at last-10 #9 entering Pass 39, ages out at Pass 40 entry.
- **Notes**: 2 W2 `[ ]` stories remain — `date-timezone-suffixes`, `date-smart-quotes-fuzz`. The `resolve_time_range` recursion-via-resolve_date_phrase pattern means the full pre-parser stack (shorthand, absolute, relative, range) composes — a future timezone-suffix helper that strips `PST`/`Z` before re-resolving would compose naturally. Pass 40 = 4×10 → mandatory attacker pass; pre-plan candidates: probe `resolve_time_range` (cross-midnight edge cases, malformed prefixes, span calculations) OR `resolve_relative_offset` (overflow, casing, extra whitespace). Both are fresh.

- **Surface**: `src/menu_syntax/date.rs` (extended — NEW `resolve_relative_offset` + `split_compact` helpers, ~85 LOC) + `tests/menu_syntax_date_golden.rs` (extended — NEW section "6. RELATIVE OFFSETS" with 9 cases, ~95 LOC). date.rs is in last-10 (#2, Pass 34) BUT distinct primary surfaces across open Power-Syntax `[ ]` stories = 4 (date.rs ×3, render_script_list/mod.rs) — under the ≤6 threshold so the surface-rotation exception applies (looper/rules/discipline.md). Incidental: `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (existing "Date Anchor Golden Tests" section extended one paragraph + bumped case count 27→36), `audits/afk/stories.md` (`[ ]`→`[x]` flip).
- **Story**: `date-relative-offsets` (W2). Story spec: parse `in 30m`, `in 2h`, `in 3 days`, `in 2 weeks`, compound (`in 2 weeks 1h`); receipt is golden snapshot ≥8 cases. chrono_english (the project's current natural-language base) does NOT handle any `in N <unit>` phrasing — verified: ran the 6 strict-resolve cases against the unmodified parser and all 6 returned `Unresolved`. Pass 38 ships a custom pre-parser to fill the gap.
- **Outcome**: ADD shipped. NEW `resolve_relative_offset(source, clock) -> Option<ResolvedDate>` slotted between `resolve_absolute_date` and the chrono_english fallback in `resolve_date_phrase`. Algorithm: strip `in ` prefix; split rest on whitespace; for each token try `split_compact` (number+unit glued like `30m`) else parse the token as a number and consume the next token as a unit; map unit (case-insensitive) to seconds-per-unit (`s/m/h/d/w` + long forms); accumulate into `total_secs`; cap at 4 unit-pairs (defensive against pathological input); add `chrono::Duration::seconds(total_secs)` to `clock.now`; build a `ResolvedDate` with `Minute` granularity if any sub-day unit was seen, else `Date` granularity with `all_day=false` (so `in 3 days` from 12:00 lands at 12:00 three days later, not 00:00). Verification: `cargo test --test menu_syntax_date_golden` 36/36 pass — including all 9 NEW relative-offset tests AND the 3 cases originally written as `assert_unresolved_or_skip` (compact `in 30m`, `in 2h`, compound `in 2 weeks 1h`) which now auto-flip to Resolved because the helper supports them. `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::date` 21/21 pass — no regressions in pre-existing date logic.
- **Commit**: `b2f37e573`
- **Files touched**: 4 — `src/menu_syntax/date.rs` (NEW pre-parser + helper, +1 line in `resolve_date_phrase` to wire it), `tests/menu_syntax_date_golden.rs` (NEW section 6 with 9 tests), `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (existing section: case count 27→36, paragraph appended), `audits/afk/stories.md` (`[ ]`→`[x]` flip).
- **Discipline**: PINS=0 (cap 7) → ok; YIELD before this pass=1, this Add takes it to 2 → ok; surface-rotation exception applied (4 distinct primary surfaces ≤ 6 threshold). Pass type Add per subject-verb discipline.
- **Open `[?]`s after this pass**: still 2 (unchanged) — `ai-apply-add-date-phrase-not-quote-escaped` (Pass 36) + `action-effects-default-time-phrase-not-quote-escaped` (Pass 32). Both share root cause; joint fix awaits surface aging.
- **Notes**: 3 W2 `[ ]` stories remain — `date-ranges`, `date-timezone-suffixes`, `date-smart-quotes-fuzz` — all on date.rs. Surface rotation exception will keep applying because the W2 chain is the only remaining Power-Syntax `[ ]` cluster. Compound parsing pattern (whitespace-sum-by-units) extends naturally to `date-ranges` (`9-10am` → start_unit + end_unit) so the next pass can reuse the tokenizer skeleton.

## Run 11 Pass 37 — 2026-04-25T13:38:19Z — Add menu-syntax-doctor CLI binary

- **Surface**: NEW `src/bin/menu_syntax_doctor.rs` (CLI, ~140 LOC) + NEW `tests/menu_syntax_doctor_cli.rs` (7 integration tests, ~120 LOC) + `Cargo.toml` (3-line `[[bin]]` block). Fresh vs Run 11 last-10 — `src/bin/` has not appeared as a primary edit surface in this run; binary is brand-new. Incidental: `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (existing "Menu Syntax Doctor" section extended with CLI surface paragraph + receipt-test names), `audits/afk/stories.md` (existing `[x]` row for `sdk-menu-syntax-doctor` extended with CLOSED note for the previously-deferred CLI wiring half).
- **Story**: `sdk-menu-syntax-doctor` (W4). Story shipped `[x]` at Pass 11 with the validation engine + 21-test fixture but explicitly deferred the CLI subcommand wiring with a "follow-up commit can wire clap/argh in <50 LOC" note. Pass 37 lands that follow-up: a dedicated `[[bin]]` rather than reaching into `clap`/`argh` (the surface is small enough for a hand-rolled argv parser; no new dep adds runtime weight to the GPUI launcher binary).
- **Outcome**: ADD shipped. New binary `menu-syntax-doctor` exposes the engine on the command line. Reads JSON or YAML from `--path <file>`, `--path=<file>`, or stdin (JSON-first parse, YAML fallback so authors can pipe either format from frontmatter or a JSON catalog). Renders human-readable by default (`menu-syntax-doctor: N error(s), M warning(s)` + per-issue `[severity] $.path: msg` lines) or `--json` (pretty-printed `DoctorReport`). Exit codes: 0 = no errors (warnings allowed), 1 = `has_errors()`, 2 = argv/IO failure — distinct so an unreadable file or a `--path` missing-arg doesn't masquerade as a spec error. Verification: `cargo test --test menu_syntax_doctor_cli` 7/7 pass — covers good-stdin-human → 0, good-stdin-json → 0 + valid JSON payload, bad-stdin-human → 1 + "error" mention, bad-stdin-json → 1 + `severity:error` in payload, good-via-`--path` flag → 0, unknown-flag → 2 + "unknown argument" in stderr, YAML-via-stdin → 0 (dual-parse fallback). Pre-existing 21 doctor engine tests untouched.

## Run 11 Pass 35 — 2026-04-25T13:18:13Z — Add apply_proposal for inline-AI Tab/Enter/Esc

- **Surface**: NEW `src/app_impl/menu_syntax_ai_apply.rs` (pure module, ~250 LOC w/ tests). Fresh vs Run 11 last-10 — file did not exist. Wired via `#[path]` from `src/lib.rs` + `src/app_impl/mod.rs` (plumbing, not primary surfaces, same pattern as Pass 19/31). Incidental: `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (NEW H2 section "Cmd+Enter AI Proposal Apply" between "Cmd+Enter Inline AI Proposal" and "Capture Handler Filtering"), `audits/afk/stories.md` (story `[ ]`→`[~]`).
- **Story**: `ai-proposal-accept-dismiss` (W3). Tab/Enter accepts the inline-AI proposal edit, Esc dismisses without changing input. Story spec receipt: `setFilter "!deploy --"` + cmd-enter + tab + getState shows `filterText:"!deploy -- prod --dry-run"`. The pure decision logic ships this pass; the GPUI key handler that wires it into the live hint component is the deferred `[~]` half (requires binary rebuild + state-receipt round-trip).
- **Outcome**: ADD shipped. Without this module the inline-AI hint card has no shared, testable shape for what Accept/Dismiss should DO with each `ProposalKind`. The new `apply_proposal(current_input, proposal, action) -> ProposalEffect` makes that decision explicit and pinnable: Accept on AddTag/AddDate/AddField appends a properly-formatted token to the trimmed input (trim_end so a trailing-space input like `"+todo Buy milk   "` → `"+todo Buy milk #errands"` with single separator, no double-space); Accept on RewriteInput replaces wholesale; Accept on Decline returns Dismiss (nothing to apply); Dismiss on any kind returns Dismiss and leaves input untouched. Same shape as Pass 29's `apply_safe_effect` for Cmd+K — UI glue layer threads the returned `ProposalEffect` back into the input field. Verification: `cargo test --lib menu_syntax_ai_apply` 11/11 pass — covers Dismiss-on-any-kind, AddTag append, AddTag trailing-space-collapse, AddTag empty-input → bare token, AddDate colon-quoted-phrase, AddField key=value, RewriteInput wholesale (including the story-spec exact-match receipt + unrelated-input falsifier), Accept-on-Decline → Dismiss, Dismiss-on-Rewrite-falsifier (pins that Dismiss never silently applies), and a purity check (3 calls yield identical effects).
- **Commit**: `87ea6bfaa`
- **Files touched**: 5 — `src/app_impl/menu_syntax_ai_apply.rs` (NEW, ~250 LOC w/ tests), `src/app_impl/mod.rs` (one `#[path]` mod line), `src/lib.rs` (one `#[path]` re-export block + comment), `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (NEW H2 section), `audits/afk/stories.md` (`[ ]`→`[~]` flip).
- **Discipline**: PINS=0 (cap 7) → ok; YIELD before this pass=2, this Add takes it to 3 → ok; surface NEW (not in last-10) → no rotation pinch. Pass type Add per subject-verb discipline. Bug-yield maintained.
- **Open `[?]`s after this pass**: still 1 — `action-effects-default-time-phrase-not-quote-escaped` (action_effects.rs in last-10 #6 → fix deferred until that ages out, ~3 more passes).
- **Notes**: This completes the data-layer trilogy for the inline-AI hint card: Pass 5 shipped the request/response contract (menu_syntax/ai.rs), Pass 31 the response→proposal adapter (menu_syntax_ai.rs), Pass 35 the keypress applier (menu_syntax_ai_apply.rs). Once the inline-AI hint component lands in the renderer, all three are ready to be threaded together: `MenuSyntaxAiRequest` → call model → `parse_response` → `proposal_from_response` → render proposal → on Tab/Enter/Esc call `apply_proposal` → setFilter or dismiss.

## Run 11 Pass 34 — 2026-04-25T13:08:11Z — Add unresolved_dates reporting to resolve_capture_dates

Original spec said the function lives in `src/menu_syntax/capture.rs` but `resolve_capture_dates` actually lives in `date.rs`; the spec's intent was the behavior change, not the file location, so the function-lookup wins over the file-lookup. Before: `resolve_capture_dates` silently dropped any `DatePhrase` whose source did not parse via `resolve_date_phrase`, so `+todo Thing due:asdf` produced `dates: []` with no signal that the user had typed a `due:` key at all. Body extraction unchanged. Verification: `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::date` 21/21 pass (5 new tests covering: story-example single-unresolved-due, mixed resolved+unresolved, all-resolved-empty-unresolved-falsifier, no-dates-both-empty-falsifier, camelCase JSON serialization); `cargo test --lib menu_syntax` 368/368 pass (no regressions in execute.rs / handler_index.rs / other consumers of `ResolvedCaptureInvocation`).

## Run 11 Pass 33 — 2026-04-25T14:38:15Z — Fix dynamic schema dedup within and across lists

- **Surface**: `src/menu_syntax/metadata.rs` (production module — `dynamic_capture_schema_from_spec` `parse_list` closure rewritten + cross-list `retain` calls added) + `tests/menu_syntax_run11_attacker_pass24_dynamic_schema.rs` (3 PROBE/pinned-old tests converted in-place to `_PINNED` with inverted assertions, +3 new cross-list pins). metadata.rs aged out of last-10 — Pass 21 (its last primary entry) is now well past position 10. Incidental: `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (existing "Dynamic Capture Schemas" section extended with a sentence on the two-phase dedup + receipt-test counts), `audits/afk/stories.md` (`[?]` row flipped to `[x]` with FIXED note).
- **Story**: `dynamic-schema-no-dedup-across-required-optional` (was the last open `[?]` from Pass 24). The Pass-24 attacker filing identified that within-list duplicates (e.g. `required: ["body","body","body"]`) and cross-list overlaps (e.g. `kv:amount` in BOTH required AND optional) are not deduplicated by the schema extractor. Not a runtime correctness bug because downstream `validate`/`missing_required` collapse duplicates naturally via `iter().filter`, but it pollutes the diagnostic surface and surprises authors. Pass 33 fixes both shapes.
- **Outcome**: FIX shipped. Phase 1 (within-list): `parse_list` rewritten as an explicit loop with `if !out.contains(&req) { out.push(req); }` — preserves declaration order, keeps the first occurrence. Phase 2 (cross-list precedence required > optional > forbidden): after parsing all three lists, `optional.retain(|r| !required.contains(r))` and `forbidden.retain(|r| !required.contains(r) && !optional.contains(r))`. Verification: `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::metadata` 20/20 pass; `cargo test --test menu_syntax_run11_attacker_pass24_dynamic_schema` 27/27 pass after converting 3 pre-fix tests (`composition_14_duplicate_required_tokens_preserved_PROBE` → `_dedupe_keep_first_PINNED`, `composition_17_long_required_list_handled` → `_dedupes_to_single_entry_PINNED`, `resurrection_23_kv_in_both_required_and_optional_no_dedup` → `_required_wins_PINNED`) and adding 3 new pins (`composition_14b_cross_list_required_wins_over_optional_and_forbidden_PINNED`, `composition_14c_optional_wins_over_forbidden_when_required_silent_PINNED`, `composition_14d_distinct_fields_across_lists_all_survive_PINNED` — over-dedup falsifier).
- **Commit**: `88fa14985`
- **Files touched**: 4 — `src/menu_syntax/metadata.rs` (parse_list rewrite + cross-list retains, ~12 LOC delta inside `dynamic_capture_schema_from_spec`), `tests/menu_syntax_run11_attacker_pass24_dynamic_schema.rs` (3 conversions + 3 new pins, ~70 LOC), `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (1 sentence + receipt-count update inside existing section), `audits/afk/stories.md` ([?]→[x] row rewrite).
- **Discipline**: PINS=0 (cap 7) → ok; YIELD=1 (this pass adds another Fix → 2) → ok; surface metadata.rs had aged out of last-10. Pass type Fix per subject-verb discipline. Bug-yield maintained.
- **Open `[?]`s after this pass**: 1 — `action-effects-default-time-phrase-not-quote-escaped` (Pass 32, action_effects.rs still in last-10 #4 → fix deferred until that ages out).
- **Notes**: Adjacent follow-up identified — `doctor.rs` should warn when an author declares the same token in multiple lists, since the schema now silently picks required and the warning would alert them to the coherence bug at authoring time. Not blocking; tracked as future Add for `sdk-menu-syntax-doctor` story.

## Run 11 Pass 32 — 2026-04-25T12:51:10Z — attacker probe of action_effects (Probe — 1 [?] filed)

- **Surface**: `tests/menu_syntax_run11_attacker_pass32_action_effects.rs` (NEW) targeting [[src/menu_syntax/action_effects.rs#apply_safe_effect]] (Pass 29). Fresh vs Run 11 last-10 — distinct test path from prior attacker files (`tests/menu_syntax_run11_attacker.rs` Pass 4, `_pass8_sdk` Pass 8, `_pass12_doctor` Pass 12, `_pass16_validate` Pass 16, `_pass20_skill` Pass 20, `_pass24_dynamic_schema` Pass 24, `_pass28_capture_gate` Pass 28). action_effects.rs is at #3 in last-10 but the attacker SURFACE is the test file — same pattern Pass 4/8/12/16/20/24/28 used.
- **Story**: ATTACKER MODE (Pass 32 = 4×8). Probe the freshest unattacked surface: `apply_safe_effect` (added Pass 29, never attacker-tested). ≥20 actions required; this pass shipped 22.
- **Outcome**: probe — 1 `[?]` near-anomaly filed (`action-effects-default-time-phrase-not-quote-escaped`). Current `format!("{trimmed} start:\"{phrase}\"")` does not escape `"` characters in the phrase, so a phrase like `today "9am"` produces unbalanced quotes in the output filter that the downstream parser would mis-tokenize. Per attacker rule "never fix on a Probe pass" — filed for a follow-up Fix. The other 7 boundary findings (empty raw, empty phrase, empty argv, double-space on empty argv entry, unicode passthrough, etc.) are intentional simplicity, not bugs.
- **Commit**: `47590f6e7`
- **Adversarial categories**: Boundary (8), Composition (8), Resurrection (6) — meets ≥3 minimum.
- **Actions**: 22 — meets ≥20 minimum.
- **Files touched**: 2 — `tests/menu_syntax_run11_attacker_pass32_action_effects.rs` (NEW, 22 tests covering: empty raw + CopyFilter → empty clipboard (boundary 01); empty raw + DefaultTime → leading-space output (boundary 02 — pinned current behavior); empty phrase + DefaultTime → empty quotes `start:""` (boundary 03); quote-in-phrase → unbalanced output (boundary 04 — `[?]` filed); empty head + EditCommandArgv → `! ` (boundary 05); empty argv + CopyFilter → `!head` no trailing space (boundary 06); empty argv entry → double-space `!deploy  real` (boundary 07); unicode in payload.raw passes through (boundary 08); DefaultTime in command/refine state → Unsupported (composition 09, 15); EditCommandArgv in refine/capture state → Unsupported (composition 10, 16); CopyFilter in capture uses `payload.raw` not `target` (composition 11 — pin); CopyFilter in refine uses `query.raw` not `free_text` (composition 12 — pin); Cancel dominates 3-state cross-product (composition 13); OpenCapturesBrowser Unsupported in 3-state cross-product (composition 14); idempotence over 3 calls (resurrection 17); clone kind yields equal effect (resurrection 18); clone effect equality (resurrection 19); payload mutation between calls flows through (resurrection 20); Unsupported across 4 unsafe kinds in sequence (resurrection 21); state-tuple dispatch is pure — alternating Implemented/Unsupported in same state (resurrection 22)), `audits/afk/stories.md` (1 new `[?]` row appended).
- **Proof**:
  1. `cargo test --test menu_syntax_run11_attacker_pass32_action_effects` → `test result: ok. 22 passed; 0 failed`. The probe test (`boundary_04_default_time_with_quote_in_phrase_does_not_escape`) documents CURRENT behavior with `assert_eq!(new_text, r#"+cal task start:"today "9am"""#)` — a future Fix flipping to escaped quotes would make it red, exactly the falsifier the `[?]` story names.
  2. One near-anomaly isolated:
     - `boundary_04_default_time_with_quote_in_phrase_does_not_escape`: a phrase containing `"` (e.g. `today "9am"`) produces unbalanced quotes in the output (`+cal task start:"today "9am""`). Filed as `[?] action-effects-default-time-phrase-not-quote-escaped`. Low priority — caller is `actions_dialog.rs` which only invokes clean presets like `today 9am`/`tomorrow 8am`; the path most exposed is paste-from-doc with smart-then-straightened quotes. Fix is a 2-line `phrase.replace('\\', "\\\\").replace('"', "\\\"")` in the format! arm, OR caller-side validation rejecting `"`.
  3. Other notable findings (no `[?]`):
     - `boundary_02`: empty `payload.raw` + DefaultTime produces a leading space (`" start:\"today 9am\""`) because `trim_end()` of `""` is still `""`. Cosmetic; the call site likely never produces an empty raw.
     - `boundary_03`: empty phrase produces `start:""` empty quotes — the renderer would show no time but the filter is structurally correct. Documented.
     - `boundary_07`: empty argv entry produces double-space (`!deploy  real`). The simple `out.push(' '); out.push_str(a)` formatter is intentional.
     - `composition_11`/`12`: pinned that CopyFilter uses `payload.raw`/`query.raw` (the source-of-truth) and NOT the parsed `target`/`free_text` decomposition. Important contract: a parser bug that diverges raw and parsed must surface raw to the clipboard so the user can fix the input verbatim.
     - `composition_13`: Cancel dominates the 3-state cross-product — pinned the contract that Cancel never falls into per-state dispatch.
     - `composition_14`: OpenCapturesBrowser Unsupported in 3 states — confirms the unsafe-kinds backlog rule (Pass 29 contract: every unsafe kind returns Unsupported regardless of state).
     - `resurrection_22`: state-tuple dispatch is pure — alternating CopyFilter→Unsupported→CopyFilter in the SAME state produces matching results, proving no caching or state mutation in `apply_safe_effect`.
  4. `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::action_effects` → 12/12 still green (Pass-29 unit tests untouched). No production code edited — the attacker probe lives entirely in `tests/`.
  5. `lat check` → no lat.md changes this pass; the existing `## Cmd+K Safe Action Effects` section already covers the surface.
  6. Test compile fix: initial `Vec::<FilterPredicate>::new()` referenced a non-existent type. The actual enum is `Predicate`; fixed the import + the type annotation in `refine()`. Caught at compile time.
- **Notes**: Pass 32 (4×8 attacker). Discipline preflight: PINS=1, YIELD=2 (Pass 29 Add + Pass 30 Fix + Pass 31 Add — healthy), surface rotation satisfied via NEW test file. One `[?]` filed — total open `[?]`s now: 2 (the metadata `dynamic-schema-no-dedup-across-required-optional` from Pass 24 + this new one). Pass 33 plan: Fix the metadata `[?]` (surface aged out at Pass 32 entry, was at #10 → now position 11, out). The action_effects `[?]` waits one more pass for action_effects.rs to age out (currently at #3; needs 7 more passes to drop). The 7 documented findings without `[?]` are converted into structural pins by the test file — a future refactor that flipped any of them (Cancel-dominance order; CopyFilter raw-not-parsed; OpenCapturesBrowser per-state Unsupported sweep) trips CI before reaching a user. With this pass, Run 11 has shipped 32 passes worth of pure-data scaffolding + 5 `[?]` Fixes; only ~30min to buffer cutoff so likely 2-3 more passes possible.

  3. `cargo check --lib` → clean (16 pre-existing warnings, no new errors). The `out.retain(...)` closure uses simple `Vec` lookup instead of `HashSet` to keep the slug ordering deterministic without bringing in extra hashing — `Vec<String>` linear scan is fine for the typical handful of skills per script.
  4. `lat check` → All checks passed.

     - `composition_11_url_well_formedness_beats_forbidden_url`: a `+cal` carrying `ftp://nope` (both ill-formed AND on the forbidden list) surfaces as `Malformed.Url` with the URL-scheme reason, NOT with the "url is not allowed for +cal" reason. The url well-formedness check inside `validate()` runs BEFORE the forbidden sweep. Both reach the same field; the user sees the more actionable error first. Pinned the contract.
     - `composition_13_kv_case_insensitive_satisfaction`: an author typing `AMOUNT=12.50` satisfies a `Kv("amount")` requirement because `FieldRequirement::is_satisfied` uses `eq_ignore_ascii_case`. This means a schema author and a user can disagree on case without anyone noticing — neither a bug nor a `[?]`, but worth documenting via the test.
     - `composition_14`/`15`: DateRole-specific requirements (`Start`/`Due`/`At`/`End`) are satisfied ONLY by phrases carrying the same role; `DateRole::Inferred` (the body-suffix path) does NOT satisfy a `Start` requirement. This is the intentional explicit-key vs body-suffix split documented in `lat.md/menu-syntax#Date Resolution`.
     - `boundary_06`: target-case is preserved in the HUD (user types `+CAL`, HUD says `+CAL needs ...`) even though schema lookup is case-insensitive. Pinned.
     - `boundary_07`: regex-meta chars in the target name (`re.*x`) format cleanly into the HUD (no panic, no escaping confusion). The `format!` macro is not a regex; this is byte-safe by construction.
  3. `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::capture_gate` → 7/7 still green (Pass-25 unit tests untouched). No production code edited — the attacker probe lives entirely in `tests/`.
  4. `lat check` → no lat.md changes this pass; the existing `## Capture Validation Gate` section already covers the surface.
- **Notes**: Pass 28 (4×7 attacker). Discipline preflight: PINS=1, YIELD=2 (Pass 25/26/27 supplied), surface rotation satisfied via NEW test file. First zero-`[?]` attacker pass of Run 11 — counts as one of three needed before Run 9's escalation rule fires (≥30 actions, ≥4 categories, ≥4 surfaces). The gate is the right shape for Pass 25's small surface (~3 functions, ~100 LOC of decision logic). The 5 documented findings above are all "current behavior is intentional and correct" — the test file converts each into a structural pin so a future refactor that flipped any of them (e.g. inverting forbidden vs well-formedness order, or normalizing kv key case in `is_satisfied`) trips CI before reaching a user. ## Run 11 Pass 23 — 2026-04-25T11:48:00Z — `capture-validation-chip-rendering` (Add) [~ partial]

- **Surface**: `src/render_script_list/mod.rs` (extended) + `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (existing section extended). render_script_list/mod.rs has not been touched as primary edit surface in Run 11 — fresh.

- **Outcome**: `[~]` partial — code change wires the renderer to consume Pass 22's `status_chips` Vec and suppress double-chips. Visual screenshot receipt blocked by stale binary (built before Run 11 began).
- **Commit**: `8ec151093`
- **Files touched**: 4 — `src/render_script_list/mod.rs` (+~16 LOC: `.when(!hint.status_chips.is_empty())` block iterating `.skip(1)` past the leading mode chip and rendering each remaining chip; legacy `.when_some(hint.status_chip)` filtered to skip when status_chips is non-empty so capture surfaces render exactly the multi-chip Vec, non-capture surfaces unchanged), `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (extended "Capture Validation Snapshot" section with the renderer wiring detail), `audits/afk/stories.md` (story flipped `[~]` with receipt note).
- **Proof**:
  1. `cargo check --lib` → clean (no E0xxx errors after the rendering change).
  2. `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::main_hint -- --nocapture` → 20 passed (Pass 22's snapshot tests still pass — confirms no semantic regression on the data layer).
  3. The `.skip(1)` reads as: when `status_chips` is non-empty, the FIRST chip is the mode chip (`+ capture`) which the existing `mode_chip` field already renders. Skipping it prevents duplicate-chip layout. The remaining chips (`needs body`, `needs date`, `ready`, `malformed`) get their own divs.
  4. The double-render guard on legacy `status_chip` uses `.filter(|_| hint.status_chips.is_empty())` so the ternary stays declarative — capture surfaces render only multi-chip; advanced query / command picker surfaces keep their single-chip path unchanged.
  5. `lat check` → All checks passed (extended existing section with renderer wiring detail; no new wiki links needed since the Pass 22 section already linked all the snapshot symbols).
- **Falsifier**: regression to double-chip rendering on capture surfaces — would happen if a refactor drops the `.filter(|_| hint.status_chips.is_empty())` guard, causing the legacy `status_chip` to render alongside the multi-chip Vec. Visual proof requires binary rebuild + screenshot diff against `.test-screenshots/menu-syntax-validation-chips-cal.png` (produced once binary rebuilt). The `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::main_hint` suite stays green either way (it tests the snapshot, not the rendering); the falsifier is purely visual.
- **Notes**: Pass 23. Discipline preflight: PINS=1 (Pin allowed), YIELD=2 (no floor pressure), surface rotation satisfied (render_script_list/mod.rs not in last-10). `[~]` partial because the visual screenshot receipt requires a binary rebuild (the launcher's stale binary doesn't have the Pass 22 snapshot fields, so even running the live UI wouldn't show the new chip layout). The state-receipt portion of the story IS satisfied — `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::main_hint` continues to pass per the story's "state receipt from prior story still passes" requirement. The `.skip(1)` design is safe: the snapshot helper at main_hint.rs ALWAYS pushes the mode chip first; if a future refactor changes that contract, the rendering would lose the first chip rather than crash. A defensive Pin asserting "status_chips[0].label == mode_chip.as_ref().map(|c| c.label)" would make this explicit — tagged for a future Pin pass when chip-rendering surfaces age out.

- **Commit**: `6717cd8c9`
- **Files touched**: 5 — `src/app_impl/menu_syntax_actions.rs` (NEW, 95 LOC: `SectionMode { Replace, Prepend }` enum, `PowerSyntaxActionSection { title, mode, actions }` struct, `power_syntax_action_section(state)` function returning section with constant `"Power Syntax"` title and mode dispatched on state variant — Replace for Capture/Command, Prepend for Refine — and full action list from `current_menu_syntax_actions`; 5 unit tests covering each state variant's mode + title + actions-match-pure-spec invariant), `src/app_impl/mod.rs` (one mod line for binary build), `src/lib.rs` (NEW `#[path] pub mod menu_syntax_actions` re-export so `cargo test --lib` picks up the unit tests), `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (existing "Cmd+K Power Syntax Actions" section extended with the adapter wiring detail and links to `#power_syntax_action_section` + `#SectionMode`), `audits/afk/stories.md` (story flipped `[~]` with receipt note).
- **Proof**:
  1. `cargo test --lib menu_syntax_actions -- --nocapture` → `test result: ok. 5 passed; 0 failed`. The 5 tests: `capture_state_yields_replace_mode`, `command_state_yields_replace_mode`, `refine_state_yields_prepend_mode`, `section_title_is_constant`, `section_actions_match_pure_spec_output`.
  2. `section_actions_match_pure_spec_output` is the load-bearing invariant pin — it asserts the adapter does NOT reorder or filter the action list, just attaches title + mode. A future "smart filter" refactor that hides disabled rows in the dialog would be caught.
  3. `refine_state_yields_prepend_mode` specifically pins the Prepend semantics: when the user is refining a list (not composing a new artifact), the selected row's normal actions remain useful, so the Power Syntax section sits above them rather than replacing.
  4. The two private modules (one in `app_impl/mod.rs`, one in `lib.rs`) coexist via the same dual-include pattern used by `path_action`/`routes`/`menu_syntax_trigger_popup` — no name collision because `app_impl/mod.rs` is `include!`-merged into `main.rs` (binary) and `lib.rs` re-export is the lib crate's view; they are separate compilation units that both reference the same physical file.
  5. `lat check` → `All checks passed` after the existing actions section was extended with the adapter description and 2 new wiki links.
- **Falsifier**: any of the 5 tests reverting. Most likely future regression: a refactor that switches from `MenuSyntaxActionState` enum dispatch to a trait-based dispatch where the trait method returns mode — would silently change Replace/Prepend defaults if a contributor forgets to override for the Refine variant. The `refine_state_yields_prepend_mode` test catches that immediately (would return Replace).
- **Notes**: Pass 19. Discipline preflight: PINS=1 (Pin allowed but Add is the bug-yield-keeping choice), YIELD=1 (no floor pressure), surface rotation satisfied (NEW src/app_impl/menu_syntax_actions.rs path). Pass 20 = attacker (4×5) — likely target the new doctor or skill or actions-section adapter surfaces. Two Pass 16 [?]s remain queued; capture_schema.rs ages out of last-10 at Pass 25 (when Pass 15 falls past head -10). The dual-include `#[path]` pattern is established convention — see `src/lib.rs:128-147` for the precedent (path_action / routes / menu_syntax_trigger_popup); using it again here keeps the binary's actions-dialog code path identical to the lib's tested code path.

All three need receipts via setFilter + getState which require the binary to be rebuilt against current main.
- **Commit**: `d1f555dc0`
- **Files touched**: 5 — `src/menu_syntax/skill.rs` (NEW, 90 LOC pure module: `SkillSpec` struct with 5 fields mirroring `kit-init/types/menu-syntax.d.ts::SkillHandlerSpec`, `skill_specs_from_value(&Value)` extractor handling array + single-object shape, family-filter, slug-trim/skip-if-empty, contextRequirements non-string filtering, 9 unit tests), `src/menu_syntax/mod.rs` (one mod line + one re-export line), `scripts/examples/menu-syntax/skill-review-current-file.ts` (NEW demo declaring `/review` skill with `contextRequirements: ["currentFile"]`), `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (NEW "Skill Spec Metadata" section with 4 wiki links), `audits/afk/stories.md` (story flipped `[~]` with receipt note).

  1. `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::skill -- --nocapture` → `test result: ok. 9 passed; 0 failed`. The 9 tests cover: single-object form, array form filtered to skill.v1 only, missing/empty/whitespace slug skipped, slug trimmed, contextRequirements filters non-string entries (e.g. `[42, null]` dropped), missing optional fields default to None/empty, acceptsCaptureTarget pass-through, non-object inputs yield empty, wrong family filtered.
  2. `array_form_extracts_only_skill_v1_entries` specifically pins the family filter against a mixed `[capture.v1, skill.v1, command.v1, skill.v1]` array: returns exactly 2 SkillSpecs in source order. This is the load-bearing assertion for the future runtime — the launcher iterates a script's menuSyntax in order, so skill rows must come out in declaration order.
  3. `context_requirements_filters_non_string_entries` is a defensive boundary against malformed JSON: `["currentFile", 42, null, "selection"]` yields `["currentFile", "selection"]` (2 strings). Future story will add doctor warnings for the dropped non-strings.
  4. Demo script `scripts/examples/menu-syntax/skill-review-current-file.ts` parses cleanly (TypeScript-shaped — runtime imports `metadata`); the `console.log` lines document the contract (skill is for `:type:skill` discovery only, not auto-registered as a command).
  5. `lat check` → `All checks passed` after the new section was added with `#skill_specs_from_value` and `#SkillSpec` wiki links.
- **Falsifier**: any of the 9 tests reverting (most fragile: `array_form_extracts_only_skill_v1_entries` order-preservation — a refactor using HashMap iteration would shuffle the output Vec). Most likely future regression: someone refactors to use serde's `#[derive(Deserialize)]` with a tagged enum across all menuSyntax families and accidentally enforces strict deserialization that rejects unknown fields, breaking the permissive "unknown fields ignored" contract.
- **Notes**: Pass 18. Discipline preflight: PINS=1 (Pin allowed), YIELD=0 going in (forced Fix/Add/Extend — chose Add), surface rotation satisfied (NEW skill.rs path, never seen in last-10). Two Pass 16 [?]s remain queued — capture_schema.rs is still in last-10 (Pass 15), will age out at Pass 25 (when Pass 15 falls past head -10). The `[~]` partial-close marker is appropriate: the Rust data-shape contract is a real shipped capability (consumers can call `skill_specs_from_value` today), and the runtime UI integration is a separate concern with its own dependencies (binary rebuild + new receipts). Avoided touching `payload.rs::MenuSyntaxHandlerSpec` (which is in last-10) by parsing skill specs directly from raw JSON; future contributors may consolidate by adding a `context_requirements` field on the typed spec, but that's a separate rotation-aware pass.

## Run 11 Pass 17 — 2026-04-25T10:58:00Z — Pin `current_actions` ordering (Pin against HashMap-registry refactor)

- **Surface**: `tests/menu_syntax_actions_ordering_pin.rs` (NEW) targeting [[src/menu_syntax/actions.rs#current_actions]] (Pass 3). Fresh vs Run 11 last-10 — distinct test path (none of the existing attacker/golden test files exercise action ordering specifically). actions.rs itself is NOT touched — the pin lives entirely in tests/.
- **Story**: ATTACKER follow-up not chosen (rotation forbids touching `src/menu_syntax/capture_schema.rs` to fix the two Pass 16 [?]s — capture_schema is in last-10). Picked a Pin against a real refactor threat instead: if a contributor extracts the per-state action construction (`capture_actions`/`refine_actions`/`command_actions`) into a `HashMap<ActionKind, ActionMeta>` registry, HashMap iteration order would silently shuffle the action rows the Cmd+K dialog displays. That refactor is increasingly likely as the action set grows past 14 variants — the consolidation reads like a reasonable cleanup but breaks visual ordering + keyboard-shortcut indexing.
- **Outcome**: closed (no story flip — this is a defensive Pin, not a backlog story). Pin counts toward PIN cap (1/7 used).
- **Commit**: `6c046afc8`
- **Refactor threat**: A contributor refactors `current_actions` (and its three helpers) to use a `HashMap<MenuSyntaxActionKind, ActionMeta>` registry, iterating it to build the Vec. HashMap iteration order is unspecified; the actions-dialog renders Vec-order, with index 0 pre-selected and index-based keyboard shortcuts. Shuffling breaks both visual contract and persisted user muscle memory.
- **Files touched**: 2 — `tests/menu_syntax_actions_ordering_pin.rs` (NEW, 8 tests covering: capture-state 5-action exact ordering, capture-with-needs-date append at end + DefaultTime phrase = "today 9am", capture-without-schema omits default-time row, refine-state 4-action exact ordering, command-empty-argv omits run_with_last_argv, command-with-argv inserts run_with_last_argv at INDEX 2, save_and_copy_id disabled when body whitespace-only, idempotence across 3 calls), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry).
- **Proof**:
  1. `cargo test --test menu_syntax_actions_ordering_pin` → `test result: ok. 8 passed; 0 failed`. Each test asserts a specific ordering invariant the dialog depends on.
  2. `command_state_with_argv_inserts_run_with_last_at_index_2` is the most-load-bearing assertion — it specifically pins the `actions.insert(2, ...)` call at `src/menu_syntax/actions.rs:187`. A HashMap-based refactor would land run_with_last_argv at an unspecified position; this test would fail with the actual position revealed in the Vec.
  3. `capture_state_appends_default_time_when_cal_needs_date` pins both the append-at-end ordering AND the literal `"today 9am"` phrase value in the kind payload. A refactor that swaps the literal for `chrono::Local::now()` would be caught (non-deterministic phrase).
  4. `capture_save_and_copy_id_disabled_when_body_empty` pins the `enabled` field semantics — a refactor that filters disabled actions out of the Vec entirely would be caught (the row must STILL be present, just disabled).
  5. `current_actions_is_deterministic_across_repeated_calls` pins idempotence — catches any future memoization with unstable hashing.
  6. `lat check` → All checks passed (no lat.md change this pass — actions.rs section already exists from Pass 3).
- **Falsifier**: any of the 8 ordering assertions failing in a future build would prove the Vec semantics changed. Most likely first failure: `command_state_with_argv_inserts_run_with_last_at_index_2` (the index-2 insertion is the most fragile detail). If a refactor lands and someone "fixes" the test by re-asserting the new order without preserving the rationale, the dialog's keyboard shortcuts silently rebind — the failing falsifier should be the trigger to halt and check the dialog rendering, not to update the test.
- **Notes**: Pass 17. Discipline preflight: PINS=0 going in (room available — Pin allowed), YIELD=1 (no floor pressure), surface rotation satisfied (NEW test file path). Picked Pin instead of the obvious Fix on Pass 16 [?]s because the [?] fixes touch `src/menu_syntax/capture_schema.rs` which is in last-10 (Pass 15) — rotation forbids. Both [?] anomalies remain queued for Pass 18+ when capture_schema.rs ages out of last-10. The Pin discharge is a real defense, not a make-work pin: actions.rs has 14 action variants and the consolidation pressure is real.

- **Files touched**: 2 — `tests/menu_syntax_run11_attacker_pass16_validate.rs` (NEW, 24 tests covering empty/whitespace/uppercase-scheme/javascript-scheme/bare-https url, 10k-char/fullwidth/scientific/NaN/inf amounts, emoji+RTL body, NUL byte url, 6 composition orderings, 6 idempotence/mutation cases), `audits/afk/stories.md` (2 new `[?]` rows appended at bottom).
- **Proof**:
  1. `cargo test --test menu_syntax_run11_attacker_pass16_validate` → `test result: ok. 24 passed; 0 failed`. All 24 attacker actions ran; the two anomaly cases (`boundary_09_amount_NaN_currently_passes_PROBE`, `composition_18_forbidden_field_is_ignored_by_validate_PROBE`) assert the CURRENT permissive behavior so a future fix flips them red, signaling the contract changed.
  2. Two near-anomalies isolated:
     - `boundary_09_amount_NaN_currently_passes_PROBE` + `boundary_10_amount_inf_currently_passes_PROBE`: `f64::parse` accepts NaN/infinity. Currency must not. Filed as `[?] validate-amount-accepts-nan-inf-as-numeric`.
     - `composition_18_forbidden_field_is_ignored_by_validate_PROBE`: `validate()` ignores `schema.forbidden`. A cal capture with `priority=p1` (Priority is forbidden for cal) validates Ready. Filed as `[?] validate-ignores-schema-forbidden-fields`.
  3. One deliberate documentation pin (`boundary_05_https_with_no_authority_is_well_formed_today_PROBE`): bare `https://` passes the prefix check today; documented as permissive but not filed as a separate `[?]` since the URL parser scope is intentionally minimal in this layer.
  4. Other notable findings (no `[?]`):
     - Composition_17: `validate` runs the URL well-formedness check even when the schema doesn't require url — caught a bad url on a todo capture. This is the desired permissive-author / strict-payload boundary.
     - Composition_15: case-insensitive amount kv match (`AMOUNT=abc`) still triggers Malformed. Confirms `eq_ignore_ascii_case`.
     - Resurrection_22: empty `required` schema always returns Ready, regardless of payload contents (no Malformed checks fire because url + amount aren't present).
     - Resurrection_23: validate doesn't check that `payload.target == schema.target`. Worth pinning so future contributors know that contract.
  5. No fmt drift; no lat.md change (test-only pass — the lat section for `validate()` was added in Pass 15).
- **Notes**: Pass 16. Discipline preflight: PINS=0 (Pin still allowed but attacker forbids non-Probe verbs), YIELD=2 (no floor pressure), surface rotation satisfied (new test file path). Two `[?]` filings reset the "3-consecutive-zero anomaly" escalation counter — next attacker pass (Pass 20) does NOT need to escalate. Both anomalies are addressable in ~10 LOC each (NaN/inf check + forbidden loop) and would pair well as a follow-up Fix pass; deliberately deferred per attacker-mode rule "never fix on a Probe pass". The bare-`https://` permissive case is borderline-anomaly but documented inline rather than filed because URL parsing is intentionally a separate concern (the launcher renders the URL; full parsing belongs in a downstream consumer).

## Run 11 Pass 15 — 2026-04-25T10:18:00Z — `capture-validate-payload` (Add)

- **Surface**: `src/menu_syntax/capture_schema.rs` (extended) + `src/menu_syntax/mod.rs` (re-exports) + `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (+1 section). Fresh vs Run 11 last-10 — capture_schema.rs has not appeared as primary edit surface since Pass 1 (14 ticks ago, outside the last-10 window). The Pass 1 surface seeded the schema scaffold; Pass 15 layers `validate()` on top — additive coverage, distinct API surface.
- **Story**: capture-validate-payload — Add `validate(payload, schema) -> ValidationResult` returning `Ready | Incomplete { missing } | Malformed { field, reason }` so the UI can branch on a single decision instead of re-running ad-hoc rules. Story called for ready / missing-body / missing-date / malformed-amount cases; this pass ships those plus malformed-url and amount-format-acceptance bonuses.
- **Outcome**: closed `[x]`
- **Commit**: `64806e6ea`
- **Files touched**: 4 — `src/menu_syntax/capture_schema.rs` (+~55 LOC: `ValidationResult` enum, `validate()` function, `is_well_formed_url()` and `looks_like_amount()` helpers; +9 unit tests covering all 4 story cases plus 5 corner cases), `src/menu_syntax/mod.rs` (re-export `validate_capture_payload` and `ValidationResult`), `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (NEW "Capture Payload Validation" section linking to the new symbols), `audits/afk/stories.md` (story flipped to `[x]` with commit + receipt note).
- **Proof**:
  1. `cargo test --lib capture_schema -- --nocapture` → `test result: ok. 21 passed; 0 failed`. The 9 new tests are: `validate_ready_when_all_required_present_and_well_formed`, `validate_incomplete_missing_body_for_todo`, `validate_incomplete_missing_date_for_cal_with_body_only`, `validate_malformed_amount_kv_beats_incomplete`, `validate_malformed_url_for_link_with_garbage_url`, `validate_amount_accepts_decimals_and_signs`, `validate_empty_amount_kv_falls_through_to_incomplete`, plus the helper fixture `cal_with_body_and_date`.
  2. `validate_malformed_amount_kv_beats_incomplete` specifically pins the precedence rule: even when `body` is also missing, a non-numeric `amount=not-a-number` returns `Malformed { field: Kv("amount"), reason: ... }`, NOT `Incomplete { missing: [Body, Kv("amount")] }`. This is the design choice — surface the actionable error before the missing-field accounting.
  3. `validate_amount_accepts_decimals_and_signs` pins acceptance of `"18.50"`, `"$18.50"`, `"-5"`, `"+12.0"`, `"0"`, `"100"` — covers the leading `$`/`+`/`-` strip + `f64::parse` path.
  4. `validate_empty_amount_kv_falls_through_to_incomplete` pins that whitespace-only `amount="  "` is treated as "not provided" (Incomplete), not malformed — preserves the existing `is_satisfied` semantics for empty kv values.
  5. `lat check` → `All checks passed` after the new "Capture Payload Validation" section was added with 4 new wiki links (`#validate`, `#ValidationResult`).
- **Falsifier**: `validate_malformed_amount_kv_beats_incomplete` reverting to Incomplete (would prove the precedence rule was broken in a refactor that ran `missing_required` first) OR `validate_empty_amount_kv_falls_through_to_incomplete` returning Malformed (would prove the empty-value detection got mixed up with malformed detection). Most likely future regression: someone adds a new well-formedness check inside the `kv` loop without the `!value.trim().is_empty()` guard, causing empty values to flip from Incomplete → Malformed.
- **Notes**: Pass 15. Discipline preflight: PINS=0 (Pin allowed but not chosen), YIELD=1 (no floor pressure), surface rotation satisfied (capture_schema.rs not in last-10). This pass is an Add — extends a real behavioral surface rather than just a test fixture. Unblocks downstream stories `capture-validation-snapshot` (will thread `ValidationResult` into `MenuSyntaxMainHintSnapshot.capture_validation`) and `capture-enter-validation-gate` (will branch on `ValidationResult::Ready` before executing). Empty-kv-fallthrough decision (Incomplete, not Malformed) preserves the contract from Pass 1's `is_satisfied` so the "missing" message stays accurate.

- **Story**: date-anchors-and-times shorthand portion — Add `resolve_shorthand_phrase(source, clock)` to recognize Todoist-style tokens `noon` / `midnight` / `eod` / `eom` that `chrono_english` doesn't handle natively. Closes the deferred `assert_unresolved_or_skip` portion from Pass 13 by flipping those fixture calls to `expect_resolved`.

- **Commit**: `836dbf2f9`
- **Files touched**: 4 — `src/menu_syntax/date.rs` (+~75 lines: NEW `resolve_shorthand_phrase` helper using `chrono::{Datelike, NaiveDate, Timelike}`; called from `resolve_date_phrase` BEFORE the `chrono_english` fallback so shorthand wins; mappings noon→12:00, midnight→00:00, eod→23:59 all on today's date in clock's timezone; eom resolves to last day of current month at 23:59 with year-rollover from December → next year January 1 minus one day), `tests/menu_syntax_date_golden.rs` (4 fixtures flipped from skip to expect_resolved + 2 new tests for EOM December year-rollover and case-insensitive NOON; total cases 13 → 15), `lat.md/menu-syntax.md` (updated "Date Anchor Golden Tests" section to reflect 15-case coverage and link to `resolve_shorthand_phrase`), `audits/afk/stories.md` (updated `[~]` receipt note describing Pass 14 closure of shorthand portion + remaining disambiguation work).
- **Proof**:
  1. `cargo test --test menu_syntax_date_golden` → `test result: ok. 15 passed; 0 failed`. The 4 previously-skip tests (noon/midnight/eod/eom) now assert exact ISO outputs (T12:00:00, T00:00:00, T23:59:00 for today; 2026-04-30T23:59:00 for April EOM).
  2. `cargo test --lib menu_syntax::date` → `test result: ok. 16 passed; 0 failed`. Pre-existing date tests untouched — confirms `resolve_shorthand_phrase` integration didn't disturb the chrono_english fallback path.
  3. EOM year-rollover specifically pinned by `shorthand_eom_handles_year_rollover_for_december` (clock at 2026-12-15 → eom resolves to 2026-12-31, not 2027-01-something). Validates the `if month == 12 { year + 1, 1, 1 } else { year, month + 1, 1 }` branch + `.pred_opt()` step.
  4. Case-insensitive matching pinned by `shorthand_case_insensitive_NOON_matches_noon` (input "NOON" must resolve same as "noon").
  5. `lat check` → `All checks passed`.
- **Falsifier**: any of the 4 flipped tests reverting to `Unresolved`, OR the EOM year-rollover test resolving to January 1 of next year, OR the case-insensitive test failing. Most likely future regression: someone refactors `resolve_date_phrase` to call `parse_date_string` first and `resolve_shorthand_phrase` only on failure — would still work today but would mask any future case where chrono_english starts recognizing these tokens with different semantics (e.g. `noon` → 11:30 in some locale). The current "shorthand wins" ordering is the deliberate choice.
- **Notes**: Pass 14. YIELD-floor satisfaction: bug-yield was 0 in last-5 going into this tick, mandating Fix/Add/Extend. This Add closes a real behavioral gap (shorthand tokens) rather than just documenting one. The disambiguation rule is the remaining aspirational portion — implementing it requires inspecting `clock.now.hour()` against the parsed time after `parse_date_string` returns, then bumping the date by one day if the hour is in the past. That's a separate ~30-LOC Add pass and deliberately out of scope for this tick. The `let _ = now.hour();` line in the helper silences the `Timelike` unused-import lint when only EOM uses Datelike — a future cleanup can drop it once another shorthand token uses `Timelike` properly.

  2. Mid-pass anomaly + fix: first run all 13 tests panicked with `fixed clock` because I used ISO timestamps with offset suffixes (`-06:00`) but `MenuSyntaxClock::fixed` parses naïve `%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S` only. Fixed via `sed` removing offsets. Re-verified: 13/13 passed.
  3. `lat check` → `All checks passed` (after tightening the new section's leading paragraph from 274 chars to ≤250).

The `assert_unresolved_or_skip` helper for noon/midnight/eod/eom is intentionally permissive (accepts both Resolved and Unresolved) — if those tokens ever become aspirational MUST-resolve, the fixture's helper call would need to be replaced with `expect_resolved`.
- **Notes**: Pass 13. `[~]` signals "fixture in place, aspirational behavior pending" — a hybrid state between `[x]` (fully closed) and `[ ]` (untouched). The disambiguation rule (`at:9am` rolls to TOMORROW when current local > 9am) is the aspirational behavior; implementing it requires either upgrading `chrono_english` or adding a wrapper that inspects `clock.now` after `parse_date_string` returns. Both options are out of scope for this Verify pass — the golden fixture provides the baseline against which the future Add pass can be measured. Receipt-substitution: the spec's "≥10 cases" requirement is met (13 cases shipped). The "`at:9am` disambiguation" requirement is NOT met and is documented inline in stories.md.

Validates that JSONPath diagnostic paths stay stable, severity dispatch is correct (Warning vs Error), and pure-function semantics hold under input pathology.
- **Outcome**: log-only (probe complete; 0 anomalies; minimum composition met)
- **Commit**: `fa27b89ec`
- **Files touched**: 2 — `tests/menu_syntax_run11_attacker_pass12_doctor.rs` (NEW, 280 lines post-fmt; 24 #[test] functions), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry).
- **Adversarial categories**: 5 (Boundary, Composition, Localization & Encoding, Determinism, Lifecycle). Recipe menu requires ≥3.
- **Actions**: 24 (minimum 20). Breakdown:
  - Boundary (7): empty array yields empty report, null top-level → Warning not Error, number top-level errors with "number" type label, 50k-char target slug doesn't panic, NUL-byte slug doesn't corrupt path, 50 invalid-args each get distinct `$[0].args[N].name` indexed paths, single-spec object form uses `$.targets` not `$[0].targets`.
  - Composition (5): duplicate command-head path lists all indices in input order (`$[0], $[2], $[3]`), mixed-family array doesn't cross-pollinate required-field diagnostics, one bad entry (unknown family) doesn't block validation of subsequent entries, empty `args: []` is not an error, capture with non-array `targets: "todo"` short-circuits without ghost `targets[0]` diagnostics.
  - Localization & Encoding (5): RTL slug ("مهمة") passes (Warning only), emoji command head ("🚀") passes, smart quotes (U+201C/U+201D) preserved byte-for-byte in diagnostic message, ZWJ (U+200D) is NOT whitespace (pinned for future Unicode-aware whitespace decisions), full-width space (U+3000) IS whitespace and triggers the head error.
  - Determinism (4): 50 repeated validate calls yield equal reports, `DoctorReport` serializes with camelCase fields (`severity` lowercase per `#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]`), full serde round-trip yields equal report, severity dispatch (Warning-only doesn't trip `has_errors`, Error does).
  - Lifecycle (3): swapping two entries shifts paths but preserves message-set content (sort + compare), adding a clean entry doesn't remove dirty diagnostics, mutating input AFTER `doctor_validate` doesn't change the returned report (proves report owns its strings).
- **Anomalies**: 0. Every assertion held first run. No `[?]` filed. Mid-pass anomaly during AUTHORING: a stub `boundary_06` was left in with placeholder body that wouldn't compile; replaced with `boundary_06_50_invalid_args_each_get_distinct_indexed_path` before first build.
- **Proof**: `cargo test --test menu_syntax_run11_attacker_pass12_doctor` → `test result: ok. 24 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.00s` after a ~18s incremental build.
- **Falsifier**: Any single test failing flips this red. Most likely future regressions: (a) someone "fixes" `validate_capture` to also emit per-element `targets[0]` diagnostics when `targets` is a string instead of array — would break `composition_05`; (b) someone changes the duplicate-command path joiner from `, ` to `\n` — would break `composition_01`; (c) someone changes the `DoctorReport` serde to output a top-level `hasErrors` field — would break `determinism_02`; (d) someone introduces global state or caching in `validate` — would break `determinism_01`; (e) someone switches whitespace check to `is_unicode_whitespace` (a hypothetical broader check) — would change `localization_04` outcome from no-error to error.
- **Notes**: Pass 12. Attacker cadence satisfied (passes 4, 8, 12 of 12-tick budget). The `localization_04` (ZWJ) and `localization_05` (full-width space) tests deliberately PIN current Unicode-handling — full-width space IS whitespace, ZWJ is NOT. A future contributor changing either side must do so deliberately. The `lifecycle_03` test pins doctor's purity by mutating input after validation and confirming the report still reflects pre-mutation state — proves diagnostic strings are owned, not borrowed from the input.

Most direct: removing the `family: "capture.v1"` literal narrowing (e.g. widening to `string`) would let `family: "capture.v2"` typecheck — turning the negative probe green is the red signal. Behaviorally: any divergence between `MenuSyntaxHandlerSpec` Rust shape and the TS `.d.ts` (e.g. Rust adds a new `accepts` token but TS `CaptureAccept` union is not updated) means authors silently lose IDE feedback while the launcher accepts the field — the test fixture should be extended each time payload.rs grows.
- **Notes**: Pass 6. No GUI verification needed — the contract is purely compile-time. RPC + binary state issues from Pass 5 deferred to a later pass when a Rust surface lands. Unblocks the W4 chain (sdk-helper-functions, sdk-command-schema, sdk-skill-spec-metadata, sdk-scriptlet-power-syntax-reference, sdk-menu-syntax-doctor) by establishing the canonical TS shapes those follow-ups import.

  - Rapid-fire/determinism (20): `parse_capture("+cal Design review #work")` invoked 20× sequentially — every iteration produces identical target/body/tags vs baseline (no hidden global state, no clock drift in pure parser).
  - Composition (3): +cal-no-date → schema reports AnyDate missing → actions include capture.default_time_today_9am; +cal-with-explicit-start → body satisfied (no Body in missing); +link-no-url → schema reports Url missing.
  - Interleaved (7): capture(todo) → refine(empty) → command(deploy with argv) → capture(note) ping-pong with surface-id stability check (open_browser kind carries the right target slug across both capture states).

Behaviorally: the attacker test is itself the falsifier — `cargo test --test menu_syntax_run11_attacker` returning red flips this red.
- **Notes**: First iteration of the test had a wrong expectation (assumed `parse_capture("+todo")` returns Incomplete; actual implementation returns Ok with empty body — the parser is permissive by design, schema enforces required-field absence). Fixed test to assert the documented split (parser permissive, schema strict) — this IS the documented behavior in grammar.md; finding it via attacker mode confirmed the split. No `[?]` filed because the parser/schema split is intentional. **Receipt-only observation**: the parse_capture/CaptureFieldSchema split is documented but not pinned with a contract test in the codebase — candidate for a future Pin pass naming a refactor that would collapse the layers (e.g. "Pin parse_capture-permissive-schema-strict split against a contributor unifying parse+validate into a single fail-closed function"). ### Pass #35 — 2026-04-19T12:37Z (Run 9) — EXTEND `audits/afk/promote-tool-gaps.sh` to recognize `[~]` (retracted) story state as tracked, closing the standing re-promotion loop that made every tick's STEP 3 emit `promoted 2 slugs to stories.md backlog` (on `tool-observability` + `tool-triggerbuiltin-dropped-after-hide-show`) and forced a hand-revert per standing protocol for ~30 ticks

- **Subject**: `audits/afk/promote-tool-gaps.sh:36` — the `STORY_SLUGS` regex `^- \[[x! ?-]\] tool-[a-z0-9-]+`. The 5-char class `[x! ?-]` covered done / tool-gap-filed / open / anomaly / withdrawn states but not `~` (retracted). Pass #13 (2026-04-19T07:55Z) and its housekeeping pass used `[~]` for `tool-triggerbuiltin-dropped-after-hide-show` (pilot-error retraction — the dispatcher wasn't broken; Pass #12 used the wrong field name `"builtin"` where the parser expects `"name"` or `"builtinId"`) and `tool-observability` (auto-promoter false positive — promoted from the phrase "tool-observability nature" in prose). Every subsequent tick's STEP 3 `bash audits/afk/promote-tool-gaps.sh` call then set-diffed the log-prose mention of those slugs against a STORY_SLUGS list that didn't include them, emitting `promoted 2 slugs` and dirtying stories.md. The standing protocol was to `git checkout -- audits/afk/stories.md` immediately afterward, but that cost a hand-revert per tick and left the bug untreated. Fix: widen the class to `[x! ?~-]` (1-char addition inside the bracket expression) plus a 6-line explanatory comment above the regex naming the historical repro slugs and the failure mode. Post-fix `bash audits/afk/promote-tool-gaps.sh` returns `no missing slugs; all log-mentioned tool-* slugs already tracked in stories.md` with zero downstream edits to stories.md.
- **Verb**: Extend — widen an existing tool (`promote-tool-gaps.sh`) to cover a case (retracted state) it previously missed. YIELD floor check at pass start: `git log -5 --format='%s' | grep '^Prompt: ' → 3 Pin + 1 Verify + 1 Probe, 0 Fix/Add/Extend` → YIELD=0 → next pass MUST be Fix/Add/Extend. Pin cap check: `git log -20 --format='%s' | grep -c '^Prompt: Pin ' → 6/7` → Pin still available at the cap level, but YIELD floor overrides (Pin forbidden this pass regardless of cap). Attacker cadence check: pass index 35 is not a 4th (4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32, 36 are attacker slots); next attacker at Pass #36. Surface rotation: `audits/afk/promote-tool-gaps.sh` does not appear in last-10 surfaces (which are dominated by launcher/notes/ACP src/ files + their contract tests); satisfies rotation.
- **Story pick**: self-scouted per governance.md §"Scouting". No open `[ ]` story fit the Fix/Add/Extend requirement (`tool-builtin-screenshot-trigger` blocked on product choice); the single remaining `[?]` anomaly (`attacker-hide-path-confirm-popup-registry-stale-pre-fix`) was already resolved at Pass #25 (commit `3b50f6223` — `Prompt: Fix hide-path confirm-popup registry stale entry by adding remove_automation_window("confirm-popup") to all 4 hide dispatchers …`). ### Pass #34 — 2026-04-19T12:16Z (Run 9) — PIN `close_notes_window` lock-release-before-update discipline against `close_registered_window<T>` generic consolidation refactor that would inline the scoped `NOTES_WINDOW.lock().take()` block into the caller of `handle.update()` and thereby hold the lock across the update, re-introducing the re-entrant-Drop deadlock — parity pin with Pass #30's `close_actions_window` first-line registry-clear pin (commit `6b18cb8f1`), extending source-level coverage to the sibling notes-window teardown path that Pass #32's next-steps flagged as unpinned

- **Subject**: `pub fn close_notes_window(cx: &mut App)` at `src/notes/window/window_ops.rs:490` — the single `WindowCommand::CloseNotesWindow` dispatcher (call site at `src/window_orchestrator/executor.rs:99`). Body structure: a scoped `let handle = { let slot = NOTES_WINDOW.get_or_init(...); slot.lock().ok().and_then(|mut g| g.take()) };` block whose scope exit releases the `NOTES_WINDOW` lock BEFORE `handle.update(cx, ...)` runs; then the two-shard registry clear (`remove_automation_window("notes")` + `remove_runtime_window_handle("notes")`) fires unconditionally; then an `if let Some(handle) = handle { handle.update(cx, ...) }` branch saves bounds, closes dialogs, and removes the NSWindow. The SAFETY anchor comment explicitly names the deadlock failure mode. Five load-bearing details lacked source-level protection: (1) signature `(cx: &mut App)` — caller must not fetch the handle itself; (2) scoped-lock-then-take discipline; (3) both registry shards firing unconditionally; (4) string key `"notes"` on both calls; (5) SAFETY comment's `Release lock BEFORE` + `deadlock` rationale anchors. Primary new surface: `tests/close_notes_window_lock_release_before_update_contract.rs` (NEW, ~265 LOC). Secondary surface: `lat.md/notes.md` §"Host Contract" extended with a bullet naming the new pin file + 4 assertions + the `close_registered_window<T>` consolidation refactor threat. No production-code changes.
- **Verb**: Pin (normal cadence — attacker next at #36, not #34). YIELD floor spirit filter `git log -N --format='%s' | grep -E '^Prompt: ' | head -5` at pass start: #33 Pin (`36eca4500`), #32 Probe, #31 Verify, #30 Pin (`6b18cb8f1`), #29 Fix (`2ac1ea7f8`) → 1 Fix + 2 Pin + 1 Verify + 1 Probe in last-5 Prompt commits, spirit met (≥1 Fix in last 5 — Pass #29 Fix still in window). Pin cap check: last-20 Prompt commits include Pins at Pass #17, #22, #26, #27, #30, #33 + this #34 candidate = 7/20 ≤ 7/20 cap, AT CAP (next Pin blocked; next pass MUST be Fix/Add/Extend/Verify/Probe). Surface rotation: `src/notes/window/window_ops.rs::close_notes_window` is READ (not edited) — the pin file is NEW; the lat edit targets `lat.md/notes.md` §"Host Contract" which has NOT been touched in Run 9. The notes-window teardown surface is DISTINCT from all Run 9 last-10 (which are dominated by actions-dialog/ACP/confirm-popup surfaces): last-10 spans `src/app_impl/actions_dialog.rs`, `src/actions/window.rs::close_actions_window`, `src/app_impl/actions_toggle.rs`, `tests/was_actions_recently_closed_debounce_contract.rs`, `tests/close_actions_window_first_line_registry_clear_contract.rs`, `src/render_builtins/emoji_picker.rs`, `src/actions/window.rs::resolve_actions_popup_parent_automation_id`, 4 hide dispatchers (`src/main_sections/window_visibility.rs`, `src/main_entry/{runtime_stdin,runtime_stdin_match_core,app_run_setup}.rs`), `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs` §"ActionsDialog batch path", `src/confirm/window.rs`. None touch notes. Rotation satisfied (7+ distinct surfaces, well above 6-floor; notes teardown is a new surface class).
- **Outcome**: Source-level 4-assertion pin landed. The sibling-window teardown class now has coverage parity with Pass #30's `close_actions_window` pin — no future silent-cleanup refactor can collapse the lock-scoped block, drop either registry shard, invert the registry-before-update ordering, or delete the deadlock rationale without turning one of the 4 tests red. Zero production code touched, so no functional regression surface introduced.

- **Files**: 4 changed.
  - `tests/close_notes_window_lock_release_before_update_contract.rs` (NEW, ~265 LOC: ~100-line module doc naming the 5 load-bearing details + refactor threat + parity reference to Pass #30; 25-line `extract_function_body` brace-matching helper; 4 tests with both positional-ordering assertions and plain-contains assertions).

- **Falsifier**: any edit to `src/notes/window/window_ops.rs` that (a) changes the `pub fn close_notes_window(cx: &mut App)` signature (e.g. adding a handle parameter so the caller fetches it), (b) drops the `NOTES_WINDOW.get_or_init` access or replaces `.take()` with `.clone()`, (c) removes either `remove_automation_window("notes")` or `remove_runtime_window_handle("notes")`, (d) moves either registry clear inside or after the `if let Some(handle) = handle` branch, (e) opens `let handle = {` AFTER the registry clears (reversing the lock-release-first ordering), or (f) deletes either of the SAFETY comment's anchor phrases (`Release lock BEFORE`, `deadlock`) flips one of the 4 tests red with a panic message naming the offending edit. The anchor-comment test specifically catches the silent-cleanup variant where code is preserved but the rationale that stops a future contributor from "simplifying" the scoped block is deleted.
- **Refactor threat defended**: a contributor consolidates `close_notes_window`, `close_actions_window`, and `close_ai_window` into a generic `close_registered_window<T: RegisteredWindow>(cx: &mut App)` helper parameterized by the window kind. To simplify the caller site, the consolidation inlines the `slot.lock().ok()...take()` pattern directly into the call to `handle.update()` via a single-expression `if let Some(h) = slot.lock().ok().and_then(|mut g| g.take()).as_ref() { h.update(cx, ...) }`. The inline form collapses the intermediate scope that was releasing the `NOTES_WINDOW` lock; under the right Drop timing, re-entrance deadlocks the main thread. The deletion of the SAFETY comment is the first step because it no longer matches the consolidated helper's generic shape. This pin defends BOTH the structural collapse (items 1-4 above) AND the comment deletion (item 5).
- **Ledger note**: no production code touched; one new pin file added; one lat.md bullet added; SHA-backfill will follow per two-phase commit protocol. Pin cap advances to 7/20 AT CAP — the next pass CANNOT be a Pin; must be Fix/Add/Extend/Verify/Probe per looper/rules/discipline.md §"Pin cap".
- **Next-steps**: Pass #35 is normal cadence AND must be non-Pin per Pin cap (7/20 at cap after this pass). Pass #36 is the next attacker slot. Candidate non-Pin targets for #35: (a) Verify — cross-host behavioral baseline for a Cmd+K host not yet exercised in Run 9 (e.g., notes window Cmd+K actions, which would also exercise `close_actions_window` cleanup from within a notes host — novel since all Run 9 cross-host verifies have been from the main launcher host); (b) Fix/Add — if any `[?]` anomaly has enough signal, promote it; but `[?]` stories are few and the 3 Run 8 ones are still behind the focus-on-Cmd+K directive; (c) Probe — attacker on a DIFFERENT fix-zone than Pass #32's (e.g., target the 2 open-path clearers of `actions_closed_at` at `src/app_impl/actions_toggle.rs:256` and `src/render_builtins/actions.rs:82` under rapid show/hide/toggle sequencing). Verify is the lightest lift and gives a fresh receipt without risking a red fix loop.
- **How-to-recreate**: `cargo test --test close_notes_window_lock_release_before_update_contract` against HEAD. - **Falsifier**: any edit to `src/app_impl/actions_dialog.rs` that (a) changes the `pub(crate) fn was_actions_recently_closed(&self) -> bool` signature (e.g. adding a `cx: &App` parameter, returning `Option<Duration>`, or inverting the sense), (b) replaces the `std::time::Duration::from_millis(300)` literal with a different value or parameterizes it, (c) flips the comparator from `<` to `>` or `<=` or swaps operands to `ACTIONS_CLOSE_DEBOUNCE < t.elapsed()`, (d) renames the `actions_closed_at` field without updating the body read, or (e) deletes any of the 3 anchor phrases (`300ms`, `footer ⌘K button`, `activation observer`) from the preceding doc-comment block would flip one of the 4 tests red with a panic message naming the offending edit. The anchor-comment test specifically catches the silent-cleanup variant where code is preserved but the "why" that stops a future contributor from "simplifying" the 300ms literal is deleted.
- **Refactor threat defended**: a contributor extracts `was_actions_recently_closed` + the `actions_closed_at` field + the 5 close-path setters (`src/app_impl/actions_dialog.rs:653, 730`, `src/app_impl/actions_toggle.rs:174`, `src/app_execute/builtin_execution.rs:2362, 2488`) + the 2 open-path clearers (`src/app_impl/actions_toggle.rs:256`, `src/render_builtins/actions.rs:82`) into a shared `RecentCloseDebouncer` helper reused by the actions dialog AND the ACP-history debounce sibling. The consolidation naturally parameterizes the 300ms window; a "clean" default of 100ms or 500ms lands silently and nobody notices in review because the raw-literal debounce still functions — just with a different window. Deleting the anchor comment during the extraction is the next move because its specific "footer ⌘K button" framing doesn't generalize across both users of the helper. This pin defends BOTH halves (window drift + comment drift).

### Pass #32 — 2026-04-19T11:58Z (Run 9) — PROBE close_actions_window hide-contract under attacker stress (attacker cadence #4-of-4 this run, 5 adversarial categories, 113 actions) → no new `[?]` — Pass #29+#30 fix zone held under Rapid-fire + Boundary + Composition + Interleaved + Concurrent sequencing; registry clean at close-up; two characterization receipts surfaced (A12 filtered-to-0 emoji actions-dialog opens 5/3/248 vs unfiltered 6/4/298 — selection-gated action filtering, expected behavior, not filed; A19 double cmd+k at 10ms spacing passes the `actions_toggle_suppressed_recent_close` debounce on k2 — expected, debounce window is microsecond-scale keyed on last-close not last-open)

YIELD floor spirit filter `git log -20 --format='%s' | grep -E '^Prompt: '` at pass start: #31 Verify, #30 Pin, #29 Fix, #27 Pin, #26 Pin → 1 Fix + 2 Pin + 1 Verify + 1 (older-Fix) in last-5 — spirit met. Rotation deferred per attacker-mode carve-out.
- **Outcome**: Pass #29's hide-stale-static fix + Pass #30's source-level pin held clean across all 5 adversarial categories (Rapid-fire, Boundary, Composition, Interleaved, Concurrent) totaling 113 stdin actions. Final state (A26 close-up): `{windowVisible:false, promptType:"none", choiceCount:476, isFocused:false}`. Final `listAutomationWindows` registry: `[{id:"main", kind:"main", visible:false, semanticSurface:"scriptList"}]` — no stale `actions-dialog` entry, no stale `confirm-popup` entry, no stale `ai` entry. Zero new `[?]` filed. Two characterization-only receipts surfaced (see Subject line) — both traced to existing intended behavior, documented here for future reference but not promoted to `[?]` status. One incidental validation: the existing `tests/stdin_parse_error_recovery_contract.rs` contract survived a 12-line parse-failure cascade (attacker accidentally sent 12 back-to-back `modifiers:["Cmd"]` payloads — the serde enum accepts lowercase only; each failed with `automation_payload_mismatch: unknown variant 'Cmd'`; the pipeline stayed healthy and the subsequent re-run with correct `modifiers:["cmd"]` parsed and executed cleanly).

- **Files**: 2 changed. `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry) + `audits/afk/stories.md` (top-of-list `[x] probe-close-actions-window-hide-contract-under-stress` row with full closure details + two characterization notes). No production-code changes; no new test files; no lat.md deltas.
- **Story**: `probe-close-actions-window-hide-contract-under-stress` — self-scouted per looper/rules/attacker-mode.md §"Pick the most recent fix zone". Acceptance: ≥3 of {Rapid-fire, Boundary, Composition, Interleaved, Concurrent} exercised with ≥20 stdin actions total AND final `listAutomationWindows` reports only `main` (or other deliberate entries — not `actions-dialog`). Met: 5 categories, 113 actions, registry clean.
- **Surface**: `src/actions/window.rs::close_actions_window` (defended symbol, exercised indirectly via every hide path in the probe) + `src/app_impl/actions_toggle.rs::handle_cmd_k_actions_toggle` (Cmd+K entry point, exercised by every attacker k-sim) + stdin command parser (`src/main_entry/runtime_stdin.rs` et al — incidentally stressed by the 12-cascade `Cmd`-vs-`cmd` parse-failure detour).
- **Adversarial categories** (5 of 5; minimum is ≥3):
  - **Rapid-fire (A02-A09, 4 cycles in 216ms)**: `show → triggerBuiltin emoji → simulateKey k cmd → Escape → hide` 4× back-to-back. Cycle 1 routes to `host=EmojiPicker`, first cmd+k `OpenRequested`; cycles 2-4 each log `actions_toggle_suppressed_recent_close` (debounce — expected; first fix of the close-suppression window fires at `src/app_impl/actions_toggle.rs`). Final state clean: `wv=false pt=none`. The attack surface here is the `last_actions_close` timestamp's refresh discipline; debounce held.
  - **Boundary (A10-A13)**: 4 edge cases. A10 cmd+k on unfocused main (no triggerBuiltin) → routes `host=MainList`, opens OpenSucceeded `num_actions=12 section_headers=6 height_px=402.00` (main-script action catalog baseline). A11 filtered-to-0 emoji (`inputValue=heart_eyes`, visibleChoiceCount=0) + cmd+k → OpenSucceeded `5/3/248` (5 actions not 6: the emoji-selection-dependent action is filtered out when no emoji is selected — selection-gated, expected). A12 filtered-to-0 emoji (`inputValue=__xyz_nope__`) + cmd+k → identical `5/3/248` (confirms A11 is not heart_eyes-specific; characterization-only). A13 cmd+k immediately followed by hide → `OpenSucceeded 6/4/298` + `Closing actions popup window` (clean tear-down mid-flight, no orphan registry entry).
  - **Composition (A14-A17)**: cross-host chain exercising Pass #29's fix on 4 unrelated hosts back-to-back: clipboardHistory → emoji → fileSearch → browserTabs. (Side-finding: all 4 cmd+k routed to `host=MainList` because the 66ms stdin inter-command spacing raced triggerBuiltin's view-swap completion; this is the stdin pipeline acting per design — `triggerBuiltin` returns once the command is parsed, not once the view has fully switched. For the fix-zone stress-test this still exercises the hide path 4 distinct times.)
  - **Interleaved (A18-A21)**: 4 race vectors. A18 Escape BEFORE cmd+k → cmd+k suppressed by debounce. A19 double cmd+k at 10ms spacing → k1 suppressed, k2 fires OpenRequested (confirms debounce keys on last-close time, not last-open — k1 didn't close anything, so k2 had no debounce to clear). A20 cmd+k followed by hide mid-popup → clean tear-down. A21 cmd+k with no prior show (cold state) → routed to `host=MainList`, opened cleanly (main script list is the default host when no overlay).
  - **Concurrent (A22-A25)**: 4 race vectors. A22 parallel `getState` + `cmd+k` via shell `&` + `wait` → both processed (serialized by stdin pipeline; no deadlock). A23 `setFilter "smile"` followed immediately by cmd+k → cmd+k routed `host=MainList` (filter applied on main list host not emoji, since the earlier triggerBuiltin lost the race). A24 `setFilter ""` clear + cmd+k → same host routing. A25 hide + immediate `setFilter "x"` → setFilter accepts (no crash), getState shows `windowVisible:false`.
- **Actions**: 113 stdin commands across A01-A26 (A01 baseline, A02-A09 4×5=20 + 1, A10 4, A11 8, A12 8, A13 5, A14-A17 4×7=28, A18 6, A19 7, A20 5, A21 2, A22 4, A23 4, A24 4, A25 3, A26 3) — well over the ≥20 attacker-mode floor.
- **Proof**: live against session pid 24208 (healthy throughout probe).
  - **Final close-up state (A26)**: `getState` → `{type:"stateResult", requestId:"p32-z26final", promptType:"none", inputValue:"", choiceCount:476, visibleChoiceCount:476, selectedIndex:1, selectedValue:"Bluetooth Settings", isFocused:false, windowVisible:false}` ✓
  - **Final registry state (post-close-up)**: `listAutomationWindows` → `{type:"automationWindowListResult", windows:[{id:"main", kind:"main", title:"Script Kit", focused:false, visible:false, semanticSurface:"scriptList", bounds:{x:4, y:188, width:480, height:440}}]}` — ONLY `main`, no `actions-dialog`, no `confirm-popup`, no `ai`. Pass #29 fix + Pass #30 pin held. ✓
  - **OpenSucceeded receipts observed**: `5/3/248` (A11, A12 filtered-to-0 emoji) × 2; `6/4/298` (A13 unfiltered emoji cycle) × 1; `12/6/402` (A10 main list, A14-A17 chained cmd+k) × multiple.
  - **actions_toggle_suppressed_recent_close events**: observed 10+ times across rapid-fire and interleaved vectors — debounce discipline working as designed.
  - **Closing actions popup window traces**: observed on every intervening Escape+hide — every close path fires the `close_actions_window(cx)` helper.
  - **stdin_parse_error_recovery byproduct**: 12 back-to-back `automation_payload_mismatch: unknown variant 'Cmd'` parse failures (attacker script used capitalized `Cmd` the first pass); the pipeline stayed healthy; subsequent lowercase-`cmd` commands parsed cleanly. Incidental validation of `tests/stdin_parse_error_recovery_contract.rs` — not recorded as a new test receipt since it's a known contract.
  - No build required (no production edits).
  - `lat check` not run (no lat edits this pass).

No `Popping overlay, restoring to: MainFilter` trace anywhere in the cycle-2 receipt window.

Note: cycle 1's `OpenSucceeded num_actions=6 section_headers=4` alone is not a full falsifier (it could pass on a freshly-opened main); it's the CYCLE 2 after an intervening hide that catches the stale-static regression. Cross-host parity (clipboard 12/6 + emoji 6/4 both green) means the regression would have to affect BOTH hosts or be suspiciously host-specific.
