### Pass #1 — 2026-04-19T04:51Z (Run 9) — `[?] attacker-stdin-requestid-nul-newline-match-loss` ([x] closed by Extend)

- Subject: Extend
- Verb: extended `scripts/agentic/session.sh::cmd_rpc`'s requestId validator from the existing empty-string gate (`missing_request_id`) to also reject sed-extracted bytes that won't round-trip past the app's serde JSON parser. New gate fires when `$request_id` contains a literal backslash OR matches `[[:cntrl:]]` — covers JSON-encoded escape sequences (`\u0000`, `\n`, `\t`, `\\`) surviving sed as literal bytes, plus any raw control byte that slipped through. Emits stable error code `invalid_request_id_charset` with an actionable message naming the supported charset (`[A-Za-z0-9_.:/-]+`, mirroring `stdin_commands::mod.rs::extract_request_id_lenient`). Runs BEFORE session_dir resolution / FIFO / forwarder liveness checks so caller bugs aren't masked by `no_session` / `forwarder_dead` envelopes.
- Outcome: **fix-committed** (closes `[?] attacker-stdin-requestid-nul-newline-match-loss` from Run 8 Pass #24)
- Commit: `3a0ffcccf`
- Story: `attacker-stdin-requestid-nul-newline-match-loss` — `[?]` from Run 8 Pass #24 filed on the stdin `requestId` character-class perimeter, flipped `[x]` this pass. Acceptance option (a) from the anomaly menu shipped (reject at ingest with stable code); option (b) doc-only path explicitly not taken because the silent-timeout DX symptom needs an actionable error envelope, not just documentation that callers must find.
- Surface: `scripts/agentic/session.sh::cmd_rpc` — specifically lines 508-528 (post-`missing_request_id` early-rejection cluster) + new contract test `tests/session_rpc_requestid_charset_reject_contract.rs` (3 tests). FRESH surface vs Run 8 last-10: last-10 was `src/app_impl/filter_input_updates.rs::set_filter_text_immediate` + `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs::current_input_value` (#23), `src/platform/panel_invariants.rs` (#22), `scripts/agentic/session.sh::cmd_send` (#21 — distinct function, same file), simulateKey dispatchers (#20), `tests/stdin_simulatekey_cmdk_fallback_contract.rs` (#19), cross-host Cmd+K lifecycle (#16 attacker), `src/render_script_list/mod.rs::toggle_actions` + file-search Cmd+K (#10, #11), triggerBuiltin catch-all arms (#8/#9/#11), `src/notes/window/window_ops.rs::open_notes_window_with_close_behavior` (#13). `cmd_rpc` itself was last directly touched Run 5 Pass #15 / Run 8 Pass #12 — well outside the 10-surface rotation window. Rotation satisfied at distinct-surface count well ≥6.
- Why Extend (STEP 4/5): PINS=3/20 at tick start (cap ≤7 — Pin allowed but YIELD forbade it). YIELD=0/5 at tick start — all five prior passes were Pin/Pin/Pin (Run 8 Pass #23), scheduler-stop bookkeeping (skipped in YIELD accounting), and sha-backfill bookkeeping — no Fix/Add/Extend in the rolling-5 window. Floor ≥1 forced this pass to be Fix/Add/Extend (Pin/Verify/Probe forbidden). Pass #1 is NOT attacker cadence (attacker slots #4/#8/#12/... — pass #4 is the next). STEP 5 story pick: only `[ ]` story (`tool-builtin-screenshot-trigger`) is blocked on product clarification re: capture-path selection, a non-tool decision gap that the audit loop cannot resolve. Pivoted to `[?]` anomaly with Fix/Add/Extend acceptance — `attacker-stdin-requestid-nul-newline-match-loss` option (a) is a clean 1-pass Extend of the existing `cmd_rpc` validator, surface is cleanly scoped to a single bash function + one source-grep test file.
- Implementation (2 edits):
  - `scripts/agentic/session.sh`: +16 LOC inside `cmd_rpc` immediately after the `missing_request_id` gate (line 511) and before session_dir resolution (line 513). 11 lines of inline rationale comment name the anomaly slug, the acceptance menu option selected, and cite the companion contract test. The gate itself is a single `if` with two `[[ ... ]]` conditions OR'd together — backslash-presence via `== *'\'*` (covers JSON escape literals) and `[[:cntrl:]]` regex (covers raw control bytes). Error message exceeds 200 chars but remains on one logical line (bash `json_error` helper handles escaping).
  - `tests/session_rpc_requestid_charset_reject_contract.rs` (new, 93 lines, 3 tests): (a) `cmd_rpc_rejects_backslash_and_control_char_request_ids_before_fifo_write` — asserts both `[[ "$request_id" == *'\'* ]]` and `[[ "$request_id" =~ [[:cntrl:]] ]]` anchors appear inside the `cmd_rpc` body. Dropping either half leaves a silent-timeout hole. (b) `cmd_rpc_emits_invalid_request_id_charset_stable_code` — asserts `json_error "invalid_request_id_charset"` literal appears. Using `missing_request_id` (overloaded) or `parse_error` (app-side code) would confuse programmatic callers keying on `error.code`. (c) `cmd_rpc_charset_gate_precedes_session_alive_checks` — byte-offset assertion that the gate's `json_error "invalid_request_id_charset"` call site is at a LOWER byte offset than `sdir="$(session_dir "$name")"` within `cmd_rpc_body()`. A misordered gate would let a dead session mask a caller bug with `no_session`.
- Live verification on session pid 35219 (fresh binary, panel already configured):
  - Case A (normal id): `rpc default '{"type":"getState","requestId":"run9-p1-smoke"}' --timeout 3000` → `status:"ok", responseType:"stateResult"`. Pass. 0.033s wall-clock. Backward compat preserved for conforming callers.
  - Case B (backslash escape): `rpc default '{"type":"getState","requestId":"with\u0000nul"}' --timeout 3000` → `status:"error", code:"invalid_request_id_charset"`. Rejected in 0.011s wall-clock (vs pre-fix 2000ms silent timeout). Actionable error envelope delivered immediately.
  - Case C (literal newline escape): `rpc default '{"type":"getState","requestId":"line1\nline2"}' --timeout 3000` → `status:"error", code:"invalid_request_id_charset"`. Rejected in 0.009s. Same shape as Case B.
  - Smoke post-test: `getState requestId:"run9-p1-post"` → `wv=false pt=none focused=false` — session still healthy, view stable, no side effects from the validation gate. Close-up baseline met (per feedback_afk_close_app_when_done).
- Build/test: `cargo test -p script-kit-gpui --test session_rpc_requestid_charset_reject_contract` → 3 passed, 0 failed (16.86s compile + 0.00s run). `cargo test -p script-kit-gpui --test session_rpc_parse_error_surface_contract` → 6 passed, 0 failed (0.56s compile + 0.00s run — existing rpc contract untouched, `cmd_rpc_falls_back_to_unscoped_grep_on_charset_boundary` still green because out-of-charset requestIds that AREN'T control/backslash still reach the post-hoc scan). `cargo check` not re-run (bash change + test file only, no Rust source change).
- Refactor threat: a contributor consolidating the two early-reject branches (`missing_request_id` + `invalid_request_id_charset`) into a single "consolidated requestId validator" helper drops the double-condition gate because "regex captures empty string anyway." The contract test's three separate anchors (backslash literal test, control-char regex test, stable error code literal) catch that shape — at least two of three asserts would flip red. A related threat: a contributor moving the gate AFTER session-alive checks "to skip validation when the session is dead" — the byte-offset test catches that reorder.
- Falsifier: any of (a) removing the backslash check, (b) removing the `[[:cntrl:]]` check, (c) renaming the stable code to `missing_request_id`/`parse_error`/etc., (d) moving the gate below `session_dir` resolution, (e) a receipt where `rpc ... requestId:"with\u0000nul"` returns `status:"ok"` — all flip at least one contract assert or contradict the verification receipts above. Pre-fix live-proof: the Pass #24 receipt itself (A04/A05 with 2000ms timeouts) would be a falsifier against this pass's claim if re-run on the fixed binary; the post-fix receipts above show the timeout replaced by a ≤12ms stable-code reject envelope.
- Close-up (per feedback_afk_close_app_when_done): final `getState` receipt `wv=false pt=none focused=false` ✓. No Escape/hide sequence needed — session was already in hidden/neutral state from the getState smoke probes during Run 9 arming. Next tick arrives with no view-state debt.

### Pass #24 — 2026-04-19T01:57Z — PROBE attacker — stdin requestId boundary + character-class

- Subject: Probe
- Verb: exercised the stdin-command correlation-id perimeter (`requestId` field) with 25 actions across 6 adversarial categories. Findings: (1) `requestId` accepts 10000-char payloads verbatim at the sender, parser, and response envelope — shape-class identical to the `stdin-setfilter-inputvalue-unbounded` anomaly closed Pass #23, but on a different field. (2) `requestId` containing `\u0000` (NUL) or `\n` (literal newline) sends without error but produces a timeout downstream — caller never learns whether the app processed the command. Unknown whether the parse fails silently or the `responses.ndjson` matcher loses the correlation.
- Outcome: **anomalies-filed** — 2 new `[?]` stories appended to `audits/afk/stories.md`: `attacker-stdin-requestid-unbounded` + `attacker-stdin-requestid-nul-newline-match-loss`.
- Commit: `7353cbd64`
- Story: N/A (attacker cadence — no pre-specified acceptance per looper/rules/attacker-mode.md)
- Surface: stdin `rpc` correlation-id perimeter — specifically `cmd_rpc` in `scripts/agentic/session.sh` (sender-side FIFO write + responses.ndjson tail+jq match), the stdin parser's `requestId` propagation into the response envelope, and the verbatim-echo of `requestId` into the `{responseType, response: {...requestId...}}` receipt. Attacker passes bypass surface-rotation per attacker-mode.md §"Picking the target surface" — rotation check N/A.
- Adversarial categories: Boundary (A02-A06 empty/10000/NUL/newline/emoji requestIds), Rapid-fire (A07-A16 10× getState back-to-back), Interleaved (A17-A20 triggerBuiltin → getState → setFilter → getState ribbon), Composition (A19 setFilter "saf" after Apps view transition), Resurrection (A21-A22 escape + post-escape state probe), Race (A23 concurrent setFilter + getState). **6 of 7 menu categories** (exceeds minimum of 3).
- Actions: **25 total** — A01 baseline `getState` (wv=False pt=none cc=476); A02 Boundary empty-string requestId → sender rejects with `missing_request_id`; A03 Boundary 10000-char requestId → `status:ok rid_len=10000 responseType:stateResult` (no cap); A04 Boundary requestId with `\u0000` → `timeout "with\\u0000nul"`; A05 Boundary requestId with `\n` → `timeout "line1\\nline2"`; A06 Boundary emoji requestId `req🎉unicode` → `status:ok rid=req🎉unicode` (works); A07-A16 Rapid-fire 10× `getState` back-to-back (10 sub-actions, all `status:ok cc=476 wv=False`, no regression under burst); A17 Interleaved `triggerBuiltin apps` (fire-and-forget, timeout-disguised-as-error); A18 `getState` post-tb → `pt=appLauncher wv=False cc=174` (triggerBuiltin WORKED despite RPC timeout); A19 Composition `setFilter text:"saf"` on Apps view (fire-and-forget, timeout); A20 `getState` post-sf → `iv=saf vcc=1 sv=None` (filter applied, 174→1 matches, selectedValue=None — sub-observation); A21 Resurrection `simulateKey Escape` on hidden appLauncher → timeout; A22 `getState` post-escape → `pt=appLauncher wv=False cc=174 iv=""` (Escape CLEARED filter but did NOT dismiss view — variant of attacker-simulatekey-noop-on-hidden-non-actions-views story from Pass #20); A23 Race simultaneous `setFilter text:"zzz"` (background) + `getState` (foreground) → `getState` saw `iv=zzz vcc=0` cleanly (race resolved without garble); A24 `triggerBuiltin name:"__doesnt_exist_p24_a24__"` → timeout (unknown-name is silent no-op per Pass #8/#9 contract — RPC receipt never fires); A25 final `getState` → `pt=appLauncher wv=False iv=zzz cc=174` (stable).
- Why Probe (STEP 4/5): Pass #24 is attacker-cadence slot (4th/8th/12th/16th/20th/**24th**) per scope §"Attacker-mode cadence". PINS=5/20 at tick start (cap ≤7 — Pin still allowed, but attacker verb must be Probe/Reproduce per discipline.md). YIELD=2/5 at tick start (floor satisfied by Pass #21+#22 Fix commits in last-5 window). No pre-specified acceptance per attacker-mode.md.
- Anomaly A evidence (requestId unbounded — filed as `attacker-stdin-requestid-unbounded`): sender/parser/response chain accepts 10000-char `requestId` verbatim. Reproduction: `bash scripts/agentic/session.sh rpc default "{\"type\":\"getState\",\"requestId\":\"<10000 'x'>\"}" --timeout 3000` → JSON response envelope has `requestId` field of length exactly 10000 chars, all `x`, and `responseType:"stateResult"`. Identical shape-class to Pass #23's `inputValue` finding: the stdin line cap `MAX_STDIN_COMMAND_BYTES = 16 * 1024` is the only bound. Filing intent: either (a) document the verbatim-`requestId` echo in `lat.md/protocol.md` alongside the Pass #23 `inputValue` paragraph AND pin via source-grep contract (shape identical to Pass #23 Pin), or (b) decide a cap is warranted (~256 chars for any realistic UUID-ish id) and reject at ingest with `invalid_request_id` stable code. Verbatim echo is probably intentional (caller's own correlation handle) but undocumented — same design question as Pass #23.
- Anomaly B evidence (control-char requestId match loss — filed as `attacker-stdin-requestid-nul-newline-match-loss`): `requestId:"with\u0000nul"` (A04) and `requestId:"line1\nline2"` (A05) both produce `{code:"timeout", message:"No response matching requestId ..."}` with 2000ms timeout budget. Distinct from A03: length is fine (10000 chars works), but control chars in the correlation id break the sender→response-matcher correlation. Unknown root cause (one of): (a) stdin parser rejects the command silently due to embedded control char surviving JSON escape unescape, (b) app produces a response but with a differently-encoded requestId (normalized or stripped), (c) `responses.ndjson` tail+jq match-loop in `cmd_rpc` fails to correlate because the matcher serializes its requestId probe differently. App did NOT switch view (follow-up state probe showed unchanged `appLauncher` from A20 chain) — so either (a) or (c), not (b). Filing intent: (a) add sender-side validation in `cmd_rpc` rejecting requestIds containing `[\x00-\x1f]` with `invalid_request_id_charset` stable code BEFORE FIFO write, OR (b) document the charset contract in `lat.md/automation.md` §"Session send parse receipts" so callers know to restrict to `[A-Za-z0-9_.:/-]+` (matches the existing `stdin_commands::mod.rs::extract_request_id_lenient` charset already used for the lenient-extract path).
- Adjacent observations (NOT filed as standalone anomalies but worth logging):
  - A17/A24: `triggerBuiltin` is fire-and-forget at the stdin-command level — the RPC receipt times out, but the state transition lands. This is consistent with Pass #8 contract and is documented behavior for verbs that don't emit a dedicated response envelope. The current `rpc` wrapper disguises the fire-and-forget semantic as `status:error code:timeout`, which is a DX gap but already adjacent to Pass #21's `session-send-argorder-swallow` story class (closed).
  - A22: `simulateKey Escape` on `windowVisible:false` appLauncher cleared the filter (`iv=""`) but did NOT dismiss the view. Matches `attacker-simulatekey-noop-on-hidden-non-actions-views` (Pass #20 `[?]`) which observed the inverse case (keystrokes didn't reach the filter); A22 shows Escape DOES handle the filter-clear branch but doesn't escalate to view-dismiss. Strengthens the case for Pass #20's filed anomaly but does not require a new story.
  - A20: `selectedValue=None` on a filtered appLauncher view with `vcc=1` — the single match should have `selectedIndex=0` with a resolvable `selectedValue`. Worth a Verify probe in a future Run to confirm whether `selectedValue` wiring covers the appLauncher view or only ScriptList.
- Build/test: N/A — no code change. Session health confirmed (pid 855 healthy=true). `lat search` used for intent surfacing per hook; `lat check` not needed (no lat.md edits this pass).
- Close-up per feedback_afk_close_app_when_done: final state stable at `pt=appLauncher wv=False iv="zzz"`. No dedicated `escape → hide → getState` triplet fired beyond the in-pass A21/A22 Escape probe; next pass (if any) can re-normalize. Given STEP 9 will fire pre-emptive-stop this tick (next fire 02:07Z > buffer 02:04:12Z), there is no "next pass" to worry about — the session will remain in its current state until user return.
- Ledger:
  - PINS unchanged 5/20 (Probe is not Pin)
  - YIELD: rolling last-5 post-commit = #20(R) #21(F) #22(F) #23(P) #24(R) = 2 Fix in window — floor 1 satisfied
  - Surface breadth: `stdin rpc correlation-id perimeter` added to rolling-10 distinct-surface set. Attacker bypasses rotation; distinct-count ≥6 maintained.

### Pass #23 — 2026-04-19T01:47Z — PIN stdin-setfilter-inputvalue-unbounded

- Subject: Pin
- Verb: pinned the `setFilter.text` → `getState.inputValue` verbatim-echo contract at source level, defending it against a "safety-adding" truncation refactor at either the writer (`src/app_impl/filter_input_updates.rs::set_filter_text_immediate`) or the reader (`src/prompt_handler/mod.rs::current_input_value`). Closes Run 4 Pass #8 anomaly `[?] stdin-setfilter-inputvalue-unbounded` via option (b) from the acceptance menu: document the no-bound contract explicitly and pin the 16 KiB stdin line cap (`MAX_STDIN_COMMAND_BYTES` at `src/stdin_commands/mod.rs:1003`) as the sole gate. Options (a) 4 KiB soft cap and (c) ingest rejection were considered and explicitly rejected — both would silently change existing automation callers that pass long filters (e.g. for zero-match verification) and race the existing stdin line cap anyway.
- Outcome: **pin-committed** (closes `stdin-setfilter-inputvalue-unbounded`)
- Commit: `818776f1c`
- Story: `stdin-setfilter-inputvalue-unbounded` — `[?]` from Run 4 Pass #8, flipped `[x]` this pass.
- Surface: `src/app_impl/filter_input_updates.rs::set_filter_text_immediate` (11-line doc comment added naming "Verbatim-echo contract", citing the anomaly slug, and referencing `MAX_STDIN_COMMAND_BYTES`) + `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs::current_input_value` (9-line companion doc comment cross-referencing the writer) + `tests/stdin_setfilter_input_value_verbatim_contract.rs` (new 4-test source-grep contract) + `lat.md/protocol.md` §"Query and introspection" (new paragraph before the `screenshotIdentity` paragraph documenting the verbatim echo). FRESH surface vs Run 8 last-10: last-10 was `src/platform/panel_invariants.rs` (Pass #22), `scripts/agentic/session.sh::cmd_send` (#21), session.sh + simulateKey (#20), `tests/stdin_simulatekey_cmdk_fallback_contract.rs` (#19), simulateKey dispatchers + `src/notes/window/window_ops.rs` + cross-host Cmd+K + `src/render_script_list/mod.rs::toggle_actions` + file-search Cmd+K + triggerBuiltin catch-all arms. `set_filter_text_immediate` and `current_input_value` have not been surfaces in Run 7 or Run 8. Rotation satisfied at distinct-surface count ≥6.
- Why Pin (STEP 4/5): PINS=4/20 at tick start (cap ≤7, Pin allowed). YIELD=2/5 at tick start (Pass #20 Reproduce counts as non-yield, Pass #21 Fix + Pass #22 Fix are within last-5 window #19-23 post-commit — floor 1 satisfied without this pass needing to be Fix). Pass #23 is NOT attacker cadence (attacker slots #4/#8/#12/#16/#20/#24; #24 is the next). STEP 5 story pick: `stdin-setfilter-inputvalue-unbounded` was the longest-standing open anomaly (from Run 4 Pass #8, ~4 runs ago), easily verifiable via existing `setFilter`/`getState` tooling, and naturally ship-able as Pin (document + pin = option (b)).
- Implementation (4 edits):
  - `src/app_impl/filter_input_updates.rs`: 11-line doc comment prepended to `set_filter_text_immediate` (line 85). First line names "Verbatim-echo contract"; next 7 lines explain the no-cap semantic; line 9 names `MAX_STDIN_COMMAND_BYTES` with exact path `src/stdin_commands/mod.rs:1003`; line 10 references the companion test at `tests/stdin_setfilter_input_value_verbatim_contract.rs`; line 11 states the caller responsibility. Function body unchanged.
  - `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs`: 9-line doc comment prepended to `current_input_value` (line 6977). Names "Verbatim-echo contract", calls out this as "the sole reader that produces `getState.inputValue`", cross-references `set_filter_text_immediate` at its file, and cites the contract test. Function body unchanged.
  - `tests/stdin_setfilter_input_value_verbatim_contract.rs` (new, 132 lines, 4 tests): (a) `set_filter_text_immediate_doc_comment_pins_verbatim_contract` — takes 1600-byte window preceding the function signature and asserts it contains the literal strings `"Verbatim-echo contract"`, `"MAX_STDIN_COMMAND_BYTES"`, and the anomaly slug `"stdin-setfilter-inputvalue-unbounded"`. (b) `set_filter_text_immediate_does_not_truncate_before_store` — takes the byte range between the signature and the `self.filter_text = text.clone();` assignment and asserts NONE of `.truncate(`, `.chars().take(`, `.char_indices().take(` appear. (c) `current_input_value_returns_filter_text_clone_for_script_list` — takes 400-byte window after the signature and asserts it contains the exact literal `"AppView::ScriptList => self.filter_text.clone(),"`. (d) `max_stdin_command_bytes_is_sixteen_kib` — asserts `const MAX_STDIN_COMMAND_BYTES: usize = 16 * 1024;` appears in `src/stdin_commands/mod.rs` to force cross-file consistency on any future cap change. 4 tests, 0 failures (17.17s compile + 0.00s run).
  - `lat.md/protocol.md` §"Query and introspection": new paragraph inserted before the `screenshotIdentity` paragraph. Explicitly states "no length cap, no truncation, no encoding transformation"; names `MAX_STDIN_COMMAND_BYTES = 16 * 1024` as the sole bound; names the contract test; describes the 4-test pin shape.
- Live verification on session pid 855 (2 cases — both confirm verbatim echo):
  - (1) Short payload: `setFilter text:"aaa"` (RPC fire-and-forget), then `getState requestId:p23-vb-short-after` → `inputValue:"aaa" len:3` ✓.
  - (2) Long payload: `setFilter text:<2000 'x'>` (RPC fire-and-forget), then `getState requestId:p23-vb-long-after` → `inputValue len:2000 all_x:True` ✓ (no truncation marker, no length warning, full 2000-char payload echoed).
- Build/test: `cargo test -p script-kit-gpui --test stdin_setfilter_input_value_verbatim_contract` → 4 passed, 0 failed (17.17s compile + 0.00s run). `cargo check -p script-kit-gpui` → clean. `lat check` → All checks passed (scanned 24 .md, 1662 .rs).
- Refactor threat: a well-meaning contributor adds a "safety" cap (`text.truncate(4096)`, `text.chars().take(N).collect()`, or an equivalent wrapper at `current_input_value`'s ScriptList arm), thinking it defends against large payloads, when the stdin line cap `MAX_STDIN_COMMAND_BYTES` (16 KiB) already bounds the ingest path. The pin's negative assertion on `.truncate(`/`.chars().take(` between signature and store catches this shape-class before merge — source-level grep, no runtime component required.
- Falsifier: any receipt showing `getState.inputValue.length < setFilter.text.length` for a payload ≤ 16 KiB, OR any commit that inserts `.truncate(N)`/`.chars().take(N)` between `set_filter_text_immediate`'s signature and its first `self.filter_text = text.clone();` store — both fail the contract test.
- Close-up (per feedback_afk_close_app_when_done): simulateKey Escape + hide + getState exercised inline at verification; final `getState.windowVisible:false, promptType:"none"` (expected — hidden from prior tick).
- Ledger:
  - PINS post-commit: 5/20 (was 4, +1 for this Pin — still well under 7 cap)
  - YIELD: rolling last-5 post-commit = #19(F) #20(R) #21(F) #22(F) #23(P) = 3 Fix in window — floor 1 easily satisfied
  - Surface breadth: `filter_input_updates.rs::set_filter_text_immediate` + `prompt_handler/mod.rs::current_input_value` added to rolling-10 distinct-surface set. Count ≥6 well-satisfied.

### Pass #22 — 2026-04-19T01:27Z — FIX tool-panel-invariants-cold-start-race

- Subject: Fix
- Verb: cut the cold-start panic path at `src/platform/panel_invariants.rs:326:13` by splitting the `is_key_window` check out of the fail-loud `mismatched` bucket into a new `soft_mismatched` bucket via a new `record_soft` method. Before: the `PostMakeKey` phase read `[window isKeyWindow]` immediately after `[window makeKeyWindow]`, and because AppKit dispatches the key-window promotion asynchronously, cold-start observations returned false → `mismatched.push(...)` → `r.ok() == false` → `panic!` in debug builds. After: the `is_key_window` check goes through `record_soft` which pushes into `soft_mismatched` only; `finish()` still calls a new `log_soft_report` emitting `PANEL_INVARIANTS SOFT` lines but does NOT panic; `ok()` is unchanged (still returns `mismatched.is_empty()`). All 11 other `PostMakeKey` invariants continue to use fail-loud `record` because they read synchronously-configured state (level, style mask, collection behavior, activation policy, animation behavior, restorable, autosave-name, plus the class/thread/window-presence guards) that cannot race.
- Outcome: **fix-committed** (closes `tool-panel-invariants-cold-start-race`)
- Commit: `82e8ebd89`
- Story: `tool-panel-invariants-cold-start-race` — `[ ]` from auto-promotion after Pass #19, flipped `[x]` this pass.
- Surface: `src/platform/panel_invariants.rs` (added `record_soft` method, `soft_mismatched: Vec<Invariant>` field on both macos + non-macos report structs, `log_soft_report` helper, `finish()` soft-routing branch; migrated `is_key_window` call to `record_soft` with a 11-line explanatory comment) + `tests/panel_invariants_soft_is_key_window_contract.rs` (new 7-test file) + `lat.md/windowing.md` §"Main Panel Invariants Contract" (new "Soft invariants" subsection). FRESH surface vs Run 8 last-10: last-10 was `tests/stdin_simulatekey_cmdk_fallback_contract.rs`, simulateKey dispatchers, `src/notes/window/window_ops.rs`, cross-host Cmd+K lifecycle, `src/render_script_list/mod.rs::toggle_actions`, file-search Cmd+K, triggerBuiltin catch-all arms, `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_core.rs::TriggerBuiltin`, `src/ai/acp/handlers.rs::session_notification`, `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs`, `scripts/agentic/session.sh::cmd_send`. `panel_invariants.rs` has not been a surface in Run 7 or Run 8 (last touched as contract surface Run 5-era). Rotation satisfied at distinct-surface count ≥6.
- Why Fix (STEP 4/5): PINS=5/20 (cap ≤7 — Pin still allowed but not this pass). YIELD=1/5 (floor 1 satisfied; Pass #21 counted; bringing YIELD=2/5 after commit). Pass #22 is NOT attacker (attacker cadence at #4/#8/#12/#16/#20/#24). STEP 5 story pick: `tool-panel-invariants-cold-start-race` is the top-down first `[ ]` in open-stories queue (the only other `[ ]` is `tool-builtin-screenshot-trigger` which requires product clarification). The story was previously deferred Pass #21 because "reproducing the race requires cold-restart which would disrupt the running session" — but the FIX itself does not require the race to fire. The source-level correctness is provable: the panic path is `finish()` → `panic!` gated on `!r.ok()`, `r.ok()` returns `mismatched.is_empty()`, and `is_key_window` no longer pushes into `mismatched`. So a structural fix + contract-test pin + lat.md update is fully ship-able without a live-repro cold-restart.
- Implementation (3 edits):
  - `src/platform/panel_invariants.rs`: (a) `PanelInvariantReport` gains `pub soft_mismatched: Vec<Invariant>` field (both `cfg(target_os="macos")` and `cfg(not(target_os="macos"))` stubs), with a 10-line doc comment explaining why — references this pass (Run 8 Pass #22, 2026-04-19) by name and the observed panic-site (panel_invariants.rs:326:13). (b) new `record_soft` method on the macos impl, same signature as `record` but routes to `soft_mismatched`. (c) the `is_key_window` block in `assert_main_panel_invariants` changes `r.record(` to `r.record_soft(`, preceded by an 11-line comment explaining the AppKit-timing rationale and listing which other invariants are NOT soft and why. (d) `finish()` gains a trailing `if !r.soft_mismatched.is_empty() { log_soft_report(context, phase, &r); }` after the existing fail-loud block — soft failures still feed structured logs. (e) new `log_soft_report` helper twin to `log_report` but tags lines with `"SOFT context={}"` and `"SOFT {}"` for log-triage discrimination.
  - `tests/panel_invariants_soft_is_key_window_contract.rs` (new, 154 lines, 7 tests): source-grep-based — `record_soft_method_exists_on_report`, `soft_mismatched_field_exists_on_report`, `log_soft_report_function_exists` (also asserts `"SOFT context={}"` tag), `finish_routes_soft_mismatches_to_log_soft_report` (splits on `fn finish(` and asserts both `log_soft_report(context, phase, &r)` and `!r.soft_mismatched.is_empty()` appear in finish's body), `is_key_window_uses_record_soft_and_only_it` (byte-index: finds the `"is_key_window"` literal, searches backward for nearest `r.record_soft(`, asserts no `r.record(` appears between them), `record_soft_has_exactly_one_call_site` (count of `r.record_soft(` == 1 — any future contributor softening another invariant must update this test), `non_racing_invariants_continue_to_use_record` (for each of the 11 non-racing invariant names, asserts no `r.record_soft(` appears between the nearest preceding `r.record(` and the name literal). 7 tests, 0 failures, 22.18s compile + 0.00s run.
  - `lat.md/windowing.md` §"Main Panel Invariants Contract" gains a new "Soft invariants" subsection (3 paragraphs, all ≤250-char leading-paragraph compliant per lat check). Documents the soft-bucket split, names the 11 fail-loud invariants explicitly, references the new contract test file, and calls out the refactor-threat (revert, or softening another invariant without updating the test's "exactly 1 call site" assertion).
- Live verification NOT performed — scope caveat documented in the commit body and stories.md entry. The race fires only on cold session restart, which would kill the running AFK loop's session (pid 855) and abort the pass. The fix is source-structure correct: `finish()` panics iff `!r.ok()` iff `!mismatched.is_empty()`, and the `is_key_window` check now pushes into `soft_mismatched` exclusively. A future pass with a dedicated cold-restart slot can confirm end-to-end disappearance of the panic; the structural guarantee shipped this pass is independent of that live-proof.
- Build/test: `cargo test -p script-kit-gpui --test panel_invariants_soft_is_key_window_contract` → 7 passed, 0 failed (22.18s compile + 0.00s run). `cargo test -p script-kit-gpui --test panel_invariants_contract` → 6 passed, 0 failed (0.57s compile + 0.00s run — existing contract unaffected). `cargo check -p script-kit-gpui` → clean (12.85s user). `lat check` → All checks passed (first attempt tripped a 966-char leading-paragraph warning on the new "Soft invariants" subsection; broke into 3 paragraphs with ≤250-char lead — re-check passed).
- Surface discipline: the split is surgical. No behavior change for any show path that succeeds the `PostMakeKey` invariant (the fast path). The only observable difference is that a failing `is_key_window` now (a) logs a `PANEL_INVARIANTS SOFT` line instead of a `PANEL_INVARIANTS FAIL` line, and (b) does not panic in debug builds. This matches AppKit's actual semantics and aligns with the existing release-build behavior (release already didn't panic — it logged and continued).
- Refactor threat: a contributor who thinks "the soft-bucket is busywork, just remove the `is_key_window` check entirely" loses the log signal that `makeKeyWindow` truly failed (distinct from the async race). A contributor who "softens all invariants for consistency" defeats the fail-loud guard on the non-racing checks. Both are caught by the contract test's "exactly 1 call site" + "non-racing invariants use record" asserts.
- Falsifier: any of (a) revert `is_key_window` to `record`, (b) remove `record_soft` method, (c) remove `soft_mismatched` field, (d) add `record_soft` to a second call site without updating the contract-test "exactly 1" expectation, (e) drop `log_soft_report` or the `finish()` routing — all flip at least one assert in the new contract test. cargo check would pass in most of these cases but tests would red. This is the structural guarantee.
- Close-up (per feedback_afk_close_app_when_done): simulateKey Escape and hide RPCs timed out — window was already hidden from an earlier RPC step during the pass. Final `getState.windowVisible:false, promptType:"none"` ✓. The three-verb sequence was exercised; timeouts on escape/hide are acceptable no-ops when window is already hidden (not a regression).
- Ledger:
  - PINS unchanged 5/20 (not a Pin pass)
  - YIELD: rolling 5 window was #17-21 = 1 Fix (#21). Post-Pass #22: rolling window #18-22 = 2 Fix (#19, #21 drop into the #18+ window? actually #17 was Verify, #18 Verify, #19 Fix, #20 Reproduce, #21 Fix, #22 Fix). Last-5 post-commit = #18(V) #19(F) #20(R) #21(F) #22(F) = 3 YIELD. Floor 1 satisfied (was 1, now 3).
  - Surface breadth: `src/platform/panel_invariants.rs` added to rolling-10 distinct-surface set. Last-10 distinct surfaces post-Pass-#22: 10+ (no-duplicate-eviction, rotation floor 6 well-satisfied).

### Pass #21 — 2026-04-19T01:17Z — FIX attacker-session-send-argorder-swallow

- Subject: Fix
- Verb: closed the Pass #20 client-side silent-swallow anomaly (commit `d3a15cefc`) by adding a shape-check guard to `cmd_send` in `scripts/agentic/session.sh`. Pre-fix: `send SESSION --await-parse JSON` bound `cmd="--await-parse"` via `local cmd="${2:-}"`, wrote the literal 13-byte string `--await-parse\n` to the input FIFO, and returned `{sent:true}` — the stdin parser logged `stdin_parse_failed line_len=13 error="invalid number at line 1 column 2"` but the caller saw no indication of failure. Post-fix: the same invocation returns `{schemaVersion:1, status:"error", error:{code:"flag_as_command", message:"First non-session arg looks like a flag..."}}` BEFORE the FIFO is ever touched — the stdin parser never sees the mangled payload. Attacker-mode.md §"Commit" mandates that a fix coming out of exploration splits into two commits: the Probe/Reproduce that surfaces it (Pass #20), then the Fix in a subsequent non-attacker pass (this one).
- Outcome: **fix-committed** (closes `attacker-session-send-argorder-swallow`)
- Commit: `ee48d8e3a`
- Story: `attacker-session-send-argorder-swallow` — `[?]` from Pass #20, flipped `[x]` this pass.
- Surface: `scripts/agentic/session.sh::cmd_send` (guard added after `missing_command` check, before FIFO write) + `tests/session_send_await_parse_contract.rs::cmd_send_rejects_flag_as_command` (new test, 4 asserts) + `lat.md/automation.md` §"Session send parse receipts" (new paragraph documenting `flag_as_command` stable-contract code). FRESH surface to Run 8's last-10 (last-10 dominated by `src/main_entry/` stdin dispatchers, `src/app_impl/actions_dialog.rs`, `src/ui_foundation/mod.rs::printable_char`, `src/app_impl/startup_new_actions.rs`, `tests/stdin_simulatekey_actions_dialog_key_char_routing_contract.rs`; `scripts/agentic/session.sh` was last a Run 5 Pass #8 surface — outside last-10 window).
- Why Fix (STEP 4/5): PINS=5/20 (cap ≤7, Pin still allowed but not needed this pass). YIELD=1/5 (floor satisfied by Pass #19's Fix within the last-5 window #17-21; this Fix brings YIELD=2/5 after commit). Pass #21 is NOT attacker cadence (next attacker slot is #24). Story selection per STEP 5 "tool-* drain first" had `tool-panel-invariants-cold-start-race` at the top of the open-`[ ]` queue, but that story requires disrupting the running session (cold-restart to reproduce the `post_make_key` race at `src/platform/panel_invariants.rs:326:13`) and is a DESIGN question (is cold-start `is_key_window=false` a bug or an expected AppKit timing artifact?) — deferred for a dedicated pass. Next eligible slug was `attacker-session-send-argorder-swallow`, actionable (source-level fix), testable (existing contract-test file), high-DX-value (closes client-side analogue of Pass #9's server-side DX hole). Matches attacker-mode.md's "split Reproduce + Fix" pattern.
- Implementation (1 code edit + 1 new test + 1 lat.md paragraph):
  - `scripts/agentic/session.sh` lines 303-318: inserted a 10-line shape-check block AFTER `if [ -z "$cmd" ]; then json_error "missing_command" ... fi` and BEFORE `local sdir`. Body: `if [[ "$cmd" == --* ]]; then json_error "flag_as_command" "First non-session arg looks like a flag (got: '${cmd}'). Usage: ... — flags go AFTER the JSON payload."; return 1; fi`. Comment block credits Pass #20's Reproduce commit (`d3a15cefc`) and names the contract-test anchor.
  - `tests/session_send_await_parse_contract.rs` (new test `cmd_send_rejects_flag_as_command`): 4 asserts — (a) SESSION_SH contains `if [[ "$cmd" == --* ]]; then` guard literal, (b) SESSION_SH contains `json_error "flag_as_command"` envelope literal, (c) ordering: `missing_command` idx < guard idx (empty CMD still errors missing_command, not flag_as_command), (d) ordering: guard idx < FIFO write idx (`printf '%s\n' "$cmd" > "$input_fifo"`) — the guard runs BEFORE stdin is touched. Test suite now 8 tests (up from 7), all green, 16.52s compile + 0.00s run.
  - `lat.md/automation.md` §"Session send parse receipts" (between the `invalid_timeout` paragraph at line 139 and the concurrent-requestId paragraph at line 141): new paragraph documenting `flag_as_command` as stable-contract code alongside `invalid_timeout`. Explains the pre-fix failure mode (13-byte FIFO write, `stdin_parse_failed`, silent `{sent:true}`), the post-fix behavior (error envelope before FIFO touch), the guard's ordering invariants (after `missing_command`, before FIFO write), and the 4-assert contract pin.
- Guard placement rationale: AFTER `missing_command` is essential — empty `$cmd` must continue to error with `missing_command` for backwards compatibility (callers may match `.error.code == "missing_command"`). BEFORE FIFO write is essential — if the guard ran after, the stdin parser would already have emitted a `stdin_parse_failed` event and dirtied the app.log; the whole point is to reject at the sender without involving the app. Both ordering invariants are structural asserts in the new test so a future refactor that inverts them fails loudly.
- Regex vs literal-prefix check: used bash `[[ "$cmd" == --* ]]` glob match (not `=~ ^--`) to avoid pulling in the regex engine and to stay within `set -euo pipefail`'s safe subset. `==` with `--*` is equivalent to "starts with `--`" and handles flags of any length (`--await-parse`, `--timeout`, `--verbose`, etc.) with identical cost. The glob-vs-regex choice is not contract-pinned because both produce the same behavior on any bash 4+ — future refactors are free to switch between them.
- Live verification on session pid 855 (3 cases):
  - (1) Wrong-order (the fixed bug): `bash scripts/agentic/session.sh send default --await-parse '{"type":"getState","requestId":"p21-wrongorder"}'` → `{schemaVersion:1, status:"error", error:{code:"flag_as_command", message:"First non-session arg looks like a flag (got: '--await-parse'). Usage: session.sh send SESSION_NAME JSON_COMMAND [--await-parse [--timeout MS]] — flags go AFTER the JSON payload."}}` ✓. Exit code 1.
  - (2) Correct-order (regression check): `bash scripts/agentic/session.sh send default '{"type":"getState","requestId":"p21-correctorder"}' --await-parse` → `{schemaVersion:1, status:"ok", session:"default", sent:true, parseOutcome:"parsed", commandType:"getState"}` ✓. Proves the fix is surgical and didn't affect the correct arg-order path.
  - (3) Empty CMD (distinct error path): `bash scripts/agentic/session.sh send default ''` → `{schemaVersion:1, status:"error", error:{code:"missing_command", message:"Usage: session.sh send SESSION_NAME JSON_COMMAND [--await-parse [--timeout MS]]"}}` ✓. Proves the guard ordering — empty CMD hits `missing_command` FIRST, not `flag_as_command`.
- Adjacent DX observation: this fix closes the client-side silent-drop gap that's the mirror-image of Pass #9's server-side fix. Pass #9 (`automation_payload_mismatch`) surfaces field-level serde errors when a known automation verb has a bad payload — the DIAGNOSTIC side. Pass #21 prevents the sender from producing a malformed line in the first place when the typo is arg-order — the SHAPE side. Together they cover both halves of the "malformed stdin command" surface: server catches semantic errors (unknown field, wrong type), client catches syntactic errors (flag where JSON expected).
- Refactor threat (would fail the test): a contributor consolidating `missing_command` + `invalid_timeout` + `flag_as_command` into a generic `validate_send_args` helper and inverting the emit order would trip one or more of the 4 asserts. Similarly a "simplification" that renames `flag_as_command` to `bad_args` breaks callers that match on the exact error code.
- Falsifier: any commit that removes the `if [[ "$cmd" == --* ]]` guard from session.sh, OR reorders so the guard runs AFTER the FIFO write, OR renames the error code from `flag_as_command` — all three independently flip at least one of the 4 asserts red.
- Build/test: `cargo test -p script-kit-gpui --test session_send_await_parse_contract` → 8 passed, 0 failed (16.52s compile + 0.00s run). `lat check` → All checks passed (scanned 24 .md, 1660 .rs, 37 .sh, and others).
- Close-up (per feedback_afk_close_app_when_done): 3-verb sequence exercised inline during live verification. Final `getState.windowVisible:false, promptType:"none"`.
- Ledger: PINS=5/20 (unchanged — Fix is not Pin). YIELD=2/5 (this Fix lifts the last-5 window #17-21 from 1 Yield to 2; strengthens the floor). Surface rotation: `scripts/agentic/session.sh::cmd_send` is FRESH (not in last-10). Attacker-mode split: Pass #20 Reproduce → Pass #21 Fix is the canonical pattern per attacker-mode.md §"Commit".

### Pass #20 — 2026-04-19T01:12Z — REPRODUCE attacker session.sh arg-order swallow + simulateKey noop on hidden non-Actions views

- Subject: Reproduce
- Verb: reproduced two distinct anomalies on the stdin + session.sh perimeter during attacker-cadence Pass #20 (every-4th slot per looper/rules/attacker-mode.md). Anomaly A = DX hole: `session.sh send SESSION --await-parse JSON` arg-order typo is silently swallowed — `cmd_send` at `scripts/agentic/session.sh:276-281` binds `cmd="--await-parse"` via the unguarded `${2:-}` bind, writes the bare string `--await-parse\n` to the input FIFO, and returns `{sent:true}` as if the JSON had been delivered. The stdin parser rejects with `line_len=13 error="invalid number at line 1 column 2"` (13 == `len("--await-parse")`); the caller sees no parseOutcome. Anomaly B = keyboard-routing gap: no-modifier printable-char `simulateKey` against a view transitioned via `triggerBuiltin` WITHOUT a following `show` (i.e. `windowVisible:false`) produces `SimulateKey: Unhandled key '<ch>' in <View>` log lines for `ClipboardHistoryView` and `EmojiPicker` arms of both stdin dispatchers; filter `inputValue` stays `""` across 20 rapid-fire keystrokes.
- Outcome: **anomalies-filed** — 2 new `[?]` stories appended to `audits/afk/stories.md`: `attacker-session-send-argorder-swallow` and `attacker-simulatekey-noop-on-hidden-non-actions-views`.
- Commit: `d3a15cefc`
- Story: N/A (attacker cadence — no pre-specified acceptance per looper/rules/attacker-mode.md)
- Surface: session.sh `cmd_send` arg-parse perimeter (`scripts/agentic/session.sh:276-306`) + both stdin `SimulateKey` dispatchers' per-view `Unhandled key` fall-through (`src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_simulate_key.rs` EmojiPicker + ClipboardHistoryView arms, and `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs` parallel arms). Attacker passes bypass surface-rotation per attacker-mode.md §"Picking the target surface" — rotation check N/A.
- Adversarial categories: Boundary, Rapid-fire, Interleaved, Composition, Resurrection (5 of 7 menu categories — exceeds minimum of 3).
- Actions: **36 total** — A1 baseline `getState`; A2 Boundary 10k-char `simulateKey.key`; A3 Boundary empty-string `.key`; A4 Boundary emoji `🎉`; A5 Boundary NUL `\u0000`; A6 Boundary RTL `\u202e\u202d`; A7 Boundary ZWSP `\u200b`; A8 sanity `getState` (session still responsive); A9 Normal `simulateKey x` rpc (confirms parser baseline: `line_len=61 command_type=simulateKey`); A10-A14 Interleaved 5× `triggerBuiltin` ping-pong across emojiPicker → apps → fileSearch → emojiPicker → clipboardHistory (final semantic_surface=clipboardHistory); A15 `getState` on clipboardHistory (`promptType:"clipboardHistory", choiceCount:100, inputValue:""`); A16 Rapid-fire 20× `simulateKey z` (20 sub-actions — each parsed `line_len=62-63 command_type=simulateKey` and each emitted `SimulateKey: Unhandled key 'z' in ClipboardHistoryView`); A17 `getState` after rapid-fire (`inputValue:""` still — filter did not receive keystrokes); A18 Composition `triggerBuiltin emojiPicker` (switch view); A19 `simulateKey h` on EmojiPicker (single keystroke parallel to A16 rapid-fire but different view); A20 Resurrection `getState` post-emoji (`promptType:"emojiPicker", inputValue:"", windowVisible:false`). Rapid-fire sub-count of 20 already meets attacker-mode minimum actions by itself; the other 16 distinct actions cover the 4 remaining categories.
- Why Probe (STEP 4/5): this is attacker-cadence slot #20 (4th/8th/12th/16th/**20th**) per scope §"Attacker-mode cadence". PINS=6/20 (cap ≤7 — Pin still allowed but attacker verb must be Probe/Reproduce). YIELD=1/5 (floor satisfied — Pass #19 Fix sits within last-5 window #15-19). No pre-specified acceptance criterion per attacker-mode.md.
- Anomaly A evidence (arg-order silent swallow):
  - Reproduction: `bash scripts/agentic/session.sh send default --await-parse '{"type":"simulateKey","key":"","requestId":"p20-a03-emptykey"}'` → response envelope `{schemaVersion:1, status:"ok", session:"default", sent:true}` (NO `parseOutcome`, NO `commandType`). App log (`/tmp/sk-agentic-sessions/default/app.log`) at timestamp 35.120: `|w|S|cid=stdin:parse:5de42d03-... Failed to parse external command event_type=stdin_parse_failed line_len=13 payload_summary={len:13} error=invalid number at line 1 column 2`. Six consecutive parse_fail entries (03.756, 35.120, 35.443, 35.766, 36.096, 36.486), all with identical `line_len=13` — corresponding to the 6 `send` invocations I made (A2 and A3-A7).
  - Root cause: `cmd_send` at `scripts/agentic/session.sh:276-281` unconditionally binds `local cmd="${2:-}"` without validating shape. When invoked as `send default --await-parse JSON`, positional `$2` is `"--await-parse"` (13 chars) and the JSON ends up as `$4` which gets consumed by the flag-parse loop's `*) shift ;;` catch-all. The script then writes `--await-parse\n` to the input FIFO.
  - Control case: `bash scripts/agentic/session.sh send default '{"type":"getState","requestId":"p20-verify-correctorder"}' --await-parse` (correct order SESSION CMD --await-parse) → envelope `{schemaVersion:1, status:"ok", session:"default", sent:true, parseOutcome:"parsed", commandType:"getState"}`. Proves the script otherwise works — the bug is ONLY the silent-swallow on arg-order typo.
  - Why filed as `[?]` not fixed this pass: attacker-mode.md §"Commit" explicitly forbids `Prompt: Fix` on attacker passes — if a fix comes out of exploration, it must ship in a separate (next) pass. This Reproduce documents; a future pass can ship a `Prompt: Fix` adding a shape-check like `if [[ "$cmd" =~ ^-- ]]; then json_error "flag_as_command" "First non-session arg looks like a flag — did you mean \`send SESSION JSON --flag\`?"`.
- Anomaly B evidence (simulateKey Unhandled on hidden views):
  - Reproduction A16: 20× `send default '{"type":"simulateKey","key":"z","requestId":"p20-a16-rapid-N"}'` against ClipboardHistoryView (`promptType:"clipboardHistory", windowVisible:false`). App log shows 20 `Parsed external command command_type=simulateKey line_len=62-63` immediately followed by `SimulateKey: Unhandled key 'z' in ClipboardHistoryView`. Final `getState` (A17) shows `inputValue:""` — filter received zero keystrokes across all 20 rapid-fires.
  - Reproduction A19: 1× `simulateKey h` against EmojiPicker (`promptType:"emojiPicker", windowVisible:false`) → `SimulateKey: Unhandled key 'h' in EmojiPicker`. A20 `getState.inputValue:""`.
  - Scope caveat (why `[?]` not `[!]`): `windowVisible:false` in both cases. Keyboard routing MAY be visibility-gated by design — a view transitioned via `triggerBuiltin` without an explicit `show` would not be user-reachable anyway. But the "Unhandled" log line is the dispatcher's own signal that it fell through — not a visibility-gate early-return. A future pass should (1) confirm with `show` first and repeat, and (2) read the EmojiPicker and ClipboardHistoryView arms in both dispatchers to determine whether they have a printable-char branch analogous to the ActionsDialog one Pass #19 wired up. If they don't, the fix shape will likely mirror Pass #19 (derive `key_char`, route via a view-local filter handler).
  - Adjacent to Pass #19: Pass #19 fixed printable-char routing to ActionsDialog's filter (`actions-search` input); this anomaly is about the underlying per-View filter input BEFORE Cmd+K is pressed — a distinct routing path through the outer `match view.current_view` arms of the stdin dispatcher, not the `route_key_to_actions_dialog` shared router.
- Falsifier (for Reproduce per attacker-mode.md §"Commit"): Anomaly A = any future `session.sh send SESSION --await-parse JSON` invocation (wrong order) that returns `parseOutcome:"parsed", commandType:"<verb>"` would prove the swallow has been fixed. Anomaly B = any future `getState.inputValue:"zzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzzz"` (or `"z"`) after 20× `simulateKey z` on ClipboardHistoryView with `windowVisible:true` would prove the routing gap is closed; alternatively a source-level contract that the `SimulateKey: Unhandled key '<ch>' in ClipboardHistoryView` log line is ONLY emitted behind `if !windowVisible` would prove the anomaly is a visibility-gate intentional no-op rather than a bug.
- Panel_invariants cold-start observation: this pass did NOT invoke `{"type":"show"}` explicitly — relied on the session's existing state from Pass #19's close-up (`windowVisible:false`). Consequently `triggerBuiltin` transitions updated `promptType` but did not promote `windowVisible` to `true`. This is consistent with the `tool-panel-invariants-cold-start-race` story auto-promoted into stories.md earlier this tick — the attacker pass inadvertently exercised the "transition-without-show" code path and revealed Anomaly B as a side-effect.
- Close-up (per feedback_afk_close_app_when_done): `escape` → `hide` → `getState` executed post-anomaly-capture. Final state: `promptType:"none", inputValue:"", windowVisible:false, isFocused:false`.
- Build/test: no code changes this pass — compile is a no-op verify. Attacker-mode passes are evidence-gathering only.
- Refactor threat: N/A (no Pin).
- Ledger: PINS=6/20 (unchanged — Reproduce does NOT count toward Pin cap). YIELD=1/5 (unchanged — Reproduce is NOT a Fix/Add/Extend). Attacker-mode pass composition check: 5 categories × 36 actions × 2 anomalies filed → meets minimum composition per attacker-mode.md §"Minimum composition". Escalation counter: 0 consecutive attacker passes with zero `[?]` filings (this pass DID file 2 `[?]`), so no escalation required for the next attacker slot (Pass #24).

### Pass #19 — 2026-04-19T00:59Z — FIX tool-simulatekey-actions-dialog-filter-routing

- Subject: Fix
- Verb: routed stdin-driven `simulateKey` no-modifier keystrokes into the open ActionsDialog's filter input across all Cmd+K hosts. Pass #18 (2026-04-19T00:47Z) observed and logged the gap: `simulateKey a` with ActionsDialog open on ScriptList left `input:actions-search.value` empty because both stdin dispatchers passed `None` for `key_char: Option<&str>` to `route_key_to_actions_dialog`. The shared router's printable-char branch at `src/app_impl/actions_dialog.rs:479-486` gates on `printable_char(key_char)`, and `printable_char(None) -> None` (see `src/ui_foundation/mod.rs:698`), so `handle_char` never fired. Live GPUI passes `event.keystroke.key_char.as_deref()` per `src/app_impl/startup_new_actions.rs:35` — this pass mirrors that semantic on the stdin side.
- Outcome: **fix-committed** (closes `tool-simulatekey-actions-dialog-filter-routing`, auto-promoted from Pass #18's adjacent observation)
- Commit: `c31c88df6`
- Story: `tool-simulatekey-actions-dialog-filter-routing` (auto-promoted by `audits/afk/promote-tool-gaps.sh` pre-STEP-4 this tick — the promotion commit was `23640a7e8`)
- Surface: `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_simulate_key.rs::ExternalCommand::SimulateKey` + `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs::ExternalCommand::SimulateKey` + new `tests/stdin_simulatekey_actions_dialog_key_char_routing_contract.rs` (3 tests). FRESH surface to Run 8's log.md last-10 (last-10 dominated by `tests/actions_dialog_escape_filter_agnostic_contract.rs` (Pass #18), `tests/stdin_simulatekey_cmdk_fallback_contract.rs` (Pass #4/#5), `simulateKey` stdin dispatcher arms (Pass #2/#4/#5), `src/notes/window/window_ops.rs` (Pass #13), Cmd+K lifecycle surfaces, `TriggerBuiltin` catch-alls). The `route_key_to_actions_dialog` call shape specifically — not previously a log.md surface entry.
- Why Fix (STEP 4/5): PINS=6/20 (cap ≤7, one below cap — Pin is ALLOWED but tight); YIELD=1/5 (Pass #17 Add in the last-5 window covering #15-18-19; after this Fix, YIELD=2/5 strengthens the floor). Pass #19 is position 19 — NOT attacker (Pass #20 is). Story selection per STEP 5 "tool-* slugs drain first": `tool-simulatekey-actions-dialog-filter-routing` is the only unblocked actionable `[ ]` story (the two other `[ ]`/`[?]` candidates — `tool-builtin-screenshot-trigger`, `stdin-setfilter-inputvalue-unbounded` — remain blocked on product/design input). Matches user's standing directive `feedback_afk_focus_cmdk_actions_menu` (ActionsDialog priority across main/notes/ACP/built-ins).
- Implementation (2 edits + 1 new contract test):
  - `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_simulate_key.rs` lines 11-22: Added `let key_char: Option<&str> = if key.chars().count() == 1 { Some(key.as_str()) } else { None };` right after the existing `let key_lower = key.to_lowercase();`. Then replaced `None` at line ~38 (the 2nd argument to `view.route_key_to_actions_dialog`) with `key_char`.
  - `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs` lines 1854-1865: identical `key_char` derivation + `None` → `key_char` substitution at the parallel call site (line ~1882).
  - `tests/stdin_simulatekey_actions_dialog_key_char_routing_contract.rs` (new, 3 tests): source-grep contract asserting (a) each dispatcher's `route_key_to_actions_dialog` call passes `key_char,` as 2nd arg NOT `None,`, (b) each file contains the canonical `let key_char: Option<&str> = if key.chars().count() == 1 { ... }` derivation block, (c) the derivation block is IDENTICAL (byte-for-byte) across both files so drift can't silently break one path.
- Single-char guard rationale: a naïve `Some(key.as_str())` unconditionally would be wrong for multi-char keys like `"Escape"`, `"ArrowUp"`, `"Enter"`. All of those are handled by earlier branches (`is_key_escape`, `is_key_up`, `is_key_enter`) BEFORE the printable-char branch — BUT if anyone reorders the branches or adds a new printable-like handler upstream, `printable_char(Some("Escape"))` would slice off 'E' and feed it into the filter via `.chars().next()`. The `chars().count() == 1` guard makes the contract bulletproof against that reordering class. Uses `chars().count()` (not `len()`) to handle multi-byte UTF-8 correctly (e.g. an emoji keystroke).
- Build: `cargo check` clean (15.47s). `cargo build` clean (20.73s; fresh binary at `target/debug/script-kit-gpui` mtime 1776560176 post-edit). `cargo test --test stdin_simulatekey_actions_dialog_key_char_routing_contract` → 3 passed, 0 failed, 17.57s compile + 0.00s run.
- Live verification (post-session-restart to pid 855; required a cold start after stopping pid 30450 from Pass #18 — first-show `panel_invariants` race observed twice on restart, mitigated by skipping explicit `show` and letting `triggerBuiltin` promote the window to focused state):
  - **Host 1 — EmojiPicker** — `triggerBuiltin emojiPicker` (builtinId:"emojiPicker", NOT "builtin":"emojiPicker" — the new Pass #9 preemptive `automation_payload_mismatch` detection correctly surfaced this typo on first attempt as parse_error: `automation_payload_mismatch: "triggerBuiltin" is a known automation verb but the payload did not validate: unknown field \`builtin\`, expected one of \`builtinId\`, \`name\`, \`requestId\`` — Pass #9's fix ships real DX wins). `simulateKey k cmd` → `listAutomationWindows` → 2 windows (main + actions-dialog, parentWindowId:"main"). Baseline `getElements kind:actionsDialog` → `{input:actions-search value:"", focused:true}`, `{list:actions text:"6 actions"}`, 6 choice elements (Run, Show Info, Add Shortcut, Add Alias, Copy Deep Link, Delete Ranking Entry). `simulateKey r` (no modifier) → `getElements` → `{input:actions-search value:"r", focused:true}`, `{list:actions text:"5 actions"}`, 5 choice elements (Show Info filtered out — no 'r'). **BEFORE-FIX PREDICTION: value would stay `""`, list would stay `"6 actions"`. Actual: value="r", list="5 actions" ✓ fix confirmed.**
  - **Host 2 — ScriptList** — popped EmojiPicker via `simulateKey Escape`; `getState.promptType:"none", choiceCount:476` confirms ScriptList. `simulateKey k cmd` → `getElements kind:actionsDialog` → baseline 6 actions with input value:"". `simulateKey z` (no modifier, non-matching char) → `getElements` → `{input:actions-search value:"z", focused:true}`, `{list:actions text:"0 actions"}`, zero choice elements (totalCount:2 — only the input + list wrapper). **Fix confirmed on ScriptList too; empty-match case also proves the routing, independently of whether any choice matched the typed char.**
  - **Close-up** — `simulateKey Escape` + `{"type":"hide"}` + `getState` → `windowVisible:false, isFocused:false, promptType:"none"` ✓ matches `feedback_afk_close_app_when_done`.
- Observed adjacent issue (logged, NOT blocking this Fix's acceptance): on cold session restart, `{"type":"show"}` triggered `panel_invariants` panics twice in a row (`src/platform/panel_invariants.rs:326:13`, `post_make_key` mismatch `is_key_window expected=true actual=false`). The third restart succeeded WITHOUT an explicit `show` — `triggerBuiltin` promoted the window to focused state via a different code path. This is a pre-existing flaky-first-show race specific to cold starts and unrelated to this pass's surface. Potential future story: `tool-panel-invariants-cold-start-race`.
- Refactor threat (what the contract test defends against): a contributor "simplifying" the stdin dispatchers might (a) drop the `key_char` local as "unused" (Rust warns but doesn't error), (b) rewrite the derivation as `Some(key.as_str())` unconditionally (breaks multi-char keys silently via the `printable_char` reorder hazard above), (c) use `key.len() == 1` (wrong for multi-byte UTF-8 — 2-byte chars would fall through to None and lose filter routing). Each of those is caught by at least one of the 3 contract tests.
- Falsifier: any live receipt where `getElements kind:actionsDialog` shows `input:actions-search value:""` AFTER a no-modifier `simulateKey <single-printable-char>` on an open ActionsDialog. Defeated on both hosts this pass (Host 1: value="r", Host 2: value="z").
- Close-up: session left at `windowVisible:false, promptType:"none"` per memory directive. Session pid 855 (new cold-start post pid 30450) continues.
- Subject-verb discipline ledger for Run 8 (post-Pass #19):
  - YIELD (Fix/Add/Extend): **7** — Pass #1 Fix, Pass #2 Fix, Pass #4 Extend, Pass #6 Extend, Pass #9 Fix, Pass #12 Fix, Pass #17 Add, **Pass #19 Fix** (+1 — floor strengthens)
  - Pin/Verify: **7** — Pass #3 Verify, Pass #5 Pin, Pass #10 Pin, Pass #11 Verify, Pass #13 Pin, Pass #14 Pin, Pass #15 Pin, Pass #16 Pin, Pass #18 Verify (unchanged this pass — Fix is YIELD not Pin/Verify)
  - Skip/defer: 2 (Pass #7, Pass #14 dirty-tree abort)
  - Reproduce (attacker): 1 (Pass #8)
  - Last-5 window (Passes #15-19): 2 YIELD (Pass #17 Add + Pass #19 Fix) ≥ 1 floor ✓ — floor strengthens from 1 to 2
  - Pin cap: **6/20 unchanged** (Fix is not Pin). Still one below the 7-cap.

  - **Host 1 — ScriptList Sequence A (empty filter)** — `simulateKey k cmd (p18-cmdk)` → `parseOutcome:"parsed"`. `listAutomationWindows (p18-list-open)` → 2 windows — main unchanged + `{id:"actions-dialog", kind:"actionsDialog", title:"Actions", focused:false, visible:true, semanticSurface:"actionsDialog", parentWindowId:"main", parentKind:"main", bounds:{x:586,y:355,width:320,height:298}}`. `simulateKey Escape (p18-esc-empty)` → `parseOutcome:"parsed"`. `listAutomationWindows (p18-list-escape-empty)` → 1 window (actions-dialog REMOVED). **One-keystroke dismissal confirmed on empty filter.**

  - **Host 2 — EmojiPicker** — `triggerBuiltin emojiPicker (p18-trigger-emoji)` → `parseOutcome:"parsed"`. `getState (p18-emoji-state)` → `promptType:"emojiPicker", promptId:"emoji-picker", choiceCount:296, visibleChoiceCount:296, isFocused:true, windowVisible:true`. `simulateKey k cmd (p18-emoji-cmdk)` → `parseOutcome:"parsed"`. `listAutomationWindows (p18-emoji-list-open)` → 2 windows — `{id:"main", semanticSurface:"emojiPicker", bounds:{x:371,y:311,width:750,height:500}}` + `{id:"actions-dialog", kind:"actionsDialog", parentWindowId:"main", parentKind:"main", bounds:{x:586,y:355,width:320,height:298}}`. `simulateKey Escape (p18-emoji-esc)` → `parseOutcome:"parsed"`. `listAutomationWindows (p18-emoji-list-esc)` → 1 window (actions-dialog REMOVED; main `semanticSurface:"emojiPicker"` preserved per Pass #21 Run 7 / Run 8 Pass #21 — no regression of the surface-flip invariant). **One-keystroke dismissal confirmed on EmojiPicker host.**

Pass #4 Run 8 shipped a generic Cmd+K fallback for outer-arm views without per-view arms, but the ScriptList ARM itself (which IS present) only handles Cmd+K to toggle — bare alphanumeric keystrokes on an open ActionsDialog don't propagate to `actions_dialog.set_search_text(...)`. ### Pass #17 — 2026-04-19T00:36Z — ADD tool-rpc-preempt-parse-failure-await

- Subject: Add
- Verb: shipped preemptive `stdin_parse_failed` detection inside `scripts/agentic/await-response.ts`'s existing poll loop, short-circuiting malformed-payload RPC round-trips from the full `--timeout` budget (3000-5000ms) to ~38ms via a single-iteration fast-fail path
- Outcome: **add-committed** (`[ ]` → `[x]`)
- Commit: `9fdfa7f95`
- Story: `tool-rpc-preempt-parse-failure-await`
- Surface: `scripts/agentic/await-response.ts` (new primary surface, FRESH to Run 8 last-10) + `tests/agentic_await_response_preempt_parse_failure_contract.rs` (new contract file) + `lat.md/automation.md` (doc update) + `audits/afk/stories.md` (flip) + `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry).
- Why Add (YIELD floor): last-5 window before this pass (Passes #12, #13, #14, #15, #16) had Pass #12 Fix as the single YIELD; Pass #13, #15, #16 were Pins and #14 was a dirty-tree abort skip. YIELD floor at 0/5 risked dropping below the 1-per-5 minimum on the next Pin. The `tool-rpc-preempt-parse-failure-await` story was the top-down `[ ]` tool-slug candidate and auto-promoted from Pass #15's log prose, so it satisfies STEP 4 YIELD floor AND STEP 5 top-down priority simultaneously.
- Implementation (4 anchored additions in `await-response.ts`, ~70 new LOC):
  1. Exit-code docstring: added `3 = parse error detected preemptively (stdin_parse_failed for this requestId)` to the file header.
  2. Top-level constants (after `POLL_INTERVAL`): `const PARSE_ERROR_EXIT_CODE = 3;`, `const REQUEST_ID_CHARSET = /^[A-Za-z0-9_.:/-]+$/;`, `const ERROR_MSG_MAX_CHARS = 200;` — charset mirrors Rust `extract_request_id_lenient` AND shell `cmd_rpc` line ~598; length mirrors shell `cut -c1-200`.
  3. New function `scanForParseFailure(logPath, requestId, startOffset): [string | null, number]` — reads `log_path` tail from `startOffset`, splits on newlines, filters on `cidMarker = \`cid=stdin:req:${requestId} \`` (trailing space per session.sh cmd_rpc line ~599 — prevents `p17-get` matching `p17-get-foo`), requires `line.includes("event_type=stdin_parse_failed")`, extracts via `line.indexOf(" error=")` + substring, strips ` at line N column M` via `/ at line \d+ column \d+.*$/` regex replace, truncates at `ERROR_MSG_MAX_CHARS`. Returns `[errMsg, newOffset]`.
  4. Poll-loop integration: added `const charsetSafeRequestId = REQUEST_ID_CHARSET.test(requestId);` + `let parseFailScanOffset = startOffset;` before `while (Date.now() < deadline) {`. Inside the loop, preempt block runs BEFORE `scanLog(...)`: `if (charsetSafeRequestId)` → call `scanForParseFailure(logPath, requestId, parseFailScanOffset)` → if errMsg → `printResult(errorResult(sessionName, requestId, "parse_error", errMsg)); process.exit(PARSE_ERROR_EXIT_CODE);`. Order matters — preempt-first means the fast-fail path doesn't waste a POLL_INTERVAL (~50ms) on a typed-scan that will never find anything.
- Contract tests (new file `tests/agentic_await_response_preempt_parse_failure_contract.rs`, 7 source-grep tests via `include_str!("../scripts/agentic/await-response.ts")`):
  1. `await_response_declares_parse_error_exit_code_three` — pins the named constant + `process.exit(PARSE_ERROR_EXIT_CODE)` call (not a hardcoded `process.exit(3)` that diverges from the constant).
  2. `await_response_charset_gates_preemptive_scan_like_session_sh` — pins `REQUEST_ID_CHARSET` literal AND `.test(requestId)` call.
  3. `await_response_scoped_cid_marker_has_trailing_space` — pins `const cidMarker = \`cid=stdin:req:${requestId} \`;` with trailing space.
  4. `await_response_matches_stdin_parse_failed_event_type_literal` — pins `"event_type=stdin_parse_failed"` string literal (loose `Failed to parse` would cross-correlate).
  5. `await_response_truncates_error_message_to_bounded_length` — pins `ERROR_MSG_MAX_CHARS = 200`, `errMsg.length > ERROR_MSG_MAX_CHARS` check, and the ` at line \d+ column \d+.*$` regex literal.
  6. `await_response_preempt_scan_runs_inside_poll_loop_before_typed_scan` — brace-counts from `while (Date.now() < deadline) {` and asserts the preempt block's offset < the `scanLog(...)` call's offset. Catches a reordering that would waste POLL_INTERVAL of fast-fail latency.
  7. `await_response_preempt_emits_parse_error_code_not_timeout` — pins the `errorResult(... "parse_error", ...)` emission (rejects `"parseError"` camelCase AND `"timeout"` generic fallback).
  All 7 green — `timeout 180 cargo test -p script-kit-gpui --test agentic_await_response_preempt_parse_failure_contract` → 7 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored.
- Live receipts (3, this pass, against session pid 30450):
  1. **A1 — charset-safe malformed** — `bash scripts/agentic/session.sh rpc default '{"type":"setFilter","value":"foo","requestId":"p17-preempt-v1"}' --expect setFilterResult --timeout 5000` → `{"schemaVersion":1,"status":"error","session":"default","requestId":"p17-preempt-v1","error":{"code":"parse_error","message":"automation_payload_mismatch: \"setFilter\" is a known automation verb but the payload did not validate: unknown field \`value\`, expected \`text\` or \`requestId\`"}}` in **38ms wall-clock**. ~130× speedup vs pre-fix 5000ms full-timeout baseline. Error text is the full Pass #9 `automation_payload_mismatch:` prefix — symmetric with shell post-hoc path.
  2. **A2 — positive control** — `rpc default '{"type":"getState","requestId":"p17-positive-v1"}' --expect stateResult --timeout 5000` → full stateResult envelope in **32ms**. Preempt correctly returned null (no parse failure), typed-scan picked up the `stateResult` on the next poll iteration. Demonstrates the preempt block is transparent on happy paths.
  3. **A3 — charset-unsafe fallback** — `rpc default '{"type":"bogusVerbA3","requestId":"p17-charset+unsafe"}' --expect setFilterResult --timeout 5000` → `{"code":"parse_error","message":"unknown variant \`bogusVerbA3\`..."}` in **3092ms**. Preempt correctly skipped because `+` fails `REQUEST_ID_CHARSET.test(...)`; shell post-hoc unscoped-grep fallback (Pass #12's charset gate on cmd_rpc line ~598) caught the parse failure. Confirms the two-tier detection strategy is intact: charset-safe ids → fast-fail ~40ms via preempt, charset-unsafe → fallback via shell post-hoc (~3s, bounded by shell `tail_bytes` window).
- lat.md update: `lat.md/automation.md` §"Session send parse receipts" — replaced the "Caveat: … future extension" trailing sentence with a new paragraph documenting the preemptive path, the charset mirror across the three implementations (Rust/shell/TS), exit code 3 as stable contract, and the 7-test pin anchor. `lat check` → clean (0 errors).
- Rotation: `scripts/agentic/await-response.ts` is FRESH — not in the last-10 surface list (which is dominated by `src/stdin_commands/mod.rs`, `src/protocol/message/variants/system_control.rs`, `src/actions/window.rs`, `src/main_entry/*`, and `tests/stdin_parse_*`). The new contract test file is also fresh.
- Build: `timeout 30 cargo check -p script-kit-gpui` → clean (no Rust source changes — only new contract test file + TS file). `timeout 180 cargo test -p script-kit-gpui --test agentic_await_response_preempt_parse_failure_contract` → 7/7 green (38.4s compile + 0.00s run).
- Falsifier: any commit that (a) drops `const PARSE_ERROR_EXIT_CODE = 3;`, (b) drops or negates the `if (charsetSafeRequestId)` guard (e.g. unscoping the preempt scan — would cross-correlate with concurrent parse failures on other in-flight sends), (c) reorders the preempt block to run AFTER `scanLog(...)` within the poll iteration (would re-introduce up to POLL_INTERVAL of fast-fail latency), or (d) changes `errorResult(... "parse_error", ...)` to `"parseError"` camelCase or `"timeout"` generic would flip at least one of the 7 contract tests AND regress the A1 receipt's 38ms fast-fail back toward 5000ms.
- Close-up: session pid 30450 left running with `windowVisible:false` (escape → hide → getState sequence at end of pass per `feedback_afk_close_app_when_done`).
- Subject-verb discipline ledger for Run 8 (post-Pass #17):
  - YIELD (Fix/Add/Extend): **6** — Pass #1 Fix, Pass #2 Fix, Pass #4 Extend, Pass #6 Extend, Pass #9 Fix, Pass #12 Fix, **Pass #17 Add** (+1)
  - Pin/Verify: **5** — Pass #3 Verify, Pass #5 Pin, Pass #10 Pin, Pass #11 Verify, Pass #13 Pin, Pass #15 Pin, Pass #16 Pin (note: Passes #13/#15/#16 committed to body without ledger update; divergence flagged but not this pass's scope to reconcile — pin count reflects body-committed pins per commit log)
  - Skip/defer: 2 (Pass #7, Pass #14 dirty-tree abort)
  - Reproduce (attacker): 1 (Pass #8)
  - Last-5 window (Passes #13,14,15,16,17): 1 YIELD (Pass #17 Add) ≥ 1 floor ✓ — floor restored. Pass #14 abort counts as a skip, not a pin.
  - Pin cap: ~5/20 (divergence-adjusted), well under 7/20.
  - Surface rotation: `scripts/agentic/await-response.ts` fresh to Run 8 last-10 ✓ — satisfies ≥6 distinct surfaces in last-10.
  - Attacker cadence: Pass #17 is position 17, NOT a 4/8/12/16/20 slot. Next attacker-cadence is Pass #20.

**User story:** `tool-rpc-preempt-parse-failure-await` — see stories.md for full entry (flipped `[ ]` → `[x]` in this pass's commit).
**Pass:** #17 of AFK audit Run 8 2026-04-18T22:24:12Z
**Proof:** three live `session.sh rpc` receipts — A1 charset-safe malformed setFilter → `parse_error` in **38ms** (vs pre-fix 5000ms full timeout, ~130× speedup); A2 positive control getState → ok in 32ms; A3 charset-unsafe payload → `parse_error` in 3092ms via shell post-hoc fallback (preempt correctly skipped). 7/7 contract tests green.
**Falsifier:** the 38ms fast-fail regressing to ≥1000ms for charset-safe malformed payloads, OR any of the 7 contract assertions failing (exit-code constant, charset gate, scoped cid marker with trailing space, event_type literal, error-length cap + suffix strip, preempt-before-typed-scan ordering, `parse_error` code emission) would falsify this Add pass.

### Pass #10 — 2026-04-19T01:50Z — PIN stdin-parse-dispatch-structural-pin-against-if-let-ok-collapse

- Subject: Pin
- Verb: pinned Pass #9's dispatch-restructure (`match from_value::<ExternalCommand>(...) { Ok(cmd) => return ..., Err(err) => err }` + `EXTERNAL_COMMAND_VERBS.contains(t)` short-circuit emitting `automation_payload_mismatch:` prefix) against a contributor "simplifying" back to `if let Ok(command) = from_value(...) { return ... }` via a source-level structural contract test
- Outcome: **pin-committed** (new story `[x]` in stories.md)
- Commit: `79ba7346e`
- Story: `stdin-parse-dispatch-structural-pin-against-if-let-ok-collapse` (new, Pass #10 added + flipped `[x]` in same commit — rationale: pin pass, no open window)
- Surface: `tests/stdin_parse_automation_payload_mismatch_contract.rs` (new file, 127 LOC) — distinct from Pass #9's in-module unit tests (`src/stdin_commands/mod.rs::tests`, behavioural) AND distinct from the pre-existing `tests/stdin_parse_error_recovery_contract.rs` (which pins the outer listener loop's `Err(e) => { log; continue }` resilience, not the parser function's dispatch structure). FRESH surface to Run 8 — not in last-10.
- Why a structural pin on top of the Pass #9 unit tests: the 3 behavioural tests assert on the error *text* (`automation_payload_mismatch`, `unknown variant`, presence of verb name). A refactor that "cleans up" the dispatch to `if let Ok(command) = from_value(...) { return ... }` and manually re-invokes `from_value` inside the else-branch to synthesize an equivalent error string would pass the behavioural assertions while diverging from the committed structural contract. The grep-based test closes that hole: it asserts the *shape* that Pass #9 committed, so a future collapse refactor fails on the static anchors regardless of whether the resulting runtime behaviour matches.
- Test shape: `parse_stdin_command_body()` helper brace-counts from `fn parse_stdin_command(` to its matching `}` (same pattern as `tests/stdin_parse_error_recovery_contract.rs::start_stdin_listener_body`). Three assertions over that body slice:
  1. `parse_stdin_command_captures_ext_command_error_with_named_binding` — body must contain `let ext_err = match serde_json::from_value::<ExternalCommand>(`.
  2. `parse_stdin_command_err_arm_yields_error_as_value` — body must contain `Err(err) => err,`.
  3. `parse_stdin_command_short_circuits_known_verbs_with_automation_payload_mismatch` — body must contain both `EXTERNAL_COMMAND_VERBS.contains(` AND `automation_payload_mismatch:`.
  Each assertion carries an actionable failure message citing Pass #8/#9 context and pointing at `audits/afk/log.md` + commit `806db738e` for the repair rationale.
- Build + test: `timeout 180 cargo test -p script-kit-gpui --test stdin_parse_automation_payload_mismatch_contract` → 40.05s compile + `3 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored` in 0.00s runtime.
- Refactor threat (specific contributor action): a contributor who notices the `match … Ok(cmd) => return Ok(Foo(cmd)); Err(err) => err` is "just" `if let Ok(cmd) = … { return Ok(Foo(cmd)); }` might collapse it for ergonomics (especially under a clippy-inspired `collapsible_match` nudge if the lint ever fires on this shape), dropping the `ext_err` binding along with the `EXTERNAL_COMMAND_VERBS` short-circuit block that depends on it. That refactor compiles cleanly, does not fail any unit test at the enum/variant level, and silently reintroduces the Pass #8 bug (known automation verb + wrong payload surfaces the SDK vocabulary). The three source-grep assertions catch the structural collapse before it ships.
- Falsifier: any commit that removes `let ext_err = match`, replaces `Err(err) => err,` with `Err(e) => return Err(e.into())` / `Err(_) => unreachable!()` / a bare `?`, or drops either `EXTERNAL_COMMAND_VERBS.contains(` or the `automation_payload_mismatch:` literal from `parse_stdin_command`'s body fails one or more of the 3 structural asserts with the actionable diagnostic message.
- lat.md update: none required. Pass #9 already wired `[[src/stdin_commands/mod.rs#parse_stdin_command]]` + `[[src/stdin_commands/mod.rs#EXTERNAL_COMMAND_VERBS]]` links into `lat.md/automation.md` §"Session send parse receipts"; this contract test references those same symbols, and contract-test files live outside the `lat.md/` knowledge graph. `lat check` run post-edit for hygiene — clean.
- Close-up: default session was left running by Pass #9 (pid 30450) and is not needed for this test-only pass; no UI interaction occurred. Not re-hiding.
- Subject-verb discipline ledger for Run 8 (post-Pass #10):
  - YIELD (Fix/Add/Extend): **5** — Pass #1 Fix, Pass #2 Fix, Pass #4 Extend, Pass #6 Extend, Pass #9 Fix
  - Pin/Verify: **3** — Pass #3 Verify, Pass #5 Pin, Pass #10 Pin
  - Skip/defer: 1 (Pass #7)
  - Reproduce (attacker): 1 (Pass #8)
  - Last-5 window (Passes #6,7,8,9,10): 2 YIELD (Pass #6 Extend, Pass #9 Fix) ≥ 1 floor ✓
  - Pin cap: 2/20 → **3/20** after this commit; under 7/20 cap with 4 slots headroom.
  - Surface rotation: new surface — `tests/stdin_parse_automation_payload_mismatch_contract.rs` not in last-10 (rotation trivially satisfied for net-new file).
  - Attacker cadence: Pass #10 is position 10, not a 4/8/12/… slot. Non-attacker pass is correct.
