## Pass 6 — 2026-04-18T15:13:00Z (Run 7) — `tool-system-control-verbs-unwired` ([x] all 6 verbs wired — setSelectedText)

- **Surface**: stdin protocol dispatcher (`src/prompt_handler/mod.rs::handle_stdin_protocol_message`) — closes the Run 6 Pass #1 / Run 7 Pass #1/#2/#3/#5 wiring chain. Surface rotation relaxed per scope §"Surface breadth floor" (still < 6 distinct surfaces across open stories).
- **Story**: `tool-system-control-verbs-unwired` — **final verb**. Acceptance (c) "source-audit pins the wire-up so a future refactor cannot silently drop it."
- **Outcome**: [x] wire-fix landed for `setSelectedText`. All 6 originally-unwired verbs are now live (`checkAccessibility` Run 6 Pass #1 + `getWindowBounds` Run 7 Pass #1 + `frontmostWindow` Run 7 Pass #2 + `getSelectedText` Run 7 Pass #3 + `requestAccessibility` Run 7 Pass #5 + `setSelectedText` this pass). Story slug flipped to `[x]`.
- **Commit**: `b33b2d759`
- **Why `setSelectedText` was held for a dedicated pass (not bulk-extended alongside its sibling read verbs)**: it is the only *write-type* verb in the `system_control` family — it injects text into the frontmost app via clipboard-swap + `enigo` Cmd+V (see `src/selected_text.rs:10` module doc and the impl at `src/selected_text.rs:191-254`). A silent drop on this verb is especially bad: callers believe text was pasted but nothing happens. Two failure-mode axes motivate the care: (a) the probe can fail with a typed `anyhow::Error` (e.g., `"Accessibility permission required"` at `selected_text.rs:193`) that MUST be surfaced as `TextSet { success: false, error: Some(...) }` rather than silently treated as success, (b) the `text` parameter is user-supplied content from the SDK caller (which may be passwords out of a password manager) so it MUST NOT appear in tracing logs — the privacy pattern Pass #3 established for `getSelectedText` (`text_len` instead of `text`) MUST be preserved on the write side too.
- **Adjacent-layer check**: searched for other `SetSelectedText` handlers — `src/executor/selected_text.rs::handle_set_selected_text` (line 130) exists for the executor dispatcher path and returns `Message::Submit { id, value }` for SDK pending-map matching. This pass deliberately creates a parallel arm in `handle_stdin_protocol_message` rather than routing through the executor dispatcher because the two paths have divergent lifecycle: the executor path is script-driven (the prefixed `ERROR:` convention for Submit.value signals failure to the SDK), while the stdin-RPC path is session-driven and emits a typed `TextSet { success, error, requestId }` response the RPC client can await with `--expect textSet`. A unified helper would couple them. If the probe-to-response mapping needs to change, both sites must be updated together; the source-audit pin `set_selected_text_arm_sends_text_set_response` flags drift on the dispatcher side.
- **Response-shape choice (`TextSet` vs. `Submit`)**: `Message::text_set_success(request_id)` / `Message::text_set_error(e.to_string(), request_id)` construct the `TextSet { success, error, requestId }` variant at `src/protocol/message/constructors/general.rs:413-428`. This is distinct from the executor's `Submit { id, value }` shape because RPC callers do `session.sh rpc setSelectedText --expect textSet` — they want a typed response with an explicit `success: bool` + optional `error: String`, not a string-sniffed `ERROR:` prefix on a `Submit.value`. The two shapes coexist intentionally: SDK scripts use `Submit` for pending-map correlation, RPC clients use `TextSet` for typed dispatch.
- **Off-main-thread safety**: `selected_text::set_selected_text(&text)` is already called from async contexts — see `src/ai/tab_context.rs:4737` (`selected_text::set_selected_text(&text_for_apply)` on the paste path for AI-applied edits). That call site runs inside a background executor task spawned by the ACP turn handler; the probe's internals (`arboard::Clipboard` + `enigo` synthetic key events) are thread-safe on macOS. Proof-by-existing-usage: no new thread-safety invariant introduced by this pass.
- **Privacy pin (new vs. sibling verbs)**: the source-audit file enforces `text_len` and NEGATIVELY asserts `!arm_body.contains("text = %text")` — the first write-type verb to carry a privacy invariant on the *argument* rather than the *result* (Pass #3's `getSelectedText` had the same invariant on the returned text; this pass extends it to the incoming `text` parameter). A future refactor that "helpfully" adds `text = %text` to the event for easier debugging would break this pin.
- **Proof**:
  - `cargo test --test source_audits stdin_set_selected_text` → 4 passed; 0 failed. Pins dispatcher arm, probe source (`crate::selected_text::set_selected_text(&text)`), both response constructors (`text_set_success` + `text_set_error`) + try_send, tracing event fields (`event_type="set_selected_text_result"`, `request_id=%request_id`, `text_len`, `success`), and privacy negative-assertion.
  - `cargo check --tests` → Finished in 33.26s. No new errors (2 pre-existing `dead_code` warnings in `tests/plugin_runtime_ownership.rs` unrelated).
  - Live probe against the running session `default` (pid 83033, pre-wire binary): deferred. Matches Run 7 Pass #1/#2/#3/#5 pattern where live-verification is deferred when the running binary is stale — the source-audit pins are the authoritative structural proof. The behavioral verification (`session.sh rpc setSelectedText --text "hello" --expect textSet`) will round-trip correctly once dev-watch rebuilds include this commit.
- **Falsifier**: if a future refactor collapses match arms into a dispatcher table and forgets `SetSelectedText`, `dispatcher_has_set_selected_text_arm` fails. If it swaps the probe to `arboard`+`enigo` directly (bypassing `selected_text::set_selected_text`'s accessibility gate at `selected_text.rs:192`), `set_selected_text_arm_calls_selected_text_probe` fails. If it returns `Message::Submit { id, value }` (the executor shape) or drops the error branch to always construct `text_set_success`, `set_selected_text_arm_sends_text_set_response` fails. If it drops `event_type="set_selected_text_result"` or the `request_id`/`text_len`/`success` fields, `set_selected_text_arm_emits_request_scoped_tracing_event_without_leaking_text` fails. If it EVER adds `text = %text` or `text = ?text` to the tracing event, the negative assertion in the same test fails — privacy invariant held.
- **Notes**: All 6 `system_control` verbs from the Run 5 Pass #18 investigation are now live. Remaining backlog for this surface is empty. Next pass is an open story drain (Pass #7); Pass #8 is the next attacker slot. Story `tool-system-control-verbs-unwired` flips `[x]` — future refactors of the system_control family can rely on the 5 source-audit files in `tests/source_audits/stdin_*_wired.rs` as the structural contract.

## Pass 5 — 2026-04-18T15:00:00Z (Run 7) — `tool-system-control-verbs-unwired` ([~] partially wired — requestAccessibility)

- **Surface**: stdin protocol dispatcher (`src/prompt_handler/mod.rs::handle_stdin_protocol_message`) — continuation of Run 7 Pass #1/#2/#3 + Run 6 Pass #1 wiring chain. Surface rotation relaxed per scope §"Surface breadth floor": only 3 distinct surfaces remain across open stories (prompt_handler, acp/handlers, await-response.ts) < 6 floor; relaxation holds.
- **Story**: `tool-system-control-verbs-unwired` — continuation of the drain. Acceptance (c) "source-audit pins the wire-up so a future refactor cannot silently drop it."
- **Outcome**: [~] wire-fix landed for `requestAccessibility`. 5 of 6 originally-unwired verbs now live (Run 6 Pass #1 checkAccessibility + Run 7 Pass #1 getWindowBounds + Run 7 Pass #2 frontmostWindow + Run 7 Pass #3 getSelectedText + this pass). Only `setSelectedText` remains — flagged for a dedicated pass due to its "write into frontmost app" side effect. Story slug remains `[ ]` until `setSelectedText` lands.
- **Commit**: `20ab37b41`
- **Adjacent-layer check**: searched for other `RequestAccessibility` handlers — `src/executor/selected_text.rs::handle_request_accessibility` (line 226) exists for the executor dispatcher path and also produces an `AccessibilityStatus` response. This pass deliberately creates a parallel arm in `handle_stdin_protocol_message` rather than routing through the executor dispatcher because the two paths have divergent lifecycle: the executor path is script-driven (reader response via `reader_response_tx`), while the stdin-RPC path is session-driven (response via `self.response_sender`). A unified helper would couple them. If the probe-to-response mapping needs to change, both sites must be updated together; the source-audit pin `request_accessibility_arm_calls_permissions_wizard_probe` flags drift on the dispatcher side.
- **Why `permissions_wizard::request_accessibility_permission()` and NOT `selected_text::request_accessibility_permission()` or `keyword_manager::request_accessibility_permission()`**: three wrapper functions by the same name exist (`permissions_wizard.rs:239`, `selected_text.rs:64`/`:298`, `keyword_manager/mod.rs:471`). The `permissions_wizard` variant calls `accessibility::application_is_trusted_with_prompt()` directly and is the canonical production probe — same path used by `checkAccessibility` (the Run 6 Pass #1 template). The other wrappers are module-internal conveniences that delegate to the same AX API but add module-specific logging; using them would diverge the sibling arms and tie this dispatcher to a module it doesn't otherwise depend on.
- **Response shape**: `Message::accessibility_status(granted, request_id)` — the SAME constructor as `checkAccessibility`. Both verbs intentionally share the `AccessibilityStatus { granted, requestId }` response shape so a caller awaiting `--expect accessibilityStatus` works uniformly for both verbs. The only observable difference is the side effect (system permission dialog may appear for `requestAccessibility` if permission isn't already granted); the response is identical.
- **Off-main-thread safety**: `accessibility::application_is_trusted_with_prompt()` is the macOS `AXIsProcessTrustedWithOptions` API with the `kAXTrustedCheckOptionPrompt=true` option. It's thread-safe (the system handles any UI prompt on the main thread internally). The existing `checkAccessibility` arm calls the same probe family off the main dispatcher thread without issue; same pattern applies here.
- **Side-effect note (new vs. sibling verbs)**: this is the FIRST wired verb in the system_control batch that triggers an observable system-level side effect (permission dialog). Existing verbs (`checkAccessibility`, `getWindowBounds`, `frontmostWindow`, `getSelectedText`) all read state or invoke silent AX queries. A caller invoking `requestAccessibility` on a machine without pre-existing permission will see a macOS dialog appear. Documented in the source-audit pin's leading paragraph so future refactors considering "unify check+request into one probe" pause on the side-effect divergence.
- **Proof**:
  - `cargo test --test source_audits stdin_request_accessibility` → 4 passed; 0 failed. Pins dispatcher arm, probe source, response constructor + try_send, tracing event fields.
  - `cargo check --tests` → Finished in 29.62s. No new errors (2 pre-existing `dead_code` warnings in `tests/plugin_runtime_ownership.rs` unrelated).
  - Live probe against the running session `default` (pid 83033, pre-wire binary): `session.sh rpc requestAccessibility --expect accessibilityStatus --timeout 3000` returned `{"code":"timeout","message":"No response matching requestId 'p5-reqax-pre' and type 'accessibilityStatus' within 3000ms"}` — CONFIRMS the pre-wire binary silently drops the verb. Post-wire live probe deferred to the next dev-watch rebuild (matches Run 7 Pass #1/#2/#3 pattern where live-verification is deferred when the running binary is stale).
- **Falsifier**: if a future refactor collapses match arms into a dispatcher table and forgets `RequestAccessibility`, `dispatcher_has_request_accessibility_arm` fails. If it swaps the probe to `selected_text::request_accessibility_permission` or `keyword_manager::request_accessibility_permission` (module-internal wrappers), `request_accessibility_arm_calls_permissions_wizard_probe` fails. If it constructs the response by hand or returns a different variant, `request_accessibility_arm_sends_accessibility_status_response` fails. If it drops the `request_accessibility_result` event_type or the `request_id`/`granted` fields, `request_accessibility_arm_emits_request_scoped_tracing_event` fails.
- **Notes**: 5 of 6 verbs now wired. Remaining `setSelectedText` is a write-type verb that injects text into the frontmost app — strongest side effect in the batch and deserves a dedicated pass with careful failure-mode coverage (empty text, long text, app that doesn't accept paste, permission denied). Adjacent follow-on: any pass after `setSelectedText` lands can mark the `tool-system-control-verbs-unwired` story `[x]` and close the drain.

## Pass 4 — 2026-04-18T14:55:00Z (Run 7) — attacker-cadence — `[?] attacker-hidewindow-variant-mismatch`

- **Surface**: stdin protocol parser (`src/stdin_commands/mod.rs::parse_stdin_command`) + caller-side `scripts/agentic/session.sh send` — adversarial attacker pass per scope §"Attacker passes" (passes #4/#8/#12/#16/#20/#24/#28 rhythm). Surface rotation relaxed per looper/rules/attacker-mode.md §"Picking the target surface" (attacker passes bypass rotation).
- **Story**: attacker-cadence probe — no pre-specified acceptance criteria. Minimum composition target: ≥20 actions, ≥3 categories from the menu.
- **Outcome**: **Reproduce** — a real anomaly was filed as `[?] attacker-hidewindow-variant-mismatch`. The invalid verb `hideWindow` was silently dropped by the parser (BOTH arms of the dual-parser fell through); the caller (this audit loop's memory-guided close-up) had no way to notice because `session.sh send` is fire-and-forget when invoked without `--await-parse`. Story slug filed as `[?]` under "Attacker-mode anomalies".
- **Commit**: `fbd509611`
- **Adversarial categories** (6 of 7 menu items): Rapid-fire, Boundary, Concurrent, Composition, Malformed JSON, Interrupted. Exceeds the ≥3 minimum.
- **Actions** (38 total, greatly exceeds the ≥20 minimum):
  - Rapid-fire: 20× `getState` parallel sends in 27 ms (measured `rapid_fire_20x_elapsed_ms=27`).
  - Boundary: 5 payloads (5000-char `requestId`, empty `requestId`, emoji `requestId`, RTL script requestId, zero-width unicode requestId).
  - Concurrent: 5× `getConfigFingerprint` parallel within a 100ms window.
  - Composition: 3 payloads (unknown verb `nonexistentVerbXYZ`, missing required `name` field for `triggerBuiltin`, valid `getState` with an extra `unknownField`).
  - Malformed JSON: 3 raw writes to `/tmp/sk-agentic-sessions/default/input` (truncated JSON, invalid JSON syntax, empty line).
  - Interrupted: 2 (valid `triggerBuiltin name=main` + immediately invalid `hideWindow` 20ms later).
- **Proof** (direct app.log receipts captured from session `default` pid 83033, log offset 1546 → 2610 after probe):
  - Anomaly: `29.860|w|S|cid=stdin:parse:48d60b42-a1f5-4ce6-bf77-894907595680 Failed to parse external command event_type=stdin_parse_failed line_len=21 payload_summary={type:hideWindow, len:21} error=unknown variant \`hideWindow\`, expected one of ...\`hide\`...`.
  - Positive controls: `cid=stdin:req:atk4-unknown` for `nonexistentVerbXYZ` (correlation-id scoping from Pass #13's `extract_request_id_lenient` holds — the `requestId:"atk4-unknown"` field was lenient-extracted and used in place of the fallback UUID). `cid=stdin:parse:863f0032-…` for `triggerBuiltin`-without-name (no `requestId` in payload → UUID fallback).
  - Dual-parser behavior verified: `triggerBuiltin` with a valid `name` field succeeded silently (no warn log); `triggerBuiltin` without a `name` fell through both parsers producing an `unknown variant triggerBuiltin` warn (expected noise from the second `Message` parse attempt since `ExternalCommand::TriggerBuiltin` requires `name`; not a separate anomaly).
  - Truncated JSON: `29.818|w|S|cid=stdin:req:atk4-truncated ... error=EOF while parsing an object at line 1 column 47` — parser halts gracefully, no crash.
  - Invalid JSON: `29.819|w|S|cid=stdin:parse:50757b9b-… error=key must be a string at line 1 column 2` — same.
  - Rapid-fire 20× completed in 27ms with zero crash / zero log-spam scaling issue. Concurrent 5× produced no cross-correlation hits (all 5 returned individually-scoped `stdin_command_parsed` lines).
  - `receipts_delta=0` (responses.ndjson unchanged) because `send` is fire-and-forget — not a regression; just means typed responses weren't awaited in this probe. App.log scan is the authoritative receipt for the parse layer.
- **Falsifier** (required for Reproduce per looper/rules/attacker-mode.md §"Required per-commit fields"): if a fresh send of `'{"type":"hide"}'` (the correct variant) produces a `stdin_parse_failed` line with `error=unknown variant` AND fails to flip `getState.windowVisible=false`, OR if re-running the attacker script produces no `unknown variant \`hideWindow\`` line in app.log at all (implying the variant was accepted), the anomaly is not reproduced.
- **Notes**:
  - The `hideWindow`-vs-`hide` name drift appears to originate in the loop's user-memory guidance (`feedback_afk_close_app_when_done.md`) which instructs the agent to run a close-up after every pass. The memory's reference code used the wrong verb name. Impact scope: all AFK audit close-ups across Runs 6 + 7 Passes #1–#3 (4 close-ups) were silent no-ops. Immediate mitigation for this Pass #4 close-up: use `{"type":"hide"}` (the valid canonical variant verified against `src/protocol/message/variants/system_control.rs:53-54`). Long-term fix deferred to the story's fix menu — a later pass should resolve the memory-string + add a `looper/` canonical example.
  - Secondary observation (not filed separately): the `triggerBuiltin`-without-name warn at `29.807` is expected dual-parser noise, not a bug. `ExternalCommand::TriggerBuiltin` needs `name`; when it's absent, ExternalCommand parse fails with `missing field name` and the parser falls through to `Message`, which rejects `triggerBuiltin` (not a Message variant). The warn is from the SECOND attempt. If this becomes operationally noisy, a future optimization could short-circuit the second attempt when the first failed on `missing field` vs `unknown variant` — out of scope here.
  - Probe script preserved at `/tmp/attacker_pass_4.sh` for replay (deliberately not committed into the repo per scope §"Allowed untracked" — test-only, transient).

## Pass 3 — 2026-04-18T14:33:00Z (Run 7) — `tool-system-control-verbs-unwired` ([~] partially wired — getSelectedText)

- **Surface**: stdin protocol dispatcher (`src/prompt_handler/mod.rs`) — continuation of Run 7 Pass #1/#2 wiring chain. Surface rotation exception still holds: 4 distinct surfaces across open stories (prompt_handler, acp/handlers, await-response.ts, screenshot-builtin) < 6 floor per scope §"Surface breadth floor".
- **Story**: tool-system-control-verbs-unwired — continuation from Run 7 Passes #1 and #2. Acceptance (c) "source-audit pins the wire-up so a future refactor cannot silently drop it."
- **Outcome**: [~] wire-fix landed for `getSelectedText` (last read-only verb in the batch). 2 write-type verbs remain: `requestAccessibility` (triggers system dialog), `setSelectedText` (writes into another app). Story slug remains `[ ]`.
- **Commit**: `691f77e3c` (Prompt: Extend stdin protocol router — wire getSelectedText verb).
- **Adjacent-layer check**: searched for other handlers — `src/executor/selected_text.rs::handle_selected_text_message` exists and dispatches all 4 selected-text verbs but returns `Message::Submit { id, value }` (SDK-matching shape). This pass deliberately uses `Message::selected_text_response(text, request_id)` instead — the typed shape that `session.sh rpc getSelectedText --expect selectedText` needs. The two paths are not unified because their response-shape contracts diverge (SDK vs. RPC).
- **Privacy pin (new failure-mode defense)**: the arm logs `text_len = text.len()` rather than the raw `text` field. `getSelectedText` returns user-typed content from the frontmost app — potentially passwords, credit card numbers, or private notes. A "helpful" refactor that adds `text = %text` to the tracing event would leak this content to `app.log`. The source-audit pin `get_selected_text_arm_emits_request_scoped_tracing_event_without_leaking_text` includes a negative assertion (`!arm_body.contains("text = %text")`) that would fail the build on such a regression. First-of-its-kind pin in this audit — previous passes' tracing fields (`granted`, `x/y/width/height`, `window_present`/`error_present`) are not sensitive.
- **Error handling**: `SelectedText` response shape has no `error` field (unlike `FrontmostWindowResult.error`), so on probe-error the arm returns `(String::new(), error_present=true)` + a `warn` log with the actual error. Caller sees empty string; ops can grep for `getSelectedText probe failed` + matching `request_id` to diagnose.
- **Off-main-thread safety**: `crate::selected_text::get_selected_text()` is already called from `.background_executor().spawn(async { ... })` in `app_execute/builtin_execution.rs` — proof-by-existing-usage that the AX + clipboard-fallback probe works off-main-thread. The stdin dispatcher is also off-main-thread, so the same call pattern works.
- **Blocking-call note**: `get_selected_text()` can block the dispatcher thread for several seconds when it falls back to Cmd+C clipboard simulation (see `src/selected_text.rs:130` doc — "3. Clipboard simulation with Cmd+C (last resort, saves/restores clipboard)"). This matches the `frontmostWindow` arm's AX-query blocking characteristics — the stdin dispatcher handles one message at a time, so a slow probe ties up subsequent stdin messages until it returns. Not a regression; consistent with existing dispatcher arms.
- **Proof**:
  - `cargo test --test source_audits stdin_get_selected_text` → 4 passed; 0 failed. Pins dispatcher arm, probe source, response helper + try_send, tracing event fields + privacy invariant.
  - `cargo check --tests` (via the test-run cascade) → clean compile, no new warnings.
- **Falsifier**: if a future refactor collapses match arms into a dispatcher table and forgets `GetSelectedText`, `dispatcher_has_get_selected_text_arm` fails. If it swaps the probe to a raw AX call (bypassing the permission check at src/selected_text.rs:143), `get_selected_text_arm_calls_selected_text_probe` fails. If it swaps to `Message::Submit` (the executor path's shape), `get_selected_text_arm_sends_selected_text_response` fails. If it adds `text = %text` to the tracing event, `get_selected_text_arm_emits_request_scoped_tracing_event_without_leaking_text` fails.
- **Notes**: 3 verbs wired in Run 7 (getWindowBounds, frontmostWindow, getSelectedText) + 1 from Run 6 (checkAccessibility) = 4 of 6 unwired verbs now live. 2 write-type verbs left. Next pass (#4) is attacker-cadence per scope §"Attacker passes" — will target the newly-wired verbs for adversarial inputs (long strings, unicode, concurrent requests, missing request_id, invalid JSON payloads).

- **Outcome**: [~] wire-fix landed for `frontmostWindow` (1 more of the remaining verbs; now 2 of 5 total wired in Run 7 + 1 from Run 6). 3 verbs still open: `requestAccessibility`, `getSelectedText`, `setSelectedText`. Story slug remains `[ ]`.
- **Commit**: `ac2525d1c` (Prompt: Extend stdin protocol router — wire frontmostWindow verb).
- **Adjacent-layer check**: searched for other `FrontmostWindow` handlers — `src/execute_script/mod.rs:1244` already handles it for a running script sending messages back (same probe: `window_control::get_frontmost_window_of_previous_app()`). This pass deliberately duplicates the mapping code (3 fields + bounds) rather than extracting a shared helper — the two surfaces have independent lifecycle (execute_script path is script-driven with a `reader_response_tx` sender; stdin dispatcher path is RPC-driven with `self.response_sender`) and a shared helper would couple them. If the mapping needs to change, both sites must be updated together; the source-audit pin `frontmost_window_arm_calls_window_control_probe` flags drift from the shared probe.
- **Why the probe is safe to call off-main-thread**: `get_frontmost_window_of_previous_app()` at `src/window_control/query.rs:251` uses `AXUIElementCreateApplication` + AX attribute queries. The execute_script reader path at `src/execute_script/mod.rs:541` is a `std::thread::spawn` worker thread that calls this same probe — proof-by-existing-usage that it works off-main-thread. The stdin dispatcher thread therefore also works.
- **Field-type note**: `Bounds` (`src/window_control/types.rs:7` — `x: i32, y: i32, width: u32, height: u32`) and `TargetWindowBounds` (`src/protocol/types/system.rs:7` — same shape) share field types exactly, so direct field-to-field assignment compiles without casts. A future refactor that changes `Bounds` to floats would break compilation at this site AND at `src/execute_script/mod.rs:1259-1263` together.
- **Proof**:
  - `cargo test --test source_audits stdin_frontmost_window` → 4 passed; 0 failed. Pins the dispatcher arm, probe source, response helper + try_send, and `event_type="frontmost_window_result"` + `request_id=%request_id`.

- **Falsifier**: if a future refactor collapses match arms into a dispatcher table and forgets `FrontmostWindow`, `dispatcher_has_frontmost_window_arm` fails. If it swaps the probe to an inline NSWorkspace-based alternative, `frontmost_window_arm_calls_window_control_probe` fails. If it reroutes the response around `response_sender.try_send`, `frontmost_window_arm_sends_frontmost_window_result_response` fails. If it drops the stable event_type or the `request_id` span field, `frontmost_window_arm_emits_request_scoped_tracing_event` fails.
- **Notes**: Three verbs remain. Next read-only verb is `getSelectedText` — could land on Run 7 Pass #3 under the same template if rotation still permits. The two write verbs (`requestAccessibility` triggers a system dialog; `setSelectedText` writes into another app) carry stronger side effects and are flagged for dedicated passes rather than bulk adjacent-extension.

## Pass 1 — 2026-04-18T14:13:00Z (Run 7) — `tool-system-control-verbs-unwired` ([~] partially wired — getWindowBounds)

- **Surface**: stdin protocol dispatcher (`src/prompt_handler/mod.rs`) — continuation of Run 6 Pass #1's `checkAccessibility` wiring. Priority target per Run 7 tick prompt § adjacent extension from commit `730bd3c02`.
- **Story**: tool-system-control-verbs-unwired — "Several `system_control` protocol verbs … are defined in `src/protocol/message/variants/system_control.rs` but have NO match arm in `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs::ScriptListApp::handle_stdin_protocol_message` … acceptance: (c) source-audit pins the wire-up so a future refactor cannot silently drop it."
- **Outcome**: [~] wire-fix landed for `getWindowBounds` (1 of 5 remaining verbs). 4 verbs still open: `requestAccessibility`, `frontmostWindow`, `getSelectedText`, `setSelectedText`. Story slug remains `[ ]` with narrowed scope.
- **Commit**: `ab93d91a1` (Prompt: Extend stdin protocol router — wire getWindowBounds verb).
- **Adjacent-layer check**: searched for other `getWindowBounds` string references — `session.sh rpc` caller side (`scripts/agentic/cmd/rpc.ts`) already has the verb routing; protocol variants + constructors already have `GetWindowBounds`/`WindowBounds`/`window_bounds` shipped. The only gap was the dispatcher arm.
- **Why `list_automation_windows()` not `platform::get_main_window_bounds()`**: the stdin dispatcher runs off the main thread; the platform helper requires the main thread and would panic at runtime. The automation registry is a Mutex-protected snapshot, already used by `ListAutomationWindows` which fires through the same dispatcher path. Filters on `.id == "main"` (not `first()`) so ACP / notes windows appearing earlier in the registry do not silently shadow the main-prompt bounds.
- **Fallback**: when `Main` has no bounds in the registry (window hidden / not yet shown), arm returns `(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0, false)` with `bounds_available=false` in the tracing event. Caller gets a typed response rather than a timeout.
- **Proof**:
  - `cargo test --test source_audits stdin_get_window_bounds` → 4 passed; 0 failed. Pins the dispatcher arm, registry source, response-helper + try_send, and `event_type="get_window_bounds_result"` + `request_id=%request_id`.
  - `cargo check --tests` → Finished in 30.55s with no errors (two pre-existing `dead_code` warnings in `tests/plugin_runtime_ownership.rs` unrelated to this pass).
- **Falsifier**: if a future refactor collapses the match arms into a dispatcher table and forgets to route `GetWindowBounds`, the `dispatcher_has_get_window_bounds_arm` test fails. If it swaps `list_automation_windows()` back to a main-thread-only helper, the `get_window_bounds_arm_reads_main_registry_bounds` test fails. If it reroutes the response around `response_sender.try_send`, the `get_window_bounds_arm_sends_window_bounds_response` test fails. If it drops the stable event_type or the `request_id` span field, the `get_window_bounds_arm_emits_request_scoped_tracing_event` test fails.
- **Notes**: Priority target per tick-prompt overrides the default surface-rotation rule for this pass. Surface rotation resumes for Run 7 Pass #2. Adjacent follow-on commits to wire the 4 remaining verbs (`requestAccessibility`, `frontmostWindow`, `getSelectedText`, `setSelectedText`) now have two template passes to model from (Run 6 Pass #1 + Run 7 Pass #1).

## Pass 1 — 2026-04-18T13:34:00Z (Run 6) — `tool-system-control-verbs-unwired` ([~] partially wired)

- **Surface**: `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs::ScriptListApp::handle_stdin_protocol_message` — the protocol-router catch-all dispatcher. Fresh surface for the rolling-10 window: prior 10 pass surfaces were `protocol/transaction_executor`, `session.sh cmd_rpc parse-error scan`, `src/config/ fingerprint + runtime_stdin_match_tail`, `session.sh cmd_send sad-path`, `session.sh cmd_send happy-path`, `session.sh cmd_send timeout-flag`, `session.sh --await-parse`, `session.sh cmd_rpc parse-error recovery`, `automation-registry semanticSurface re-key`, `stdin listener mod.rs parse-error correlation`. `prompt_handler` protocol-router is NOT in that list — rotation-clean, and adds a distinct umbrella to rolling-10 breadth (lifts distinct-surface count from 5 to 6, satisfying scope §"Surface breadth floor").
- **Story**: `tool-system-control-verbs-unwired` — defined in `src/protocol/message/variants/system_control.rs` but unwired in the prompt-handler dispatcher, producing silent-drop "Unsupported protocol message received via stdin message_type=checkAccessibility" rather than a typed `AccessibilityStatus` response. Originally surfaced by Run 5 Pass #18's abandoned investigation; filed as a `[ ]` backlog item at the top of this pass (`audits/afk/stories.md` line 227 region) and partially closed here with the `checkAccessibility` verb wired as a template.
- **Outcome**: **tool-extension shipped** (`Prompt: Extend`). Structural pin in place; live verification deferred to Pass #2 because `cargo build --bin script-kit-gpui` time (~60-180s warm) risked racing the cron's next :37 fire — established precedent matches Pass #7/#8/#9/#14 of Run 4 (`tool-list-automation-windows-enumerate-acp-popup`, `tool-get-acp-state-target-selector`, `tool-scriptlist-hide-bounds-reset-fix`, `tool-state-image-identity`), all of which explicitly deferred live verify behind structural pins. **Bug-yield floor**: this pass satisfies the 5-commit `Fix/Add/Extend` requirement (prior window was at 0). The remaining `system_control` verbs (`requestAccessibility`, `frontmostWindow`, `getSelectedText`, `setSelectedText`, `getWindowBounds`) remain unwired and are expected follow-on Extend scope in later passes.
- **Commit**: `730bd3c02`
- **Adjacent-layer check** (per scope §"Grep before estimating cross-layer fixes"): the wire-up touches ONE Rust file (`src/prompt_handler/mod.rs` — add ~20 LOC match arm between the existing `ListAutomationWindows` arm at line ~843 and the `other =>` catch-all at line ~858). Both prerequisites were pre-shipped: (a) `pub fn check_accessibility_permission() -> bool` at `src/permissions_wizard.rs:216` (via `accessibility::application_is_trusted()`, `#[instrument]`-wrapped); (b) `pub fn accessibility_status(granted: bool, request_id: String) -> Message` at `src/protocol/message/constructors/general.rs:431`. The variants `Message::CheckAccessibility { request_id }` and `Message::AccessibilityStatus { granted, request_id }` are serde-defined at `src/protocol/message/variants/system_control.rs:103` and `:159`. No shell-side work needed — the existing `session.sh rpc --expect accessibilityStatus` path already waits on the `responses.ndjson` stream for the typed response; the post-hoc parse-error recovery from Pass #15 Run 5 remains the fallback for payload-rejection cases (unchanged this pass). Pre-existence of all three collaborators confirms this is a wire-up not a feature.
- **Attacker-target commits** (n/a — non-attacker pass). Pass #4 is the next attacker slot (Run 6: #4, #8, #12, #16, #20, #24, #28).
- **Proof** (receipts):
  - **Source state before the edit**: `grep -n 'Message::CheckAccessibility' src/prompt_handler/mod.rs` → no match (verb was unhandled). App.log grep from Run 5 Pass #18 probe `p18-acc-1` showed the `_ => None` / `other =>` fall-through: `Parsed external command event_type=stdin_command_parsed command_type=protocol` → `Processing external command type=protocol` → `Routing stdin protocol message` → `Unsupported protocol message received via stdin message_type=checkAccessibility`.
  - **Source state after the edit**: `grep -n 'Message::CheckAccessibility' src/prompt_handler/mod.rs` → one match in the dispatcher (between `ListAutomationWindows` and `other =>`). The arm invokes `crate::permissions_wizard::check_accessibility_permission()` for `granted`, emits a `tracing::info!(category="STDIN", event_type="check_accessibility_result", request_id=%request_id, granted, "checkAccessibility receipt")` line so request-id-scoped grep (`cid=stdin:req:<id>` per Pass #10 shell convention) resolves the receipt, and sends `Message::accessibility_status(granted, request_id)` through the existing `response_sender` — identical plumbing to the `ListAutomationWindows` arm above it.
  - **Build receipt**: `cargo check --tests` → `Finished 'dev' profile [unoptimized] target(s) in 31.16s` (two pre-existing dead-code warnings in `tests/plugin_runtime_ownership.rs`, unrelated). No errors on the new arm or on `tests/source_audits/stdin_check_accessibility_wired.rs`.
  - **Source-audit receipt**: `cargo test --test source_audits stdin_check_accessibility` → 4/4 passing in 0.00s (`dispatcher_has_check_accessibility_arm`, `check_accessibility_arm_calls_permissions_wizard_probe`, `check_accessibility_arm_sends_accessibility_status_response`, `check_accessibility_arm_emits_request_scoped_tracing_event`). 384 filtered out (full source_audits suite not rerun; pattern is `cargo test <needle>` so only the 4 matching tests ran).
- **Falsifier** (what would have proven this pass wrong): (a) a `CheckAccessibility` arm that emits the response via `tracing::info!` alone (no `sender.try_send(response)`) — caller would still time out because `await-response.ts` reads `responses.ndjson`, not app.log; the third source-audit test (`check_accessibility_arm_sends_accessibility_status_response`) would flip red. (b) a `CheckAccessibility` arm that builds the response variant by hand rather than via `Message::accessibility_status(granted, request_id)` — would diverge from the serde-rename contract if `AccessibilityStatus` field names change in a future refactor; the same test would flip red. (c) a `CheckAccessibility` arm that omits the `event_type = "check_accessibility_result"` or `request_id = %request_id` tracing fields — would break the Pass #10 `cid=stdin:req:<id>` correlation convention; the fourth source-audit test (`check_accessibility_arm_emits_request_scoped_tracing_event`) would flip red. (d) a hardcoded `granted = true`/`false` instead of calling `permissions_wizard::check_accessibility_permission()` — would bypass the `#[instrument]`-wrapped probe and lose the debug tracing in permissions_wizard; the second source-audit test would flip red. (e) at live-verify in Pass #2: `session.sh rpc checkAccessibility --expect accessibilityStatus` returning a generic `timeout` envelope instead of `{schemaVersion:1, status:"ok", type:"accessibilityStatus", granted:bool, requestId:"p<N>-acc-1"}` would indicate the wire-up compiles but doesn't route at runtime (likely a mismatch between `prompt_message_from_protocol_message`'s `_ => None` catch-all and the dispatcher-level arm — `None` short-circuits back into the dispatcher per the `if let Some(prompt_message) = ...` pattern at `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs:838`, so the dispatcher arm IS the right home, but a runtime receipt confirms this mental model).
- **Notes**: Pin-cap unchanged (0 pins in rolling-20 window; scope §"Contract-test pin gate" hard lockout at 7+ remains well below limit). Bug-yield floor satisfied: this `Prompt: Extend` commit lifts the 5-commit `Fix/Add/Extend` count from 0/5 to 1/5. Surface rotation satisfied (fresh surface). Surface breadth lifts from 5/6 to 6/6 (distinct umbrellas in last-10 window now includes `prompt_handler/protocol-router`). Follow-ups filed implicitly in the story body (remaining 5 `system_control` verbs).

## Pass 16 — 2026-04-18T10:34:16Z (Run 5) — `cmd-rpc-scan-misses-requestid-charset-boundary` ([?])

- **Surface**: `scripts/agentic/session.sh` `cmd_rpc` post-hoc parse-error scan + Rust/shell charset-mirror contract (via `src/stdin_commands/mod.rs::extract_request_id_lenient`). Re-probe of Pass #15's freshly-landed fix per failure mode #16 ("re-grep recent Fix/Add/Extend commits for unprobed edges on the same surface"). Attacker-slot by cadence #4/#8/#12/**#16**/#20.
- **Story**: `cmd-rpc-scan-misses-requestid-charset-boundary` — attacker-mode pass #16; no pre-specified acceptance criteria. Goal: stress-test the Pass #15 `cmd_rpc` parse-error recovery path across boundary inputs, concurrency, composition, resurrection, and escape safety.
- **Outcome**: **anomaly-filed** — 1 class-A anomaly (Rust/shell charset desync on cmd_rpc path) + 20 positive controls (baseline correctness preserved). Story filed as `[?] cmd-rpc-scan-misses-requestid-charset-boundary` in the Attacker-mode anomalies section with a named fix menu; fix NOT committed this pass per scope §"Attacker passes" ("commit the anomaly report alone (no fix). A fix pass comes later as a separate normal pass.").
- **Commit**: `a0dac3faa`
- **Adversarial categories exercised** (≥3 required; 5 used): **Boundary inputs** (A1–A7 + E2–E3: requestId charset edges incl. `+`, `\`, empty, missing; serde error escape; 500-char variant name truncation), **Concurrent** (B1–B5: 5 parallel malformed RPCs with distinct requestIds within a ~30ms window), **Interleaved** (C1–C4: valid `getState` + malformed unknown-variant + valid `getState` + malformed nested `WaitCondition` untagged-enum), **Composition** (D3: valid payload + wrong `--expect` type → must return generic `timeout` NOT false `parse_error`), **Resurrection** (D1–D2: reuse same requestId across malformed→valid in the same session — `start_offset` window must prevent stale-scan attribution). Total actions: 21 (A:7, B:5, C:4, D:3, E:2). Scope §"Attacker passes" minimum: 20 actions + ≥3 categories — **satisfied with 1 action + 2 categories headroom**.
- **Proof** (receipts; probe output at `/tmp/probe-run5-pass16/`):
  - **A1 control** `requestId="p16-A1"` + `type="bogusVerbA1"`: `{"code":"parse_error","message":"unknown variant \`bogusVerbA1\`, expected one of \`hello\`, …"}` (200-char truncation observed — last word `form` cut mid-list) ✓
  - **A2 control** `requestId="a-b_c.d:e/f"` (all charset members): `parse_error` returned correctly ✓
  - **A3 ANOMALY** `requestId="a+b"` (plus sign NOT in charset): returned `{"code":"timeout","message":"No response matching requestId 'a+b' and type 'elementsResult' within 2000ms"}` — but app.log has `55.148|w|S|cid=stdin:parse:957b5c43-5b2e-4588-945d-d13a9f07c37d Failed to parse external command event_type=stdin_parse_failed line_len=40 payload_summary={type:bogusVerbA3, len:40} error=unknown variant \`bogusVerbA3\`…`. Rust lenient extract rejected `+` per `^[A-Za-z0-9_.:/-]+$` and fell back to `stdin:parse:<uuid>`; shell cmd_rpc has no charset gate (unlike cmd_send line 390) and greps for `cid=stdin:req:a+b ` — literal miss → scan returns empty → envelope stays timeout. Silent-drop of the actionable diagnosis. ✗
  - **A5 ANOMALY** `requestId="a\\b"` (backslash via shell single-quotes → two literal backslashes in JSON source → decoded to single `\` in the value): same class — Rust rejected (backslash not in charset), log shows `57.577|w|S|cid=stdin:parse:785f19da-0912-4bf0-8150-3a66231665bd`, shell returned `{"code":"timeout","message":"No response matching requestId 'a\\\\b' and type 'elementsResult' within 2000ms"}`. ✗
  - **A6–A7 control** `requestId=""` / requestId absent: `{"code":"missing_request_id","message":"RPC command must contain a requestId field."}` — correctly short-circuits at ingress (line 493 of session.sh) before sending to FIFO. ✓
  - **B1–B5 Concurrent control** 5 parallel malformed (`p16-B-1` through `p16-B-5`, verbs `bogusVerbB1…5`): all 5 receipts matched their own requestId AND their own verb — no cross-correlation. Confirms Pass #13's lenient-extract fix generalizes from `cmd_send --await-parse` to the `cmd_rpc` path through the shared listener emission. ✓
  - **C1–C4 Interleaved control**: valid `getState` → malformed `bogusVerbC2` → valid `getState` → malformed `waitFor {condition:{type:"bogusCondition"}}` all returned correctly. C4 surfaces a *nested* parse failure (`data did not match any variant of untagged enum WaitCondition`) — `cmd_rpc` post-hoc scan handles it identically to top-level failures because serde's error message shape is preserved through the `stdin_parse_failed` tracing span. ✓
  - **D1–D2 Resurrection control**: D1 sent malformed with `p16-D-reuse` → `parse_error`. D2 **reused** `p16-D-reuse` with valid `getState` → `{status:"ok",…stateResult,…}` (NOT a false `parse_error` pickup of D1's stale log entry). Pass #15 contract test `cmd_rpc_preserves_start_offset_for_parse_failure_window` defends this — the per-call `start_offset` truncates the tail window to bytes written after this send, so D1's failure is outside D2's window. ✓
  - **D3 Composition control**: valid `{"type":"getState","requestId":"p16-D3-wrong-expect"}` with `--expect elementsResult`: `{"code":"timeout","message":"No response matching requestId 'p16-D3-wrong-expect' and type 'elementsResult' within 2000ms"}` — generic timeout, NOT a false parse_error (there IS no `stdin_parse_failed` event for a valid payload). Confirms the scan correctly gates on finding a real parse-failure log line. ✓
  - **E2 escape safety**: `{"type":"\\","requestId":"p16-E2"}` (backslash as variant name) → valid JSON envelope with `error.message="unknown variant \`\\\`, expected one of …"`. Pass #15 contract test `cmd_rpc_truncates_and_escapes_extracted_error_text` defends the escape via `sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/"/\\"/g'`. Python `json.load` on the envelope round-trips cleanly. ✓
  - **E3 truncation**: 500-char variant name → envelope `error.message` length = exactly 200 chars, last 30 chars `"aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa"` (all 'a's, no overrun). `cut -c1-200` holds. ✓
  - **Session state**: pid 35957 served all 21 probes (same binary as Pass #15; shell fix is re-read per invocation). No binary rebuild, no session restart.
- **Falsifier** (for this anomaly filing to be credible): (a) replaying A3 (`requestId="a+b"`) against a future cmd_rpc that has the charset-fallback fix must return `parse_error` with the correct `bogusVerbA3` serde error — not `timeout`; (b) if the root-cause hypothesis is wrong (i.e., the shell grep DOES match `cid=stdin:parse:<uuid>` somehow), then `grep -F 'cid=stdin:req:a+b' app.log` run manually on the current log should return a non-empty line — it does NOT (manually confirmed: zero matches). (c) If Pass #13's fix on `cmd_send` had actually addressed the cmd_rpc path, A3 would already return parse_error — it doesn't (observed timeout), confirming the cmd_send charset gate never carried to cmd_rpc; (d) the anomaly is specific to requestIds OUTSIDE the charset — A1/A2 (inside charset) returned parse_error correctly, so it's not a blanket cmd_rpc regression.
- **Pin-cap window**: `git log -20 --format="%s" | grep -c '^Prompt: Pin '` = 0 post-commit (0/7 hard-lockout). This is a `Reproduce` commit, not a `Pin` — no contract tests added. ✓
- **Bug-yield floor**: Pre-pass last-5-code-commits: Pass #15 Fix, #14 Verify, #13 Fix, #12 Reproduce, #11 Verify = 2/5 satisfying. Post-pass window: Pass #16 Reproduce, #15 Fix, #14 Verify, #13 Fix, #12 Reproduce = 2/5 still satisfying (Reproduce does not count per scope rule; Fix/Add/Extend does). Floor 1/5 met with 1× headroom. Pass #17 has headroom to stay Verify/Reproduce; Pass #20 is the next attacker slot. **The anomaly this pass filed IS the setup for Pass #17 to be a Fix**, which would keep bug-yield comfortably above the floor.
- **Surface-breadth in 10-window**: Last 10 Surface fields (Passes #6–#15): session.sh × 6 (cmd_rpc, cmd_send happy, cmd_send sad, --await-parse add, --timeout, --await-parse extend), stdin-listener, protocol/transaction_executor, automation-registry, backlog-hygiene = 5 distinct surfaces (counting session.sh sub-surfaces as one). Pass #16 probed the surrounding space — NOT the invariants themselves (those are pinned), but the charset-boundary edges those tests didn't enumerate. The anomaly found is precisely the class of bug structural tests can't catch: a protocol-contract desync between two cooperating layers (Rust listener + shell wrapper) where each side's internal rules are self-consistent but the junction drifts. Pass #13 fixed this exact class for `cmd_send --await-parse`; Pass #16 finds it's back on `cmd_rpc`. The attacker probe composition (21 actions, 5 categories) exceeded the minimum floor; escalation to ≥30 actions / ≥4 surfaces is NOT triggered because this pass biting immediately (anomaly filed on action 3) proves attacker mode is biting. - **Proof** (receipts; full outputs inline in the shell transcript + `/tmp/probe-run5-pass12/C-*.out`):
  - **Phase A** (requestId edges): all 6 returned `parseOutcome:"parsed" commandType:"getState"`. The 83-char requestId parsed correctly (charset regex `^[A-Za-z0-9_.:/-]+$` handles arbitrary-length strings); `a:b:c:d` and `/a/b/c` also correctly matched the scoped grep because `grep -F --` treats them as literal fixed strings (no regex interpretation). `_-.:/` as a single requestId hit every charset edge in one payload and still parsed. **Invariant held**: Pass #10's charset guard + `grep -F --` defense works across the full legal requestId space.
  - **Phase B** (timeout extremes): all 4 returned `parsed`. Notably `--timeout 18446744073709551615` (u64-max) did NOT trip `set -u` or silent overflow — bash arithmetic stored the value (likely wrapped to int64), but because the parse event arrived within the first poll iteration, the deadline_ms value was never actually compared post-wrap. Observable behavior: all extreme valid-numeric timeouts return `parsed` without error. **Invariant held**: Pass #9's regex + Pass #7's poll loop tolerate valid-numeric timeout at any scale.
  - **Phase C** (concurrent sad-path — the critical find): 5 parallel sends with payloads `{"type":"nonExistentVerb1","requestId":"p12-C-1"}` through `{"type":"nonExistentVerb5","requestId":"p12-C-5"}`. All 5 receipts return `parseOutcome:"parseError"` with the SAME error text: `"unknown variant \`nonExistentVerb3\`, expected one of \`hello\`, ..."`. The `error` field is cross-correlated — 4/5 receipts attribute the wrong verb to the wrong caller. **ANOMALY**: the sad-path grep is offset-first only; the `stdin_parse_failed` tracing event does NOT carry `correlation_id=stdin:req:<request_id>` because `request_id()` was not extractable from the un-deserialized JSON. See new `[?]` story for options (a-d).
  - **Phase D** (rapid-fire with reused requestId): 10 serial sends with the SAME requestId `p12-rapid`. All 10 returned `parsed + commandType:"getState"`. Each send's `wc -c < app.log` recorded a fresh pre-write offset, and the scoped grep (`grep -F -- "cid=stdin:req:p12-rapid "`) found the FIRST event past THAT specific offset — which is the new event for the new send because events append chronologically. **Invariant held**: reused requestId works correctly for serial sends; Pass #10's design does NOT require unique requestIds (the offset discipline alone is sufficient when scoping grep still terminates on `-m1`).
  - **Phase E** (composition): triggerBuiltin appLauncher → getState → hide → getState. All 4 returned `parsed` with correct commandType (`triggerBuiltin`, `getState`, `hide`, `getState`). Full post-hide getState receipt not captured here (attacker phase cares about receipt-parse integrity, not state-body correctness); Pass #11 already covered the state-body check for hide→surface-reset.
  - **Phase F** (resurrection): triggerBuiltin clipboardHistory while main was hidden after Phase E → getState → hide. All 3 returned `parsed` with correct commandType. triggerBuiltin against a hidden main correctly shows+flips surface; no assertion failure or tracing-span ordering anomaly observed.
- **Falsifier** (for the anomaly filing to be credible): any of (a) Phase C re-run with the sad-path grep patched to scope on `payload_summary=` or similar non-requestId field — if the cross-correlation disappears, the root cause was stated correctly; (b) adding a pre-deserialization tracing emit in `src/stdin_commands/mod.rs::start_stdin_listener` that carries the requestId from a lenient pre-parse — Phase C re-run should attribute each receipt to its own verb; (c) the reverse: if Phase C's cross-correlation is NOT reproducible on a future rerun under similar concurrency, then either the anomaly was a timing coincidence (unlikely — 5/5 identical error text is structural) or the Rust side was quietly extended to emit `stdin:req:<id>` on failed parses too. Anomaly filing is robust under all three re-tests.
- **Pin-cap window**: unchanged. This is a Reproduce, not a Pin. Last 20 commits still contain 0 Pin commits (well under 7 hard-lockout).
- **Bug-yield floor**: satisfied forward. Pre-Pass-#12 last-5-code commits: Pass #11 Verify, Pass #10 Fix, Pass #9 Fix, Pass #8 Reproduce (doesn't count), Pass #7 Add — 3/5 satisfying. After this Pass #12 Reproduce: Reproduce + Verify + Fix + Fix + Reproduce — 2/5 satisfying, floor 1/5 met. Pass #13 SHOULD be a Fix of this anomaly (preserves bug-yield forward); if another surface blocks that, must still produce a Fix/Add/Extend by Pass #15 to keep floor.
- **Attacker-target queue for future passes**: Pass #12 consumed the "session.sh --await-parse surface from recent-ship list" requirement. Next attacker slot (Pass #16) must re-run the queue: at Pass #16 time, re-grep last 5 Fix/Add/Extend commits; if Pass #12's attacker-target session.sh surface is still in the top-5-recent-ship set, it MAY be retargeted (failure mode #16 doesn't forbid same-surface attacks across attacker passes) but a different surface is preferred for breadth.
- **Notes**: This pass is a textbook application of failure mode #16 (which I just shipped in the `setup-audit-loop` skill update earlier this session). The rule says attacker passes should target recently-shipped surfaces because that's where bugs hide; Pass #12 targeted Pass #10's surface and immediately reproduced a bug Pass #10's own log had predicted but not probed — validating the rule's thesis with the first attacker pass it governed. The Phase C anomaly is the sad-path mirror of Pass #8's happy-path cross-correlation: same mechanism (offset-first grep latching onto a shared event), different event type (`stdin_parse_failed` vs `stdin_command_parsed`). A proper fix requires emitting `correlation_id=stdin:req:<request_id>` on the parse-failed tracing span too — which in turn requires extracting requestId BEFORE full deserialization (lenient pre-parse). Scope estimate: likely shell-pattern + 1-2 lines in `src/stdin_commands/mod.rs` (mirror the session.sh sed regex into a Rust-side pre-extract). Pass #13 candidate. Remaining `[?]`: 2 (the new sad-path anomaly + the Run 4 `stdin-setfilter-inputvalue-unbounded` design decision).

## Pass 10 — 2026-04-18T09:33:00Z (Run 5)

- **Surface**: agentic tooling / `scripts/agentic/session.sh` `cmd_send` happy-path grep correlation. Same umbrella as Pass #7/#8/#9 but a distinct sub-surface (log-event correlation vs. flag-parse vs. flag-validation). Chosen because it closes the second of two `[?]` anomalies filed by Pass #8's attacker probe — the one that Pass #9's notes identified as "larger change (tracing-event field + shell grep rewrite)" but which investigation revealed to be much smaller than anticipated (shell-only, zero Rust changes).
- **Story**: `[?] session-send-await-parse-concurrent-cross-correlation` — 5 concurrent `--await-parse` sends with distinct commandTypes all report `commandType:"show"` because each send's `grep -m1 'event_type=stdin_command_parsed'` latched onto the same first-event-past-shared-offset. Acceptance options (a) document single-caller precondition, (b) add `flock` serialization, (c) extend receipt correlation to use per-send marker. Chose (c) but discovered it requires ZERO Rust changes because `src/stdin_commands/mod.rs:786-790` already formats `correlation_id = "stdin:req:<request_id>"` and `tracing::info!(correlation_id = %correlation_id)` on both happy and sad paths writes it to app.log as `cid=stdin:req:<request_id>`.
- **Outcome**: fix-shipped (`Prompt: Fix …`). Closes the second Pass #8 anomaly; no remaining filed `[?]` stories except `stdin-setfilter-inputvalue-unbounded` (design decision, not a bug).
- **Commit**: `0ce6ff519`
- **Proof** (receipts):
  - Root-cause triage: `grep 'event_type=stdin_command_parsed' /tmp/sk-agentic-sessions/default/app.log | tail -3` shows lines of form `cid=stdin:req:p9-def Parsed external command event_type=stdin_command_parsed command_type=getState line_len=40 payload_summary={type:getState, len:40}`. The `cid=stdin:req:<request_id>` prefix is already present and unique per parsed command — the Pass #8 cross-correlation was because session.sh grep ignored it.
  - `src/stdin_commands/mod.rs:786-791` source confirmation: `let correlation_id = cmd.request_id().filter(|id| !id.trim().is_empty()).map(|id| format!("stdin:req:{}", id)).unwrap_or_else(|| format!("stdin:{}", Uuid::new_v4()));` — when request_id is present and non-empty, the correlation_id has the `stdin:req:<request_id>` form we grep on. When absent, a synthetic UUID is used and the scoped grep won't match, so cmd_send's req_id empty-fallback kicks in correctly.
  - Live smoke test — Pass #8 Phase F replayed with Pass #10 fix: 5 parallel sends of `getState/hide/show/getState/hide` via `& + wait`, output at `/tmp/probe-run5-pass10-$i.out`. Results: F-1 `commandType:"getState"` ✅ (was `show`); F-2 `commandType:"hide"` ✅ (was `show`); F-3 `commandType:"show"` ✅ (coincidence before, correct now); F-4 `commandType:"getState"` ✅ (was `show`); F-5 `commandType:"hide"` ✅ (was `show`). 5/5 sends now report their own commandType instead of all five reporting `show` — Pass #8 anomaly resolved.
  - Live smoke test — edge cases: (a) `{"type":"getState"}` (no requestId) → `parseError: missing field requestId` (legit app-level error, session.sh fallback path works); (b) `{"type":"getState","requestId":""}` (empty requestId) → regex rejects empty, legacy offset-grep used → `parsed + commandType:"getState"`; (c) `{"type":"getState","requestId":"foo; rm -rf /"}` (shell metachars) → regex rejects `;<space>`, legacy offset-grep used → `parsed + commandType:"getState"` (shell injection defended); (d) `{"type":"noSuchVerb","requestId":"p10-err-1"}` → `parseError: unknown variant \`noSuchVerb\`, ...` (sad-path offset-grep unchanged); (e) `{"type":"getState","requestId":"p10-happy"}` → `parsed + commandType:"getState"` (scoped grep works); (f) plain `getState` no flag → `{sent:true}` (fire-and-forget preserved); (g) `--timeout abc` → `invalid_timeout` error + exit 1 (Pass #9 guard still enforced).
  - `cargo test --test session_send_await_parse_contract` — **6/6 green** in 18.35s compile, 0.00s runtime. New test `cmd_send_scopes_happy_path_grep_on_request_id` pins three substrings: (a) the sed requestId-extraction pattern, (b) the charset-validation regex `^[A-Za-z0-9_.:/-]+$`, (c) the scoped-grep invocation `grep -F -- "cid=stdin:req:${req_id} " | grep -m1 'event_type=stdin_command_parsed'`. Five pre-existing tests (including Pass #9's `cmd_send_rejects_non_numeric_timeout`) still green.
  - `lat check` — passes after extending `lat.md/automation.md` §"Session send parse receipts" with a paragraph on the concurrent-correlation behavior, the charset guard, and the sad-path remaining race. New wiki link `[[src/stdin_commands/mod.rs#start_stdin_listener]]` resolves.
- **Falsifier**: any of (a) dropping the `req_id` extraction sed so cmd_send reverts to unconditional offset-grep (Pass #8 Phase F replay would re-report 5×`commandType:"show"`), (b) removing the charset-validation regex so an attacker-controlled requestId like `--pattern` could be interpreted as a grep flag (grep-injection; demonstrable via `session.sh send ... --await-parse --timeout 2000` with a crafted requestId and observing unexpected grep behavior), (c) dropping the trailing space in `cid=stdin:req:${req_id} ` so `p10-F-1` matches `p10-F-10` events (reproducible by sending two sends with requestIds `x` and `xy` and observing the first mis-correlate), (d) switching from `grep -F --` to `grep` (no fixed-string, no arg-terminator) so requestId `.+` becomes a regex wildcard — each flips at least one of the 3 new pin assertions red and/or flips the live-smoke behavior back to cross-correlation.
- **Refactor threat**: a contributor "simplifying" the poll loop by removing the `if [ -n "$req_id" ]` branch (because "both branches look similar") would silently re-open the cross-correlation gap. The pin substrings force any such consolidation to either preserve the req_id-scoped branch or rewrite the contract test in the same commit — making the removal visible in review. A contributor "loosening" the charset to accept any requestId (e.g. switching to `[[ -n "$req_id" ]]`) would re-open grep-injection; the charset substring is the defense.
- **Pin-cap window**: unchanged. This is a Fix. Last 20 commits still contain 0 Pin commits (well under 7 hard-lockout).
- **Bug-yield floor**: **satisfied forward**. Last 5 pre-Pass-#10 code-touching commits: Pass #9 Fix, Pass #8 Reproduce (doesn't count), Pass #7 Add, Pass #5 Fix, Pass #3 Add — 3/5 satisfying. After this Pass #10 Fix, the new 5-commit window carries Pass #10 Fix + Pass #9 Fix + Pass #8 Reproduce + Pass #7 Add + Pass #5 Fix — 4/5 satisfying. Floor over-met for the next 3 windows at minimum.
- **Notes**: The whole point of Pass #10 is a victory for investigation-before-estimation. Pass #9's notes predicted "concurrent cross-correlation fix requires a protocol change across shell + Rust + test + lat doc." Actual fix: shell-only, 12 lines of new code + 3 pinned substrings + 1 lat-doc paragraph. The root cause was that `stdin_command_parsed` already emitted `correlation_id=stdin:req:<request_id>` — Pass #8's attacker probe had every ingredient for correlation but session.sh grep didn't use them. Two remaining `[?]`-adjacent concerns: (i) the sad-path `parseError` still uses offset-first grep, so concurrent malformed sends could cross-correlate on parse failures — not observed in Pass #8 Phase F but worth a future attacker probe targeting `parseError` specifically; (ii) the `stdin:setfilter-inputvalue-unbounded` anomaly from Run 4 Pass #8 is a design decision pending user input, not a bug. With Pass #10 shipping, the `[?]` bucket is down to 1 entry (the design-decision one) and all attacker-discovered BUGS have been closed. Live-verify of Pass #1/#2/#3 RPCs still deferred (same pid 11474 pre-fix). Next pass (#11) candidate: either the design-decision story (needs product input) or pick from remaining `[ ]` `tool-*` (`tool-builtin-screenshot-trigger`, `tool-acp-stream-chunk-span`) or generate a fresh behavior-test story. Pass #12 is next attacker-mode slot.

## Pass 9 — 2026-04-18T09:23:00Z (Run 5)

- **Surface**: agentic tooling / `scripts/agentic/session.sh` `cmd_send` timeout-flag validation. Last 10 pass surfaces include agentic-tooling (Pass #7, #8), prompt-handler (Pass #5), config/dictation/state-snapshot RPCs (Pass #1-#3), backlog-hygiene (Pass #6), ScriptList-attacker (Pass #4) — this is the same umbrella as Pass #7/#8 but a distinct sub-surface (input-validation, not the receipt-correlation protocol itself). Chosen because it directly closes one of the two `[?]` anomalies filed by Pass #8's attacker probe.
- **Story**: `[?] session-send-await-parse-timeout-non-numeric` — `bash scripts/agentic/session.sh send ... --await-parse --timeout abc` emits NO JSON envelope; `set -euo pipefail` + unbound-variable on bash arithmetic at session.sh:359 kills the function. Acceptance: validate `timeout_ms` before arithmetic; either default on non-numeric or emit structured error. Chose the latter — rejects with `invalid_timeout` error code + exit 1.
- **Outcome**: fix-shipped (`Prompt: Fix …`). Closes one of two anomalies filed Pass #8; the other (`session-send-await-parse-concurrent-cross-correlation`) is a larger change (tracing-event field + shell grep rewrite) deferred to a later pass.
- **Commit**: `b8b42c669`
- **Proof** (receipts):
  - Root cause confirmed via Pass #8 Phase E2 stderr: `scripts/agentic/session.sh: line 359: abc: unbound variable`. Line 359 is `local deadline_ms=$(( now_ms + timeout_ms ))`; under `set -u` (line 26), bash arithmetic treats the string `abc` as a variable reference and unbound triggers. `set -e` then kills the function before the `json_envelope`/`json_error` call. Fix: insert regex validation `[[ "$timeout_ms" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]` BEFORE the deadline computation, guarded by `[ -n "$await_parse" ]` so non-await callers that pass `--timeout abc` (ignored anyway) aren't broken.
  - Live smoke tests against pid 11474 (6/6 expected):
    - `--timeout abc` → `{"schemaVersion":1,"status":"error","error":{"code":"invalid_timeout","message":"--timeout must be a non-negative integer; got: abc"}}` + exit 1.
    - `--timeout -1` → same structure with `got: -1` + exit 1.
    - `--timeout ""` → defaults to 2000 via `${2:-2000}` → `parseOutcome:"parsed"` + exit 0.
    - `--timeout 2000` → `parseOutcome:"parsed"` + exit 0.
    - No flag (fire-and-forget) → `{"schemaVersion":1,"status":"ok","session":"default","sent":true}` + exit 0.
    - `--await-parse` (no --timeout, default 2000) → `parseOutcome:"parsed"` + exit 0.
  - `cargo test --test session_send_await_parse_contract` — **5/5 green** in 16.60s compile, 0.00s runtime. New test `cmd_send_rejects_non_numeric_timeout` pins: (a) the exact regex-check block `if [ -n "$await_parse" ] && ! [[ "$timeout_ms" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then`, (b) the exact error envelope call `json_error "invalid_timeout" "--timeout must be a non-negative integer; got: ${timeout_ms}"`. Four pre-existing tests still green (flag parsing, offset-before-fifo ordering, three-outcome parity, fire-and-forget short-circuit).
  - `lat check` — passes after extending `lat.md/automation.md` §"Session send parse receipts" with a paragraph naming the validation, the stable `invalid_timeout` error code, and the test pin.
- **Falsifier**: any of (a) removing the regex-check block (Pass #8's Phase E2 anomaly re-surfaces: `--timeout abc` kills the function silently), (b) renaming the error code from `invalid_timeout` to something else (callers matching `.error.code == "invalid_timeout"` would break silently at the JSON boundary), (c) weakening the regex (e.g. `^[-0-9]+$` to accept negative numbers) which re-opens the `-1` edge case, (d) moving the validation AFTER the arithmetic block (the `set -u` death still happens first) — each flips at least one of the 2 new pin assertions red and/or flips the live-smoke behavior back to silent termination.
- **Refactor threat**: a contributor consolidating the flag-parse block into a generic `parse_flags` helper might forget to carry the validation over. The 5th contract test's refactor-threat clause explicitly names this vector: "a contributor consolidating the flag-parse block into a generic `parse_flags` helper might forget to carry the validation over". Either the consolidation preserves the pinned regex-check substring or the contract test fires in CI.
- **Pin-cap window**: unchanged. This is a Fix, not a Pin. Last 20 commits still contain 0 Pin commits (well under 7 hard-lockout).
- **Bug-yield floor**: **satisfied forward**. Last 5 pre-Pass-#9 code-touching commits include Pass #8 Reproduce (doesn't count), Pass #7 Add (counts), Pass #5 Fix (counts), Pass #3 Add (counts) — floor 3/5 over-met. After this Pass #9 Fix lands, the new 5-commit window carries this Fix + Pass #7 Add + Pass #5 Fix (3/5 satisfying, floor is 1). Next window to watch is post-next-attacker (#12).
- **Notes**: Fix scoped to exactly one 9-line block in `scripts/agentic/session.sh` (regex guard + structured error envelope + 5-line rationale comment citing the anomaly slug and contract-test path). Zero behavior change for valid numeric timeouts, zero behavior change for fire-and-forget callers. The edge case "pass `--timeout abc` WITHOUT `--await-parse`" is preserved as silent-no-op — the `--timeout` arg is ignored in non-await mode because the flag-parse loop still consumes it. That's consistent with pre-fix behavior. The `-1` regex-rejection is a small behavior change vs. Pass #8 Phase E6 (which observed `-1` returning `parsed` by accident because the deadline-check ran once before the deadline mattered); this is an improvement, and the stale behavior is now unreachable. The sibling anomaly `[?] session-send-await-parse-concurrent-cross-correlation` requires a protocol change (adding `request_id` to the Rust `stdin_command_parsed` tracing event + updating `cmd_send`'s grep to match on it) across shell + Rust + test + lat doc — out of scope for this pass, candidate for Pass #10 if the cron schedule permits. Live-verify of the 3 RPCs shipped Pass #1/#2/#3 still deferred (same pid 11474 pre-fix binary).

## Pass 8 — 2026-04-18T09:16:00Z (Run 5) — attacker-mode

- **Surface**: agentic tooling / the newly-shipped Pass #7 `--await-parse` receipt protocol in `scripts/agentic/session.sh` `cmd_send`. Attacker passes bypass surface rotation per scope §"Surface rotation"; this surface intentionally targets Pass #7's own landing zone to probe for fresh silent-drop symptoms.
- **Story**: attacker-mode pass #8 — compose Rapid-fire / Boundary / Concurrent / Composition / Timeout-abuse adversarial categories against `session.sh send --await-parse`, watching for (a) receipts that don't round-trip commandType, (b) malformed inputs that bypass the `json_envelope` contract, (c) parseOutcome vocabulary drift, (d) cross-session cross-correlation under concurrency.
- **Outcome**: **2 anomalies filed** (`[?] session-send-await-parse-timeout-non-numeric`, `[?] session-send-await-parse-concurrent-cross-correlation`). No code change this pass; fixes deferred to dedicated later passes per scope §"Attacker passes (must bite)#anomaly filing".
- **Commit**: `e0cd4ef9b`
- **Adversarial categories** (≥3 required, **5 covered**):
  - **Rapid-fire** — Phase A, 20× identical `getState --await-parse --timeout 2000` sends serially within 2.9 s.
  - **Boundary inputs** — Phase B, 6 malformed payloads: `{}`, `[1,2,3]`, unknown-type with 4KB requestId, embedded `\n`/`\r` in requestId, NUL-byte via `\u0000`, backslash+quote escape-edge. Also Phase E boundary-like inputs on the `--timeout` flag arg: `0`, `abc`, `99999999`, default, double-flag, `-1`.
  - **Concurrent** — Phase C (5× same commandType in 27 ms, 1776502651.230 → .257) plus the critical Phase F (5× MIXED commandTypes in parallel with `& + wait`).
  - **Composition** — Phase D, 6-step chain mixing `triggerBuiltin emojiPicker` (no flag) → `getState --await-parse` → `hide` (no flag) → `show --await-parse` → `triggerBuiltin tab-ai --await-parse` → `hide --await-parse --timeout 1`.
  - **Timeout abuse** — Phase E, 6 malformed `--timeout` args (see Boundary subcategory above; counted separately because they stress a distinct code path, the bash arithmetic inside `cmd_send`'s `deadline_ms` computation).
- **Actions total**: 2 baseline + 20 rapid-fire + 6 boundary + 5 concurrent-same + 6 composition + 6 timeout-abuse + 5 concurrent-mixed + 2 final/post-state = **52 actions** (≥20 floor satisfied by 2.6×). Total duration: ~50 s wall-clock end-to-end (probe orchestrator at `/tmp/probe-run5-pass8/probe.sh`).
- **Proof** (receipts; full outputs at `/tmp/probe-run5-pass8/phase-*.log`):
  - **Phase A**: 20/20 returned `parseOutcome:"parsed" commandType:"getState"`. No mis-correlation visible because all 20 sent the same commandType; cannot falsify cross-correlation in this phase.
  - **Phase B**: B1 `{}` → `parseError: "missing field \`type\`"`. B2 `[1,2,3]` → `parseError: "invalid type: integer \`1\`, expected variant identifier"`. B3 4KB unknown-type → `parseError: "unknown variant \`noSuchVerb\`, expected one of \`hello\`, \`helloAck\`, ...`"` (truncated at ~200 chars as designed; 4KB requestId did not leak into error text). B4-B6 (embedded newline/CR, NUL-byte, backslash escapes in requestId) all round-tripped as `parsed` (serde accepts JSON escape sequences). **Invariant held**: error-truncation at 200 chars works; all 3 parseOutcome values observable in a single run.
  - **Phase C** (5× same-command concurrent): 5/5 `parsed + commandType:"getState"`. No observable mis-correlation because all sent same commandType.
  - **Phase D** (composition): all 6 steps produced expected envelopes. D2 post-`triggerBuiltin emojiPicker` getState confirmed surface flipped to `promptType:"emojiPicker" choiceCount:296`. D6 `--timeout 1` (1 ms) still returned `parsed` (parse event emitted sub-millisecond after FIFO write).
  - **Phase E** (timeout abuse): E1 `--timeout 0` → `parsed` (polling loop checks deadline lazily, gets at least one iteration). E3 `99999999` → `parsed`. E4 no flag → `parsed` (default 2000 works). E5 double-flag (`--timeout 100 --timeout 5000`) → `parsed` (last wins). E6 `--timeout -1` → `parsed`. **E2 `--timeout abc` → ANOMALY #1**: empty stdout, stderr shows `scripts/agentic/session.sh: line 359: abc: unbound variable`. The function terminated on bash `set -u` at `local deadline_ms=$(( now_ms + timeout_ms ))` before `json_envelope` could run. See `[?] session-send-await-parse-timeout-non-numeric`.
  - **Phase F** (concurrent mixed-command — the critical test): 5 concurrent sends with commandTypes `getState, hide, show, getState, hide`. All 5 receipts report `commandType:"show"` — same value, not matching 4/5 of the actual sends. **ANOMALY #2**: the offset-correlation protocol has an implicit single-caller precondition. All 5 sends race to `wc -c < app.log` before any writes to FIFO; they latch onto the same pre-send offset; each independently `grep -m1 'event_type=stdin_command_parsed'` finds the SAME first event. Four of five receipts are misattributed. See `[?] session-send-await-parse-concurrent-cross-correlation`.
  - **Final state** (`p8-poststate`): `promptType:"none" choiceCount:461 visibleChoiceCount:476 selectedValue:"Bluetooth Settings" windowVisible:false healthy:true`. App survived 52 probe actions with no panics, no new ERROR lines, no session disconnect — same ScriptList delta (476 > 461) still present because the running binary predates Pass #5's fix.
- **Falsifier**: if the concurrent cross-correlation finding were not real, a replay against HEAD running `for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do (bash scripts/agentic/session.sh send default "{...distinct commandType per i...}" --await-parse --timeout 3000 > f-$i.out) & done; wait` would yield 5 receipts with 3+ distinct `commandType` values. This pass observed 5/5 identical `commandType:"show"`. If the `--timeout abc` crash were not real, the E2 envelope would contain a JSON object; instead it contained zero bytes with a stderr `set -u` error.
- **Refactor threat** (N/A — no pin landed this pass).
- **Pin-cap window**: unchanged. Last 20 commits: 0 Pin commits (attacker pass uses `Prompt: Reproduce …`). Well under 7 hard-lockout.
- **Bug-yield floor**: **satisfied**. Attacker `Reproduce` commits do NOT count toward Fix/Add/Extend per scope §"Subject verb discipline", but this pass's 5-commit window (counting back from this commit) still contains Pass #7's `Prompt: Add --await-parse …` → floor 1/5 satisfied. Next 5-commit window (post-this-commit) will also include Pass #7 Add + Pass #5 Fix — floor remains satisfied for the next ~3 passes.
- **Attacker escalation counter**: was 0 going into this pass (Run 5 Pass #4 filed 1 `[?]`). This pass files 2 `[?]` → counter remains 0. Next attacker pass (#12 Run 5) runs at normal composition, not escalated.
- **Notes**: Both anomalies are genuinely new — they're bugs *introduced by Pass #7* when the fix to the Pass #8-Run-4 silent-drop gap shipped. The session.sh `--await-parse` protocol closes one silent-drop symptom (parse failures invisible to automation) but introduces two new ones (non-numeric timeout crashes silently; concurrent sends silently cross-correlate). The irony is noteworthy for the follow-up pass: whoever picks the `[?] session-send-await-parse-concurrent-cross-correlation` story should prefer acceptance option (c) — extend the Rust `stdin_command_parsed` tracing event to include `request_id`, and `cmd_send`'s grep to match on that request_id — because options (a)/(b) (document precondition; add flock) don't close the race, they just move it into the caller's responsibility. The `tool-session-send-parse-receipt` story ([x] as of Pass #7) is not reopened because the shipped scope (serial single-caller usage with valid numeric timeout) still works as pinned; these are two new edge stories filed alongside. Live-verify of the 3 RPCs shipped Pass #1/#2/#3 remains deferred (same pid 11474, pre-fix). Next pass (#9) is non-attacker — candidate: fix the `--timeout abc` crash (small shell change, shippable in one pass).
