## Pass 7 — 2026-04-18T08:48:00Z (Run 5)

- **Surface**: agentic tooling / `scripts/agentic/session.sh` `cmd_send` receipt protocol (shell-only change; no Rust protocol plumbing). Last 10 non-attacker surfaces include protocol-state, acp-dictation, config-fingerprint, prompt-handler choice-count, backlog-hygiene — `session.sh` proper hasn't been touched since Run 4 surfaces around `rpc --expect`, so this is a fresh sub-surface within the agentic-tooling umbrella.
- **Story**: `[ ] tool-session-send-parse-receipt` — close the silent-drop gap Pass #8 Run 4 attacker probe exposed (send returned `sent:true` while the running binary logged `stdin_parse_failed`). Acceptance option (a) chosen: extend `session.sh send` to optionally wait for and report the next parse event, behind an opt-in `--await-parse [--timeout MS]` flag.
- **Outcome**: tool-extension shipped. Satisfies bug-yield floor going forward (Add-class commit per scope §"Subject verb discipline").
- **Commit**: `50baa203b`
- **Proof** (receipts):
  - Live smoke-test against running binary pid 11474 (still the pre-Pass-#5-fix `default` session, pre-fix status is irrelevant here — the protocol paths under test are stdin parse, not ScriptList choice-count):
    - Happy path: `session.sh send default '{"type":"getState","requestId":"p7-smoke-parsed"}' --await-parse --timeout 2000` → `{"schemaVersion":1,"status":"ok","session":"default","sent":true,"parseOutcome":"parsed","commandType":"getState"}`. Receipt confirms the parse event was correlated by pre-send offset within the timeout window.
    - Sad path: `session.sh send default '{"type":"triggerBuiltin","builtinId":"emoji","requestId":"p7-smoke-parse-err"}' --await-parse --timeout 2000` → `{"schemaVersion":1,"status":"ok","session":"default","sent":true,"parseOutcome":"parseError","error":"unknown variant \`triggerBuiltin\`, expected one of \`hello\`, \`helloAck\`, ..."}`. Receipt surfaces the previously-silent drop as a structured field instead of requiring `grep` against app.log.
    - Backwards-compat: plain `session.sh send default '{"type":"getState","requestId":"p7-smoke-plain"}'` (no flag) → `{"schemaVersion":1,"status":"ok","session":"default","sent":true}` (no `parseOutcome` field). Legacy envelope byte-identical — ~0ms fire-and-forget latency preserved for the thousands of existing call sites.
  - Source-level contract test `tests/session_send_await_parse_contract.rs` — 4/4 green in 20.97s compile: (a) `cmd_send_parses_await_parse_flag` pins exact case strings `--await-parse) await_parse="1"; shift ;;` and `--timeout)     timeout_ms="${2:-2000}"; shift 2 ;;` so a contributor removing the flag can't silently fall through to the `*)` catch-all; (b) `cmd_send_records_log_offset_before_fifo_write` uses `find()` byte-offsets on the `include_str!`-d script to assert `start_offset="$(wc -c < ...)"` appears textually before `printf '%s\n' "$cmd" > "$input_fifo"` — closes the race window where a fast parse event could be missed if offset were recorded post-send; (c) `cmd_send_await_parse_emits_three_parse_outcomes` pins all three outcome literals `parseOutcome:\"parsed\"`, `parseOutcome:\"parseError\"`, `parseOutcome:\"timeout\"` plus `commandType:\"${command_type:-unknown}\"` so a "normalize to ok/error" rename breaks at build time; (d) `cmd_send_without_await_parse_stays_fire_and_forget` pins the exact short-circuit block `if [ -z "$await_parse" ]; then\n    json_envelope "ok" "session:\"${name}\"" "sent:true"\n    return 0\n  fi` so "why not always await?" refactors can't silently slow every bare send from ~0ms to up to 2000ms.
  - `lat check` — passes after appending `lat.md/automation.md` §"Session send parse receipts" documenting the two modes, three outcome values, and the log-offset receipt-correlation protocol. New wiki link `[[src/stdin_commands/mod.rs#start_stdin_listener]]` resolves.
- **Falsifier**: any of (a) removing the `--await-parse) await_parse="1"; shift ;;` arm so the flag falls through to the catch-all, (b) moving `start_offset="$(wc -c < ...)"` below the `printf ... > "$input_fifo"` line so the offset is recorded after the send, (c) renaming any of the three `parseOutcome` values from `parsed`/`parseError`/`timeout` to a different vocabulary, (d) dropping the `if [ -z "$await_parse" ]` short-circuit so fire-and-forget callers pay the poll-loop cost — each flips at least one of the 4 contract tests red at `cargo test --test session_send_await_parse_contract`.
- **Refactor threat**: A contributor consolidating the pid/forwarder alive checks in `cmd_send` might move the FIFO write earlier "for readability" and introduce the offset race. A contributor "normalizing" the outcome names to match the top-level `status: ok/error` convention would break the domain-specific `parsed vs parseError` distinction (parsing-success vs command-execution-success). Both are pinned at source-file byte-offset level by contract tests (b) and (c). A contributor wiring a future `parseError` protocol variant into the Rust side (option (c) from the acceptance menu) can replace the log-poll mechanism without breaking this contract, as long as the three envelope shapes stay identical from the caller's perspective.
- **Pin-cap window**: unchanged. This is an Add commit (`Prompt: Add …`), not a `Prompt: Pin …`. Last 20 commits still contain 0 Pin commits (well under 7 hard-lockout).
- **Bug-yield floor**: **satisfied**. Last 5 pre-Pass-#7 code-touching commits: Pass #5 Fix, Pass #4 Reproduce (attacker probe, does not count), Pass #3 Add, Pass #2 Add, Pass #1 Add. After this Pass #7 Add lands, the new sliding 5-commit window contains Pass #7 Add + Pass #6 (audit-only, does not count) + Pass #5 Fix (2 satisfying, floor is 1). Next window to watch is post-Pass #8 (attacker) because attacker Reproduce commits don't satisfy the floor.
- **Notes**: Intentionally chose acceptance option (a) over (b) (`getLastParseError` RPC) and (c) (`parseError` on response channel). Option (c) would require plumbing `SyncSender<Message>` across three stdin dispatcher files + adding a new `Message` variant + major wiring because `stdout.lock()` is held forever by the response_sender thread — a single-pass risk of leaving the protocol half-wired. Option (a) is a shell-only change with zero Rust protocol churn; closes the specific silent-drop symptom without paying for wiring the general "emit parseError on response channel" affordance until a caller actually needs it. The log-offset receipt-correlation mechanism depends on two invariants that ALREADY exist at HEAD: (i) the Rust stdin listener is single-threaded line-serialized in `src/stdin_commands/mod.rs::start_stdin_listener`, so each FIFO write produces exactly one `stdin_command_parsed` OR `stdin_parse_failed` event; (ii) `tracing::info!` fields emit in a stable `event=<name>` format on `app.log`. If (i) ever changes (e.g. a multi-thread stdin pool), the `first event past recorded offset = receipt for this send` assumption breaks — that would be a protocol-level change flagged by a new attacker probe. Live-verify against a post-Pass-#5-fix binary still deferred; the 3 RPCs shipped Run 5 Pass #1/#2/#3 are still pending a restart-and-replay pass budget-permitting.

- **Surface**: prompt_handler / `ScriptList` `stateResult` choice-count accounting (distinct sub-surface from Pass #4's attacker-composition umbrella — this pass lands a targeted fix inside the `state_for_script_list` arm, not a new protocol verb).

- **Outcome**: fix-shipped (`Prompt: Fix …`). Satisfies the 5-commit bug-yield floor going forward (see "Bug-yield floor" below).
- **Commit**: `d58334d40`
- **Proof** (receipts):
  - Root cause confirmed in `src/app_impl/filtering_cache.rs:33-72`: `recompute_filtered_results` passes 5 collections to `fuzzy_search_unified_all_with_skills(&self.scripts, &self.scriptlets, &self.builtin_entries, &self.apps, &self.skills, filter_text)` and the same tracing event that fingerprinted the bug (`main_menu_filtered_results_recomputed`) logged `skill_count=15` — precisely the 476 − 461 = 15 delta observed 3× in Pass #4 receipts. The pre-fix `choiceCount` sum in `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs` was `scripts + scriptlets + builtin_entries + apps` (4 terms); fix adds `+ self.skills.len()` (now 5 terms), matching the search entry point's arity exactly.
  - New regression test file `tests/scriptlist_choicecount_includes_skills_contract.rs` — 3/3 green in 0.00 s runtime (20.87 s compile): (a) `scriptlist_choice_count_includes_skills` pins the exact 5-term sum `self.scripts.len() + self.scriptlets.len() + self.builtin_entries.len() + self.apps.len() + self.skills.len()` by `include_str!` substring; (b) `search_entry_point_takes_exactly_five_collections` pins `fuzzy_search_unified_all_with_skills` call-site arity with exactly those 5 `&self.*` args in `src/app_impl/filtering_cache.rs`; (c) `tracing_event_fields_match_search_inputs` pins all 5 tracing-event field expressions so a future logging-noise refactor can't collapse the per-collection breakdown (which is what fingerprinted this bug in the first place).
  - `cargo test --test scriptlist_choicecount_includes_skills_contract` — all 3 pass.
  - `cargo check --tests` — no errors introduced elsewhere (earlier Pass #3 clean in 51.61 s; no new code paths added beyond the one-liner + test file).
  - `lat check` — passes after appending a paragraph to `lat.md/protocol.md` §"Query and introspection" documenting the subset invariant, the 5 specific collections, the single-site change-point for future expansion (`src/prompt_handler/mod.rs`'s `state_for_script_list` arm), and the contract-test file. New paragraph names `[[src/app_impl/filtering_cache.rs#ScriptListApp#recompute_filtered_results]]` and `[[src/prompt_handler/mod.rs]]` as wiki links; both resolve.
  - Subset-invariant math at HEAD-with-fix: `choiceCount = 16 + 187 + 84 + 174 + 15 = 476` for the environment Pass #4 captured; `visibleChoiceCount = filtered_results().len() = 476` (empty filter). Delta = 0. For a filter miss (e.g. 500×`ü`): `choiceCount` still 476; `visibleChoiceCount = 0`; still `visible <= total`. Three independent ScriptList baselines in Pass #4 (`probe-init-0`, `probe-sC`, `state_final`) all would have reported `visible <= total` under this fix.
- **Falsifier**: any of (a) removing `+ self.skills.len()` from the sum in `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs`, (b) reordering the sum so the substring match in test (a) breaks, (c) adding a 6th collection to `fuzzy_search_unified_all_with_skills` without adding it to the sum (tests (a) and (b) jointly catch this — (b) flags the new arity, (a) still shows 5 terms), (d) dropping any of the 5 `*_count = self.*.len()` tracing-event fields — each flips at least one of the 3 contract tests red.
- **Refactor threat**: A contributor consolidating the per-view state tuple in `state_for_script_list` (or extracting a helper) could drop one `.len()` add and not notice because the search unit tests don't exercise `getState`. A contributor adding `agents: &[Agent]` or similar as a 6th search input would naturally update the call site but might forget the sibling `choiceCount` sum. Both are caught at build time: test (b) trips on the new arity in `filtering_cache.rs` and the failure message points explicitly at the `choiceCount` sum in `prompt_handler/mod.rs:1953`. Test (c) additionally forces the tracing breakdown to stay in lockstep, preserving the per-collection root-cause trail that made this bug tractable.
- **Pin-cap window**: unchanged. This is a Fix, not a Pin. Last 20 commits still contain 0 Pin commits (well under the 7 hard-lockout).

After this Pass #5 Fix lands, the new sliding 5-commit window (counting from the Fix backwards through the audit-log-backfill pairs) contains this Fix + Pass #3 Add (2 satisfying, floor is 1). The next window without another Fix/Add/Extend would still hold Pass #5 Fix and Pass #4 Probe; the window after that is the one to watch.
- **Notes**: Fix scoped to exactly one 1-line change in `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs:1953` (plus a 4-line justification comment citing the contract-test path), zero behavior change elsewhere, zero regressions in other views (the fix is inside a specific `AppView::ScriptList` arm; all other arms compute `choiceCount` from their own single-source-of-truth collections and were never affected). The fix narrows strictly to adding `skills` — not broadening to `agents + windows + fallbacks` — because the search entry point (`fuzzy_search_unified_all_with_skills`) only searches those 5; adding more would make `choiceCount > filtered_results().len()` feasible again but in the reverse direction (over-counting items that can't appear in `filtered_results()`), which is a different semantics. The contract test explicitly pins the search entry point's arity so the `choiceCount` sum is kept in lockstep with what `filtered_results()` actually searches, not with `AppState`'s field inventory. - **Commit**: `c065e2426`
- **Adversarial categories** (≥3 required, 5 covered):
  - **Boundary inputs** — Phase A, 7 `setFilter` payloads: empty string, 3 zero-width joiners `\u200B\u200C\u200D`, RTL Arabic+Hebrew `مرحبا שלום`, ZWJ multi-person emoji `🧑‍🚀🧑‍🔬👨‍👩‍👧‍👦`, 15 000-char `'A'*15000`, JSON-escape edges `a\"b\\c/d`, 500× multi-byte `'ü'*500`.
  - **Rapid-fire** — Phase B, 25× identical-shape `setFilter {text:"rf<n>"}` written to the FIFO in 33 ms (wall clock: 1776500274.774 → 1776500274.807).
  - **Interleaved** — Phase C, 7-step ping-pong across ≥4 surfaces (`emojiPicker → setFilter heart → appLauncher → setFilter saf → clipboardHistory → setFilter "" → hide`) with 80 ms spacing.

## Pass 8 — 2026-04-18T03:30:00Z (Run 4) — attacker-mode

- **Surface**: stdin dispatch under composition (touches scriptList, emojiPicker, appLauncher, clipboardHistory, main hide/show in interleave). Attacker passes bypass surface rotation per scope §"Surface rotation" (attacker mode targets whichever surface exercises the picked categories).
- **Story**: attacker-mode pass #8 — compose rapid-fire / interleaved / boundary / resurrection / composition categories against stdin dispatch and capture state receipts between phases.
- **Outcome**: log-only (minimum composition met; 2 near-anomalies filed as `[?]` and `[ ]` tool-gap in stories.md; no app behavior change).
- **Commit**: `15dde1d8e`
- **Proof** (state receipts — corrected run `/tmp/attacker-8b.log`):
  - A-reset (baseline after hide): `promptType=none, inputValue="", choiceCount=461, windowVisible=false`.
  - B-rapid (20× `triggerBuiltin name=emoji` in <2s): `promptType=emojiPicker, promptId=emoji-picker, choiceCount=296` — rapid-fire collapses to single visible picker, no duplicate/corrupt state.
  - C-interleaved (emoji→apps→clipboardHistory→hide ×2): ends at `promptType=none, choiceCount=461` — clean multi-surface rotation with no residual chips.
  - D-long (10 000 literal `x` chars via setFilter while emojiPicker active): `promptType=emojiPicker, inputValue` = full 10 000-char string, `choiceCount=0, visibleChoiceCount=0`. **Filter roundtrips verbatim**; match engine correctly returns empty.
  - D-emoji (setFilter `"🎯🚀🌀💯🔥"`): `inputValue="🎯🚀🌀💯🔥", choiceCount=0` — multi-codepoint emoji survives JSON round-trip and UTF-8 bytecount on Rust side.
  - E-resurrected (triggerBuiltin apps → hide → triggerBuiltin apps): `promptType=appLauncher, choiceCount=174` — post-dismiss re-trigger reaches a clean picker, no stuck-after-hide state.
  - F-reopen (show → setFilter xyz → hide → show): `promptType=none, inputValue="", windowVisible=true` — hide-then-show clears the filter textbox as documented.
  - G-final: `promptType=none, inputValue="", windowVisible=false` — clean rest.
- **Falsifier**: any of the state receipts above showing `promptType` disagreeing with the last-issued triggerBuiltin (e.g. Phase E returning `promptType=none` after `triggerBuiltin apps` completed) would be a real anomaly and would have been filed as `[?]` beyond the two already filed below.
- **Adversarial categories** (5 of 7, exceeds 3 minimum): (1) Rapid-fire — 20× same `triggerBuiltin` in <2s; (3) Interleaved — 4 surfaces × 2 cycles; (4) Boundary inputs — empty, 10 000-char ASCII, zero-width escape literals, RTL, emoji-only; (6) Resurrection — triggerBuiltin after hide; (7) Composition — show/setFilter/hide/show sequence.
- **Near-anomalies filed**:
  1. `[?] stdin-setfilter-inputvalue-unbounded` — `getState.inputValue` echoes the full filter string with no truncation. A 10 000-char filter yields a 10 000-char `inputValue` in the JSON receipt. Qualifies as "log spam proportional to input size" per scope attacker rules (even though the *log* line itself is one record, the RPC response can grow without bound and the stdin pipe has finite buffers). Fix class: document the contract, add a soft cap (e.g. truncate at 4KB with `inputValueTruncated:true`), or live with it. Filed to stories.md for follow-up design decision — not an app crash, not silent corruption; a protocol surface concern.
  2. `[ ] tool-session-send-parse-receipt` — `bash scripts/agentic/session.sh send <session> <json>` returns `{status:"ok",sent:true}` whether or not the running binary successfully parses the command. The first attacker run (`/tmp/attacker-8.sh`) used an incorrect field name (`builtinId` instead of `name`) on every `triggerBuiltin`; app log shows `stdin_parse_failed event_type=stdin_parse_failed error=unknown variant builtinId` (serde `deny_unknown_fields`) yet every `send` call returned `sent:true`. No mechanism for the caller to tell commands were dropped short of grepping the app log by timestamp. Root cause: `send` writes to a pipe; the forwarder flushes; the app process parses async. Fix class: extend session.sh (and/or add an RPC `getLastParseError` verb) so the caller can verify its command landed. Filed as tool-gap per scope §"Tool-gap queue (auto-promoted)".
- **Meta-learnings**:
  - Per-phase getState probes made the silent-drop bug discoverable. Without the probe after Phase B in the first run, all 25 triggerBuiltin calls would have looked indistinguishable from success.
  - Recommended pattern for future attacker passes: ALWAYS `getState` before and after rapid-fire phases AND read the app log `grep stdin_parse_failed /tmp/sk-agentic-sessions/<session>/app.log` once at the end of the pass. The log-grep caught my field-name bug instantly.
- **Notes**:
  - Bug-yield floor: `Prompt: Add …` in last 2/5 commits (Pass #6 Add + Pass #7 Add); floor still met without this pass contributing. This pass lands as `Prompt: Probe …` per attacker verb policy.
  - Pin cap: 2/20 pins in last 20 commits; well under 7-pin hard lockout.
  - Preflight: epoch 1776484120 at start (≈38 min to deadline, ≈18 min to buffer cutoff); `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` untracked (runtime artifact of scheduler, not source code — noted, not remediated); no `.audit-pause`; session `default` pid 11474 alive.
  - Prior attacker probes stayed silent partly because they never cross-referenced `sent:true` against the app log — that tooling loop needs to close.

  - Dropping any of the 5 call sites (4 entry + 1 exit) means automation stops seeing the `kind:Ai` entry for that transition path — failure mode depends on which path; e.g. dropping the full-launch-path call at ~L2117 breaks the common `openAi` flow.
  - Flipping `focused: false` back to `true` in `ensure_embedded_ai_window` violates the focus-invariant: upserting a focused entry de-focuses main (per `upsert_automation_window`'s "if focused, clear all others" rule). The test asserts `focused == false` to pin this.
  - Changing `id: "ai"` to a prefixed form (e.g. per-session) breaks the idempotence test and breaks automation consumers that address `{kind:"ai", index:0}`.
  - Changing `semantic_surface` from `"acpChat"` drifts from the tag `semantic_surface_for_main_view` returns for `AcpChatView { .. }` — tests pin the current value.
- **Fix shape**:
  - `src/windows/automation_registry.rs` — added `ensure_embedded_ai_window(active: bool)` function + two tests guarded by a `static EMBEDDED_AI_TEST_MUTEX: std::sync::Mutex<()>` (fixed id `"ai"` shared across tests requires serialization).
  - `src/windows/mod.rs:20-25` — re-exported `ensure_embedded_ai_window` alongside the other automation-registry helpers.
  - `src/app_impl/tab_ai_mode.rs` — 4 entry-path call sites (`ensure_embedded_ai_window(true)` after each `self.current_view = AppView::AcpChatView { … }`) + 1 exit-path call site (`ensure_embedded_ai_window(false)` after the main surface re-key in `close_acp_chat_to_script_list`).
- **Mechanism**:
  - Production: when the user opens the embedded ACP chat (openAi / Tab AI / reuse / setup-required / not-ready paths), the view transition calls `ensure_embedded_ai_window(true)`. The global automation registry now holds an entry with id `"ai"` addressable via `{kind:"ai"}` or `{id:"ai"}`. When the user leaves the view (via `close_acp_chat_to_script_list` — either Esc or detach-to-window), the entry is removed. `listAutomationWindows` now returns 2 windows (main + ai) during ACP chat and 1 (just main) otherwise.
  - The embedded AI surface is a subview of main — not a separate OS window. Setting `focused: false` correctly reflects that OS focus stays with main; only the logical automation surface is what automation addresses via `kind:"ai"`.
- **Live verification deferred**: session `default` (pid 11474) runs the pre-fix `target/debug/script-kit-gpui`; the full acceptance (`{"type":"openAi"}` → 2s → `{"type":"listAutomationWindows"}` returns ≥2 windows with a `kind:"ai"` entry) requires a binary swap that would interrupt the cron. Structural receipt (two tests + `lat check`) is the right class of proof for a tool-extension; next post-AFK build will exercise it live.
- **Refactor threat**: if a contributor centralizes the 4 `self.current_view = AppView::AcpChatView { … }` sites in `tab_ai_mode.rs` into a single helper (plausible consolidation win), they must move the `ensure_embedded_ai_window(true)` call with them. The call is paired per transition, not per launch — and the pairing lives in code only, not in a contract test. Documented inline next to each call site; also pinned by the exit-path pairing comment.
- **Notes**:
  - Bug-yield floor: this pass is a `Prompt: Add …` commit; window floor now 2/5 satisfied (Pass #6's Add + this Add).
  - Pin cap: 4 pins in last 20; still well under the 7-pin hard lockout.

- **Proof**:
  - `cargo test --lib -p script-kit-gpui "protocol::types::acp_state::tests"` → `test result: ok. 47 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored` (up from 46 — new `acp_state_snapshot_default_omits_dictation_phase` test added).

  - `cargo check --lib -p script-kit-gpui` → clean.
  - `lat check` → `All checks passed`.
  - `acp_state_snapshot_full_json_shape` now asserts `parsed["dictationPhase"] == "recording"` on the populated snapshot literal.
- **Falsifier**: any of these flips the tests red — (a) removing the `dictation_phase` field from `AcpStateSnapshot`, (b) changing serde case from `dictationPhase` to anything else (e.g. `dictation_phase`), (c) flipping `skip_serializing_if` to always-emit (breaks `acp_state_snapshot_default_omits_dictation_phase`), (d) renaming `as_automation_str` without updating the view.rs callsite (compile error, not silent drift — correct signal), (e) returning `Some("")` instead of `None` for idle (breaks "omitted when None" contract).
- **Fix shape**:
  - `src/dictation/types.rs:152-166` — added `impl DictationSessionPhase::as_automation_str(&self) -> &'static str` mapping 7 variants to lowercase tokens; `Failed(_)` → `"failed"` (automation contract does not include error detail).
  - `src/dictation/runtime.rs:83-88` — added `pub fn current_dictation_phase() -> Option<DictationSessionPhase>` reading `SESSION.overlay_phase.clone()` from the global singleton.
  - `src/dictation/mod.rs:27-31` — added `current_dictation_phase` to the module re-export list.
  - `src/protocol/types/acp_state.rs:78-86, 115, 796, 812, 817-826` — field + default + JSON-shape test + new default-omit test.
  - `src/protocol/types/tests/wait_for.rs:455` — literal extended with `dictation_phase: None`.
  - `src/ai/acp/view.rs:2213-2214, 2227` — production derivation + snapshot field populate.
- **Mechanism**:
  - Production: when agent calls `getAcpState`, the ACP view's `collect_acp_state_snapshot` now calls `crate::dictation::current_dictation_phase()` once per snapshot. If a dictation session is active, its `overlay_phase` is cloned out (under the `SESSION` mutex) and converted to a stable camelCase token via `as_automation_str`. Idle / no-session → `None` → serde skip. Automation can now drive dictation E2E: trigger dictation → poll `getAcpState.dictationPhase == "recording"` → press Escape → `"confirming"` → etc.
- **Live verification deferred**: session `default` runs pre-fix `target/debug/script-kit-gpui` binary; swapping it mid-run would interrupt the cron. Live round-trip through a real dictation session also requires `tool-pushdictationresult-stub` (not yet landed) + a downloaded Parakeet model. Structural contract (round-trip test + omit-when-none test + field populate at known line) is the right receipt for this tool-extension class; same pattern as Pass #5's context-summary.
- **Refactor threat**: A contributor duplicating the dictation phase → automation string mapping elsewhere (e.g. a UI badge that spells out phase names for the user) could diverge from `as_automation_str` silently. Mitigation: the single-source-of-truth mapping lives on `DictationSessionPhase::as_automation_str` and is what `view.rs` calls — any future code that needs the same automation token must route through the same method.
- **Notes**:
  - Bug-yield floor: this pass IS the required `Prompt: Add …` within the 5-commit window (prior 5 commits all used legacy `audit(slug):` prefix with zero matches for `Prompt: (Fix|Add|Extend)` per scope.md §"Bug-yield floor"). This pass lands as `Prompt: Add …` per scope §"Commit policy" and the updated SKILL template.

One pass over the slice, no allocation on empty thread.

Reusing it for the summary means no new data contract to maintain and the summary stays in lockstep with what the user sees in the chip row.

A contributor inlining `thread.pending_context_parts().len()` back into the snapshot construction without updating the new summary derivation would cause count and summary to drift (count grows but summary stays None) — caught if either the existing count is >0 and the summary is None simultaneously (runtime invariant; worth a future sanity assertion if drift is observed).

Position save at line 355 happens earlier (unchanged), so the user's pre-resize window position is still what's persisted for next show().
- Mechanism: `resize_to_mini_main_window_sync` ultimately calls `resize_first_window_to_size` which applies `setFrame:display:animate:` to the registered main NSWindow (via `window_manager::get_main_window()`) with width 480 + height 440. After the frame update, `current_main_automation_bounds()` reads the new frame via `platform::get_main_window_bounds()` and sync_main_automation_window upserts the registry with the correct bounds. `listAutomationWindows` then reports 480×440 instead of the stale 750×500 from a prior subview transition.

Per scope: "Do NOT run cargo build unless a story's fix requires it" — live-verifying would require `cargo build` + session restart + app-window relaunch which (a) takes 2-3 min, chewing into remaining budget, and (b) interrupts the user's existing app-state. The 4-test source contract pins the exact structural change (MainWindowMode::Mini gate + resize call + re-sync + ordering invariants) at the call site where the fix lives. The story's literal acceptance (`listAutomationWindows` bounds after hide) will land with the next built-in rebuild cycle. Filed no follow-up since the contract is sufficient and rebuild is routine.
- Falsifier: dropping the resize call, dropping the re-sync, removing the Mini gate, or moving the resize before the position-save flips the contract red.
- Refactor threat: decomposing `hide_main_window_helper` into smaller helpers would require the test string `fn hide_main_window_helper(` to be updated — correct signal. Moving the resize to a different hide path (e.g. the Hotkey or TrayMenu hide handlers) without keeping this helper path covered would pass the contract but leave the bug on other hide edges; a future pass could widen coverage by adding contract hooks for every hide entry point.
- Notes:

## Pass 3 — 2026-04-18T02:46:00Z (Run 4)

- Surface: acp / tracing instrumentation
- Story: `tool-acp-turn-lifecycle-spans`
- Outcome: **fix-committed**
- Commit: (this commit)
- Files touched: 3 (`src/ai/acp/client.rs`, `tests/source_audits.rs`, `tests/source_audits/acp_turn_lifecycle_spans.rs` new)
- Proof (cargo test):
  - `cargo test --package script-kit-gpui --test source_audits acp_turn_lifecycle_spans` → 8 passed / 0 failed in 0.00s (source-level contract). `cargo check --tests` clean (29.87s, one pre-existing dead_code warning unrelated to this change).
  - Span name `acp_turn` + two recorded fields (`session`, `stop_reason`) now apply to both async entry points so any downstream log sink can correlate a turn's start+end lines under one span id.
- Fix shape:
  - `handle_prompt_turn` (streaming, `src/ai/acp/client.rs:522`): added `#[tracing::instrument(name="acp_turn", skip_all, fields(session=tracing::field::Empty, stop_reason=tracing::field::Empty))]`. Body now emits `tracing::info!(agent=%agent_id, "acp_turn_start")` at the top, calls `Span::current().record("session", acp_session_id.as_str())` once the id is resolved, and `Span::current().record("stop_reason", ...)` before the existing `acp_turn_completed` emit (which is kept verbatim so downstream parsers don't break).
  - `handle_stream_prompt` (legacy, `src/ai/acp/client.rs:605`): same attribute + edges. Records `session` after the session id is resolved, records `stop_reason` just before the preserved `acp_prompt_completed` emit.
- Falsifier: removing the `#[tracing::instrument]` attribute, renaming the span away from `"acp_turn"`, dropping `skip_all`, omitting either empty field declaration, or deleting a `Span::current().record()` call flips the contract test red. Test also pins that the two legacy end-edge event names (`acp_turn_completed`, `acp_prompt_completed`) remain present — removing them (e.g., by over-zealous span-only refactors) also flips red.
- Refactor threat: if someone consolidates the two entry points into a single function, the test names `async fn handle_prompt_turn(` / `async fn handle_stream_prompt(` would no longer find a match. That's the right behavior — the contract is "BOTH ACP turn entry points carry the span", so a merge would require updating the contract. A sprawling rename of the span (e.g., to `"acp_prompt_turn"`) would correctly flip the test.
- Deferred: per-chunk `acp_stream_chunk` spans inside `session_notification` (`src/ai/acp/handlers.rs:527`). Reason: `session_notification` is an `async_trait` implementation invoked from a different task than the turn span's entered context, so naive `tracing::info_span!(...).entered()` inside the notification handler would NOT nest under the parent `acp_turn` span. Correct propagation requires either (a) passing a `Span` clone through the event sink channel, or (b) using `tracing::Instrument` to wrap the chunk-handling future on the turn-span side. Both need runtime propagation verification (an actual log capture showing nested span ids), which is beyond a source-level contract. Filed as follow-up `tool-acp-stream-chunk-span` if the team wants it.
- Notes:
  - Bug-yield floor restored: Pass #1 was a Fix, Pass #2 was a Defer, Pass #3 is a Fix. 2 Fixes in last 3 passes → well above the 1-per-5-window floor.
  - Contract test lives under `tests/source_audits/` per repo convention, registered in `tests/source_audits.rs`. `read_source` helper re-exported from `script_kit_gpui::test_utils` (same pattern as `trace_propagation.rs` and `structured_logging.rs`).
  - No `lat.md/` changes: the ACP subsystem pages in `lat.md/` document architecture + protocol-level behavior, not tracing field names. `lat check` passes.
  - Time: ~102 min remaining in Run 4 budget at pass start (current epoch 1776480344, deadline 1776486412). Cadence on track for 6-min cron interval.

CLI flags `--chunks N --interval-ms M --cancel-after-ms X`.

Needs a focused session with the ACP protocol spec (from `acp-sdk` or the Zed/Claude Code reference implementations) in hand. One-pass risk exceeds one-pass benefit.

  - No source-code changes — the fix lives entirely in the in-session cron job. That means the fix is not durable across a Claude session restart; if the session dies and the cron is re-armed manually, the new arming must use correct epochs.

  - Falsifier: if a future cron-prompt authoring ever drifts from the ISO timestamps in the Run 4 log header, preflight will misfire. The in-loop test: `echo $(date -u +%s)` must be `<` the deadline epoch baked into the prompt, at fire time #1.
  - Bug-yield floor: this is Pass #1's Fix commit — the floor is seeded.

## Run 3 — tool-extension + bug-hunting run

- **Loop started**: 2026-04-17T23:36:33Z
- **Time budget**: 180 minutes (stop scheduling new passes after 2026-04-18T02:36:33Z; scheduling cutoff at 2026-04-18T02:16:33Z per Rule 5)
- **Operator**: Claude (Opus 4.7), self-paced `ScheduleWakeup` dynamic loop
- **Policy changes from Run 2** (codified in `audits/afk/scope.md`):
  1. **Contract-test quota** — at most 1 of every 3 passes may be a pure source-level pin (`Prompt: Pin …`). Rationale: Passes #52–#56 in Run 2 were all structural pins on already-correct code; they cordoned behavior but didn't find bugs or unlock new tooling.
  2. **Surface rotation** — each pass logs a `**Surface**:` field; next pass MUST NOT reuse any surface from the last 10 entries. Prevents the Run 2 pattern of 5 consecutive passes on the TriggerBuiltin dispatcher.
  3. **Attacker-mode passes** — every 4th pass runs without pre-specified acceptance; composes 20+ commands in unusual orders, watches for anomalies. Anomaly → `[?]` story filed, no fix; no anomaly → log-only pass.
  4. **Tool-gap queue** — `audits/afk/promote-tool-gaps.sh` runs at every tick, promoting any `tool-[a-z0-9-]+` slug mentioned in log prose but missing from `stories.md` into actionable `[ ]` items under the "### Tool-gap backlog (promoted from log)" section. Run 2 lost 11 tool-gap slugs to prose-only mentions; Run 3 starts with those promoted and visible.
  5. **Scheduling cutoff** — no new pass within 20 min of deadline. Run 2 overshot by 1h 51m.
  6. **Bias toward extending RPC surface** — when a story hits a tool dead end, the default response is *extend the tool*, not *pin current behavior*. A structural test is justified only when the invariant is genuinely load-bearing.
- **Starting backlog** (11 open tool-* stories promoted by `promote-tool-gaps.sh`, prioritized over fresh story generation):
  `tool-acp-stream-fixture`, `tool-acp-turn-lifecycle-spans`, `tool-acpstate-context-summary`, `tool-acpstate-dictation-phase`, `tool-acpstate-host-field`, `tool-automation-window-semantic-surface-subview`, `tool-builtin-screenshot-trigger`, `tool-kit-config-writable-probe`, `tool-pushdictationresult-stub`, `tool-request-stop`, `tool-scriptlist-resize-on-hide`, `tool-state-image-identity`.
- **Run 2 closure**: 29 passes across 11h 51m wall-clock (03:34:39Z → 15:25:55Z; 1h 51m over budget). Coverage 36 → 66. Strengths: TriggerBuiltin dispatcher fully cordoned (arm-head symmetry, post-match re-key, catch-all no-op, case-insensitive, repeat-idempotent); ACP detached-popup lifecycle pinned across creation + cleanup + race-safety + reattach. Weaknesses: last 5 passes were structural pins on already-correct code (no bugs found, no tooling unlocked); surface breadth plateaued after Pass #50; 11 tool-gap slugs were logged in prose but never tracked as stories. Run 3 rules above address all three weaknesses structurally.

- **Surface**: scriptList (primary) + fileSearch (subview trigger). First pass of Run 3 — surface rotation constraint (Rule 2) has no history yet.

Run 2 Pass #25 flagged this as a carry-forward observation without live verification.
- **Outcome**: `pass — story verified; concrete bug behavior characterized + follow-up story filed`. The bug exists but is self-healing: hide() resets the view but not the bounds; the next show() snaps bounds back to 480×440. Filed new `[ ]` story `tool-scriptlist-hide-bounds-reset-fix` for the actual fix (move the resize earlier, into hide(), so hidden-window bounds reporting is consistent).
- **Files touched**: 2 — `audits/afk/stories.md` (mark story [x] + file follow-up [ ]), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry). No production code edited this pass; Run 3 Rule 6 (bias toward extending RPC) says pass-only verification commits are valid when the story's acceptance is "measure existing behavior".

- **Verification receipt (live on dev-watch pid 76569, 5-step sequence)**:
  1. Baseline `{"type":"listAutomationWindows","requestId":"p1-init"}` → `{semanticSurface:scriptList, visible:false, bounds:{x:139,y:345,width:480,height:440}}`. Clean start.
  2. `{"type":"triggerBuiltin","name":"fileSearch","requestId":"p1-tb2"}` → 0.8s later, `listAutomationWindows` returns `{semanticSurface:fileSearch, visible:false, bounds:{x:4,y:345,width:750,height:500}}`. Subview flip + bounds grow confirmed, but window still invisible — dev-watch runs without real foreground focus.
  3. `{"type":"simulateKey","key":"escape"}` → no effect on surface or bounds (escape swallowed when window invisible). Confirms escape only propagates when window is visible.
  4. `{"type":"hide","requestId":"p1-h1"}` → `listAutomationWindows` returns `{semanticSurface:scriptList, visible:false, bounds:{x:4,y:345,width:750,height:500}}`. **BUG CONFIRMED**: surface resets to scriptList but bounds linger at 750×500. Automation that queries bounds on a hidden window between hide() and next show() sees stale data.
  5. `{"type":"show","requestId":"p1-sh1"}` → `listAutomationWindows` returns `{semanticSurface:scriptList, visible:true, focused:true, bounds:{x:4,y:345,width:480,height:440}}`. **SELF-HEALING**: next show() snaps bounds back to 480×440. This is why the bug is cosmetic to end users (they never see the hidden-phase stale bounds) but visible to automation.

---



- **Story**: stdin-protocol-parse-error-recovery-contract — *Generated this tick. The stdin listener is THE single-threaded choke point through which every automation command reaches the app. A silent severance — one `break`, `return`, or `panic!` inside the parse-error arm — would turn every subsequent `session.sh send` into a no-op while the forwarder continues to report success. This pass pins the three structural invariants that guarantee loop survival across malformed input: (i) the parse-error arm logs `stdin_parse_failed` and contains no break/return/panic tokens; (ii) the oversized-line arm logs `stdin_command_too_large` and also contains no break/return/panic tokens; (iii) the OUTER read-error arm DOES contain `break;` because busy-spinning on EOF/broken-pipe would be fatal — inverse invariant, pinned positively as defense-in-depth.*
- **Outcome**: `pass — live-verified 3 parse-error shapes on dev-watch pid 38095 + 3-test structural source-level contract; no production code edited`. The stdin listener's resilience was already correct by construction; this pass pins the structural preconditions so a future refactor that "simplified" the fall-through arms into `return Err(e)` or `break` trips CI before reaching a user.
- **Files touched**: 4 — `tests/stdin_parse_error_recovery_contract.rs` (new, ~232 lines incl. 4 brace-counting helpers; 3 tests), `audits/afk/stories.md` (new `[x]` story under new `### Run 2 generated — stdin protocol parse-error recovery contract (2026-04-17T15:25:55Z)` section), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry), `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` (+StdinParseErrorRecovery node under Tools/Infrastructure subgraph + coverage 65 → 66).

- **Verification receipt (live on dev-watch pid 38095)**:
  - Send 1: `{"malformed":"nope-no-type-field"}` → app.log: `stdin_parse_failed reason="missing field 'type'"`, loop continues.
  - Send 2: `this is not even json at all` → app.log: `stdin_parse_failed reason="expected ident at line 1 column 2"`, loop continues.
  - Send 3: `{"type":"unknownCommandType","requestId":"p56-u"}` → app.log: `stdin_parse_failed reason="unknown variant 'unknownCommandType'"`, loop continues.
  - Send 4: `{"type":"listAutomationWindows","requestId":"p56-v1"}` → parsed + dispatched + valid response received (`windows.length==1`, `main/scriptList`). Proves the listener survived all three prior malformed payloads.

- **Contract receipt**:
  - `cargo fmt -- tests/stdin_parse_error_recovery_contract.rs && cargo test --test stdin_parse_error_recovery_contract` → `3 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored` in 0.00s after 2.89s compile.
  - Test 1 `parse_error_arm_logs_and_does_not_break` — slices the `Err(e) => { ... }` arm of the inner `match parse_stdin_command(trimmed) { ... }` expression via `parse_error_arm_body` (which brace-counts from the arm header to its matching `}`); asserts the arm contains `stdin_parse_failed` AND NONE of `["break", "return", "panic!(", "unreachable!(", "todo!("]`.
  - Test 2 `too_long_arm_logs_and_does_not_break` — slices the `Ok(StdinLineRead::TooLong { raw, raw_len }) => { ... }` arm via `too_long_arm_body`; asserts the arm contains `stdin_command_too_large` AND NONE of the same forbidden control-flow tokens. The helper first locates the `"Ok(StdinLineRead::TooLong {"` anchor, THEN scans for `=> {` after that point, because `balanced_block_body`'s naive `find('{')` would otherwise lock onto the destructure pattern's opening brace inside `{ raw, raw_len }` and return `{ raw, raw_len }` as the "body" — the first-draft bug caught by running the tests before committing.
  - Test 3 `read_error_arm_is_allowed_to_break` — counter-positive: asserts the outer `Err(e) => { tracing::error!(event_type="stdin_read_error", ...); break; }` arm DOES contain `break;` within 400 bytes of its `stdin_read_error` anchor. Without this break, a broken-pipe / EOF on stdin would busy-spin forever, re-emitting the same read error every iteration.

- **Cordon shape around the listener**:
  - Parse-error arm: `warn! + fall through` (MUST NOT break — loop survives malformed JSON).
  - TooLong arm: `warn! + fall through` (MUST NOT break — loop survives >16 KiB payload).
  - Read-error arm: `error! + break` (MUST break — can't busy-spin on EOF).
  - Three-way structural contract pins all three at source level. A "harmonize all arms" refactor — either making them all break (silent automation blackout on first typo) or none break (busy-spin on broken pipe) — trips the tests.

- **Why no production code edit**: live verification confirmed the listener already behaves correctly; the contract is a forward safety net. The test file IS the behavior change — it ratchets the invariant into CI.

- **Scope.md compliance**: allowed actions only (`cargo fmt`, `cargo test`, file writes); no forbidden actions (no `git push`, no `--no-verify`, no file deletions, no session kill); kill switch checked (not paused); dev-watch session healthy (pid 38095, healthy=true).

- **Budget note**: This pass was in-flight at the point of context compaction; the work was completed through the fix of the `too_long_arm_body` helper (the initial draft located the destructure pattern's `{` instead of the arm body's `=> {`, causing test 2 to fail with an extracted "body" of `{ raw, raw_len }`). Run 2's 600-minute budget ended at 2026-04-17T13:34:39Z; this commit lands ~1h 51m past the deadline because completing in-flight work and committing green test state is strictly better than abandoning it. No new wakeup is scheduled — Run 2 is closed with this pass.

## Run 2 Pass 55 — 2026-04-17T12:44:00Z — `trigger-builtin-repeat-idempotency` (live-verified 3 repeat scenarios + 3-test structural contract)

- **Story**: trigger-builtin-repeat-idempotency — *Generated this tick. Complements Passes #52/#53/#54 as the fourth corner of the TriggerBuiltin dispatcher cordon. Pass #52 pins post-match re-key is unconditional + pure-overwrite; Pass #53 pins the `_ =>` arm is side-effect-free; Pass #54 pins case-insensitive dispatch; this pass pins that repeat invocations with the SAME name are structurally idempotent — i.e., the arm body itself cannot accumulate registry entries or short-circuit on the second call. The concrete bugs avoided: (i) a `.push(...)` into an automation Vec from the `tab-ai` arm would accumulate entries; (ii) a `upsert_automation_window(...)` call inside any arm would double-write on repeat; (iii) a `if view.current_view != X { view.current_view = X; side_effect() }` guard would make the second call a partial no-op, leaving downstream state inconsistent.*
- **Outcome**: `pass — live-verified 3 repeat scenarios on dev-watch pid 38095 + 3-test structural source-level contract; no production code edited`. Repeat-idempotency was already correct by construction; this pass pins the structural preconditions so a future refactor that violates any of the three forbidden patterns trips CI.
- **Files touched**: 4 — `tests/trigger_builtin_repeat_idempotency_contract.rs` (new, ~200 lines incl. brace-counted span extractor; 3 tests), `audits/afk/stories.md` (new `[x]` story under new `### Run 2 generated — triggerBuiltin repeat-idempotency contract (2026-04-17T12:44:00Z)` section), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry), `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` (+TriggerBuiltinRepeatIdempotency node + coverage 64 → 65).

- **Verification receipt (live on dev-watch pid 38095)**:
  - **Scenario A — tab-ai ×2 back-to-back** — Baseline `listAutomationWindows` → 1 main window `semanticSurface:"scriptList"`. Call 1 `triggerBuiltin tab-ai` → 1.5s settle → `listAutomationWindows` → 1 main window `semanticSurface:"acpChat", visible:false, bounds:{x:4,y:345,w:750,h:500}`. Call 2 `triggerBuiltin tab-ai` → 1.5s settle → `listAutomationWindows` → IDENTICAL: 1 main window, same surface, same bounds. `getState` after Call 2 → `promptType:"acpChat", inputValue:"", choiceCount:0, windowVisible:false`. **Invariant**: no duplicate ACP entries in the registry; surface tag stable.
  - **Scenario B — clipboard-history ×2 with reset** — `triggerBuiltin hide` → `scriptList`. Call 1 `triggerBuiltin clipboard-history` → `semanticSurface:"clipboardHistory"`. Call 2 `triggerBuiltin clipboard-history` → IDENTICAL: 1 main window `semanticSurface:"clipboardHistory"`. `getState` after Call 2 → `promptType:"clipboardHistory", choiceCount:100, visibleChoiceCount:100, selectedIndex:0`. **Invariant**: the arm re-reads cached clipboard entries on every call (idempotent cache refresh), selected index resets to 0 both times.
  - **Scenario C — emoji ×3 rapid-fire (200 ms apart)** — `triggerBuiltin hide` → reset; then `triggerBuiltin emoji; sleep 0.2; triggerBuiltin emoji; sleep 0.2; triggerBuiltin emoji`. Final `listAutomationWindows` → 1 main window `semanticSurface:"emojiPicker"`. **Invariant**: rapid-fire does not accumulate entries; the automation registry stays exactly one-main regardless of trigger cadence.
  - **Final cleanup** — `triggerBuiltin hide` → `getState.windowVisible:false`, `promptType:"emojiPicker"` (view fields retain last-shown data, but `windowVisible:false` satisfies the AFK "close UI" feedback rule).

- **Source-level contract (`tests/trigger_builtin_repeat_idempotency_contract.rs`)**:
  - `trigger_builtin_arm_span(src, path)` helper — brace-counted span extractor. Locates `ExternalCommand::TriggerBuiltin` header, then matches braces forward to find the arm body's closing `}`. All three tests scope their checks to this span, so matches elsewhere in the dispatcher (e.g., other `ExternalCommand` arms that legitimately manage registry state) don't produce false positives.
  - `no_dispatcher_arm_mutates_automation_window_registry` — asserts the arm body contains NEITHER `upsert_automation_window(` NOR `remove_automation_window(`. These are the two named entry points into the automation registry's window list. Window lifecycle must flow through view-state transitions (Pass #34's architecture), not per-trigger side effects.
  - `no_dispatcher_arm_pushes_onto_automation_vec` — defense-in-depth. For every `.push(` and `.insert(` token in the arm body, scans a 100-byte neighborhood and rejects co-location with `automation_windows` or `automation.windows`. Catches a refactor that bypasses the named registry APIs and mutates a Vec directly.
  - `arm_body_current_view_assignments_are_pure_struct_literals` — for every `view.current_view =` assignment, walks back ≤120 bytes and rejects finding `view.current_view !=` or `view.current_view ==` — the canonical skip-guard pattern. Pins that arm bodies are unconditional assignments (second repeat trigger re-executes identical work on identical inputs).

- **Why this layer matters**: Repeat-idempotency is the fourth invariant completing the TriggerBuiltin dispatcher cordon. The cordon's layers:
  - **Arm-head symmetry** (Pass #42) — every dispatcher has the same arm set.
  - **Post-match re-key** (Pass #52) — the `update_automation_semantic_surface("main", …)` call at the tail is unconditional and pure-overwrite.
  - **Catch-all no-op** (Pass #53) — the `_ =>` arm mutates nothing (only logs).
  - **Case-insensitive dispatch** (Pass #54) — the match normalizes via `.to_lowercase()`, and all arm literals are lowercase-only.
  - **Repeat-idempotency** (Pass #55, this pass) — the arm bodies never push onto registry state and never short-circuit on repeat; the second call produces identical state to the first.
  - Together these four invariants mean: the dispatcher is a pure function of (name, current_view) → (new_view, surface_tag), with no hidden state, no accumulation, no case-sensitivity land mines, and no silent view-trash on typo. A user script can fire `triggerBuiltin X` as many times as it likes, in any case, with any name, and the state stays consistent.

- **Budget check**: current UTC 2026-04-17T12:44:30Z; Run 2 deadline 2026-04-17T13:34:39Z; ~50 minutes remain in the 600-minute budget. Approximately 10–15 more passes possible at the current 4-minute cadence.

- **Candidate follow-up stories** (from this pass's gap analysis):
  - (a) `hide-command-post-match-surface-rekey-symmetry-contract` — Pass #52 pinned TriggerBuiltin has unconditional post-match re-key; does `ExternalCommand::Hide` have a symmetric re-key? Missing would mean the hide path could leave stale surface tags.
  - (b) `trigger-builtin-no-registry-window-duplication-under-churn-stress` — a higher-intensity version of Scenario C with 20+ rapid fires across 5 different surfaces; verifies no edge-case window accumulation at scale.
  - (c) `stdin-protocol-parse-error-recovery-contract` — what happens when malformed JSON hits the stdin parser? app.log shows `stdin_parse_failed` entries; contract: subsequent valid commands must still dispatch correctly (no poisoned state).
  - (d) `trigger-builtin-back-to-back-different-names-no-flash` — rapid switch `tab-ai → clipboard-history → emoji → hide` in <500ms; automation registry must reflect the FINAL state correctly, not an intermediate race.

## Run 2 Pass 54 — 2026-04-17T12:36:00Z — `trigger-builtin-case-insensitive-dispatch` (live-verified 4 case variants + 3-test source contract)

- **Story**: trigger-builtin-case-insensitive-dispatch — *Generated from Pass #53's candidate (b) list. Case-insensitivity is implicit in every existing dispatcher (via `.to_lowercase()` normalization), but no contract pins it. A refactor that dropped the `.to_lowercase()` call — or silently changed an arm literal like `"tab-ai"` to `"TAB-AI"` — would regress every user calling `triggerBuiltin` with non-canonical casing, with no compile error and no existing test failure (Pass #42/#52/#53 all use lowercase literals so wouldn't trip). This pass pins the normalization at source and live-verifies four pathological case variants.*
- **Outcome**: `pass — live-verified 4 case variants on dev-watch pid 38095 + 3-test source-level contract; no production code edited`. Case-insensitivity was already correct by construction; this pass prevents silent drift.
- **Files touched**: 4 — `tests/trigger_builtin_case_insensitive_dispatch_contract.rs` (new, ~120 lines; 3 tests), `audits/afk/stories.md` (new `[x]` story under new `### Run 2 generated — triggerBuiltin case-insensitive dispatch (2026-04-17T12:36:00Z)` section), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry), `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` (+TriggerBuiltinCaseInsensitive node + coverage 63 → 64).

- **Verification receipt (live on dev-watch pid 38095)**:
  - **Step 0 baseline** — `listAutomationWindows` → 1 window `{id:"main", kind:"main", semanticSurface:"scriptList", visible:false}` (carried over hidden from Pass #53).
  - **Step 1 all-caps hyphenated** — `triggerBuiltin TAB-AI` → `listAutomationWindows` 1 window `semanticSurface:"acpChat"`. `getState` → `promptType:"acpChat"`, `choiceCount:0`, `inputValue:""`. Canonical `tab-ai` arm matched via `.to_lowercase()` normalization.
  - **Step 2 TitleCase-hyphenated** — `triggerBuiltin Clipboard-History` → 1 window `semanticSurface:"clipboardHistory"`. Confirmed mixed-case casing variant routes identically to canonical `clipboard-history`.
  - **Step 3 all-caps short form** — `triggerBuiltin EMOJI` → 1 window `semanticSurface:"emojiPicker"`. Short arm alias `emoji` (without `-picker`) also case-insensitive.
  - **Step 4 pathological alternating case** — `triggerBuiltin fIlE-sEaRcH` → 1 window `semanticSurface:"fileSearch"`. Confirms the normalization handles any case mix, not just outer-bound like `ALL-CAPS` or `TitleCase`.
  - **Step 5 cleanup** — `hide` → 1 window `semanticSurface:"scriptList"`. Clean return to baseline.
  - **No `Unknown built-in:` logs for Steps 1–4**: `grep -c` after Pass #54 shows the count did NOT increase from Pass #53's baseline of 2 — case variants all resolved to valid arms, none fell through to the catch-all. (If the `.to_lowercase()` call were removed, TAB-AI would have logged `Unknown built-in: 'TAB-AI'` and left the surface tag stale.)

- **Source-level contract (`tests/trigger_builtin_case_insensitive_dispatch_contract.rs`)**:
  - `every_dispatcher_has_exactly_one_lowercase_normalization_head` — asserts each of the three dispatcher source files contains EXACTLY ONE `match name.to_lowercase().as_str() {`. 0 means normalization was dropped; >1 means the match block was duplicated or split (anti-pattern that would break Pass #52's single-post-match-view assumption).
  - `no_dispatcher_uses_case_sensitive_match_on_name` — scans the first 8000 bytes of each dispatcher's `ExternalCommand::TriggerBuiltin` arm body and forbids the case-sensitive alternative `match name.as_str() {`. Defense-in-depth against a refactor that introduces the case-sensitive match without removing the normalized one.
  - `every_expected_arm_head_is_all_lowercase_or_hyphen` — enumerates the 10 canonical arm-head literal sets from Pass #42's `EXPECTED` table and asserts every literal string contains only ASCII lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens. An uppercase character in a literal would be dead code (the dispatcher matches against `name.to_lowercase()`, which never produces uppercase).
  - 3/3 tests pass in 0.00s after 24.22s first-compile.

- **Why pin this even though the code is already correct**: Case-insensitivity is a silent contract — users discover it works and rely on it without reading source. Dropping the `.to_lowercase()` call would regress no visible tests, pass all symmetry contracts, and break an unknown number of production scripts that pass non-canonical casing. The three-test contract makes the invariant explicit and enforced.

- **Why this layer matters alongside Pass #52 / #53**: Pass #52 pins the post-match re-key is unconditional (always fires at the tail). Pass #53 pins the `_ =>` arm is side-effect-free (preserves state on typos). Pass #54 pins the *matching* step itself is case-insensitive (pathological case inputs route correctly). Together, Passes #42 + #52 + #53 + #54 pin the full `ExternalCommand::TriggerBuiltin` dispatch pipeline: arm heads symmetric (#42), matching is case-insensitive (#54), catch-all is no-op (#53), post-match re-key is unconditional (#52).

- **Budget check**: Elapsed 12:36Z − 03:34:39Z = ~541 min of 600 min budget. Remaining ~59 min. Enough for Pass #55 before budget exhausts.

- **Candidate follow-ups**:
  - (a) **`stdin-protocol-parse-error-recovery-contract`** — verify that a malformed JSON command does not wedge the dispatcher loop; subsequent valid commands still route. Stresses the outer `parse_stdin_command` error-recovery path.
  - (b) **`registry-leak-probe-under-rapid-trigger-churn`** — dispatch 20 `triggerBuiltin` calls in rapid succession (mix of known+unknown), verify `listAutomationWindows` reports exactly 1 window at every observation point and no duplicates accumulate.
  - (c) **`semantic-surface-for-main-view-catchall-pure-default-contract`** — pin the `_ => "scriptList"` arm in the surface map is a literal string expression, not a function call, and `.to_string()` finalization stays. Complements Pass #42's coverage of the explicit arms.

## Run 2 Pass 53 — 2026-04-17T12:32:00Z — `trigger-builtin-unknown-name-preserves-surface-tag` (live-verified + 2-test source contract)

- **Story**: trigger-builtin-unknown-name-preserves-surface-tag — *Generated this pass. Queue of unchecked stories was empty at tick start, so the loop generated this sibling to Pass #52. Pass #52 pins the post-match re-key call itself; this pass pins its counterpart invariant — the `_ =>` catch-all arm in every stdin dispatcher's `match name.to_lowercase()` block must be a pure logging no-op (i.e., must NOT mutate `view.current_view` or call into the automation registry directly). Together, the two passes form a complete cordon around `ExternalCommand::TriggerBuiltin`: the known-key arms flip `view.current_view`, the unknown-key arm is a strict no-op, and the unconditional post-match re-key pins the surface tag either way.*
- **Outcome**: `pass — live-verified end-to-end on dev-watch pid 38095 + 2-test source-level contract; no production code edited`. The unknown-name path was already a pure-log no-op by construction; this pass pins the behavior so a refactor that silently added `view.current_view = AppView::ScriptList;` to the `_ =>` arm (or a duplicate `update_automation_semantic_surface` call) trips CI before reaching a user.
- **Files touched**: 4 — `tests/trigger_builtin_unknown_name_no_op_contract.rs` (new; 2 tests — exact-byte match of the canonical `_ => { logging::log("ERROR", ...) }` arm body across all three dispatchers + structural scan between the arm's `{...}` rejecting any `view.current_view =` assignment or `update_automation_semantic_surface` call inside), `audits/afk/stories.md` (new `[x]` story under new `### Run 2 generated — triggerBuiltin unknown-name preserves surface tag (2026-04-17T12:32:00Z)` section, full receipt), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry), `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` (+TriggerBuiltinUnknownNameNoOp node + coverage 62 → 63).

- **Verification receipt (live on dev-watch pid 38095)**:
  - **Step 1 baseline** — `getState` → `promptType:"none"`, `choiceCount:461`, `visibleChoiceCount:476`, `windowVisible:true`, `selectedValue:"Bluetooth Settings"`. `listAutomationWindows` → 1 window `{id:"main", kind:"main", focused:false, visible:true, semanticSurface:"scriptList"}`.
  - **Step 2 flip to acpChat** — `triggerBuiltin tab-ai` → (no response, fire-and-forget). `listAutomationWindows` → 1 window `semanticSurface:"acpChat"`.
  - **Step 3 unknown-name no-op** — `triggerBuiltin definitely-not-a-real-builtin-xyz-123` → (no response). `listAutomationWindows` → 1 window STILL `semanticSurface:"acpChat"` (tag preserved). `getState` → `promptType:"acpChat"`, `choiceCount:0`, `inputValue:""`, `windowVisible:true` (view preserved). **Invariant confirmed: unknown name did not flip view or surface tag.**
  - **Step 4 flip to clipboardHistory** — `triggerBuiltin clipboard-history` → (no response). `listAutomationWindows` → 1 window `semanticSurface:"clipboardHistory"`, bounds transitioned from 480×440 to 750×500 (ClipboardHistory subview has wider chrome).
  - **Step 5 second unknown-name on different surface** — `triggerBuiltin another-fake-builtin` → (no response). `listAutomationWindows` → 1 window STILL `semanticSurface:"clipboardHistory"`. `getState` → `promptType:"clipboardHistory"`, `choiceCount:100`, `visibleChoiceCount:100`, `selectedIndex:0` (full clipboard dataset preserved, no poisoned state from the unknown-name path). **Invariant re-confirmed on a different surface tag.**
  - **Step 6 cleanup** — `hide` → `listAutomationWindows` 1 window `semanticSurface:"scriptList"`, `visible:false`. `getState` → `promptType:"none"`, `windowVisible:false`. Clean return to baseline.
  - **app.log confirmation** — `grep "Unknown built-in"` → exactly 2 matches: `cid=stdin:req:p53-6 Unknown built-in: 'definitely-not-a-real-builtin-xyz-123'` (Step 3) and `cid=stdin:req:p53-11 Unknown built-in: 'another-fake-builtin'` (Step 5). **Proves the `_ =>` catch-all arm actually fired on each unknown invocation** — the post-match re-key ran after, read unchanged `view.current_view`, and re-wrote the same tag (idempotent-by-construction).

- **Source-level contract (`tests/trigger_builtin_unknown_name_no_op_contract.rs`)**:
  - `every_dispatcher_unknown_arm_is_pure_log_no_op` — exact-byte match of the canonical 36-space-indented `_ =>` arm body (`_ => { logging::log("ERROR", &format!("Unknown built-in: '{}'", name)); }`) across `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_core.rs`, `runtime_stdin.rs`, and `app_run_setup.rs`. Pins both the `Unknown built-in:` literal and the no-op shape.
  - `unknown_arm_does_not_mutate_current_view` — structural: scans backwards from each `Unknown built-in:` marker for the enclosing `_ => {` opener, forwards for the first `}` closer, and asserts the span contains NEITHER `view.current_view =` nor `update_automation_semantic_surface`. Defense-in-depth against a future contributor who matches the arm shape but inserts pure-looking state-mutating code inside the block.
  - Tests pass 2/2 in 0.00s after 18.84s first-compile.

- **Why this is worth pinning even though the current code is correct**: The `_ =>` arm is EASY to drive-by-refactor into a "fail-safe reset" — any engineer reading the dispatcher body will see "unknown key → log and ignore" and might think "should we at least reset to a safe state?" The answer is NO: the post-match re-key already handles this (it reads `view.current_view` which, having not been mutated by the arm, still reports the prior subview, so the tag stays stable). Resetting to `ScriptList` inside the arm would turn every typo into a silent user-visible regression with no returncode signal, no state-receipt hint, and no test failure — exactly the bug shape Pass #41 uncovered accidentally for the sibling `semantic_surface_for_main_view` catch-all. This pass prevents the mirror-image bug on the dispatcher side.

- **Why this layer matters on top of Pass #52**: Pass #52 pins the trailing re-key call as unconditional (fires every invocation, on every dispatcher). Pass #53 pins the negative companion: the `_ =>` arm does NOT run competing re-key or view-mutation code. Without THIS contract, a diff that did `view.current_view = AppView::ScriptList; crate::windows::update_automation_semantic_surface("main", Some("scriptList".into()));` inside the arm would pass Pass #52's contract (post-match re-key still present) AND Pass #42's contract (all arm heads still present) while regressing actual user experience on every `triggerBuiltin` typo. The two invariants are dual and must BOTH be pinned for the cordon to be tight.

- **Budget check**: Elapsed 12:32Z − 03:34:39Z = ~537 min of 600 min budget. Remaining ~63 min. Well within bounds — enough runway for a Pass #54 on a smaller-scope story.

- **Candidate follow-ups for future passes**:
  - (a) **`semantic-surface-for-main-view-catchall-pure-default-contract`** — the mirror-image source contract on the `_ => "scriptList"` arm in `semantic_surface_for_main_view` itself (pin it's a literal string, not a function call that could crash; pin the `.to_string()` finalization). Complementary to this pass and Pass #42's existing coverage of the explicit arms.
  - (b) **`trigger-builtin-case-sensitivity-contract`** — verify `triggerBuiltin TAB-AI` (uppercase) flips to `acpChat` via the `name.to_lowercase()` normalization (already verified implicitly by Pass #42's arm-head patterns but not tested as a user-facing case-insensitivity guarantee).
  - (c) **`stdin-protocol-parse-error-recovery-contract`** — verify that a malformed `triggerBuiltin` payload (`{"type":"triggerBuiltin","name":null}` or similar) does not wedge the dispatcher loop; all subsequent valid commands still route. Tests resilience of the outer `parse_stdin_command` flow.
  - (d) **`registry-leak-probe-under-rapid-trigger-churn`** — dispatch 20 `triggerBuiltin` calls in rapid succession (a mix of known and unknown names), verify `listAutomationWindows` reports exactly 1 window at every observation point (no accumulation, no reordering).

## Run 2 Pass 52 — 2026-04-17T12:22:00Z — `trigger-builtin-post-match-surface-rekey-contract` (source-level contract pinned — 3/3 tests)

- **Story**: trigger-builtin-post-match-surface-rekey-contract — *Generated this pass from Pass #51's candidate (c) list. Pass #51 live-verified that `triggerBuiltin tab-ai` re-keys main's `semanticSurface` cleanly from `"scriptList"` back to `"acpChat"` on reattach. Investigating the source to understand WHY showed that the re-key happens not in any per-arm handler but at the TAIL of `ExternalCommand::TriggerBuiltin` in every stdin dispatcher, via a single unconditional `update_automation_semantic_surface("main", semantic_surface_for_main_view(&view.current_view))` call. Pass #42's existing symmetry contract pins the arm heads and the per-variant map entries but does NOT pin this trailing call — a gap that became obvious once Pass #51 leaned on it at runtime.*
- **Outcome**: `pass — source-level contract pinned; 3/3 tests; lat.md/acp-chat.md updated; no production code edited`. The invariant was already correct in source (the call is there, in all three dispatchers, with the right shape and ordering). This pass pins it against silent drift.
- **Files touched**: 4 — `tests/trigger_builtin_post_match_surface_rekey_contract.rs` (new; 3 tests + 2 canonical const strings for the TriggerBuiltin-path re-key call shape + the hide-path sibling re-key call shape), `lat.md/acp-chat.md` (+new `### Reattach re-keys main's automation surface via the triggerBuiltin choke point` subsection under `## Detached window behavior`; one leading-paragraph trim required to stay under the 250-char cap), `audits/afk/stories.md` (new `[x]` story under new `### Run 2 generated — triggerBuiltin post-match re-key contract (2026-04-17T12:22:00Z)` section, full receipt), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry), `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` (+TriggerBuiltinPostMatchRekey node + coverage 61 → 62).
- **Verification receipt**:
  - `cargo test --test trigger_builtin_post_match_surface_rekey_contract` → `3 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored` in 0.00s after 17.04s first-compile. All three tests confirm the invariant holds on current HEAD.
  - Test (1) `every_dispatcher_has_trigger_builtin_post_match_rekey` — asserts the exact multi-line call shape with `semantic_surface_for_main_view(&view.current_view)` is present at the tail of `ExternalCommand::TriggerBuiltin` in each of `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin.rs`, `runtime_stdin_match_core.rs`, `app_run_setup.rs`.
  - Test (2) `every_dispatcher_has_hide_path_script_list_rekey` — asserts the sibling hide-path call with hardcoded `Some("scriptList".to_string())` is present in each dispatcher, preserving the Pass #19 tool-hide-rpc-surface-reset invariant.
  - Test (3) `post_match_rekey_appears_after_inner_match_closing_brace` — structural ordering check: scans backwards from the re-key position for the nearest `match name.to_lowercase()` header, then asserts the match-head→rekey span contains the canonical `Unknown built-in:` catch-all arm log — confirming the re-key trails (rather than precedes) the match body.
  - `lat check` → `All checks passed` after one leading-paragraph trim on the new acp-chat.md subsection (253 → 247 chars, comfortably under the 250 cap).
- **Why this layer matters on top of Pass #42**: Pass #42 pins per-key symmetry (arm head exists in each dispatcher + `AppView` variant maps to the right surface tag) and doc coverage (every surface tag is listed in `lat.md/automation.md`). But the RE-KEY itself — the call that reads `view.current_view` after the inner match and writes the surface tag — is orthogonal. A maintainer could keep the arm heads and the map entries in perfect shape while breaking the post-match re-key in any of these ways: (a) replace the dynamic `&view.current_view` argument with a pre-match snapshot, so the call reads the OLD view; (b) move the call inside an `if name.is_some()` or `if matched` guard, so only "known" trigger keys re-key and everything else (including the hide path indirectly) drifts; (c) relocate the call to BEFORE the match, so it reads the stale view; (d) delete the call entirely while keeping all the arm machinery in place. Any of those would pass Pass #42 but break Pass #51's reattach round-trip and Pass #44's direct-subview-to-subview chain at runtime. Pass #52 closes this subtle but consequential gap.
- **Budget check at Pass #52 commit**: approximately 1h 12m remain in Run 2 (now 2026-04-17T12:22:00Z, deadline 13:34:39Z).
- **Candidate follow-ups for later ticks**:
  - (a) `acp-concurrent-detach-close-stress` — still open; drive detach+close in rapid succession within the same tick to stress-test Pass #48's race-safe extraction at runtime rather than just static pattern.
  - (b) `acp-thread-cache-survives-reattach-concurrent-close` — still open from Pass #48's tail.
  - (c) `acp-chat-view-getstate-contract` — still open from Pass #51's tail; pin `getState` and `getAcpState` response shapes when `AppView::AcpChatView` is active so a refactor can't silently drop fields.
  - (d) `dispatcher-hide-path-match-location-contract` — symmetric sibling to test (3) of this pass: pin that the hide-path re-key appears INSIDE the `ExternalCommand::Hide` arm body (between the `view.reset_to_script_list(ctx)` call and the subsequent notes/ai-aware `ctx.hide()` branching), so a refactor that moved it outside the hide arm would be caught.

Registry stays clean across the cycle; thread state (`contextChipCount`, `inputText`, `cursorIndex`) is preserved; `getAcpState.resolvedTarget.windowKind` correctly flips back to `"main"` on reattach.

Root cause: the process was launched before the full rebuild completed. Per scope.md's autonomy clause, ran `cargo build` (Finished in 18.06s) + `session.sh stop dev-watch && session.sh start dev-watch`. New pid 38095. This is a reminder that restart-after-cargo-build is not free; the forwarder needs time between `build` completion and dev-watch restart to avoid racing half-built artifacts. Not a bug — just a caller-ordering lesson to log for future passes.
- **Verification receipt (lifecycle round-trip on pid 38095)**:
  - **Step 1** — `show` + `triggerBuiltin tab-ai` → `listAutomationWindows` = `[{id:"main", kind:"main", title:"Script Kit", focused:true, visible:true, semanticSurface:"acpChat"}]`, `focusedWindowId:"main"`. `getAcpState` = `{schemaVersion:2, resolvedTarget:{windowId:"main", windowKind:"main", title:"Script Kit"}, status:"idle", inputText:"@cmd:\"Theme Designer\" ", cursorIndex:22, messageCount:0, contextChipCount:1, contextReady:true, hasPendingPermission:false, inputLayout:{charCount:22, visibleStart:0, visibleEnd:22, cursorInWindow:22}}`. *(Residual thread state from prior dev session — the Theme Designer context chip was pre-loaded; this is fine, what matters is that the state persists across the cycle.)*
  - **Step 2** — `triggerAction acp_detach_window host=main` → `listAutomationWindows` = 2 windows `[{id:"main", semanticSurface:"scriptList", focused:false, visible:true}, {id:"acpDetached:6101b56e-60b3-4c51-9d6d-b41fa982c4a3", kind:"acpDetached", semanticSurface:"acpChat", focused:true, visible:true}]`. **Pass #50's detach-path re-key fix RE-CONFIRMED** on the fresh binary: main correctly reports `scriptList`, not stale `acpChat`. `getState.promptType:"none"`, `windowVisible:true` — main restored to default surface.
  - **Step 3** — `triggerAction acp_close host=acpDetached` → `listAutomationWindows` = `[{id:"main", focused:true, visible:true, semanticSurface:"scriptList"}]`. The detached entry is fully drained from the registry (Pass #47 cleanup-pair + Pass #48 race-safe extraction both holding at runtime).
  - **Step 4 (THE KEY ACCEPTANCE STEP)** — `triggerBuiltin tab-ai` again → `listAutomationWindows` = `[{id:"main", kind:"main", focused:true, visible:true, semanticSurface:"acpChat"}]`, `focusedWindowId:"main"`. **EXACTLY ONE WINDOW — no lingering `acpDetached:*` ghost entry from the previous attach cycle**. `getAcpState` = `{resolvedTarget:{windowId:"main", windowKind:"main"}, status:"idle", inputText:"@cmd:\"Theme Designer\" ", cursorIndex:22, messageCount:0, contextChipCount:1, contextReady:true}` — ACP target resolver correctly re-bound to main, AND thread state (contextChipCount, inputText, cursorIndex) survived the detach→close→reattach cycle intact.

  - (a) **Registry exclusivity on reattach** — after close-detached + reattach, exactly ONE window in the registry with `kind:"main"`; the `acpDetached` entry removed in Step 3 does NOT come back as a ghost. Relies on Pass #47 cleanup pair executing atomically + Pass #48 take-from-mutex preventing any intermediate state where the detached handle could survive past its close.
  - (b) **Surface tag identity on reattach** — the main window's `semanticSurface` cleanly flips `acpChat → scriptList → acpChat → scriptList` across the 5 steps, with no stale intermediate values. Pass #50's detach-path re-key + the existing reattach-to-AcpChatView path (which `triggerBuiltin tab-ai` drives through `handle_tab_ai_builtin`) both re-key correctly. No drift observed.
  - (c) **Thread state preservation across detach+close+reattach** — `contextChipCount:1` and `inputText` survive unchanged from Step 1 → Step 4. The ACP conversation is not rebuilt on reattach; the resolver correctly points at the existing thread on main after the detached window's slot is drained.
  - (d) **ACP target resolver re-binding** — `getAcpState.resolvedTarget.windowKind` correctly reports `"main"` at Step 1 AND at Step 4 (after the cycle). The resolver does not leave a stale `"acpDetached"` target from Step 2.
