## Run 2 Pass 48 — 2026-04-17T11:52:00Z — `detached-popup-concurrent-close-safety` (source-level contract pinned — 3/3 tests)

- **Story**: detached-popup-concurrent-close-safety — *Generated this pass, flagged as a follow-up at the tail of Pass #47. Pass #47 pinned the two-path cleanup-pair duplication; Pass #48 pins the race-safe extraction pattern that makes the cleanup pair exactly-once. The detached popup has THREE cleanup sites against one `CHAT_WINDOW` Mutex: (a) placeholder on_close (src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs line 124), (b) thread on_close (line 193), (c) close_chat_window helper (line 528). All three follow the identical shape `let slot = CHAT_WINDOW.get_or_init(...); slot.lock() + g.take()` — the `.take()` is what makes the race safe: winner gets `Some(state)` and runs cleanup, loser gets `None` and no-ops. A drive-by refactor replacing `.take()` with `.clone()` would silently break exactly-once.*
- **Outcome**: `source-level contract pinned — 3/3 tests`. New `tests/detached_acp_concurrent_close_safety_contract.rs` with (1) `every_close_site_takes_state_out_of_the_mutex` — counts `g.take()` occurrences, asserts ≥3; (2) `close_sites_use_the_same_chat_window_mutex` — asserts `static CHAT_WINDOW:` declared exactly once AND `CHAT_WINDOW.get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(None))` appears ≥3 times; (3) `no_close_site_uses_non_take_extraction` — forbids clone-out-of-mutex patterns with whitespace anchors. First-draft bug: the unanchored `g.clone()` pattern false-matched `dialog.clone()` at line 743; fixed by anchoring each forbidden pattern with a leading ` ` (space) or `\t` (tab) so only standalone `g` bindings match.
- **Files touched**: 4 — `tests/detached_acp_concurrent_close_safety_contract.rs` (new; 3 tests + 1 helper + extensive docstring cross-referencing the three close sites by file:line), `lat.md/acp-chat.md` (+subsection `### Concurrent close safety — take-from-mutex pattern` under `## Detached window behavior`, 3 paragraphs: invariant statement, race model with the three concrete lock-sites enumerated, reason-clone-would-break-it with the specific double-registry-cleanup failure mode), `audits/afk/stories.md` (new `[x]` story under new `### Run 2 generated — detached popup concurrent close safety (2026-04-17T11:52:00Z)` section, full receipt including the `g.clone()` false-positive debug note), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry). No production source file edited — behavior is correct from Pass #29 onward; this pass closes the static-test gap for race-safe extraction.
- **Verification receipt**:
  - `cargo test --test detached_acp_concurrent_close_safety_contract` → `test result: ok. 3 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored` in 0.00s after 17.88s first-compile.
  - `lat check` → `All checks passed`.
  - Grep-confirmed `g.take()` sites: lines 124 (placeholder on_close), 193 (thread on_close), 528 (close_chat_window) — exactly 3, matching the asserted lower bound.
  - Grep-confirmed `CHAT_WINDOW.get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(None))` appears at 13 sites in the file (read-for-state, read-for-render, lock-for-cleanup), far above the ≥3 lower bound; future reducers that consolidated these into a helper would still need to keep ≥3 to satisfy the test, so the bound is robust against idiomatic refactors without being tight enough to catch them.
  - Debug trail for the `g.clone()` false-positive: first test run surfaced `FAILED — src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs contains g.clone()` pointing at `dialog.clone()` line 743 (substring match). Fixed by changing the forbidden-pattern array from bare `["g.clone()", ...]` to `[" g.clone()", "\tg.clone()", ...]` — the leading whitespace ensures `g` is a standalone binding, not the tail of an identifier. Recorded the lesson in inline test docstring so future maintainers understand why the patterns have leading spaces.
- **Notes**: The ACP-detached-popup lifecycle now has FOUR source-level contract pins covering distinct concerns: (1) Pass #43 maps `AppView::AcpChatView` → `"acpChat"` in `semantic_surface_for_main_view` (Main-hosted surface tag); (2) Pass #46 pins the single `upsert_automation_window` call shape with kind+surface parity for the detached popup (creation); (3) Pass #47 pins the two-path cleanup-pair duplication (close ordering); (4) Pass #48 pins the `g.take()` extraction pattern (race-safe cleanup). A future refactor would have to break all four simultaneously to silently regress any ACP-detached automation assumption, creation invariant, close-cleanup invariant, OR concurrency invariant. Budget check at Pass #48 commit: approximately 1h 40m remain in Run 2 (now 2026-04-17T11:52:00Z, deadline 13:34:39Z).
- **Candidate follow-ups for later ticks**: (a) `detached-popup-close-live-verify` — replay Pass #29's live dispatch chain (`triggerAction host=acpDetached action_id=acp_close` → registry empty after close) against the current binary to prove the three-pin cordon holds end-to-end, not just at static shape. (b) `acp-thread-cache-survives-reattach-concurrent-close` — a more ambitious live test: open detached ACP, fire `close_chat_window` + simulate user Cmd+W simultaneously via two fire-and-forget stdin sends, verify the subsequent re-open restores the cached embedded view identity (no double-free of the AcpThread entity, no stale CHAT_WINDOW slot).

## Run 2 Pass 47 — 2026-04-17T11:42:00Z — `detached-popup-close-cleanup-contract` (source-level contract pinned — 2/2 tests)

- **Story**: detached-popup-close-cleanup-contract — *Generated this pass, flagged as a candidate at the tail of Pass #46's log entry. Pass #29 fixed a registry-leak bug where external `TriggerAction host=acpDetached action_id=acp_close` closed the detached window visually but left a stale entry in `listAutomationWindows` forever. The bug lived in the INTERACTION between two close paths: `close_chat_window(cx)` `take()`s `CHAT_WINDOW.slot` before calling `window.remove_window()`, causing the window's own `on_window_should_close` callback to later find the slot empty and silently skip its own registry-cleanup branch. Pass #29's fix duplicated the cleanup pair (`remove_runtime_window_handle(id)` + `remove_automation_window(id)`) inside `close_chat_window` so the helper drains the registry itself. No static test currently prevents a "simplification" refactor from re-collapsing the duplication and reintroducing the leak — the bug is invisible from either path's local perspective.*
- **Outcome**: `source-level contract pinned — 2/2 tests`. New `tests/detached_acp_close_cleanup_contract.rs` with (1) `close_chat_window_cleans_up_registry_before_remove_window` — slices the fn body from header to the next top-level `\npub fn`/`\nfn `, asserts the adjacent cleanup pair exists inside, is gated by `if let Some(ref id) = state.automation_id`, appears before `window.remove_window();` (semicolon matters — an earlier draft matched the doc comment at line 533 containing the bare string `window.remove_window()`; fixed by anchoring to the trailing semicolon), and that `save_window_from_gpui(WindowRole::AcpChat, ...)` persistence remains. (2) `both_close_paths_carry_the_cleanup_pair` — whole-file scan using a 160-byte sliding window after each `remove_runtime_window_handle(id)` occurrence; requires at least two adjacent pairs (one per path) AND that the count of `remove_automation_window(id)` calls equals the adjacent-pair count so no automation-remove can be orphaned without a matching runtime-remove. Both tests pass in 0.00s after a 19.50s first-compile.
- **Files touched**: 5 — `tests/detached_acp_close_cleanup_contract.rs` (new; 2 tests + 2 helpers), `lat.md/acp-chat.md` (+subsection `### Close cleanup — both paths drain the registry pair` under `## Detached window behavior`, 4 paragraphs enumerating both entry paths and their distinct sequencing hazards), `audits/afk/stories.md` (new `[x]` story + full receipt prepended under new `### Run 2 generated — detached popup close cleanup contract (2026-04-17T11:40:00Z)` section), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry prepended), `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` (+DetachedCloseCleanup node; coverage 56 → 57). No production code edited — behavior is correct from Pass #29; this pass closes the static-test gap.
- **Verification receipt**:
  - `cargo test --test detached_acp_close_cleanup_contract` → `test result: ok. 2 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored`.
  - `lat check` → `All checks passed`.
  - Grep-confirmed 4 `remove_runtime_window_handle(id)` sites in the file: (1) line 53 — outer helper, no pair needed (automation window not yet registered at that stage); (2) line 126 — placeholder on_window_should_close, no pair needed (same reason); (3) line 195 — on_window_should_close inside `open_chat_window_with_thread`, PAIR present (titlebar-close path); (4) line 543 — inside `close_chat_window`, PAIR present (external-caller path). Count-pair-site test correctly returns 2.
  - Debug trail for the "remove_window before cleanup" false positive: first draft used `body.find("window.remove_window()")` which matched the doc comment at line 533 (`// entry before window.remove_window(). The on_close callback`) — the contract test's own error message revealed the byte offset 318 in body-relative coords, which corresponded to the comment. Anchoring to `window.remove_window();` (with semicolon) narrowed the match to the real call site at line 554. Lesson recorded in the test's inline docstring to warn future maintainers.
- **Notes**: The ACP-detached-popup lifecycle is now fully contract-pinned across creation, dispatch, and cleanup: Pass #46 pins the single `upsert_automation_window` call shape with kind+surface parity (creation); Pass #29 pins the `host=acpDetached` routing in the external dispatcher via `tests/trigger_action_acp_detached_host_contract.rs` (dispatch); Pass #47 pins the two-path cleanup-pair duplication (close). A future mechanical refactor would have to break all three simultaneously to silently regress any ACP-detached automation assumption. The ACP-surface automation contract now has FOUR source-level pins total: (1) Pass #43 Main-hosted map entry in `semantic_surface_for_main_view`; (2) Pass #45 stdin-hide asymmetry guard; (3) Pass #46 detached-popup creation; (4) Pass #47 detached-popup close cleanup.

(b) `detached-popup-concurrent-close-safety` — investigate whether simultaneous titlebar close + external `close_chat_window` could race on the `CHAT_WINDOW.slot` mutex (currently the on_window_should_close cleanup inside `open_chat_window_with_thread` and `close_chat_window` both lock the same Mutex, so rust-level safety is guaranteed, but ordering semantics — which path observes the full state — isn't contract-pinned). ## Run 2 Pass 46 — 2026-04-17T11:30:00Z — `detached-popup-acp-surface-parity-contract` (source-level contract pinned — 2/2 tests)

- **Story**: detached-popup-acp-surface-parity-contract — *Generated this pass. Pass #45 pinned the hotkey-detach path's asymmetry with stdin-hide, but left a complementary gap: what does the DETACHED popup actually advertise to automation consumers once it exists? The answer lives in `src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs:283-293` — ONE `upsert_automation_window(AutomationWindowInfo { kind: AutomationWindowKind::AcpDetached, semantic_surface: Some("acpChat".to_string()), .. })` call. The `semantic_surface` is intentionally the SAME `"acpChat"` tag that `semantic_surface_for_main_view` returns for the Main-hosted `AcpChatView` (Pass #43). This parity is load-bearing: consumers filter on `semanticSurface == "acpChat"` to find ACP chat regardless of attachment (the `kind` field is the disambiguator). Existing automation tests hardcode this — `tests/automation/detached_acp_targeting.rs:67` asserts `resolved.semantic_surface.as_deref() == Some("acpChat")` on a window whose kind is `AcpDetached`. A drive-by rename of the detached popup's surface tag (e.g. to `"acpDetached"`) would silently break consumers and force a swarm of test rewrites. No static test currently prevents that rename.*
- **Outcome**: `source-level contract pinned — 2/2 tests`. New `tests/detached_acp_popup_registry_surface_contract.rs` with (1) `chat_window_registers_detached_popup_with_acp_chat_surface_parity` — slices the `upsert_automation_window(...)` struct literal between the opening brace and the matching `});` and asserts the body contains `kind: crate::protocol::AutomationWindowKind::AcpDetached` AND `semantic_surface: Some("acpChat".to_string())`; (2) `chat_window_has_single_detached_upsert_site` — counts `upsert_automation_window(` occurrences (must be 1) AND `AutomationWindowKind::AcpDetached` mentions (must be 1) in the whole file, preventing a second upsert site that could fork the kind+surface pair. Both tests pass in 0.00s after a 19.28s first-compile (+0.22s during `cargo test` re-link).
- **Files touched**: 4 — `tests/detached_acp_popup_registry_surface_contract.rs` (new; 2 tests + 2 helpers), `lat.md/acp-chat.md` (+subsection `### Automation registry parity — detached popup shares `acpChat` surface tag` under `## Detached window behavior`, ~3 paragraphs cross-referencing the new contract test, the parallel Main-hosted map entry in `semantic_surface_for_main_view`, and the 4 automation test files that hardcode `Some("acpChat")` on `AcpDetached` fixtures), `audits/afk/stories.md` (new story `[x]` with receipt), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry). No source file edited (the contract codifies what's already there; Pass #46's gain is that the invariant is now CI-gated against drive-by drift).

- **Notes**: Pass #46 is a pure source-level pin — no production code or behavior changed. This is intentional: the behavior is correct, and the pin closes a supplier-side gap (what the registration site guarantees) that complements the Pass #45 consumer-side pin (what the stdin-hide dispatcher guarantees NOT to do). After Pass #46, the ACP-surface automation contract has three pins: (1) Main-hosted map entry (Pass #43: `semantic_surface_for_main_view` contains `AcpChatView => "acpChat"`), (2) stdin-hide asymmetry guard (Pass #45: `ExternalCommand::Hide` arms reset+rekey without detach), (3) detached-popup registration (Pass #46: single upsert site pairs `AcpDetached` kind with `"acpChat"` surface). A future refactor would need to break all three simultaneously to silently regress any single consumer assumption — the three-test cordon is the explicit design.

(b) `detached-popup-close-cleanup-contract` — Pass #29 added `remove_automation_window` inside `close_chat_window` to fix a registry leak; add a source-level contract pinning that `close_chat_window` MUST call both `remove_runtime_window_handle` and `remove_automation_window` before `window.remove_window()` (the fix shape is recorded in `stories.md` under `tool-trigger-action-acpdetached-host` but not currently contract-pinned).

## Run 2 Pass 45 — 2026-04-17T11:22:00Z — `hide-path-asymmetry-hotkey-vs-stdin-contract` (source-level contract pinned — 3/3 tests)

- **Story**: hide-path-asymmetry-hotkey-vs-stdin-contract — *Generated this pass. Pass #44's probe ("does stdin hide with AcpChatView active spawn a detached popup?") surfaced a non-obvious architectural asymmetry: the global-hotkey toggle path intentionally detaches ACP on hide (keeps main visible on ScriptList, spawns popup via `open_chat_window_with_thread`, fires `hotkey_detach_acp_requested`) whereas the stdin `ExternalCommand::Hide` path in all three dispatchers unconditionally resets-and-hides. Both behaviors are correct for their respective user intent ("toggle my launcher, keep my chat alive" vs. "make this window go away"), but that means the two code paths MUST stay distinct — a future refactor that folds detach into a shared dismiss helper would silently break automation workflows that chain `triggerBuiltin tab-ai → hide`, because the hide would spawn a popup from code instead of just hiding.*
- **Outcome**: `source-level contract pinned — 3/3 tests`. New `tests/stdin_hide_no_acp_detach_branch_contract.rs` with a `hide_arm_body` helper that slices each dispatcher source file between `ExternalCommand::Hide {` and the next `ExternalCommand::` head (conservative: every byte between the markers), then asserts BOTH halves of the contract per dispatcher: (a) positive — the Hide arm body contains `view.reset_to_script_list(ctx)` AND `update_automation_semantic_surface` AND `"scriptList"`; (b) negative — the Hide arm body does NOT contain any of five detach sentinels: `open_chat_window_with_thread`, `hotkey_detach_acp_requested`, `hotkey_detach_acp_completed`, `hotkey_detach_acp_aborted`, `hotkey_detach_acp_failed`, or an `AppView::AcpChatView` branch. This second list is deliberately broader than just the detach call itself — any AcpChatView-gated conditional in the Hide arm body is a leading indicator that the asymmetry has started drifting, so catching it before the detach call fully lands gives a longer window to revert. Documented the asymmetry in `lat.md/acp-chat.md` as `### Hide path asymmetry — hotkey detaches, stdin hides` under `## Detached window behavior`, explaining the two user intents and cross-referencing this contract + the companion positive-half `tests/hide_rpc_surface_reset_contract.rs`.
- **Files touched**: 4 — `tests/stdin_hide_no_acp_detach_branch_contract.rs` (new; 3 tests + helper), `lat.md/acp-chat.md` (+subsection), `audits/afk/stories.md` (new story + `[x]`), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry), `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` (+HidePathAsymmetry node + coverage 54 → 55).
- **Proof**: `cargo test --test stdin_hide_no_acp_detach_branch_contract` → 3/3 passed (all three dispatchers both satisfy the positive assertions and contain none of the six detach sentinels). Behavior live-verified in Pass #44's probe sequence on dev-watch pid 89365: `triggerBuiltin tab-ai` + `show` → `listAutomationWindows[0]={kind:"main", visible:true, semanticSurface:"acpChat"}`; `hide` → `listAutomationWindows[0]={kind:"main", visible:false, semanticSurface:"scriptList"}` with NO second detached window in the registry. `lat check` clean after the acp-chat.md subsection edit (paragraph-length rule required shortening the first paragraph).
- **Next**: Two adjacent stories worth generating in future passes: (1) `hotkey-detach-acp-roundtrip-integrity` — simulate the hotkey-with-AcpChatView-active path end-to-end if the simulate-key tool supports global hotkey synthesis, verify the detached popup appears in `listAutomationWindows` with a distinct window id + surface tag, and return-to-panel preserves embedded view identity; (2) `detached-acp-popup-registry-surface` — formalize a `semanticSurface` tag (e.g. `acpDetached`) for the detached popup kind so automation consumers can distinguish main-hosted vs. detached ACP without parsing window titles. Both require the hotkey path to fire, which may be outside the stdin protocol's current reach — a tool-gap marker if so.

## Run 2 Pass 44 — 2026-04-17T11:14:00Z — `direct-subview-to-subview-trigger-surface-rekey` (live-verified end-to-end — 10 direct flips + final hide reset)

Passes #42 and #43 verified every `triggerBuiltin` → surface re-key from a scriptList baseline with `hide` resets between triggers. Final `hide` reset to `scriptList` as expected. This means the dispatcher's post-match `update_automation_semantic_surface("main", semantic_surface_for_main_view(&view.current_view))` call fires unconditionally for every `triggerBuiltin` match arm (apps, emoji, etc.) in all three stdin dispatcher source files, producing a pure-overwrite re-key regardless of the PRIOR `AppView::current_view` state. This is a pure verification pass.

Live-verify that Pass #33 (the `StateResult.visibleChoiceCount` field) and Pass #34 (the `write_filter_to_current_subview` stdin routing) compose correctly on the clipboard-history surface specifically — i.e., that the corrected acceptance clause for `empty-clipboard-state` (`getState.visibleChoiceCount=0` on a filter-miss while `choiceCount` stays at the dataset size) actually lands end-to-end. Then pin the `ClipboardHistoryView` getState arm at `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs:2119-2143` at source level so a future refactor can't silently regress the receipt by reading `self.filter_text`, transposing the `choiceCount` and `visibleChoiceCount` tuple slots, breaking case-insensitivity, or drifting the compare target away from `e.text_preview`.*
- **Why now**: Seed-list walk exhausted — only `[!] empty-clipboard-state` remains, and its four sub-gaps split into two that can be closed at the **live + contract** level right now (sub-gaps 2 and 3, thanks to the upstream Passes #33 and #34) and two that require either explicit user opt-in (sub-gap 1 is destructive) or an independent tool-extension pass (sub-gap 4 is a `getElements` filter-aware receipt that warrants its own story). Closing the two reachable sub-gaps at the live level on the actual clipboard-history surface — the exact target Pass #14 recorded as blocked — converts `empty-clipboard-state` from "corrected acceptance structurally reachable" (Pass #33 claim) to "corrected acceptance live-verified on the target surface" (Pass #36 claim), and the new contract test guards the receipt against the consumer-side regression class not covered by Pass #33's protocol-field contract or Pass #34's routing contract.
- **Outcome**: `live-verified + contract-pinned` — four back-to-back state receipts against dev-watch (pid 17907) captured the full filter→receipt sweep on clipboard-history, a new 3-test source-level contract pins the arm shape of `collect_state`'s `ClipboardHistoryView` match arm, and the `[!] empty-clipboard-state` story's footnote is amended to record the live-level closure of sub-gaps (2) and (3).
- **Commit**: `3ae6553e0`
- **Files touched**: 4 — `tests/clipboard_history_state_filter_receipt_contract.rs` (new, 3 tests pinning the arm at `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs:2119-2143`: variant destructure; case-insensitive `.contains(&filter_lower)` on `e.text_preview.to_lowercase()`; tuple-slot ordering with `entries.len(),` strictly BEFORE `filtered_count,`), `audits/afk/stories.md` (new `Run 2 generated — clipboard-history filter-miss live receipt (2026-04-17T10:10:00Z)` section with the `[x] clipboard-history-filter-miss-visible-count-live` story; amended `[!] empty-clipboard-state` footnote to append the Pass #36 live-verification receipt + sub-gap 2+3 live-level closure note), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry), `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` (add `ClipboardFilterMissLive` node under Subviews + bump coverage stats).
- **Root context**: The `ClipboardHistoryView` arm of `collect_state` at `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs:2119-2143` builds a 6-tuple `(promptType, ..., inputValue, choiceCount, visibleChoiceCount, selectedIndex, selectedValue)` that is destructured at `mod.rs:1899-1908` and forwarded into `Message::state_result(...)`. The arm destructures `filter, selected_index` from the variant (not `self.*`), computes `filtered_count` via `entries.iter().filter(|e| e.text_preview.to_lowercase().contains(&filter_lower)).count()`, and returns `entries.len()` for choiceCount + `filtered_count` for visibleChoiceCount — exactly the receipt shape `empty-clipboard-state`'s corrected clause requires. Pass #14 proved that was NOT the case across the stack: Pass #14-era `setFilter` stdin routed to the main-menu ranker instead of the subview, so even though the arm WAS correctly shaped, the variant's `filter` field stayed stale, and `filtered_count` never narrowed. Pass #34 fixed the routing; Pass #36 live-verifies the composition and locks the arm at source level.
- **Fix shape**: No source change to `src/prompt_handler/mod.rs` — the arm is already correctly shaped. This is a contract-only pass. The test file `tests/clipboard_history_state_filter_receipt_contract.rs` contains 3 tests scoped to the arm body via `.find("AppView::ClipboardHistoryView {\n                        filter,")` through `.find("\n                    AppView::AcpHistoryView")`: (1) destructure lock — asserts `filter,\n...\nselected_index,\n                    } => {` substring, ruling out `..` patterns or `self.filter_text` reads; (2) narrowing-computation lock — asserts `let entries = &self.cached_clipboard_entries;`, `let filter_lower = filter.to_lowercase();`, `.contains(&filter_lower)`, `e.text_preview.to_lowercase().contains(&filter_lower)` — covers both case-insensitivity AND target-field alignment with the UI-rendered text; (3) tuple-slot ordering lock — asserts `"clipboardHistory".to_string(),`, `filter.clone(),`, `entries.len(),`, `filtered_count,`, `*selected_index as i32,` plus a `find`-position inequality proving `entries.len(),` precedes `filtered_count,` inside the arm body (transposing them would collapse the narrowing-vs-empty distinction — a filter-miss would look identical to an empty clipboard at the state-receipt level, exactly the regression class `empty-clipboard-state` exists to rule out).
- **Proof (contract)**: `cargo test --test clipboard_history_state_filter_receipt_contract` → `test result: ok. 3 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured` in 0.00s after 20.53s compile.
- **Proof (live)**: Four back-to-back `getState` receipts against dev-watch (pid 17907): (1) baseline after `triggerBuiltin clipboard-history` → `{promptType:"clipboardHistory", inputValue:"", choiceCount:100, visibleChoiceCount:100, selectedIndex:0}`; (2) `setFilter "zzz_no_match_test_string"` → `{inputValue:"zzz_no_match_test_string", choiceCount:100, visibleChoiceCount:0}` — **the filter-miss receipt**, sub-gap (3)'s corrected acceptance clause reachable via stdin-driven filter writes on the clipboard-history surface, NOT the main-menu ranker (which would show `visibleChoiceCount=0` on scripts and `promptType=none`, not `promptType=clipboardHistory`); (3) `setFilter "a"` → `{inputValue:"a", choiceCount:100, visibleChoiceCount:52}` — the narrowing-hit receipt bounds the other direction, ruling out the pathological case where the arm always returns zero; (4) `setFilter ""` → `{inputValue:"", choiceCount:100, visibleChoiceCount:100}` — the clear-filter restoration, proving the stdin routing is idempotent against the empty string. All four receipts arrived within 3000ms of stdin dispatch. Cleanup: `hideMainWindow` → `getState p36-hide` confirmed `windowVisible:false`.
- **Out-of-scope (not touched this pass, intentionally)**: (a) `empty-clipboard-state` sub-gap (1) — adding `ExternalCommand::ClearClipboardHistory` is destructive and needs explicit user opt-in per scope.md. (b) `empty-clipboard-state` sub-gap (4) — `getElements target=Main` not respecting the active filter is a `getElements` plumbing change that warrants its own story and contract. (c) The user's parallel observation from Pass #35 about the emoji picker rendering at 750×500 ScriptList window size (`emoji-picker-compact-window`) remains unclaimed — orthogonal to the filter-receipt story and requires a new `ViewType::EmojiPicker` variant plus 4-entry-point routing in `src/window_resize/mod.rs`. (d) The dead file `src/app_impl/startup_new_arrow.rs` that Pass #35 noted is still on disk — deletion is forbidden by scope.md absent an explicit opt-in, even though its stale comment could mislead a future reader; the Pass #35 comment fix in `src/render_builtins/emoji_picker.rs` is the safe in-scope mitigation.
- **Budget receipts**: `.audit-pause` absent, dev-watch healthy (pid 17907, forwarder alive), elapsed ~6h 35m of the 10h Run 2 window (~3h 25m remaining), scope.md rules honored (no forbidden actions, one commit per pass, prompt-style message format, no git-history rewrites, no file deletions).
- **Notes**: This pass closes the live-verification gap that Passes #33 (protocol field) and #34 (stdin routing) could not on their own — each of those was a source-level lock on an individual piece of the pipeline, but neither exercised the full end-to-end path on the specific surface Pass #14 flagged. The three-pass sequence (`#33` → `#34` → `#36`) forms a layered defense on `empty-clipboard-state`: protocol ↔ routing ↔ consumer-arm shape, with CI coverage at every layer and one live receipt per run to anchor the acceptance clause. Remaining Run 2 budget (~3h 25m) is available for: (a) an `emoji-picker-compact-window` story closing the user's Pass #35 parallel complaint about window sizing, (b) a `getElements`-filter-aware story closing `empty-clipboard-state` sub-gap (4), or (c) cross-surface stress tests not yet explored. No follow-up from this pass is time-critical.

## Run 2 Pass 35 — 2026-04-17T09:55:00Z — `emoji-picker-up-down-arrow-nav` (fix shipped, live-verified, contract-pinned)

- **Story**: emoji-picker-up-down-arrow-nav — *Direct user report from the operator running this audit run: "I would love for you to audit the emoji picker. It's using the old, expanded prompt and I can't use the up/down arrows to navigate around the grib of emoji," with a mid-work clarification "just fyi, I think the emoji up/down issue might be due to how I need to be focused in the input menu and pressing the up/down arrow keys. I just don't think they're registered or something?" Confirmed live: while the filter input has focus, Up/Down arrow keys produced zero change in the grid's `selectedIndex`, while Left/Right worked correctly.*
- **Why now**: User-reported bug against a high-traffic builtin (emoji picker), with a clear bounded repro path (simulateGpuiEvent arrow keys against `{type:main}`) and a concrete acceptance gate (`selectedIndex` advances by `GRID_COLS` on each Down). Higher leverage than another speculative contract pass against the remaining `empty-clipboard-state` sub-gaps.
- **Outcome**: `fix-shipped + live-verified + contract-pinned` — new Up/Down arm in `cx.intercept_keystrokes` dispatches emoji-grid navigation via `EmojiGridLayout::move_index` + `scroll_row_for_index` (category-aware row math, not naive `± GRID_COLS` arithmetic); live verification shows selectedIndex advancing `0 → 8 → 16` on two Downs and retreating `16 → 8` on one Up against `triggerBuiltin emoji-picker`.
- **Commit**: _pending — this pass_
- **Files touched**: 5 — `src/app_impl/startup.rs` (new `AppView::EmojiPickerView` arm in the Up/Down match inside `cx.intercept_keystrokes`), `src/render_builtins/emoji_picker.rs` (replaced stale comment pointing at dead file `src/app_impl/startup_new_arrow.rs` with a correct anchor to the active interceptor + this pass's contract test), `tests/emoji_picker_arrow_up_down_contract.rs` (new, 2 tests pinning arm shape + Left/Right isolation), `audits/afk/stories.md` (new generated section `Run 2 generated — emoji picker Up/Down arrow nav (2026-04-17T09:55:00Z)` with the `[x] emoji-picker-up-down-arrow-nav` story), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry), `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` (add new `EmojiUpDownNav` node + bump coverage stats).
- **Root cause**:
  - `src/app_impl/startup.rs:1035` installs a global `cx.intercept_keystrokes` handler. Left/Right emoji navigation lives in an inline `if (is_left || is_right) && no_direction_modifiers { ... }` block earlier in the handler and handles `AppView::EmojiPickerView` with single-step column arithmetic via `compute_scroll_row`.
  - The Up/Down match *immediately after* that Left/Right block had arms for every other grid/list subview (Clipboard, AppLauncher, WindowSwitcher, BrowserTabs, DesignGallery, ThemeChooser, ProcessManager, Settings, AcpHistory, BrowserHistory, DictationHistory, NotesBrowse, CurrentAppCommands, SearchAiPresets, ScriptList) but **no arm for `AppView::EmojiPickerView`** — Up/Down keystrokes fell through the catchall `_ => { /* Don't intercept arrows for other views (let normal handling work) */ }`, the GPUI Input widget (which holds focus) received the raw events, and it used them for text-cursor movement. Against an empty/short filter that's a silent no-op; against any length it moves the text cursor but never the emoji-grid `selected_index`.
  - A misleading stale comment in `src/render_builtins/emoji_picker.rs` pointed at `src/app_impl/startup_new_arrow.rs` — a **dead file** (never `include!`d, never compiled; only ever referenced via test-file `fs::read_to_string`) that DID contain a correct Up/Down arm. That dead-file reference very likely obscured the gap during prior code review — a reader sees the parallel-file arm and assumes the active file has one too.
- **Fix**: new Up/Down match arm in `src/app_impl/startup.rs`:
  ```rust
  AppView::EmojiPickerView { filter, selected_index, selected_category } => {
      let ordered = crate::emoji::filtered_ordered_emojis(filter, *selected_category);
      let filtered_len = ordered.len();
      if filtered_len == 0 { /* pin to 0 + stop_propagation + return */ }
      if *selected_index >= filtered_len { *selected_index = filtered_len - 1; }
      let layout = crate::emoji::build_emoji_grid_layout(&ordered, crate::emoji::GRID_COLS, |e| e.category);
      let direction = if is_up { EmojiNavDirection::Up } else { EmojiNavDirection::Down };
      *selected_index = layout.move_index(*selected_index, direction);
      let row = layout.scroll_row_for_index(*selected_index);
      this.emoji_scroll_handle.scroll_to_item(row, ScrollStrategy::Nearest);
      this.input_mode = InputMode::Keyboard;
      this.hovered_index = None;
      cx.notify();
      cx.stop_propagation();
  }
  ```
  Deliberately uses `build_emoji_grid_layout` + `move_index` (not `± GRID_COLS` arithmetic) so category-header rows are respected — without this, Up/Down at a category boundary would land on a header row instead of the next emoji. The `cx.stop_propagation()` is the single most important call in the arm: without it the Input widget would *also* process the keystroke and move the text cursor, making the fix only half-work.
- **Proof (contract)**: `cargo test --test emoji_picker_arrow_up_down_contract` → `test result: ok. 2 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.00s` after 10.65s compile.
  - `up_down_match_has_emoji_picker_arm_before_fallthrough` — scopes to the Up/Down block between `if (is_up || is_down) && no_direction_modifiers {` and the `// Don't intercept arrows for other views (let normal handling work)` catchall comment, then pins the arm's exact shape: destructures `filter` + `selected_index` + `selected_category`; calls `filtered_ordered_emojis` + `build_emoji_grid_layout`; uses `GRID_COLS`; dispatches via `EmojiNavDirection::{Up,Down}`; calls `.move_index(*selected_index, direction)` + `.scroll_row_for_index(*selected_index)`; calls `this.emoji_scroll_handle.scroll_to_item(row, ScrollStrategy::Nearest)`; sets `InputMode::Keyboard`; clears `hovered_index`; guards `filtered_len == 0`; ends with `cx.stop_propagation()`.
  - `left_right_emoji_arm_still_uses_compute_scroll_row_for_single_step` — pins that the separate Left/Right block still uses `compute_scroll_row`, scoping Pass #35 strictly to Up/Down so a future "cleanup" refactor doesn't gratuitously fold both branches and accidentally regress Left/Right's column-wrap semantics.
- **Proof (live)**: dev-watch required a restart to pick up the new binary — pid 95663 had been running 44m48s (pre-fix binary), so the first live attempt showed `success:true, dispatchPath:"main_deferred"` but `selectedIndex` stayed at 0 (expected regression signal that the running binary didn't yet include the arm). After `session.sh stop dev-watch && session.sh start dev-watch` (new pid 80467 against the rebuilt binary), the live receipts were:
  - `triggerBuiltin emoji-picker` → `getState` → `promptType:"emojiPicker", selectedIndex:0`
  - `simulateGpuiEvent keyDown down target=main` → response `{success:true, dispatchPath:"main_deferred"}`; `getState` after → `selectedIndex:8` (requestId `p35b-post1`)
  - Second `simulateGpuiEvent keyDown down` → `selectedIndex:16` (requestId `p35b-post2`)
  - `simulateGpuiEvent keyDown up` → `selectedIndex:8` (requestId `p35b-post3`)
  - Δ = +8 / +8 / -8 — exactly `GRID_COLS` per step, consistent with `move_index` jumping one row in the category-aware row-index space. The filter input's value stayed the empty string across all three keystrokes — confirms `cx.stop_propagation()` prevented the Input widget from additionally processing the arrow keys as text-cursor movements.
- **Out-of-scope (captured for follow-up)**: the user's parallel complaint that *"it's using the old, expanded prompt"* — the emoji picker renders at the 750×500 ScriptList window size instead of a compact Raycast-style window. Fixing that requires a new `ViewType::EmojiPicker` variant in `src/window_resize/mod.rs` plus routing through all 4 emoji entry points (`src/app_execute/builtin_execution.rs:2356` and the 3 stdin dispatchers at `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_core.rs`, `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin.rs`, `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs`). Not auto-claimed this pass — bounded Up/Down scope per scope.md's "defer instead of committing sprawl" rule. The dead file `src/app_impl/startup_new_arrow.rs` is a separate cleanup candidate but deletion is ambiguous under scope's forbidden-action list (`Deleting any file`) so deferred.
- **Budget receipts**: `.audit-pause` absent, dev-watch healthy (pid 80467, forwarder alive), elapsed ~6h 21m of the 10h Run 2 window (~3h 39m remaining), scope.md rules honored (no forbidden actions, one commit per pass, prompt-style message format).
- **Notes**: Pass #34's impl fix (commit `eadb5006f`, `Route stdin setFilter into the active subview's variant filter`) landed the `write_filter_to_current_subview` + `sync_builtin_query_state` routing for stdin `setFilter` on builtin subviews (including `EmojiPickerView`) but did NOT add its own log.md or stories.md entries. That commit's audit footprint is recorded only in git history; this Pass #35 entry is the first audit artifact appended to the log after Pass #33. Cross-reference for any future Run 2 wrap that iterates git log: Pass #34's story-slug is `set_filter_routes_to_active_subview_contract` and its contract test is `tests/set_filter_routes_to_active_subview_contract.rs`.

## Run 2 Pass 33 — 2026-04-17T09:25:00Z — `tool-state-result-visible-choice-count-contract` (contract pinned, closes `empty-clipboard-state` sub-gap 3)

- **Story**: tool-state-result-visible-choice-count-contract — *Generated this pass. Pass #14 surfaced four distinct tooling gaps blocking `empty-clipboard-state`. Sub-gap (3) was that the story's acceptance clause `getState.choiceCount=0` is structurally unreachable: `choiceCount` reports the total dataset regardless of active filter. The filter-aware field `visibleChoiceCount` already exists on `StateResult` but nothing in the source-level test suite pins its variant declaration, its `#[serde(rename = "visibleChoiceCount")]` camelCase JSON contract, or the constructor plumbing that forwards it into the `Message::StateResult` literal. A mechanical refactor — or a well-intentioned "cleanup" merging the two counts into one — could silently break every automation harness that currently distinguishes "dataset empty" (both zero) from "filter matched nothing" (total > 0, visible == 0). Ship a source-level contract test locking all three invariants, and correct the `empty-clipboard-state` story's sub-gap (3) note to record the structural closure.*
- **Outcome**: `[x]` — tool-extension contract pass shipped; sub-gap 3 of `empty-clipboard-state` structurally closed.
- **Commit**: `<sha>` *(filled in after commit)*
- **Files touched**: 4 changed (1 new contract test, 1 lat.md update, 2 audit artifact updates)
  - NEW `tests/state_result_visible_choice_count_contract.rs` — 3 tests covering variant declaration (`choice_count: usize,` + `visible_choice_count: usize,` as distinct fields), camelCase JSON renames (`#[serde(rename = "choiceCount")]` + `#[serde(rename = "visibleChoiceCount")]`), and constructor plumbing (`Message::state_result` accepts both as distinct `usize` args AND forwards `visible_choice_count` into the struct literal).
  - `lat.md/protocol.md` — added one paragraph to the `Query and introspection` section: "`stateResult` carries both `choiceCount` (total dataset) and `visibleChoiceCount` (filter-aware). Automation harnesses verifying empty-filter-result acceptance clauses must key on `visibleChoiceCount`, never `choiceCount` — the latter is unchanged by the active filter and so cannot drop to zero on a filter miss. Both field names are pinned at source level in `tests/state_result_visible_choice_count_contract.rs`."
  - `audits/afk/stories.md` — added new "Run 2 generated — stateResult filter-count contract (2026-04-17T09:25:00Z)" section with the Pass #33 story as `[x]` + its acceptance + closure receipt; amended the `empty-clipboard-state [!]` story to append a Pass #33 sub-gap 3 closure note pointing at the new contract test + correct acceptance field name.
  - `audits/afk/log.md` — prepended this Pass #33 entry.
  - `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` — added `ToolStateResultVisibleCount["tool-state-result-visible-choice-count-contract ✅"]:::pass` node to the Tools subgraph; bumped coverage stats from "43 pass" → "44 pass" (gap count unchanged at 1 — the parent `empty-clipboard-state` story remains `[!]` with 3 sub-gaps still open, so it still counts as one gap).
- **Proof**:
  - `cargo test --test state_result_visible_choice_count_contract` → `test result: ok. 3 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.00s` after 19.25s compile.
  - `lat check` → `All checks passed` (the updated protocol.md paragraph stays under the 250-char leading-paragraph budget; the test file has no `@lat:` wiki-link rot because the three `@lat: [[lat.md/protocol#Protocol#Query and introspection]]` refs resolve cleanly).
  - Live dev-watch sanity: `session.sh rpc default '{"type":"getState","requestId":"p33-baseline"}'` → `{promptType:"none", choiceCount:461, visibleChoiceCount:476, windowVisible:false}` — confirms both fields are emitted side-by-side on the live wire (the observed `visibleChoiceCount > choiceCount` on `promptType:"none"` is an orthogonal pre-existing observation about the no-prompt state and is NOT in scope for this contract pass; the contract test only pins field presence + naming + plumbing, not relative magnitudes).
- **Budget receipts**: `.audit-pause` absent, dev-watch healthy (pid 17907, forwarder alive), elapsed ~5h 46m of the 10h Run 2 window, scope.md rules honored (no forbidden actions, one commit per pass, prompt-style message format).
- **Notes**: No source-code changes to the protocol itself — this is a pure contract-pinning + documentation pass. The three remaining sub-gaps of `empty-clipboard-state` are candidates for future passes: (1) add `ExternalCommand::ClearClipboardHistory {test_only: true}` gated to dev-watch (destructive — needs explicit user opt-in), (2) route `setFilter` to the currently-active prompt view's filter input when `promptType != scriptList` (app-behavior change, medium scope), (4) have `getElements` respect the active filter so empty-state renders can be proven via a stable semanticId (app-behavior change, medium scope). ## Run 2 Pass 32 — 2026-04-17T09:35:00Z — `tool-paste-clipboard-into-acp-composer` (tool shipped, closes `clipboard-to-acp-paste [!]`)

- **Story**: tool-paste-clipboard-into-acp-composer — *Generated this pass. Pass #30 closed `clipboard-to-acp-paste`'s PRIMARY blocker at substrate level (simulateGpuiEvent Enter against clipboard-history now succeeds + is pinned). Pass #31 pinned the composer's paste receiver (`paste_text_from_clipboard`) invariants at source level. The remaining gap was the automation substrate: there was NO path to actually INVOKE the pinned receiver against the ACP composer — `simulateKey cmd+v` routes through CGEvent to the OS frontmost app (the invoking terminal during automation runs), and `clipboard-history`'s accept action similarly targets the OS frontmost. Ship `ExternalCommand::PasteClipboardIntoAcp { requestId }` to bypass the OS frontmost heuristic entirely and close the paste-to-ACP half of the story.*
- **Why now**: `clipboard-to-acp-paste [!]` was the last open `[!]` cross-surface workflow story with a bounded substrate gap. Pass #30 + #31 together had built up all the prerequisites (pinned main-window re-acquire + pinned paste-receiver invariants); this pass is the natural next step to wire the tool that lets live verification actually exercise both. Alternative was `empty-clipboard-state [!]` but that has 4 sub-gaps vs. this story's 1, so higher leverage.
- **Outcome**: `tool-shipped + live-verified` — new `ExternalCommand::PasteClipboardIntoAcp` stdin command closes `clipboard-to-acp-paste [!]` via live round-trip: `echo "hello p32 round-trip" | pbcopy` → `show` → `openAi` (opens ACP Chat with `status:idle`) → `pasteClipboardIntoAcp` (fire-and-forget) → `getAcpState.inputText == "@cmd:\"Theme Designer\" hello p32 round-trip\n"` with cursor at index 43 (jumped +21 chars = `"hello p32 round-trip\n".len()`, proving character-for-character landing). Tracing `stdin_acp_command_received command=pasteClipboardIntoAcp` → `stdin_acp_command_finished status=success` 1ms later.
- **Commit**: _pending — this pass_
- **Files touched**: 9 — `src/stdin_commands/mod.rs` (new `PasteClipboardIntoAcp` variant + routing in `request_id()` + `command_type()`), `src/ai/acp/view.rs` (promoted `paste_text_from_clipboard` to `pub(crate)`), `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs` + `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin.rs` + `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_tail.rs` (handler arm in all 3 dispatchers — triple-embedded stdin pattern per memories 6330/6331), `tests/paste_clipboard_into_acp_contract.rs` (new, 5 tests), `audits/afk/stories.md` (new generated section + flipped `clipboard-to-acp-paste` from `[!]` to `[x]`), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry), `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` (new Tools subgraph node + flipped ClipboardAcp from ⚠️ to ✅ + stats bump).
- **Proof**: 5 contract tests pass (`cargo test --test paste_clipboard_into_acp_contract` → `test result: ok. 5 passed; 0 failed`); live `getAcpState` receipt `"@cmd:\"Theme Designer\" hello p32 round-trip\n"` with `cursorIndex:43`; tracing receipts `stdin_acp_command_received` + `stdin_acp_command_finished status=success` within 1ms (request_id `p32-paste`). `cargo build` clean (45.64s); `lat check` green; `cargo fmt` applied (test file re-flowed one line).
- **Integration**: the full `clipboard-to-acp-paste` user story (clipboardHistory → filter → accept → tab-ai → paste → composer shows phrase) now has a deterministic automation path: swap the original `Cmd+V`-to-frontmost step for `pasteClipboardIntoAcp` targeting the composer directly. Pass #31's contract test still guards the receiver's tokenization/threshold/alias behavior, so the two passes together form a durable end-to-end substrate: tool ships the entry, contract pins the processing.
- **Notes**: doc-comment on the new variant explicitly anchors the design intent (`bypassing the OS Cmd+V heuristic` + `routes pastes to the frontmost app`), and the contract test's 5th assertion pins those two phrases so a future refactor that accidentally swaps the implementation for a `CGEvent cmd+v` send (which would re-enter the frontmost trap) would leave the comment lying and trip the test. Error discrimination is load-bearing: `"ACP chat view is not active"` vs. `"clipboard is empty or text fetch failed"` lets automation harnesses distinguish routing failures from clipboard failures — conflating them would mask broken arboard/clipboard access as wrong-view errors.

## Run 2 Pass 31 — 2026-04-17T09:02:00Z — `acp-composer-paste-text-invariants` (contract shipped, composer-receiver-pin)

- **Story**: acp-composer-paste-text-invariants — *Generated this pass. Pass #30 closed `clipboard-to-acp-paste`'s PRIMARY blocker (simulateGpuiEvent Enter against clipboard-history) end-to-end at substrate level, but the paste-to-ACP half is still unverified — clipboard-history's accept pastes to the OS-frontmost app (the invoking terminal during automation runs), not the ACP composer. The full-flow verification needs either a frontmost-ordering trick (open tab-ai BEFORE clipboard-history) or a new `ExternalCommand::PasteClipboardInto {target:"acp"}` substrate. Both paths land in `view.rs::paste_text_from_clipboard` — so pinning the composer's paste receiver at source level gives any future full-flow verification a locked-down target. Independent of `clipboard-to-acp-paste`, the receiver has LOAD-BEARING INVARIANTS that lat.md documents: CRLF normalization, empty-text short-circuit, threshold funnel, `TextBlock` context-part shape, typed-mention alias registration, `@text:"Pasted text #n"` token format + documented thresholds (8 lines / 600 chars). Any silent drift here would make the lat.md documentation a lie AND would break portal preview rendering (portal_contract.rs keys on the `TextBlock` shape).*
- **Why now**: Only 2 `[!]` stories remain (`clipboard-to-acp-paste`, `empty-clipboard-state`) and both have deeper tool-gap blockers. Rather than re-attempt the full clipboard-to-acp-paste flow this pass (risky — would paste clipboard content into the invoking terminal) or open the multi-sub-gap `empty-clipboard-state` investigation, generate an orthogonal bounded story that RECOVERS the paste-to-ACP substrate gain (by source-pinning the receiver side) without needing net-new substrate or live verification.
- **Outcome**: `contract-shipped` — source-level contract test `tests/acp_composer_paste_text_contract.rs` (6 tests, all pass in 0.00s after 22s build) pins the `paste_text_from_clipboard` handler + thresholds + token format + `prepare_pasted_text` purity. No source changes to `src/ai/acp/view.rs` or `src/pasted_text.rs`; the invariants are already in place, this pass locks them against mechanical-refactor drift.
- **Commit**: _pending — this pass_
- **Files touched**: 4 — `tests/acp_composer_paste_text_contract.rs` (new), `audits/afk/stories.md` (new generated section with `[x] acp-composer-paste-text-invariants` entry), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry), `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` (new ACP node `AcpComposerPaste ✅` + stats bump to 41 pass).
- **Investigation**:
  - `lat search "ACP composer paste clipboard"` returned `[[lat.md/acp-chat#ACP Chat#ACP composer]]` as the top match. `lat section` on that path confirmed lat.md documents: "Large clipboard text pastes collapse into inline `@text:\"Pasted text #n +...\"` tokens instead of flooding the composer. ACP keeps the full pasted payload in a `TextBlock` context part..." — i.e., the invariants this pass pins are lat.md-load-bearing.
  - `grep -rn "paste_text_from_clipboard" tests/` → no existing contract or unit tests referencing the handler. Gap confirmed.
  - Read `src/ai/acp/view.rs:1164-1205` (the handler) and `src/pasted_text.rs:1-90` (thresholds + prepare fn). Confirmed the shapes documented in lat.md are literally present in source.
- **Contract test** at `tests/acp_composer_paste_text_contract.rs` (6 tests, `@lat: [[lat.md/acp-chat#ACP Chat#ACP composer]]`):
  1. `paste_text_from_clipboard_uses_arboard_and_normalizes_line_endings` — pins signature `fn paste_text_from_clipboard(&mut self, cx: &mut Context<Self>) -> bool`, `arboard::Clipboard::new()`, `clipboard.get_text()`, and the exact `.replace("\r\n", "\n").replace('\r', "\n")` CRLF normalization in that order.
  2. `paste_text_from_clipboard_short_circuits_on_empty_text` — pins the `if normalized.is_empty() { return false; }` guard before `prepare_pasted_text`. Regression here would register zero-length typed-mention aliases.
  3. `paste_text_from_clipboard_routes_through_prepare_and_inserts_at_cursor` — pins `crate::pasted_text::prepare_pasted_text(&normalized, &self.pasted_text_tokens)` call seam + `thread.input.insert_str(&insertion_text)` insertion primitive + `self.live_thread().update(cx, move |thread, cx|` wrap. Regressions would either bifurcate threshold logic, skip undo-stack entry, or miss the `cx.notify()` composer re-render trigger.
  4. `paste_text_from_clipboard_registers_token_as_text_block_context_part` — pins `AiContextPart::TextBlock {`, `clipboard://pasted-text/` URI prefix, `mime_type: Some("text/plain".to_string())`, `self.pasted_text_tokens.push(token.clone())`, `self.typed_mention_aliases.insert(token.token, part)`, `self.sync_inline_mentions(cx)`. Regressions would break portal_contract.rs preview-description lookup + stop @chip re-render.
  5. `paste_text_thresholds_and_token_format_match_lat_md_documentation` — pins `const PASTED_TEXT_LINE_THRESHOLD: usize = 8;`, `const PASTED_TEXT_CHAR_THRESHOLD: usize = 600;`, `format!("@text:\"{label}\"")`, `Pasted text #` label prefix. Drifting any constant or format without updating lat.md makes the documentation a lie.
  6. `prepare_pasted_text_is_pure_and_respects_existing_tokens` — pins `pub(crate) fn prepare_pasted_text(text: &str, existing_tokens: &[PastedTextToken]) -> PreparedPastedText` signature + `next_token_index(existing_tokens)` consumption. Ensures the composer remains the only mutator of `self.pasted_text_tokens` — any write-side escape would make duplicate `Pasted text #N` labels possible across multiple pastes.
- **Build + test evidence**: `cargo test --test acp_composer_paste_text_contract` → `running 6 tests ... test result: ok. 6 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.00s`. First run produced two `invalid format string` compile errors (unescaped `{` / `}` in `assert!` panic messages at lines 98 and 157); fixed by escaping as `{{` / `}}` in the message strings — `assert!` treats the message argument as a format string. `cargo fmt` applied (split one wrapped `.contains(...)` call across two lines). `lat check` → `All checks passed` (6 new `@lat: [[lat.md/acp-chat#ACP Chat#ACP composer]]` refs resolve against the existing canonical section).
- **Substrate win**: The paste-to-ACP receiver side of `clipboard-to-acp-paste [!]` is now source-pinned. When the full-flow story is re-attempted — via frontmost-reorder trick or new `PasteClipboardInto` substrate — the composer's paste behavior is CI-gated; any diff that changes the paste-receiver contract trips the contract test at `cargo test` time instead of showing up as a silent paste-to-ACP regression. Complementary to Pass #30 (which pinned the dispatcher side of the substrate).
- **Notes**: Dev-watch session status not exercised this pass — source-level contract testing needs no live app. Budget ~5h 32m elapsed at commit time (4h 28m remaining). No kill switch.

This is the end-to-end proof that `apply_simulated_event(window, cx, &event_for_spawn)` was actually invoked on the next tick inside `cx.update_window`; if the deferred spawn had been dropped, the window would still be visible and focused.
