## Run 2 Pass 29 — 2026-04-17T08:28:00Z — `tool-trigger-action-acpdetached-host` (fix shipped, tool-extension)

- **Story**: tool-trigger-action-acpdetached-host — *Pass #28 carry-forward. Once a detached ACP window exists (stable `acpDetached:<uuid>` id), automation has no path to close it: `triggerAction acp_close host=main|acpChat` all returned `sdk_fallback no_effect` because the main view's `handle_action` maps `acp_close` to `close_tab_ai_harness_terminal` (closes the TabAI harness, not the detached window), while the detached window's OWN close handler lives in `src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs:1024` and is only reachable when the popup is opened from INSIDE the detached window. This pass adds the missing host routing so external TriggerAction can reach the detached dispatcher.*
- **Outcome**: fix shipped (new enum variant + parser arm + router arm + public helper + close-registry-cleanup fix + 5-test contract + live end-to-end receipt).
- **Investigation**:
  - `lat search` on the routing topic surfaced `actions#Shared actions dialog` and `automation#Automation#Window metadata`; read-through confirmed no existing TriggerAction contract test that I'd have to extend.
  - `grep "Some(\"acp" src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs` showed the full host match block only accepts `acpChat` / `acpHistory` — parser gap confirmed.
  - Read `ActionsDialogHost` enum at `src/main_sections/app_view_state.rs:347-380` + `execute_action_for_actions_host` at `src/app_impl/actions_dialog.rs:141-176` — confirmed all detached-host requests currently fall through `_ => self.handle_action(action_id, window, cx)` to the main view.
  - Read detached dispatcher at `src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs:826-1034` — confirmed `dispatch_detached_action` already handles `acp_close`, `acp_reattach_panel`, `acp_expand_all`, `acp_collapse_all`, and model-switch actions; simply needs an external entry point that forwards to `dispatch_detached_action_checked`.
  - `grep "close_chat_window\|on_close" src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs` exposed the sequencing bug: the `chat_window_options` on_close callback removes from both `remove_runtime_window_handle` AND `remove_automation_window`, but only when `CHAT_WINDOW.slot` still has state — `close_chat_window` `.take()`s it first so the callback's cleanup branch never runs; the automation registry was leaking detached entries on close.
- **Fix shipped**:
  - `src/main_sections/app_view_state.rs` — added `ActionsDialogHost::AcpDetached` variant with doc comment explaining the routing rationale.
  - `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs:2635` — added `Some("acpDetached") => Some(ActionsDialogHost::AcpDetached)` between the existing `acpChat` and unknown-host fallback arms.
  - `src/app_impl/actions_dialog.rs:172` — added explicit `ActionsDialogHost::AcpDetached =>` arm BEFORE the wildcard fallback, calls `crate::ai::acp::chat_window::dispatch_action_to_detached(&action_id, cx)` and logs `event=actions_host_execute_acp_detached action_id dispatched`.
  - `src/app_impl/actions_toggle.rs:155` — added `AcpDetached => "AcpDetached"` in the host-label helper.
  - `src/app_impl/actions_dialog.rs:201` — added defensive-only `AcpDetached => false` arm in `toggle_actions_for_host` (main view never advertises this host; the detached window has its own Cmd+K via `toggle_detached_actions`).
  - `src/app_impl/actions_dialog.rs:576` — grouped `AcpDetached` with the `main_filter` focus-restore fallback (safe default; detached owns its own focus restoration).
  - `src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs` — new `pub fn dispatch_action_to_detached(action_id: &str, cx: &mut App) -> bool` that retrieves the detached view's WeakEntity from `CHAT_WINDOW` and forwards to `dispatch_detached_action_checked`, returning `false` if no detached window exists so callers can distinguish a dropped dispatch from a successful one.
  - `src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs::close_chat_window` — added `remove_automation_window(id)` alongside `remove_runtime_window_handle(id)` before `window.remove_window()` (with explanatory comment about the `CHAT_WINDOW.slot.take()` sequencing hazard).
- **Contract test**: `tests/trigger_action_acp_detached_host_contract.rs` (5 tests): (1) enum variant + doc comment anchor; (2) parser arm `Some("acpDetached") => Some(ActionsDialogHost::AcpDetached)`; (3) router calls `dispatch_action_to_detached`; (4) helper signature + delegation to `dispatch_detached_action_checked`; (5) detached dispatcher's `"acp_close"` arm still calls `close_chat_window` and emits `event="detached_action_close"`.
- **Proof (live receipts against dev-watch pid 5504)**:
  - Baseline `listAutomationWindows` → `[{id:"main", visible:false, semanticSurface:"scriptList"}]` — 1 window.
  - `triggerBuiltin tab-ai` + `triggerAction host=acpChat action_id=acp_detach_window` → 2 windows: `main` (visible:false) + `acpDetached:3b93ece0-888b-4e0e-8980-e44ed151d7e9` (focused:true, visible:true, semanticSurface:"acpChat").
  - **Close via new host** `triggerAction host=acpDetached action_id=acp_close` — app.log chain:
    - `TriggerAction: host=AcpDetached action_id='acp_close' popup_open=false` (parser accepted)
    - `event=actions_host_execute host=AcpDetached action_id=acp_close` (router matched)
    - `Selected ACP Actions Menu item event=acp_actions_menu_selected host=detached action_id=acp_close` (`dispatch_detached_action` entered)
    - `automation.runtime_handle_removed window_id=acpDetached:3b93ece0-...` (runtime handle gone)
    - `Window state saved successfully` + `Saved acp_chat bounds: (24, 498) 480x440` (bounds persisted)
    - `event=detached_action_close` (close arm executed)
    - `event=actions_host_execute_acp_detached action_id=acp_close dispatched=true` (Pass #29 instrumentation confirms full round-trip)
  - Final `listAutomationWindows` → `[{id:"main", visible:false, semanticSurface:"scriptList"}]` — detached gone from BOTH the OS (`window.remove_window()`) AND the automation registry (`remove_automation_window`). No zombie entry.
  - Cleanup: app already hidden via earlier `getState.windowVisible:false`.
- **Files touched**: 7 files (6 source + 1 new test + 3 docs).
  - `src/main_sections/app_view_state.rs` (+5 lines — enum variant)
  - `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs` (+3 lines — host parser arm)
  - `src/app_impl/actions_dialog.rs` (+19 lines across 3 match sites)
  - `src/app_impl/actions_toggle.rs` (+1 line — host label)
  - `src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs` (+38 lines — new helper + close-path registry cleanup)
  - `tests/trigger_action_acp_detached_host_contract.rs` (+164 lines — 5 tests)
  - `audits/afk/stories.md` — new story in "Run 2 generated — detached-cleanup tool extension" section marked `[x]` with full receipt.
  - `audits/afk/log.md` — this entry.
  - `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` — stats delta.
- **LAT updates**: none required. The new helper is automation-internal routing; `[[lat.md/automation#Automation#Window metadata]]` already documents the upsert/remove contract that `close_chat_window`'s registry cleanup now correctly honors from every entry point. `lat check` green.
- **Notes**:
  - The `close_chat_window` sequencing fix is the deeper change: it makes the detached-window close idempotent regardless of who fires first (titlebar button's on_close vs external TriggerAction). Before Pass #29, closing the detached window via its titlebar Cmd+W was correct (on_close ran, cleaned both registries), but closing via the helper (e.g. from `acp_reattach_panel` — which ALSO calls `close_chat_window`) left the automation registry stale. The fix fixes BOTH close paths, not just Pass #29's new TriggerAction path.
  - Audit-hygiene win: detached windows created mid-pass no longer leak across passes. Before Pass #29, an audit pass that opened a detached window without closing it would persist into subsequent passes (dev-watch restart required to clear). After Pass #29, every close path (titlebar, helper, new TriggerAction) tears down both the window AND the registry entry.
  - The detached window's `toggle_detached_actions` Cmd+K handler at `src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs:582` is unchanged — in-window action popups still route through the same `dispatch_detached_action_checked` that the new external helper calls. External and in-window callers share one dispatch decision.
  - Coverage diagram delta: `DetachRoundtrip` was already `✅` from Pass #28. Pass #29 adds the new `ToolTriggerActionAcpDetached` node under the Tools subgraph as a new `✅ tool` to make the closed gap visible.

## Run 2 Pass 28 — 2026-04-17T08:15:00Z — `detached-acp-roundtrip` (live-verified, tool-gap close)

- **Story**: detached-acp-roundtrip — *Pass #17 recorded this as a tool gap because (a) `simulateKey enter` over the actions popup didn't route to `submit_selected_action` on AcpChatView and (b) there was no way to observe the detached window from automation. Run 2 Pass #1 (`tool-actions-popup-enter`) added `show_actions_popup && key_lower == "enter"` arms to every parent view, Run 2 Pass #2 (`tool-trigger-action-command`) shipped `ExternalCommand::TriggerAction {action_id, host}`, and Run 2 Pass #7 added `upsert_automation_window` in `src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs` so detached ACP appears in `listAutomationWindows`. Pass #28 re-runs the original story live to confirm all three upstream fixes close the gap end-to-end.*
- **Outcome**: pass (live-verified end-to-end; no source change — substrate was shipped by Run 2 Passes #1/#2/#7).
- **Investigation**: minimal. Baseline confirmed detached absent, then `triggerAction acp_detach_window` created the detached window with a stable UUID'd automation id. Target-routed `getAcpState` proved thread isolation. Re-entry proved the no-dupe invariant.
- **Fix / code shipped**: no source change. stories.md `[!]` → `[x]` + log entry + diagram flip = full scope.
- **Proof (live receipts against dev-watch pid 28957)**:
  - p28-base `listAutomationWindows` → `[{id:"main", visible:false, semanticSurface:"scriptList"}]` — 1 window, no detached.
  - p28-show `show` + p28-tabai `triggerBuiltin name=tab-ai` + p28-acpstate `getAcpState` → `{resolvedTarget.windowId:"main", inputText:"@cmd:\"Theme Designer\" ", contextChipCount:1}` (Pass #24 carryover chip).
  - p28-cmdk `simulateKey cmd+k` + p28-wins1 → 2 windows: `{id:"main", visible:true}` + `{id:"actions-dialog", kind:"actionsDialog", parentWindowId:"main"}` — popup opened over main.
  - p28-els2 `getElements target={kind:"actionsDialog"}` → 22 ACP actions including `choice:19:acp_detach_window` text="Keep Open in Window" (not "Detach" — label distinction worth noting for future filter patterns).
  - **Detach**: p28-ta `triggerAction action_id=acp_detach_window host=main` (fire-and-forget) + p28-wins2 `listAutomationWindows` → 2 windows:
    - `{id:"main", kind:"main", focused:false, visible:true, semanticSurface:"scriptList"}` — main reverts to scriptList.
    - `{id:"acpDetached:cfbae5a7-ee59-4ba9-ba8c-f0f9823d2dc5", kind:"acpDetached", focused:true, visible:true, semanticSurface:"acpChat"}` — detached window created with stable UUID.
  - **Thread isolation (story acceptance clause 1)**: p28-detin `batch target={kind:"acpDetached",index:0} setInput "hello from detached window"` + p28-det-acp `getAcpState target={kind:"acpDetached",index:0}` → `{resolvedTarget.windowId:"acpDetached:cfbae5a7-...", windowKind:"acpDetached", inputText:"hello from detached window", cursorIndex:26, contextChipCount:1}` (chip inherited via transfer from embedded).
  - **Main has no ACP (story acceptance clause 2)**: p28-main-acp `getAcpState target={kind:"main",index:0}` → `{resolvedTarget.windowId:"main", windowKind:"main", status:"notAcp", inputText:"", messageCount:0, contextChipCount:0}` — **main does not contain the detached text; status:"notAcp" proves the ACP surface has moved out of main**.
  - **No-dupe on re-entry (story acceptance clause 3)**: p28-tabai2 `triggerBuiltin name=tab-ai` + p28-wins3 → same 2 windows, detached id UNCHANGED (`acpDetached:cfbae5a7-ee59-4ba9-ba8c-f0f9823d2dc5`), focus transferred to detached (`focusedWindowId:"acpDetached:..."`), main stays `scriptList`. Entry-path contract (`If a detached ACP window is already open, the app focuses that window instead of opening a second one`) holds at the automation level.
  - Cleanup: p28-cl-close triggerAction acp_close (no-effect — see cleanup gap below), p28-cl-hide hide main → `getState.windowVisible:false`. Detached window persists through hide of main.
- **Files touched**: 3 docs = 3 files (0 source).
  - `audits/afk/stories.md` — flipped `detached-acp-roundtrip` from `[!]` to `[x]` with full Pass #28 receipt.
  - `audits/afk/log.md` — this entry.
  - `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` — flipped `DetachRoundtrip` from `⚠️ gap` to `✅ pass`, updated coverage stats to 38 pass / 2 gap.
- **LAT updates**: none needed. `lat check` green. The relevant `[[lat.md/acp-chat#ACP Chat#Detached window behavior]]` section already documents the entry-path and host-isolation invariants this story verifies.
- **Notes**:
  - **Detached-cleanup-via-automation gap discovered** (carry-forward observation, NOT a Pass #28 blocker): Closing the detached window via automation is not reachable. Attempted paths ALL failed: (1) `simulateKey cmd+w` via CGEvent was absorbed but didn't close (OS focus not on detached despite `focused:true` in registry); (2) `simulateGpuiEvent target={kind:acpDetached} event={keyDown cmd+w}` landed synchronously (`success:true, dispatchPath:"exact_handle"`) but didn't close; (3) `triggerAction acp_close host=main` routed to `host=MainList action=acp_close`, which hit `Unknown SDK action ... handler=sdk_fallback status=no_effect`; (4) `triggerAction acp_close host=acpChat` routed to `host=AcpChat` but same `sdk_fallback no_effect` result; (5) `triggerAction acp_reattach_panel` same fallback. Root cause: `acp_close` and `acp_reattach_panel` are built-in ACP UI actions handled by the detached window's OWN action dispatcher, not the MainList or AcpChat shared-actions hosts. `execute_action_for_actions_host` doesn't route to the detached window's own host. **Proposed follow-up story**: `tool-trigger-action-acpdetached-host` — extend the `ActionsDialogHost` enum with `AcpDetached` and route `triggerAction host=acpDetached` to the detached window's action dispatcher (wherever `acp_close` / `acp_reattach_panel` actually handle their own events when invoked via the popup from inside the detached window). Alternative: add `ExternalCommand::CloseDetachedAcp {window_id: Option<String>}` gated to the agentic runtime that directly calls the close-helper in `src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs`. Without one of these, detached windows created during an audit pass persist across passes until dev-watch restart. This is a cleanup-only limitation; it does not affect story acceptance.
  - Label clarity observation: the action is `acp_detach_window` / `acp_reattach_panel` but the UI labels say "Keep Open in Window" / "Return to Panel". Future filter-by-label automation should target either the id or the literal UI text, not semantic guesses like "detach" — filtering for "detach" returned 0 actions.
  - The detached window kind id format is `acpDetached:<uuid>` (e.g. `acpDetached:cfbae5a7-ee59-4ba9-ba8c-f0f9823d2dc5`), a stable-per-detach UUID. Re-entry via `triggerBuiltin tab-ai` preserves this id (proving the same AcpThread/view). For target-routed commands like `getAcpState target={kind:"acpDetached",index:0}`, the `index:0` form works — the automation registry indexes by kind.

## Run 2 Pass 27 — 2026-04-17T08:08:54Z — `native-input-focus-delta` (live-verified, tool-gap close)

- **Story**: native-input-focus-delta — *Pass #20 recorded this as a tooling gap because `bun scripts/agentic/window.ts find` returned `{windows:[], appRunning:true}` for the NonactivatingPanel main window, so `macos-input.ts --ensure-focus` could never confirm "target window is frontmost at key delivery". Run 2 Pass #6 shipped the Swift-based CGWindowList helper (`macos-window-query.swift` + `.optionAll` filter + accessory-aware `checkFrontmost`) that makes the NSPanel visible to the enumerator. This pass re-runs the original story live to confirm the gap is closed end-to-end.*
- **Outcome**: pass (live-verified end-to-end; no code change — the substrate was already in place).
- **Investigation**: none beyond state checks. The `--ensure-focus` receipt contract was already sound in Pass #20 — only the environmental query was broken, and Pass #6 fixed it. Precondition check `bun scripts/agentic/window.ts find --title "Script Kit" --json` returned the NonactivatingPanel at index 0: `{windowId:54594, ownerName:"script-kit-gpui", title:"", layer:101, frontmost:true, focused:true, visible:true, bounds:{x:410, y:180, w:750, h:500}}`, proving Pass #6's Swift helper now sees the panel that CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo's default filter hid.
- **Fix / code shipped**: no source change. stories.md `[!]` → `[x]` + log entry + diagram flip = full scope.
- **Proof (live receipts against dev-watch pid 28957)**:
  - p27-base `listAutomationWindows` → `{id:"main", visible:false, semanticSurface:"scriptList"}`.
  - p27-show `{type:"show"}` (fire-and-forget envelope timeout expected).
  - Pre-check: `window.ts find --title "Script Kit"` → NonactivatingPanel at `{windowId:54594, frontmost:true, focused:true}`. Confirms Pass #6's helper enumerates the panel.
  - p27-tabai `{type:"triggerBuiltin", name:"tab-ai"}` + p27-acpstate `getState` → `{promptType:"acpChat", windowVisible:true, isFocused:false}`. ACP composer open as the input target.
  - **Defocus**: `osascript -e 'tell application "Finder" to activate'` + 1s settle. OS focus moves to Finder.
  - **The pass**: `bun scripts/agentic/macos-input.ts key a --ensure-focus --focus-title "Script Kit" --session dev-watch --json`. NDJSON receipt stream:
    - `focus_delegate_start {focusTitle:"Script Kit", retry:3, settleMs:200}` — focus transition initiated.
    - `focus_delegate_result {exitCode:0, frontmost:true, focused:true, windowId:54594}` — **focus transition confirmed: window is frontmost AND focused, matching windowId from precheck**.
    - `session_focus_resolved {sessionName:"dev-watch", frontmost:true, focused:true}` — session-level resolution.
    - `capability_ladder_gpui_ok {key:"a"}` — GPUI dispatch capability available (highest rung of the ladder).
    - `agentic.input_method_selected {chosenMethod:"gpuiDispatch", action:"key", key:"a"}` — key delivered via in-process GPUI dispatcher (faster + more reliable than quartz CGEvent).
  - Final envelope (schemaVersion:3):
    ```
    {status:"ok", command:"key", data:{key:"a", delivered:true, method:"gpuiDispatch",
     focusEnforced:true, receipt:{chosenMethod:"gpuiDispatch",
     fallbackReasons:[{method:"directBatch", reason:"not_applicable_for_key_events"}]},
     session:"dev-watch"}}
    ```
    **`delivered:true` + `focusEnforced:true` + `method:"gpuiDispatch"` = full story acceptance**: tool reported the focus transition in its receipt (every NDJSON event above) AND the target window was frontmost at the moment of key delivery (`focus_delegate_result.frontmost:true` precedes `capability_ladder_gpui_ok`).
  - Cleanup: p27-cleanup-esc1/esc2 escape × 2 (fire-and-forget timeout expected) → p27-cleanup-hide `hide` → p27-cleanup-state `getState` → `{promptType:"none", windowVisible:false, isFocused:false}`. Pass #21 hide-reset contract held.
- **Files touched**: 3 docs = 3 files (0 source).
  - `audits/afk/stories.md` — flipped `native-input-focus-delta` from `[!]` to `[x]` with full Pass #27 receipt.
  - `audits/afk/log.md` — this entry.
  - `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` — flipped `native-input-focus-delta` from `⚠️ gap` to `✅ pass` and updated coverage stats to 37 pass / 3 gap.
- **LAT updates**: none needed. `lat check` green.
- **Notes**:
  - Cross-surface proof chain: Pass #6 (Swift enumerator sees the NSPanel) → Pass #27 (macos-input.ts --ensure-focus uses that enumerator in its focus-delegate step) → focus transition receipt is observable end-to-end. Any future regression in Pass #6's Swift helper OR the app's NonactivatingPanel config would cause `focus_delegate_result.frontmost` or `focused` to flip to `false`, breaking this contract.
  - The `method:"gpuiDispatch"` choice is noteworthy: when the app is frontmost AND a session binding is healthy, the capability ladder skips quartz (CGEvent) in favor of direct GPUI dispatch. This is faster, synchronous, and doesn't require OS-level keystroke synthesis. Fallback to quartz would only kick in if the session handshake failed. For this story the path is fine — the focus-delta receipt is independent of the delivery method.
  - Observation: `inputText` in the ACP composer did NOT accumulate the "a" keystroke — the composer was `isFocused:false` in the pre-type state, and the key routed through the GPUI dispatcher but didn't reach the composer's text input handler. This is orthogonal to the story (which only asserts focus-delta receipts + frontmost-at-delivery, both confirmed). If a future story needs to prove "char lands in ACP composer", it will need to assert `isFocused:true` before typing, possibly via an explicit focus-composer command — flag as a downstream tooling story if relevant.
  - 3 ⚠️ gap stories remain after this pass: `detached-acp-roundtrip`, `empty-clipboard-state`, `clipboard-to-acp-paste`. `detached-acp-roundtrip` is closeable via Run 2 Pass #1/#2's detach-scaffold work; the clipboard gaps may be environmental (no clipboard history fixture). Next pass should assess `detached-acp-roundtrip`.

## Run 2 Pass 26 — 2026-04-17T08:00:50Z — `simulate-gpui-event-interceptor` (live-verified, tool-gap close)

- **Story**: simulate-gpui-event-interceptor — *Pass #12 recorded this as a tooling gap because `simulateGpuiEvent {target:{type:"main"}, event:{keyDown cmd+k}}` returned `handle_unavailable` due to a combined GPUI-reentrancy / handle-eviction bug. Run 2 Pass #5 (`tool-simulategpui-main-rearm`) shipped the fix: re-acquire the Main handle from the `OnceLock<AnyWindowHandle>` global on each dispatch, and if `handle.update` fails (reentrancy), defer via `cx.spawn + cx.update_window`. This pass re-runs the original story to confirm the gap is closed and the interceptor contract distinction is observable.*
- **Outcome**: pass (live-verified end-to-end; no code change — the substrate was already in place).
- **Investigation**: none beyond the trace. Pass #5's fix has been merged for several passes; this re-verifies end-to-end against the live session.
- **Fix / code shipped**: no source change. stories.md `[!]` → `[x]` + log entry + diagram flips = full scope.
- **Proof (live receipts against dev-watch pid 28957)**:
  - p26-b baseline `listAutomationWindows` → `{id:"main", visible:false, semanticSurface:"scriptList", bounds:{width:750,height:500}}` (750×500 carryover from an earlier subview — cosmetic).
  - p26-show `{type:"show"}` (fire-and-forget envelope timeout expected).
  - p26-gpui `{type:"simulateGpuiEvent", target:{type:"main"}, event:{type:"keyDown", key:"k", modifiers:["cmd"]}}` returned synchronous response envelope `{success:true, dispatchPath:"main_deferred", resolvedWindowId:"main"}` (**no RPC timeout — distinct from simulateKey's fire-and-forget CGEvent behavior**). App.log dispatch trace: `gpui_event_simulation.entity_received request_id=p26-gpui target=Some(Main) event=KeyDown { key: "k", modifiers: [Cmd], text: None }` → `gpui_event_simulation.dispatch request_id=p26-gpui window_id=main kind=Main event_type=keyDown` → `gpui_event_simulation.main_deferred_scheduled` → (8ms later) `gpui_event_simulation.main_deferred_complete` (cid swap from `stdin:req:p26-gpui` to the root GPUI context confirms the deferred spawn ran on a later tick).
  - p26-wins `listAutomationWindows` → 2 windows: `{id:"main", semanticSurface:"scriptList", focused:true}` + `{id:"actions-dialog", kind:"actionsDialog", parentWindowId:"main", parentKind:"main"}` — the actions dialog opened, proving the keyDown reached the main window's key handler and triggered `toggle_actions` via the real GPUI key pipeline.
  - Contrast: p26-key `{type:"simulateKey", key:"k", modifiers:["cmd"]}` (OS CGEvent, after reseting state) → p26-wins3 also showed the actions dialog open, BUT app.log showed only `SimulateKey: Cmd+K - toggle actions` from the stdin dispatcher, with NO `gpui_event_simulation.*` events. This proves the two commands traverse distinct pipelines — simulateGpuiEvent synthesizes a `gpui::KeyDownEvent` at the window dispatcher level; simulateKey generates a real OS keystroke via CGEvent that bypasses GPUI's dispatch entirely. The story's assertion — `simulateGpuiEvent` exercises the real key pipeline — is observable via the dispatch-trace divergence.
  - Cleanup: p26-cl1/cl2 escape × 2 → p26-hide → p26-fin `listAutomationWindows` → `{visible:false, semanticSurface:"scriptList"}`. Pass #21 hide-reset contract held.
- **Files touched**: 3 docs = 3 files (0 source).
  - `audits/afk/stories.md` — flipped `simulate-gpui-event-interceptor` from `[!]` to `[x]` with full Pass #26 receipt.
  - `audits/afk/log.md` — this entry.
  - `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` — flipped `simulate-gpui-event-interceptor` from `⚠️ gap` to `✅ pass` and updated coverage stats.
- **LAT updates**: none needed. `lat check` green.
- **Notes**:
  - The story's original assertion subtly split: *"`simulateKey cmd+k` bypasses the interceptor and may not [open the dialog]"* — in practice BOTH paths now open the dialog, because the stdin simulateKey dispatcher's `AppView::ScriptList` arm has its OWN `cmd+k → toggle_actions` branch (added in Run 1 before the simulateGpuiEvent work). So "opens dialog" is not the discriminator — the discriminator is the dispatch trace: simulateGpuiEvent emits `gpui_event_simulation.*` events, simulateKey emits only `SimulateKey: ...` log lines. Both correctly end at `toggle_actions`, but via different call stacks. The original story was written before this symmetry existed; the underlying interceptor proof remains intact.
  - Carry-forward observation: `listAutomationWindows` shows the main window `bounds:{width:750,height:500}` lingering after subview transitions (carryover from designGallery in earlier passes). Not a bug per se — just means `scriptList` doesn't force a size reset back to 480×440 on Pass #25's escape-hide. If this matters for later stories, flag as a future tooling story `tool-scriptlist-resize-on-hide`. (Nothing breaks; the 750px width is still within screen bounds.)

## Run 2 Pass 24 — 2026-04-17T07:52:00Z — `main-menu-cmd-enter-ai` (code, live-verified)

- **Story**: main-menu-cmd-enter-ai — *close the Pass #16 tooling gap: `simulateKey` had no Cmd+Enter arm for `AppView::ScriptList`, so automation callers could not exercise the universal "do this to that" AI entry point from the main menu. The real GPUI keybinding at `src/render_script_list/mod.rs:881-885` calls `try_route_global_cmd_enter_to_acp_context_capture`, but stdin fell through to the catch-all and executed the selected item as a plain Enter instead.*
- **Outcome**: fix shipped + source-level contract test + live-verified end-to-end.
- **Investigation**:
  - Grep `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_simulate_key.rs` for `AppView::ScriptList` → exactly one arm at line ~91 guarding `has_cmd && key_lower == "k"` (Cmd+K → toggle_actions) then falling through to fallback-mode handling and the plain `enter/escape/up/down/tab` chain. No Cmd+Enter branch.
  - Grep `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs` for the same pattern → identical embedded copy at line ~2023 (triple-embedded dispatcher footprint: both simulateKey dispatchers maintain per-view arms by hand).
  - Inspected the live GPUI handler at `src/render_script_list/mod.rs:881-885`: gate is `sk_is_key_enter(key) && event.keystroke.modifiers.platform && !shift && !alt && !control` and the body calls `self.try_route_global_cmd_enter_to_acp_context_capture(cx)`. The helper at `src/app_impl/tab_ai_mode.rs:1224` (`pub(crate) fn try_route_global_cmd_enter_to_acp_context_capture`) is a single shared routing decision between the live keybinding and anyone else who wants to mirror it.
  - Confirmed `AcpState` schema at `src/protocol/types/acp_state.rs` exposes `contextChipCount: usize` and `contextReady: bool` but NOT the `contextSummary` field referenced in the story text — verification plan had to use chip count + input text as the receipt instead.
- **Fix / code shipped**:
  - Injected Cmd+Enter arm in both simulateKey dispatchers (`src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_simulate_key.rs` and `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs`). The new else-if branch sits between the Cmd+K arm and the fallback/plain-enter chain, gated on `has_cmd && key_lower == "enter" && !has_shift && !_has_alt && !_has_ctrl` to mirror the live GPUI handler exactly. Body calls `view.try_route_global_cmd_enter_to_acp_context_capture(ctx)` — same function the live keybinding invokes, so both paths share one routing decision. Comment anchors the arm to the live handler line range so future refactors don't drift.
  - New contract test at `tests/simulate_key_cmd_enter_scriptlist_contract.rs` (3 tests, 153 lines): (1) `simulate_key_dispatchers_route_cmd_enter_in_scriptlist_to_acp_context_capture` — scans both files for the `// Main script list key handling` + `if has_cmd && key_lower == "k" {` anchor, then takes 60 lines of following source and asserts `has_cmd && key_lower == "enter" && !has_shift && !_has_alt && !_has_ctrl` and `view.try_route_global_cmd_enter_to_acp_context_capture(ctx);` all appear in the same region; (2) `simulate_key_cmd_enter_arm_precedes_plain_enter_in_scriptlist` — asserts the `SimulateKey: Cmd+Enter - route to ACP context capture` log string appears BEFORE `SimulateKey: Enter - execute selected` in both files, because the `else if` chaining is semantic — reversing the order would execute the selected item before the Cmd+Enter gate could route to ACP, silently regressing the story; (3) `try_route_global_cmd_enter_to_acp_context_capture_still_defined` — pins `pub(crate) fn try_route_global_cmd_enter_to_acp_context_capture(` in `src/app_impl/tab_ai_mode.rs` so any rename becomes a multi-file deliberate edit rather than an accidental stdin-only breakage.
- **Contract test result**: `cargo test --test simulate_key_cmd_enter_scriptlist_contract` → 3 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored, 0.00s.
- **Proof (live receipts against dev-watch pid 28957 after `session.sh stop/start` to load the rebuilt binary)**:
  - p24b1 baseline `listAutomationWindows` → `{id:"main", semanticSurface:"scriptList", focused:false, visible:false, bounds:{x:4,y:478,width:750,height:500}}` (width/height carry over from the last Pass #23 designGallery resize — irrelevant to this story).
  - p24s `{type:"show"}` (fire-and-forget envelope timeout expected); p24-pre `listAutomationWindows` → `{focused:true, visible:true, semanticSurface:"scriptList", bounds:{width:480,height:440}}`. Panel resized back to scriptList layout; baseline selectedIndex:0 applies.
  - p24ce `{type:"simulateKey", key:"enter", modifiers:["cmd"]}` (fire-and-forget; RPC envelope returned timeout as expected for key events). app.log shows the dispatcher fired the new arm: `40.678|i|S|cid=stdin:req:p24-cmd-enter SimulateKey: Cmd+Enter - route to ACP context capture` immediately followed by `40.678|i|U|cid=stdin:req:p24-cmd-enter event=tab_ai_global_cmd_enter_routed_to_acp source_view=ScriptList` — the stdin path now hits the shared routing helper.
  - p24-acp `getAcpState` → `{status:"idle", inputText:"@cmd:\"Theme Designer\" ", cursorIndex:22, contextChipCount:1, contextReady:true, messageCount:0}`. The currently-selected scriptList item ("Theme Designer" at index 0) was injected into ACP as a `@cmd:"Theme Designer"` chip — FocusedTarget context part, not plain frontmost-app context. This proves the universal "do this to that" AI entry point works from stdin exactly like the live keybinding.
  - Cleanup: escape → escape → hide → p24-final `listAutomationWindows` → `{visible:false, semanticSurface:"scriptList"}` — hide path correctly reset both the AppView and the automation semantic surface (Pass #21 contract holds).

  - `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs` — identical injection in the embedded copy

## Run 2 Pass 23 — 2026-04-17T07:37:30Z — `tool-design-gallery-triggerbuiltin` (code, live-verified)

- **Story**: tool-design-gallery-triggerbuiltin — *lock down the remaining subview coverage gap from Pass #22's "What is NOT yet covered" callout. The design-gallery arm already exists in all three dispatchers, but no story verifies it, so the arm's presence is load-bearing-by-coincidence and the `DesignGalleryView => "designGallery"` map entry has no test gating it.*
- **Outcome**: pass (source-level contract test + live-verified).
- **Why generate**: Pass #22 closed `windowSwitcher`; `designGallery` was the only remaining item in the main-launcher diagram's "What is NOT yet covered" section. With zero `[ ]` stories in stories.md after Pass #22, generating this story from the diagram's explicit gap callout follows scope.md § 5 ("generate 1–2 new ambitious stories of the same quality bar") and keeps the subview backlog clean.
- **Investigation**:
  - Grep across `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_core.rs`, `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin.rs`, `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs` for `design-gallery` / `DesignGallery` → 6 matches (2 per file: arm head `"design-gallery" | "designgallery" | "design gallery" =>` + `view.current_view = AppView::DesignGalleryView`). Confirmed the arm exists identically in all three dispatchers at lines 187/276/1845.
  - Grep `src/main_sections/app_view_state.rs` for `DesignGallery` → 2 matches: variant declaration at line 127 (`DesignGalleryView { filter, selected_index }`) and semantic-surface mapping at line 271 (`AppView::DesignGalleryView { .. } => "designGallery"`). Full infrastructure is in place; what's missing is a test that fails if any piece drifts.
  - Inspected the existing arm body (5 lines per dispatcher): `view.current_view = AppView::DesignGalleryView { filter: String::new(), selected_index: 0 }; view.update_window_size_deferred(window, ctx);` — no loader, no cache field (design gallery self-populates from bundled assets), so the contract test intentionally omits any `list_*()` / cache-field assertion that the windowSwitcher contract includes.
- **Fix / code shipped**:
  - No source fix required — dispatcher wiring is correct. Tool extension is the contract test itself, plus the stories.md/log.md/diagrams/lat.md documentation updates that make the arm's correctness auditable going forward.
  - `tests/design_gallery_triggerbuiltin_contract.rs` (new, 3 tests): (1) `triggerbuiltin_dispatchers_route_design_gallery_to_appview` — scans all three dispatchers for arm presence and required body lines (view flip + `filter: String::new()` + `selected_index: 0` + `update_window_size_deferred`); (2) `triggerbuiltin_dispatchers_include_design_gallery_aliases` — pins the three aliases (`"design-gallery"`, `"designgallery"`, `"design gallery"`) so dropping any one silently breaks a subset of callers; (3) `design_gallery_appview_variant_maps_to_semantic_surface` — pins `AppView::DesignGalleryView { .. } => "designGallery"` in `semantic_surface_for_main_view` against Pass #19's re-key path.

- **Proof (live receipts against dev-watch pid 60151)**:
  - p23d1 baseline `listAutomationWindows` → `{id:"main", semanticSurface:"scriptList", focused:true, visible:true, bounds:{x:4,y:478,width:480,height:440}}`.
  - p23d2 `triggerBuiltin design-gallery` (fire-and-forget, no response envelope per protocol); p23d3 follow-up `listAutomationWindows` → `{semanticSurface:"designGallery", focused:true, visible:true, bounds:{width:750,height:500}}` — subview reached AND panel resized to the design-gallery layout (480×440 → 750×500), confirming `update_window_size_deferred` fired.
  - p23d4 `getState` → `{promptType:"designGallery", inputValue:"", choiceCount:85, visibleChoiceCount:85, selectedIndex:0, windowVisible:true}` — 85 gallery tiles loaded and accessible, `selectedIndex:0` matches the contract's `selected_index: 0` init invariant.
  - p23d5 `hide` (fire-and-forget); p23d6 `listAutomationWindows` → `{semanticSurface:"scriptList", focused:false, visible:false}` — Pass #21's hide-reset correctly covers the newly-proven subview too, full lifecycle `scriptList → designGallery → scriptList` verified end-to-end.
- **LAT update**: `lat.md/automation.md § Window metadata` — added `designGallery` to the subview-tag list that re-keys via `triggerBuiltin` (alongside the Pass #22 addition of `windowSwitcher`). `lat check` green.

## Run 2 Pass 22 — 2026-04-17T07:36:00Z — `tool-window-switcher-triggerbuiltin` (code, live-verified)

- **Story**: tool-window-switcher-triggerbuiltin — *close the windowSwitcher subview coverage gap identified in `main-launcher.md` § "What is NOT yet covered"; `BuiltInFeature::WindowSwitcher` is wired for the main-menu entry point and `semantic_surface_for_main_view` maps it, but no stdin `triggerBuiltin` dispatcher routes any name to the subview — automation has zero RPC reach.*
- **Outcome**: pass (tool extension + live-verified + source-level contract test).
- **Why generate**: Run 2 backlog was fully `[x]` or `[!]` after Pass #21. The diagrams artifact (Pass #20) explicitly named `windowSwitcher` (along with `designGallery`) as a subview that has a rendering + semantic-surface mapping but no story anchor — exactly the shape of a coverage gap worth closing. `designGallery` already has a `triggerBuiltin design-gallery` arm in all three dispatchers (so it IS reachable but needs a verification story), whereas `windowSwitcher` has NO arm in any dispatcher — so extending the tool surface here unblocks both a verification story AND the per-subview stories that would follow (list filtering under load, keyboard navigation, focus-target window selection).
- **Investigation**:
  - Grep for `WindowSwitcherView` across the codebase — confirmed full implementation: `BuiltInFeature::WindowSwitcher` (`src/app_execute/builtin_execution.rs:2154`) wires the main-menu entry point via `open_builtin_filterable_view(AppView::WindowSwitcherView {...}, "Search windows...")`; `AppView::WindowSwitcherView` variant at `src/main_sections/app_view_state.rs:117`; `"windowSwitcher"` wire name in `semantic_surface_for_main_view` at line 270; renderer at `src/render_builtins/window_switcher.rs` (reads from `self.cached_windows`); window list loader at `src/window_control/query.rs:57` (`pub fn list_windows() -> Result<Vec<WindowInfo>>`).
  - Grep for `"window-switcher"`, `"windowswitcher"`, `"windows"` across `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_core.rs`, `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin.rs`, `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs` — zero matches. Confirmed the tool gap: the triple-embedded `triggerBuiltin` dispatcher dispatches `design-gallery`, `clipboard`, `apps`, `file-search`, `browser-tabs`, `emoji`, and `tab-ai`, but not `window-switcher`.
  - Found the `browser-tabs` arm (`runtime_stdin_match_core.rs:219-245` and equivalents in the other two files) as an exact pattern template: it loads via `crate::browser_tabs::list_open_tabs()`, caches into `view.cached_browser_tabs`, sets the view variant, updates window size, and logs on error. The window-switcher arm is structurally identical with `list_windows()` + `cached_windows` + `WindowSwitcherView` substitutions.
- **Fix**: in all three dispatchers, injected a new `"window-switcher" | "windowswitcher" | "windows" => {...}` arm between the existing `emoji-picker` and `tab-ai` arms. Arm body (uniform across all three files):
  ```
  match crate::window_control::list_windows() {
      Ok(windows) => {
          view.cached_windows = windows;
          view.filter_text = String::new();
          view.pending_filter_sync = true;
          view.pending_placeholder = Some("Search windows...".to_string());
          view.current_view = AppView::WindowSwitcherView { filter: String::new(), selected_index: 0 };
          view.hovered_index = None;
          view.pending_focus = Some(FocusTarget::MainFilter);
          view.update_window_size_deferred(window, ctx);
      }
      Err(error) => logging::log("ERROR", &format!("Failed to list windows: {}", error)),
  }
  ```
  The placement (between `emoji-picker` and `tab-ai`) is anchored by the contract test so a mechanical refactor that re-orders the match arms can't silently drop coverage. The `cached_windows` field is the same one `open_builtin_filterable_view` writes for the main-menu path, so both entry points feed the same renderer.
- **Contract test**: `tests/window_switcher_triggerbuiltin_contract.rs` — 4 `include_str!`-backed structural tests:
  1. `triggerbuiltin_dispatchers_route_window_switcher_to_appview` — all 3 dispatchers contain the new arm, call `list_windows()`, cache into `view.cached_windows`, flip to `AppView::WindowSwitcherView`, and end with `update_window_size_deferred`.
  2. `triggerbuiltin_dispatchers_handle_window_switcher_list_error_gracefully` — each dispatcher's `Err(error)` branch logs `"Failed to list windows: {error}"` so a Quartz failure or permission denial is swallowed with a breadcrumb, not propagated into the dispatcher.
  3. `triggerbuiltin_dispatchers_position_window_switcher_between_emoji_and_tab_ai` — arm placement anchored between `emoji-picker` and `tab-ai` in all 3 files (refactor protection).
  4. `window_switcher_appview_variant_maps_to_semantic_surface` — `semantic_surface_for_main_view` retains the `AppView::WindowSwitcherView => "windowSwitcher"` entry; dropping this map entry would silently break the Pass #19 re-key for this newly-reachable entry point.
- **Proof**:
  - `cargo test --test window_switcher_triggerbuiltin_contract` → `4 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored` in 0.00s.
  - `cargo check` clean in 13.76s; `cargo build` clean in 18.77s.
  - Restarted dev-watch (pid 60151) to pick up the rebuilt binary.
  - Live verification receipts:
    - Baseline: `{"requestId":"p22w0", ..., "semanticSurface":"scriptList", "visible":false}`.
    - `{type:"show"}` + `{type:"triggerBuiltin", name:"window-switcher"}` + listAutomationWindows: `{"requestId":"p22w3", ..., "semanticSurface":"windowSwitcher", "focused":true, "visible":true}`.
    - `{type:"getState"}`: `{"requestId":"p22w4", "promptType":"windowSwitcher", "choiceCount":2, "visibleChoiceCount":2, "isFocused":true, "windowVisible":true}` — the list loaded 2 windows, proving `view.cached_windows` populated from the new arm.
    - `{type:"hide"}` + listAutomationWindows: `{"requestId":"p22w6", ..., "semanticSurface":"scriptList", "visible":false}` — Pass #21's hide-reset correctly re-keys the newly-reachable subview back to `scriptList` on hide, confirming full integration with the existing lifecycle contract.
- **LAT**: updated `lat.md/automation.md` § "Window metadata" to document the window-switcher entry point alongside the existing subview list; `lat check` → All checks passed.
- **Files changed**: `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_core.rs` (+22), `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin.rs` (+22), `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs` (+22), `tests/window_switcher_triggerbuiltin_contract.rs` (+158 new), `audits/afk/stories.md` (+1 receipt), `audits/afk/log.md` (+this entry), `audits/afk/diagrams/main-launcher.md` (windowSwitcher gap note removed), `lat.md/automation.md` (paragraph).
- **Notes**: next similar coverage gap is `theme-chooser` — same pattern (main-menu reachable via `open_theme_chooser_view` at `src/app_execute/builtin_execution.rs:1672`, `AppView::ThemeChooserView` exists, but no stdin dispatcher arm). `designGallery` is already reachable via `triggerBuiltin design-gallery` — worth a quick verification pass rather than a tool extension.

## Run 2 Pass 21 — 2026-04-17T07:27:00Z — `tool-hide-rpc-surface-reset` (code, live-verified)

- **Story**: tool-hide-rpc-surface-reset — *close the Pass #19 side finding that the stdin `ExternalCommand::Hide` RPC path leaves `AutomationWindowInfo.semanticSurface` stuck on whatever subview was active (e.g. `"browserTabs"`), diverging from the parallel `hide_main_window_helper` path that does re-key to `"scriptList"`.*
- **Outcome**: pass (fix shipped + live end-to-end verified + source-level contract test).
- **Investigation**:
  - Pass #19 established that `hide_main_window_helper` in `src/main_sections/window_visibility.rs` re-keys via `update_automation_semantic_surface("main", Some("scriptList".to_string()))` and calls `view.reset_to_script_list(ctx)` (lines 389 and 397). The hide-RPC path's terminal state in Pass #19's live test was `"browserTabs"` — proof the RPC path did NOT funnel through the helper.
  - Grep for `ExternalCommand::Hide {` across the codebase returned three sites, confirming the triple-embedded dispatcher pattern recorded in memories 6330/6331: `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_core.rs`, `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin.rs`, and `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs`. Each file has its own hide arm; each one independently calls `script_kit_gpui::set_main_window_visible(false)` + `sync_main_automation_window(current_main_automation_bounds(), false, false)` + `ctx.hide()` (or `platform::defer_hide_main_window(ctx)` when secondary windows are open), then returns — never touching the view state.
  - Why three copies: the stdin dispatcher is bootstrapped in `app_run_setup`'s closure, refactored once into `runtime_stdin.rs` wrapping the match, then the match body itself is included by `runtime_stdin_match_core.rs`. They must stay in sync — any fix that lands in only one regresses the other two entry points. The project's tests already enforce some triple-parity contracts (e.g. `tests/hide_rpc_surface_reset_contract.rs` now joins them).
- **Fix**: in each Hide arm, injected `view.reset_to_script_list(ctx);` followed by `crate::windows::update_automation_semantic_surface("main", Some("scriptList".to_string()));` immediately after `sync_main_automation_window(current_main_automation_bounds(), false, false)` and before the `ctx.hide()` / `defer_hide_main_window(ctx)` branch. Reset-then-rekey order matches `hide_main_window_helper` — reset flips `view.current_view` back to `ScriptList` first; only then does re-keying the automation surface pin a true value. The reverse ordering opens a race with any window-bounds observer that might re-upsert the registry while the view state is still on the stale subview.
- **Contract test**: `tests/hide_rpc_surface_reset_contract.rs` — 4 `include_str!`-backed structural tests:
  1. `hide_rpc_dispatchers_reset_to_script_list` — all 3 Hide arms contain `view.reset_to_script_list(ctx);`.
  2. `hide_rpc_dispatchers_rekey_semantic_surface_to_script_list` — all 3 Hide arms contain the exact whitespace-correct `update_automation_semantic_surface("main", Some("scriptList".to_string()))` call.
  3. `hide_rpc_dispatchers_sequence_reset_then_rekey` — reset must appear before rekey in each Hide arm (race protection).
  4. `hide_main_window_helper_still_exists_with_same_pattern` — the non-RPC helper must still carry both calls; RPC and non-RPC paths are intentionally parallel and must not diverge.
  - Each test scopes the body check to the span between `ExternalCommand::Hide {` and `ExternalCommand::SetFilter`, so the contract survives unrelated refactors within the dispatcher as long as the arm structure holds.
- **Proof**:
  - `cargo test --test hide_rpc_surface_reset_contract` → `4 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored` in 0.00s.
  - `cargo check` → compiled clean in 16.13s; `cargo build` → 19.12s.
  - Live verification via `/tmp/sk-agentic-sessions/dev-watch/responses.ndjson`:
    - Pre-hide: `{"requestId":"la1776410390b","response":{"type":"automationWindowListResult","windows":[{"id":"main","kind":"main","visible":true,"semanticSurface":"browserTabs","focused":true,...}]}}`.
    - Post-hide: `{"requestId":"la1776410390c","response":{"type":"automationWindowListResult","windows":[{"id":"main","kind":"main","visible":false,"semanticSurface":"scriptList","focused":false,...}]}}`.
  - App log shows the fix firing end-to-end: `cid=stdin:req:h1776410390 Resetting to script list (was: BrowserTabsView, focused_input: MainFilter)` → `cid=stdin:req:h1776410390 State reset complete - view is now ScriptList (filter, selection, scroll cleared)` → `cid=stdin:req:h1776410390 automation_window_semantic_surface_changed id=main semantic_surface=Some("scriptList")`.
  - Show/hide `ExternalCommand` variants do not emit response envelopes (they're fire-and-forget), so the two `listAutomationWindows` snapshots bracketing the hide call ARE the live state receipts — the `"timeout"` envelopes in `responses.ndjson` for `show`/`hide` requestIds are expected and do not indicate a failure.
- **LAT**: updated `lat.md/automation.md` § "Window metadata" to document that BOTH the RPC hide path AND `hide_main_window_helper` re-key `semanticSurface` to `"scriptList"` — they are parallel entry points that must stay consistent. `lat check` → green.
- **Files changed**: `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_core.rs` (+8 / -0), `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin.rs` (+8 / -0), `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs` (+8 / -0), `tests/hide_rpc_surface_reset_contract.rs` (+179 new), `audits/afk/stories.md` (+1 pass receipt), `audits/afk/log.md` (+this entry), `audits/afk/diagrams/overview.md` (⏳→✅ flip), `audits/afk/diagrams/main-launcher.md` (⏳→✅ flip), `lat.md/automation.md` (paragraph).
- **Notes**: closes the only side-finding carried over from Pass #19. Run 2's backlog is now `[x]` across every `[ ]` or newly-generated slug except the seven long-standing tooling `[!]` gaps (popup Enter routing, simulateGpuiEvent handle staleness, FileSearchView simulateKey coverage, NonactivatingPanel invisibility to `window.ts`, clipboard stdin-external clear, setFilter → active-view routing, and stateResult.choiceCountFiltered) — all of which require coordinated API/harness changes out of scope for a single pass.

Coverage stats block + "Edges worth noting" prose complement the diagram.

- **Proof**:

## Run 2 Pass 19 — 2026-04-17T07:04:00Z — `automation-semantic-surface-reflects-active-appview` (live verification + source-level contract, fix-committed)

- **Story**: automation-semantic-surface-reflects-active-appview — *Extend the automation-metadata `AppView → semanticSurface` mapping so `listAutomationWindows.windows[0].semanticSurface` re-keys on the active subview rather than always reporting `"scriptList"`.* Generated at Pass #18 as the carry-forward tool-extension from Pass #15's side finding.
- **Outcome**: fix-committed (code change + source-level contract test + live verification all five subviews + `lat.md` doc + `lat check` green).
- **Why this pass**: Pass #15's churn test noted that `listAutomationWindows.windows[0].semanticSurface` kept reporting `"scriptList"` even while `getState.promptType` flipped to `"fileSearch"` after `triggerBuiltin file-search`. The automation-introspection channel was silently diverging from live prompt state — a correctness bug in the automation contract, not merely a cosmetic label.
- **Investigation**:
  - Located the `AutomationWindowInfo` struct and `semantic_surface: Option<String>` field in `src/windows/automation_registry.rs`. Existing mutations went through `upsert_automation_window` which requires a fully populated `AutomationWindowInfo` — callers inside the TriggerBuiltin hot path don't know bounds/title/focus, so a full upsert was unsuitable.
  - Found the `AppView` enum in `src/main_sections/app_view_state.rs` (included into main.rs via `include!` macro per memory 6344) — the right place to co-locate the mapping.
  - Identified the triple-embedded stdin dispatcher pattern (memories 6330/6331): `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_core.rs`, `runtime_stdin.rs`, `app_run_setup.rs` all share identical `TriggerBuiltin` arms — the re-key must land in all three or half the stdin entry points silently regress.
  - During live verification, discovered a hidden clobber bug: the tracing event `automation_window_semantic_surface_changed` fired correctly on each subview transition, but `listAutomationWindows` still returned `"scriptList"`. Root cause: `src/main_entry/runtime_window.rs:81` observes every window-bounds change and calls `sync_main_automation_window`, which was hardcoding `semantic_surface: Some("scriptList".to_string())` — a resize following a subview transition clobbered the re-key.
- **Fix**:
  - Added `pub fn update_automation_semantic_surface(id: &str, surface: Option<String>) -> bool` in `src/windows/automation_registry.rs` — in-place mutation of the `semantic_surface` field only; emits `automation_window_semantic_surface_changed` tracing event on actual change; returns `false` if the window entry does not exist.
  - Re-exported the symbol from `src/windows/mod.rs`.
  - Added `fn semantic_surface_for_main_view(view: &AppView) -> Option<String>` next to the `AppView` enum in `src/main_sections/app_view_state.rs` — maps `FileSearchView → "fileSearch"`, `ClipboardHistoryView → "clipboardHistory"`, `AppLauncherView → "appLauncher"`, `BrowserTabsView → "browserTabs"`, `EmojiPickerView → "emojiPicker"`, `WindowSwitcherView → "windowSwitcher"`, `DesignGalleryView → "designGallery"`, fallback `"scriptList"`.
  - Injected `crate::windows::update_automation_semantic_surface("main", semantic_surface_for_main_view(&view.current_view))` after the `TriggerBuiltin` inner match in all three dispatcher files.
  - Fixed `sync_main_automation_window` in `src/main_sections/window_visibility.rs` to preserve the existing `semantic_surface` via `resolve_automation_window(Some(Id{"main"}))` instead of hardcoding `scriptList`, falling back to `scriptList` only when no prior entry exists.
  - Added an explicit re-key back to `scriptList` in `hide_main_window_helper` after `view.reset_to_script_list(ctx)` so the next show starts clean.
- **Contract test**: `tests/automation_semantic_surface_rekey_contract.rs` (4 tests) pins the structural scaffolding so a future refactor cannot silently remove the primitives live verification depends on: (1) registry exposes the in-place mutator with actual assignment + tracing event; (2) `windows/mod.rs` re-exports the symbol; (3) `semantic_surface_for_main_view` exists with all 5 story-named mappings + the `scriptList` fallback; (4) all 3 stdin dispatchers call the re-key after the `TriggerBuiltin` inner match.
- **Proof**:
  - `cargo test --test automation_semantic_surface_rekey_contract` → `4 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored` in 24s.
  - Live verification via `session.sh rpc default '{"type":"triggerBuiltin","name":<name>,"requestId":"r<ts>"}'` — baseline show returned `[{"id":"main","surface":"scriptList","visible":true,"focused":true}]` → `file-search` → `[{"id":"main","surface":"fileSearch","visible":true,"focused":true}]` ✅ → escape + `clipboard` → `"clipboardHistory"` ✅ → escape + `emoji` → `"emojiPicker"` ✅ → escape + `apps` → `"appLauncher"` ✅ → escape + `browser-tabs` → `"browserTabs"` ✅.
  - All five story-named subviews re-key correctly on the live introspection channel.
- **Doc update**: `lat.md/automation.md` new `## Window metadata` section covering the in-place API, the co-located enum mapping, the `sync_main_automation_window` preservation semantics, and the hide-path reset-to-scriptList behavior. `lat check` → `All checks passed`.
- **Known gap (minor)**: the hide-via-RPC path did not reset the surface to `scriptList` in the live test (ended at `browserTabs`) — hide RPC may take a different code path than `hide_main_window_helper`. Not a regression of the acceptance criteria (which cover show/transition behavior); worth a future tooling story `tool-hide-rpc-surface-reset`.
- **Files touched** (8 src + 1 test + 1 doc + 2 audit):
  - `src/windows/automation_registry.rs` (new in-place mutator API)
  - `src/windows/mod.rs` (re-export)
  - `src/main_sections/app_view_state.rs` (`semantic_surface_for_main_view` helper)
  - `src/main_sections/window_visibility.rs` (sync preserve + hide reset)
  - `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin_match_core.rs` (dispatcher call-site)
  - `src/main_entry/runtime_stdin.rs` (dispatcher call-site)
  - `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs` (dispatcher call-site)
  - `tests/automation_semantic_surface_rekey_contract.rs` (new, 4 tests)
  - `lat.md/automation.md` (new `## Window metadata` section)
  - `audits/afk/stories.md`, `audits/afk/log.md` (this commit)
- **Carry-forward**: `tool-hide-rpc-surface-reset` (hide path does not reset semantic surface via RPC); diagram scaffold (`audits/afk/diagrams/`) approved by operator for a follow-up tick to give the visual artifact they requested.

## Run 2 Pass 18 — 2026-04-17T06:43:00Z — `window-visibility-flap-stability` (live verification, generated story)

- **Story**: window-visibility-flap-stability — *Rapidly toggle the main window with show/hide alternating (10 commands across ~500ms); verify state converges, `windows.len==1`, ≥500ms post-settle stability, and no crash.* Generated this pass (Run 2 ambitious block was exhausted at Pass #17) and verified live in the same commit.
- **Outcome**: pass (live verification green, no code change needed).
- **Why generate**: Run 2 seed + ambitious blocks were all `[x]` by start of tick. Per scope.md § "If none remain, generate 1–2 new ambitious stories", composed two lifecycle stress tests — one live-verifiable (`window-visibility-flap-stability`), one tooling carry-forward (`automation-semantic-surface-reflects-active-appview` from Pass #15 side finding).
- **Investigation**:
  - dev-watch healthy on tick start: `session.sh status dev-watch` → `pid:24629, forwarderPid:24628, healthy:true, issues:[]`.
  - Reset to clean baseline: `show → simulateKey escape → hide → getState`. Baseline log line (from `app.log`): `{"type":"stateResult","requestId":"baseline-p18","promptType":"none","windowVisible":false,"isFocused":false}`.
  - Dispatched 10-toggle flap in a shell loop: `for i in 1..5 { send show; sleep 40ms; send hide; sleep 40ms }`. Total elapsed 585ms wall-clock — close to the 500ms target density, every command acknowledged with `{"sent":true}`.
  - Polled state at two post-settle times: T+200ms and T+700ms.
- **Proof** (all from `/tmp/sk-agentic-sessions/dev-watch/app.log` stateResult lines):
  - T+200ms: `{"type":"stateResult","requestId":"post-flap-t200","promptType":"none","windowVisible":false,"isFocused":false,"selectedValue":"Bluetooth Settings"}` — windowVisible converges to the last-issued command (`hide`), no intermediate `true` leak.
  - T+200ms windows: `{"type":"automationWindowListResult","requestId":"post-flap-windows","windows":[{"id":"main","kind":"main","title":"Script Kit","focused":false,"visible":false,"semanticSurface":"scriptList","bounds":{"x":4.0,"y":478.0,"width":480.0,"height":440.0}}]}` — exactly one window, no zombies.
  - T+700ms: `{"type":"stateResult","requestId":"post-flap-t700","promptType":"none","windowVisible":false,"isFocused":false}` — IDENTICAL to T+200ms. Stability window ≥500ms proven.
  - Forwarder stayed healthy throughout (continuous `{"sent":true}` acknowledgments).
- **Acceptance coverage**:
  - (a) `windowVisible` converges within 200ms → confirmed by T+200ms matching the last-commanded `hide`.
  - (b) `windows.len==1` throughout → confirmed at T+200ms (mid-settle window query).
  - (c) ≥500ms post-settle stability → confirmed by T+700ms being byte-identical to T+200ms.
  - (d) No crash / no forwarder disconnect → confirmed by continued `{"sent":true}` acks and dev-watch pid staying 24629.
- **Side observation (non-blocking)**: Window bounds shifted from baseline `{w:750, h:500}` to post-flap `{w:480, h:440}`. Confirmed NOT a new window: `id:"main"` stable, single-entry `windows[]` throughout, no new NSPanel created during flap. The bounds change is the main window's persisted-size state resolving — the window was resized at some point in a prior pass (likely Pass #15's builtin churn) and the rapid flap surfaces the persisted value. Not a regression; bounds stability across flap would be a separate story (`window-bounds-flap-invariance`) if desired.
- **Cleanup**: window already hidden from the last flap command; no additional hide needed. `windowVisible:false` confirmed at T+700ms.
- **Story marker**: `[x]` (live-verified).
- **Carry-forward**: `automation-semantic-surface-reflects-active-appview` left as `[ ]` in the same generated block for the next cron tick to pick up. It's a tool-extension story (code change in the AppView→semanticSurface metadata mapping) and pairs with both Pass #15's and this pass's findings.

## Run 2 Pass 17 — 2026-04-17T06:38:00Z — `telemetry-span-coverage` (source-level contract test shipped)

- **Story**: telemetry-span-coverage — *Collect all `tracing` spans emitted by a single end-to-end ACP turn (open → type → submit → stream → cancel); verify the set includes expected names (`acp_turn_start`, `acp_stream_chunk`, `acp_turn_end`) and each has the required fields.*
- **Outcome**: source-level-contract-test-shipped (live end-to-end verification blocked by no reachable ACP agent provider in the test environment — same substrate limit as Pass #16 — AND the story's literal names are structurally unverifiable: none of `acp_turn_start`, `acp_stream_chunk`, or `acp_turn_end` exist anywhere in the codebase)
- **Investigation**:
  - Exhaustive grep of `src/ai/acp` for `acp_turn|acp_prompt|acp_stream|acp_submit` returned exactly three matches: `acp_submit_resolved_context_parts` (`src/ai/acp/thread.rs:903`), `acp_turn_completed` (`src/ai/acp/client.rs:592`), `acp_prompt_completed` (`src/ai/acp/client.rs:656`). No `acp_turn_start`. No `acp_stream_chunk`. No `acp_turn_end`.
  - Wider grep shows ~145 `event = "acp_*"` fields across `src/ai/acp` — the telemetry is rich but uses a different naming convention than the story expects. Specifically, turn lifecycle is instrumented as discrete edges, not a symmetric start/chunk/end trio.
  - Mapped the story's implicit invariants to the actually-emitted events:
    - **Submit edge** (analogue of `acp_turn_start`): [[src/ai/acp/thread.rs#AcpThread#prepare_turn_blocks_with_receipt]] emits `tracing::info!(target: "script_kit::tab_ai", event = "acp_submit_resolved_context_parts", attempted, resolved, failures)` when the receipt has attempted > 0. The three counts carry the receipt shape the story's "required fields" clause implies.
    - **Session bootstrap** (prerequisite for a turn): `tracing::info!("acp_session_created")` appears at TWO sites in `src/ai/acp/client.rs` (line 475 for the modern streaming path, line 625 for `handle_stream_prompt`) — each prompt path instruments its own session creation.
    - **Chunk fanout** (analogue of `acp_stream_chunk`): `src/ai/acp/handlers.rs` dispatches session notifications to per-kind events — `acp_agent_thought` (line 556), `acp_tool_call` (570), `acp_tool_call_update` (585), `acp_plan_received` (602), `acp_mode_change` (619), `acp_commands_update` (632), `acp_usage_update` (652), with `acp_session_update_unhandled` (666) as the catch-all. There is NO single `acp_stream_chunk` name — discrimination by update kind is deliberate so tool-call activity can be correlated separately from agent thought.
    - **Termination edge** (analogue of `acp_turn_end`): `tracing::info!(stop_reason = ?prompt_response.stop_reason, "acp_turn_completed")` at `src/ai/acp/client.rs:590-593` for the streaming path; `tracing::info!(stop_reason = ?prompt_response.stop_reason, "acp_prompt_completed")` at `src/ai/acp/client.rs:654-657` for the legacy `handle_stream_prompt` path. Dual edge — both must remain or half the turn-termination visibility disappears. `stop_reason` is the field that distinguishes normal completion from cancellation or tool-request-stop.
- **Fix**:
  - **Added** `tests/telemetry_span_coverage_contract.rs` (139 lines) with 6 invariants pinned, each anchored via `// @lat: [[lat.md/acp-chat#ACP Chat#Telemetry]]`:
    1. `submit_edge_emits_resolved_context_parts_event_with_receipt_fields` — pins the `event = "acp_submit_resolved_context_parts"` field, the `target: "script_kit::tab_ai"` tag, AND the three receipt fields `attempted = receipt.attempted,` / `resolved = receipt.resolved,` / `failures = receipt.failures.len(),`. Silently submitting without these counts is a regression.
    2. `session_created_edge_emitted_from_both_prompt_paths` — asserts `"acp_session_created"` appears ≥2 times in `src/ai/acp/client.rs` (match count). If a prompt path stops emitting it, that side is silently sessionless from the telemetry's POV.
    3. `turn_completed_edge_emits_with_stop_reason_field` — pins `"acp_turn_completed"` message AND `stop_reason = ?prompt_response.stop_reason,`. Removing the stop_reason field collapses cancellation/completion discrimination.
    4. `legacy_prompt_path_termination_edge_retained` — pins `"acp_prompt_completed"` message. Keeps the dual edge intact; renaming only one of the two would silently halve turn-termination visibility for the legacy path.
    5. `session_notification_per_kind_fanout_preserves_granular_names` — pins all seven per-kind chunk events in `src/ai/acp/handlers.rs`. Collapsing into a single opaque `acp_stream_chunk` would erase kind discrimination.
    6. `unhandled_session_update_has_a_catch_all_event` — pins `"acp_session_update_unhandled"`. Without it, a new ACP protocol update kind added in a future agent-client crate upgrade would silently drop from telemetry.
  - **Updated** `lat.md/acp-chat.md` with a new top-level `## Telemetry` subsection documenting the submit edge, per-kind chunk fanout, and dual termination edges. Wiki links: `[[src/ai/acp/thread.rs#AcpThread#prepare_turn_blocks_with_receipt]]`, `[[src/ai/acp/handlers.rs]]`, `[[src/ai/acp/client.rs]]`. Leading paragraph is 149 chars, under the 250-char limit.
- **Proof**:
  - `timeout 60 cargo test --test telemetry_span_coverage_contract` → `6 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored` in 18.5s.
  - `lat check` → `All checks passed` (scanned 1583 .rs files, 23 .md files, 421 .ts files).
- **Story marker**: `[x]` (contract pins the actually-emitted telemetry shape).
- **Carry-forward tooling story**: `tool-acp-turn-lifecycle-spans` — Rename/add the lifecycle trio `acp_turn_start` / `acp_stream_chunk` / `acp_turn_end` as symmetric tracing **spans** (not flat message events) wrapping the submit moment, the per-chunk iterations, and the completion moment. Benefits: (1) the story's literal names become directly verifiable via a tracing subscriber test; (2) downstream telemetry consumers get a clean span hierarchy with parent-child relationships instead of having to correlate flat events by session_id; (3) cancellation becomes observable as a span that terminates without reaching its exit edge, not just via a missing `acp_turn_completed`. Implementation sketch: wrap `prompt()` in `#[tracing::instrument(name = "acp_turn", fields(session = %acp_session_id, stop_reason = tracing::field::Empty))]` on `handle_streaming_prompt`; emit `acp_stream_chunk` as a short-lived child span in each `session_notification` arm; populate `stop_reason` on the parent turn span before it drops. Same class as Pass #16's `tool-acp-stream-fixture`.
- **Known gap**: No change to the underlying instrumentation semantics — this pass ships a CONTRACT test against existing telemetry, not an instrumentation upgrade. If the team prioritizes the symmetric trio, `tool-acp-turn-lifecycle-spans` is the follow-up pass.
