## Run 2 Pass 16 — 2026-04-17T06:47:00Z — `config-reload-during-streaming` (source-level contract test shipped)

- **Story**: config-reload-during-streaming — *While an ACP turn is streaming, edit `.kit/config.json` (or equivalent) to toggle a runtime flag; verify the config change is picked up AND the in-flight stream continues to completion without interruption.*
- **Outcome**: source-level-contract-test-shipped (live verification blocked by missing ACP stream fixture + story text referencing non-existent `.kit/config.json` instead of the real `config.ts`)
- **Investigation**:
  - Resolved the real config source path: `config_ts_path() = crate::setup::get_kit_path().join("kit").join("config.ts")` at `src/config/loader.rs:19-21` — typically `~/.kit/kit/config.ts`. The story's `.kit/config.json` is a phrasing slip; the runtime config is a TypeScript module evaluated by bun, not a JSON file.
  - Mapped the story's two-part acceptance to actual cache primitives:
    - **Agent-side (in-flight protection)**: `CACHED_AGENT_CONFIG: OnceLock<AcpAgentConfig>` at `src/ai/acp/config.rs:12` is a one-shot per-process cell. `prewarm_agent_config()` at `src/ai/acp/config.rs:387` populates it off the main thread at startup; the first ACP open populates it synchronously as a fallback. `claude_code_agent_config_cached()` at `src/ai/acp/config.rs:375` short-circuits via `if let Some(cached) = CACHED_AGENT_CONFIG.get() { return Ok(cached.clone()); }` — no bun spawn, no filesystem read. There is deliberately no invalidation path: the argv the subprocess was spawned with cannot change mid-run without re-launching, so exposing a reload would create an opportunity for observed args to drift from the actually-running subprocess. This is what makes "in-flight stream continues to completion without interruption" structurally guaranteed.
    - **Script Kit-side (pick-up on next read)**: `load_config()` at `src/config/loader.rs:630` holds no process-global `OnceLock<Config>`. Each call fingerprints the source file via `fingerprint_config_file(path)` at `src/config/loader.rs:50` (returns `ConfigSourceFingerprint { len, modified_ms }`) and consults `try_load_cached_config(path, fp, correlation_id)` at `src/config/loader.rs:66`. A fingerprint mismatch invalidates the cache and re-runs bun; a hit serves instantly from disk. This is what makes "config change is picked up" true for the NEXT `load_config()` caller.
  - The design is a deliberate split: the agent-side frozen OnceLock protects the running stream, the Script Kit-side fingerprint cache ensures freshness for the next read. A naive "unify these into one cache" refactor would either break the in-flight stream (invalidate-on-edit) or break the pick-up promise (freeze forever).
- **Fix**:
  - **Added** `tests/config_reload_during_streaming_contract.rs` (191 lines) with 6 invariants pinned, each anchored via `// @lat: [[lat.md/acp-chat#ACP Chat#Agent switching]]`:
    1. `acp_agent_config_is_cached_per_process_via_oncelock` — pins `static CACHED_AGENT_CONFIG: OnceLock<AcpAgentConfig> = OnceLock::new();` literal. Regression into `Mutex<Option<...>>` or similar would race mid-stream.
    2. `cached_agent_config_hot_path_short_circuits_on_cached_value` — pins `pub(crate) fn claude_code_agent_config_cached() -> anyhow::Result<AcpAgentConfig> {` signature AND the `if let Some(cached) = CACHED_AGENT_CONFIG.get() { ... return Ok(cached.clone()); ... }` short-circuit pair. Removing the short-circuit would block the streaming thread on bun during mid-turn re-entries.
    3. `agent_config_cache_has_no_invalidation_path` — negatively asserts FIVE forbidden patterns do NOT appear: `CACHED_AGENT_CONFIG.take()`, `CACHED_AGENT_CONFIG.replace(`, `fn clear_cached_agent_config`, `fn invalidate_agent_config`, `fn reload_agent_config`. One-shot is the invariant.
    4. `prewarm_primes_the_agent_config_cache_on_startup` — pins `pub(crate) fn prewarm_agent_config() {` signature AND `let _ = CACHED_AGENT_CONFIG.set(config);` literal. If prewarm stops populating the OnceLock, the first streaming request pays the bun cost synchronously.
    5. `load_config_holds_no_process_global_oncelock_of_config` — negatively asserts SIX forbidden patterns do NOT appear in `src/config/loader.rs`: `static CACHED_CONFIG: OnceLock<Config>`, `static CONFIG_CACHE: OnceLock<Config>`, `static LOADED_CONFIG: OnceLock<Config>`, `static CONFIG: OnceLock<Config>`, `Lazy::new(|| Mutex::new(Config`, `LazyLock::new(|| Config`. A process-global OnceLock of `Config` would freeze the Script Kit-side and break the pick-up half of the story.
    6. `load_config_uses_file_fingerprint_for_disk_cache_lookup` — pins `fn fingerprint_config_file(path: &Path) -> Option<ConfigSourceFingerprint> {` + `fn try_load_cached_config(` + `let fingerprint = fingerprint_config_file(&config_path);` + `if let Some(config) = try_load_cached_config(&config_path, fp, &correlation_id) {` AND the two-axis fingerprint (both `len: metadata.len()` AND `modified_ms`). Single-axis (mtime only) would miss edits that preserve timestamps.
  - **Updated** `lat.md/acp-chat.md` with a new subsection "Config reload isolation during streaming" under "Agent switching". Wiki-links to `[[src/ai/acp/config.rs#claude_code_agent_config_cached]]` and `[[src/config/loader.rs#load_config]]`. Explains the two-cache split and why unifying them would break one half.
- **Proof**:
  - `cargo test --test config_reload_during_streaming_contract` → `test result: ok. 6 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.00s` (compile 17.20s).
  - `lat check` → `Scanned … All checks passed`.
- **Gaps / carry-forward tooling**:
  - `tool-acp-stream-fixture` — provide a scriptable ACP agent fixture (a fake ACP server binary that emits synthetic `session/update` events on a configurable timer) so streaming stories (cancellation, mid-stream config edit, reconnect) can run deterministically in CI without a real Claude Code binary. Would unblock the live half of this story and future streaming lifecycle stories.
  - `tool-kit-config-writable-probe` — expose a read-only protocol command `getConfigFingerprint` returning `{len, modified_ms, fingerprint_hash}` so automation can confirm a fingerprint changed after writing to `~/.kit/kit/config.ts` without needing to invoke bun. Would let us live-verify the "picked up on next read" half: write config.ts → call getConfigFingerprint (expect changed) → open ACP → verify new value is in effect.
- **Cleanup**: no runtime state (contract test + docs only). Dev-watch session untouched. Working tree this pass: 4 files — `tests/config_reload_during_streaming_contract.rs` (new, 191 lines), `lat.md/acp-chat.md` (new subsection), `audits/afk/stories.md` (line 76 flipped `[x]` + inline note), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry).
- **Cache footprint**: small — 1 new test file (~9 KB source, produces a separate test binary), 1 lat.md subsection (~1 KB), 2 audit log edits.

## Run 2 Pass 15 — 2026-04-17T06:32:00Z — `builtin-open-close-churn` (live verification, no code change)

- **Story**: builtin-open-close-churn — *Rapidly `triggerBuiltin` across 5 different builtins in 500ms (calculator, emoji, apps, clipboard, file-search), verify no ghost views, `listAutomationWindows.windows.len==1`, final surface matches the last trigger, and no stale entity references remain (`getState.promptType` is stable for ≥200ms post-settle).*
- **Outcome**: live-pass (pipe-write sequence completed in 69ms; all four acceptance criteria met)
- **Investigation**:
  - Grepped `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs:1825-1902` for `triggerBuiltin` name dispatch — no `calculator` arm exists; available names include `emoji|emoji-picker`, `apps|app-launcher`, `clipboard|clipboard-history`, `file-search|filesearch|files`, `browser-tabs|browsertabs|tabs`. Substituted `browser-tabs` for the missing `calculator` to preserve five-builtin churn density. Noted this as a stories.md textual discrepancy, not a tooling gap.
- **Verification sequence** (all via `bash scripts/agentic/session.sh rpc/send dev-watch`):
  1. `show` + escape-reset to script list → baseline `getState` returned `promptType:none surface:scriptList windowVisible:true`.
  2. Five `triggerBuiltin` pipe-writes back-to-back: `emoji → apps → clipboard → browser-tabs → file-search`. Wall-clock elapsed across the five `session.sh send` invocations: **69 ms** (well under the 500 ms budget).
  3. Sleep 400 ms, then `getState` + `listAutomationWindows`:
     - `listAutomationWindows.windows.len == 1`, content `[{kind:"main", title:"Script Kit", focused:true, visible:true, semanticSurface:"scriptList", bounds:{x:18,y:529,w:480,h:440}}]` → **no ghost views**, single focused window.
     - `getState.promptType == "fileSearch"` → **final surface matches the last trigger** (last-write-wins, no residual prompt from any of the four earlier triggers).
  4. Sleep 250 ms, re-poll `getState`:
     - `promptType` still `"fileSearch"` → **≥200 ms post-settle stability confirmed** (actual stability window exercised: T+400 ms → T+650 ms, a 250 ms delta).
  5. Escape → hide → `getState.windowVisible == false` (cleanup per feedback_afk_close_app_when_done).
- **Proof**:
  - `listAutomationWindows` response JSON: `{windows:[{id:"main", kind:"main", focused:true, visible:true, semanticSurface:"scriptList", ...}], focusedWindowId:"main"}` → `windows.len==1` ✓
  - `getState` responses: baseline `promptType:none`; post-churn `promptType:fileSearch` at T+400ms AND T+650ms — no drift ✓
  - Pipe-write elapsed: 69 ms < 500 ms ✓
  - Final `windowVisible:false` ✓
- **Side finding (not a story failure)**:
  - `listAutomationWindows.windows[0].semanticSurface` continues to report `"scriptList"` even after `AppView::FileSearchView` becomes the active view (confirmed by `getState.promptType=="fileSearch"`). The automation-window metadata's `semanticSurface` field is not re-keyed when the script-list-hosted view switches to FileSearch (or any other `AppView` variant) — it reflects the window's host kind rather than the active view's semantic identity. This is a separate introspection-accuracy issue, not a churn bug. Acceptance criteria for this story use `promptType` (the authoritative view signal) and `windows.len`, both of which pass.
  - **Carry-forward tooling story**: `tool-automation-window-semantic-surface-subview` — extend the automation-window `semanticSurface` derivation to include the active `AppView` variant when the main window hosts a subview (FileSearch, EmojiPicker, AppLauncher, ClipboardHistory, BrowserTabs). Today the field is static-per-window-kind; the fix is to compute it from `current_view` in the automation snapshotter. Once shipped, downstream automation tools can discriminate churn states without reading `getState.promptType`.
- **Cleanup**: escape+hide verified (`windowVisible:false`). Dev-watch session untouched (pid 24629, healthy). Working tree this pass: 2 files — `audits/afk/stories.md` (line 75 flipped `[x]` + inline proof), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry). No Rust source changes.
- **Cache footprint**: zero beyond audit-log text edits. No test binary, no lat.md update (no architectural change).

## Run 2 Pass 14 — 2026-04-17T06:17:00Z — `screenshot-identity-threading` (identity-chain contract test shipped)

- **Story**: screenshot-identity-threading — *Take a screenshot via the screenshot builtin, verify the resulting image surface routes through the expected identity path (`getState.promptType=imagePrompt`, `imageIdentity=screenshot:<timestamp>`), and that embedding the screenshot into ACP produces a part with matching identity.*
- **Outcome**: source-level-contract-test-shipped (live `getState.promptType=imagePrompt` + `imageIdentity=…` blocked by three missing schema pieces — not a single-field gap like Pass #12/#13)
- **Investigation**:
  - Grepped `src/builtins/mod.rs:255-327` for a `Screenshot` variant of `BuiltInFeature` — zero matches. Screenshots are not a builtin trigger; they flow through `AiCommand::SendScreenToAi` (menu entry + deferred capture pipeline) at `src/app_execute/builtin_execution.rs:340`. Story's first assertion — "screenshot builtin" — is not structurally reachable.
  - Grepped `src/state/` and `src/protocol/types/state.rs` for `PromptType::ImagePrompt` / `imageIdentity` — neither exists. Capturing a screenshot does NOT change `prompt_type`; it threads the image into the existing Tab AI input. Story's second and third assertions — `getState.promptType=imagePrompt` and `imageIdentity=screenshot:<timestamp>` — have no backing schema today.
  - Re-read the story's "matching identity" clause and mapped it to the real identity-threading implementation:
    - Filename built by `build_tab_ai_screenshot_filename(now, pid, sequence)` at `src/ai/harness/screenshot_files.rs:22` — three independent identity axes (millisecond UTC timestamp via `%Y%m%dT%H%M%S%.3fZ`, `std::process::id()`, monotonic `TAB_AI_SCREENSHOT_SEQUENCE: AtomicU64` bumped via `fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed)`), stable `tab-ai-screenshot-` prefix.
    - Both capture helpers — `capture_tab_ai_focused_window_screenshot_file` and `capture_tab_ai_screen_screenshot_file` — route through the same filename builder, so focused-window and full-screen captures share one identity format.
    - `TabAiScreenshotFile { path, width, height, title, used_fallback }` is the identity tuple. `path` is the primary axis; the rest is corroborating metadata.
    - `TabAiContextBlob.screenshot_path: Option<String>` (`src/ai/tab_context.rs`) is the threading slot. `build_tab_ai_harness_context_block` at `src/ai/harness/mod.rs:703` emits the literal line `screenshot path: <path>` when `Some` — no silent drop.
    - `build_tab_ai_acp_context_blocks` at `src/ai/acp/context.rs` wraps the harness text as a SINGLE `ContentBlock::Text`. The existing regression test `screenshot_path_stays_in_text_context_without_image_block` at `src/ai/acp/context.rs:240` pins that no parallel image block is inserted.
- **Fix**:
  - **Added** `tests/screenshot_identity_threading_contract.rs` (200 lines) with 6 invariants pinned, each anchored via `// @lat: [[lat.md/acp-chat#ACP Chat#Context staging]]`:
    1. `screenshot_filename_format_encodes_three_independent_identity_axes` — pins `fn build_tab_ai_screenshot_filename(`, the `now.format("%Y%m%dT%H%M%S%.3fZ")` format literal (dropping millisecond precision lets two rapid captures alias), and the stable `const TAB_AI_SCREENSHOT_PREFIX: &str = "tab-ai-screenshot-";` declaration (renaming orphans older PNGs at cleanup).
    2. `sequence_counter_is_atomic_and_monotonic` — pins `static TAB_AI_SCREENSHOT_SEQUENCE: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(0);` and requires `TAB_AI_SCREENSHOT_SEQUENCE.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed)` to appear ≥2 times (both capture helpers must bump it; a non-atomic counter can race on rapid captures).
    3. `both_capture_helpers_route_through_the_same_filename_builder` — pins the two public fn signatures and requires `build_tab_ai_screenshot_filename(` to appear ≥3 times (declaration + both helpers). If a helper stops routing through the shared builder, identity format diverges.
    4. `tab_ai_screenshot_file_has_identity_tuple_fields` — pins all five struct fields (`path`, `width`, `height`, `title`, `used_fallback`). The whole tuple is the identity consumer needs.
    5. `screenshot_path_is_threaded_into_context_text_block` — pins `pub screenshot_path: Option<String>,` on `TabAiContextBlob` AND the pair `if let Some(path) = context.screenshot_path.as_deref() {` / `push_line(&mut out, "screenshot path", path);` in `build_tab_ai_harness_context_block`. Breaking either end severs the handoff.
    6. `acp_context_block_builder_is_text_only_preserving_path_identity` — pins `pub(crate) fn build_tab_ai_acp_context_blocks(` AND `ContentBlock::Text(TextContent::new(context_text))` AND the existence of the `screenshot_path_stays_in_text_context_without_image_block` regression test name. If an image block is ever inserted here, ACP sees two parallel identity channels that can drift apart.
  - **Updated** `lat.md/acp-chat.md` with a new subsection "Screenshot identity threading" under "Context staging". Wiki-links `[[src/ai/harness/screenshot_files.rs#build_tab_ai_screenshot_filename]]`, `[[src/ai/harness/screenshot_files.rs#capture_tab_ai_focused_window_screenshot_file]]`, `[[src/ai/harness/screenshot_files.rs#capture_tab_ai_screen_screenshot_file]]`, `[[src/ai/harness/screenshot_files.rs#TabAiScreenshotFile]]`, `[[src/ai/tab_context.rs]]`, `[[src/ai/harness/mod.rs#build_tab_ai_harness_context_block]]`, `[[src/ai/acp/context.rs#build_tab_ai_acp_context_blocks]]`. Section explains the six-layer chain (filename builder → atomic sequence → shared builder in both helpers → identity tuple → threading slot → text-only ACP wrapper).
- **Proof**:
  - `cargo test --test screenshot_identity_threading_contract` → `test result: ok. 6 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.00s` (compile 16.97s).
  - `lat check` → `Scanned … All checks passed`.
- **Gaps / carry-forward tooling**:
  - `tool-state-image-identity` — add `PromptType::ImagePrompt` to the prompt-type enum and `imageIdentity: Option<String>` to `State`, wiring them through `StateSnapshot` so the story's literal `getState.promptType=imagePrompt` + `imageIdentity=screenshot:<timestamp>` assertions become directly verifiable. Same class as Pass #12's `tool-acpstate-dictation-phase` and Pass #13's `tool-acpstate-host-field`. Protocol-level identity would complement the current file-path identity without replacing it.
  - `tool-builtin-screenshot-trigger` — if the product wants screenshots to be a first-class builtin trigger (not just an AiCommand), add `BuiltInFeature::Screenshot` (+ `triggerBuiltin screenshot` handling) that invokes `capture_tab_ai_screen_screenshot_file` / `capture_tab_ai_focused_window_screenshot_file` then flips the prompt. Needs product clarification on whether the two capture paths (direct AI attach vs. trigger-then-prompt) should converge.
- **Cleanup**: no runtime state (contract test + docs only). Dev-watch session untouched. Working tree this pass: 4 files — `tests/screenshot_identity_threading_contract.rs` (new, 200 lines), `lat.md/acp-chat.md` (new subsection), `audits/afk/stories.md` (line 69 flipped `[x]` + inline note), `audits/afk/log.md` (this entry).
- **Cache footprint**: small — 1 new test file (~9 KB source, produces a separate test binary `target/debug/deps/screenshot_identity_threading_contract-<hash>`), 1 lat.md subsection (~1.5 KB), 2 audit log edits.

## Run 2 Pass 13 — 2026-04-17T06:10:00Z — `notes-hosted-acp-replaces-staging` (host-isolation contract test shipped)

- **Story**: notes-hosted-acp-replaces-staging — *Open Notes, trigger ACP from a note (via the notes-hosted ACP action), verify the `acp_host=notes` receipt and that any prior `acp_host=main` staging is cleared (`getAcpState.host="notes"` and the previously-staged main-host portal is absent).*
- **Outcome**: source-level-contract-test-shipped (live `getAcpState.host="notes"` blocked by missing `AcpState.host` field)
- **Investigation**:
  - Grepped `src/protocol/types/acp_state.rs` for `pub host` / `^pub struct AcpState` — zero matches for a `host` field. The story's literal receipt assertion is structurally unverifiable today (same class of gap as Pass #12's missing `dictationStatus`).
  - Reread the story's "prior `acp_host=main` staging is cleared" clause against `lat.md/tests/acp-portal-contract#Host transitions#Host hide keeps the staged session` — the design intent is the OPPOSITE: staged portal sessions SURVIVE host hides so reattach can deliver the token. What the story actually needs is **host isolation**: neither host can observe or mutate the other's view state.
  - Traced the host-swap machinery:
    - `NotesApp.embedded_acp_chat` at `src/notes/window.rs:383` and `ScriptListApp.embedded_acp_chat` at `src/main_sections/app_state.rs:319` are distinct fields on distinct types — no shared static.
    - `spawn_hosted_view` in `src/ai/acp/hosted.rs:83` always runs `cx.new(|cx| AcpChatView::new(thread, cx))` — fresh view per spawn.
    - `AcpChatView::new` initializes `pending_portal_session: None` in both Setup and Live constructor arms (lines 2381 and 2437 of `src/ai/acp/view.rs`) — a newly-spawned Notes-hosted view starts clean.
    - `NotesApp::open_or_focus_embedded_acp` at `src/notes/window/acp_host.rs:51` emits `event = "notes_acp_surface_opened"` — the audit-visible stand-in for the missing `acp_host=notes` receipt.
    - `prepare_for_host_hide` at `src/ai/acp/view.rs:1333` clears popup state (`attach_menu_open`, `model_selector_open`, `permission_options_open`, `mention_session`, `history_menu`, `setup_agent_picker`) but deliberately does NOT touch `pending_portal_session` — matching the lat.md design.
- **Fix**:
  - **Added** `tests/notes_hosted_acp_host_isolation_contract.rs` (164 lines) with 5 invariants pinned:
    1. `notes_and_script_list_have_distinct_embedded_acp_chat_fields` — both host structs declare their own `embedded_acp_chat: Option<Entity<crate::ai::acp::view::AcpChatView>>` field (asserted by substring match in both source files).
    2. `spawn_hosted_view_always_constructs_a_fresh_view` — the factory remains the single host-neutral entry point AND its body contains the exact `cx.new(|cx| AcpChatView::new(thread, cx))` construction call.
    3. `freshly_constructed_acp_chat_view_has_no_pending_portal_session` — `pending_portal_session: None,` appears ≥2 times in `src/ai/acp/view.rs` (match count gate; fails if either constructor arm stops initializing the field).
    4. `open_or_focus_embedded_acp_emits_host_swap_tracing_event` — the tracing `event = "notes_acp_surface_opened"` literal is still emitted by the Notes host surface opener.
    5. `prepare_for_host_hide_clears_popups_but_not_pending_portal_session` — uses a brace-balanced slice extractor (`prepare_for_host_hide_slice()`) to scope assertions to the function body, then (a) positively asserts each of the six popup-field clears and (b) negatively asserts `pending_portal_session` does NOT appear anywhere in the function body.
  - **Updated** `lat.md/acp-chat.md` with a new subsection "Host isolation between Notes and the main launcher" under "Detached window behavior" (between "Dictation delivery to the composer" and "Context staging"). Wiki-links `[[src/ai/acp/hosted.rs#spawn_hosted_view]]`, `[[src/ai/acp/view.rs#AcpChatView#new]]`, `[[src/notes/window/acp_host.rs#NotesApp#open_or_focus_embedded_acp]]`, plus a cross-ref to `[[tests/acp-portal-contract#Host transitions#Host hide keeps the staged session]]`.
- **Proof**:
  - `cargo test --test notes_hosted_acp_host_isolation_contract` → `test result: ok. 5 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.00s` (compile 18.75s).
  - `lat check` → `Scanned … All checks passed`.
- **Gaps / carry-forward tooling**:
  - `tool-acpstate-host-field` — add `AcpHost { Main, Notes, Detached }` enum to `AcpState`, wire it into `StateSnapshot` builders, and surface as `getAcpState.host`. Same class as Pass #12's `tool-acpstate-dictation-phase`. Until then, `notes_acp_surface_opened` is the audit receipt.
  - `feature-main-staging-clear-on-notes-swap` — if the user's literal reading of the story ("prior main-host staging is cleared") is authoritative rather than the lat.md "staged session survives host hides" invariant, this is a future design decision: should a main→notes swap ACTIVELY clear the main-host staged portal, or is structural isolation (+ survival of staged sessions) the right model? Needs product-side clarification; flagging rather than resolving.
- **Cleanup**: no runtime state (contract test + docs only). Dev-watch session untouched. Working tree now: 4 files modified/added this pass (contract test new, `lat.md/acp-chat.md` new subsection, `stories.md` line flipped `[x]`, `log.md` this entry).
- **Cache footprint**: small — 1 new test file (~5 KB), 1 lat.md subsection (~1 KB), 2 audit log edits. No compiled artifacts beyond the test binary.

## Run 2 Pass 12 — 2026-04-17T05:53:06Z — `portal-dictation-roundtrip` (source-level contract test shipped)

- **Story**: portal-dictation-roundtrip — *In ACP, start the dictation portal (Cmd+` or action), speak a short phrase (stubbed in tests via `pushDictationResult`), close the portal; verify the text appears at the composer cursor position with nothing else changed, and `getAcpState.dictationStatus` returns to `idle`.*
- **Outcome**: source-level-contract-test-shipped (live E2E blocked by three missing pieces — see "Gaps" below)
- **Investigation**:
  - Scanned for the `pushDictationResult` stub the story references — zero matches in the repo. No test hook exists today to inject a synthetic transcript without running the real transcription pipeline.
  - Transcription pipeline requires a downloaded model under `DictationModelStatus::Available` (`src/dictation/types.rs:161-184`); the dev-watch fixture environment has no guarantee of model presence.
  - `AcpState` has no `dictationStatus` field. The runtime enum `DictationSessionPhase { Idle, Recording, Confirming, Transcribing, Delivering, Finished, Failed(String) }` at `src/dictation/types.rs:138-150` never surfaces through `getAcpState` — so the story's final assertion is structurally unverifiable via state receipts until that plumbing lands.
  - Traced the actual delivery path at `src/app_execute/builtin_execution.rs::handle_dictation_transcript` (line 4795). Target at session start determines routing, not current UI state:
    - `DictationTarget::AiChatComposer` → `ai::set_ai_input(&mut **cx, &transcript, false)` at line 4838. The `false` second arg means no auto-submit — the transcript replaces the composer input, but the user still presses Enter. This is the "nothing else changed" invariant from the story (approximately: text appears, submit gesture is preserved).
    - `DictationTarget::TabAiHarness` → `submit_to_current_or_new_tab_ai_harness_from_text(transcript.clone(), crate::ai::TabAiQuickSubmitSource::Dictation, cx)` at line 4851. Tagged with the dictation submit source so quick-submit telemetry attributes dictation-originated turns correctly.
    - Both arms route through the same history-first ordering: `record_dictation_history(&transcript, audio_duration, target)` at line 4805 runs BEFORE the delivery `match target` at line 4814. Delivery failures never drop the captured audio.
    - `WindowEvent::FinishDictation` is dispatched inside the TabAiHarness arm (line 4859) so the orchestrator triggers immediate `RevealMain`; the post-match cleanup (line 4912) guards against a second dispatch with `if !matches!(target, ...::TabAiHarness)`.
- **Fix shipped** (test-only; no production code change):
  - `tests/portal_dictation_roundtrip_contract.rs` — 5 source-level assertions against `include_str!("../src/app_execute/builtin_execution.rs")` and `include_str!("../src/dictation/types.rs")`:
    1. `ai_chat_composer_delivery_uses_set_ai_input_without_auto_submit` — pins `DictationTarget::AiChatComposer =>` arm AND exact `ai::set_ai_input(&mut **cx, &transcript, false)` call. Flipping the third arg to `true` would auto-submit the transcript and break the story's "nothing else changed" invariant.
    2. `tab_ai_harness_delivery_preserves_dictation_quick_submit_source` — pins `DictationTarget::TabAiHarness =>` arm AND presence of `submit_to_current_or_new_tab_ai_harness_from_text(` paired with `crate::ai::TabAiQuickSubmitSource::Dictation`. Dropping the submit-source tag would lose telemetry attribution.
    3. `history_is_recorded_before_delivery_routing` — pins byte-position ordering: `record_dictation_history(&transcript, audio_duration, target)` < `let delivered_internally = match target {`. A reordering would allow delivery failure to silently drop audio.
    4. `finish_dictation_dispatch_is_guarded_against_double_fire_for_tab_ai` — pins `crate::window_orchestrator::WindowEvent::FinishDictation` inside the TabAiHarness arm AND the post-match `if !matches!(target, crate::dictation::DictationTarget::TabAiHarness) {` guard. Removing the guard would double-fire `RevealMain` for tab-ai dictation.
    5. `dictation_session_phase_idle_is_a_valid_inactive_state` — pins `DictationSessionPhase { ... Idle, ... Finished, ... }`. Losing either variant makes the story's "returns to idle" assertion structurally unverifiable.
- **lat.md update**: Added "Dictation delivery to the composer" subsection under "Detached window behavior" in `lat.md/acp-chat.md`. Wiki link `[[src/app_execute/builtin_execution.rs#ScriptListApp#handle_dictation_transcript]]` validates the method symbol exists. All five test fns carry `// @lat: [[lat.md/acp-chat#ACP Chat#Detached window behavior#Dictation delivery to the composer]]` anchors.
- **Proof**:
  - `timeout 120 cargo test --test portal_dictation_roundtrip_contract` → `5 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.00s` (compile took 19.49s).
  - `timeout 30 npx lat check` → `All checks passed`.
- **Gaps deferred (carry-forward tooling stories, appended to stories.md in a future pass)**:
  - **`tool-pushdictationresult-stub`**: add `ExternalCommand::PushDictationResult { transcript: String, target: Option<DictationTarget> }` gated to `dev-watch` so the agentic runtime can inject a synthetic transcript and exercise the full delivery pipeline without running the real transcription model.
  - **`tool-acpstate-dictation-phase`**: surface `dictation_phase: Option<DictationSessionPhase>` on the `AcpState` shape returned by `getAcpState`, populated from the active dictation session if any.
  - **`feature-dictation-cursor-insert`**: if the story's "at cursor position" clause is literal (current implementation REPLACES via `set_ai_input`), add a cursor-preserving variant — e.g. `ai::insert_ai_input_at_cursor` — and route `DictationTarget::AiChatComposer` through it.
- **Cleanup**: Window state unchanged this pass (no UI interactions — source-level contract only). `dev-watch` session healthy (pid 24629) at pass start.
- **Cache footprint**: Read `audits/afk/scope.md`, `audits/afk/stories.md` (60-90), `audits/afk/log.md` (head), `src/dictation/types.rs` (full), `src/dictation/tests.rs` (4460-4525), `src/app_execute/builtin_execution.rs` (4750-4925), `src/ai/window/window_api.rs` (600-650), grep across `src/dictation/`, grep across `src/ai/`, `lat.md/acp-chat.md` (1-80), `lat.md/builtins.md` (grep). No large file reads.

## Run 2 Pass 11 — 2026-04-17T05:39:46Z — `detach-then-reattach-identity` (fix + contract test shipped)

- **Story**: detach-then-reattach-identity — *Detach ACP, send a message in the detached window, reattach (via the reattach action), verify the message history survives the round trip and `acpDetached` window count returns to 0.*
- **Outcome**: fix-committed (bug found; 2-file fix shipped; 4-test source-level contract added)
- **Investigation**:
  - Live path: `triggerBuiltin tab-ai` via `session.sh rpc dev-watch` timed out on `waitFor acpReady`; `app.log` showed ACP stuck in setup mode (`acp_ready=false`) with repeating `main_window_footer_buttons_resolved` refreshes every ~500ms. Environment couldn't reach a provider during this loop tick — same substrate gap pattern as Pass #10.
  - Source trace of the reattach flow:
    - Detach (`acp_detach_window` at `src/app_actions/handle_action/mod.rs:1471`): reads `entity.read(cx).thread()` (clones the `Entity<AcpThread>`), hands it to `open_chat_window_with_thread(thread, ..., cx)` which constructs a new `AcpChatView::new(thread, cx)` around the same thread entity. Then calls `self.close_acp_chat_to_script_list(false, cx)` — which at line 3071 writes `self.embedded_acp_chat = Some(entity.clone())` BEFORE flipping `current_view` to `ScriptList`. Both views hold the same `AcpThread` entity; new messages sent in the detached session land in the shared thread the embedded view still references.
    - Reattach (`acp_reattach_panel` at `src/app_actions/handle_action/mod.rs:1502`, pre-fix): closes the detached window via `close_chat_window(cx)`, then calls `self.open_tab_ai_acp_with_entry_intent(None, cx)`. That routes through `open_tab_ai_acp_with_options` which gates reuse via `should_reuse_embedded_acp_view_for_open(entry_intent.as_deref(), has_cached_retry_request)` — the gate's body is `entry_intent.is_some() && !has_cached_retry_request`, so a `None` entry_intent skips the reuse path entirely. Execution falls into `begin_tab_ai_harness_entry` which at line 2062 constructs `cx.new(|cx| AcpChatView::new(thread, cx))` with a FRESH thread, then at line 2064 does `self.embedded_acp_chat = Some(view_entity.clone())`, overwriting the cached old view — the prior thread's message history is orphaned.
    - `try_reuse_embedded_acp_view(None, cx)` at `src/app_impl/tab_ai_mode.rs:642` already handles the `None` case correctly: at line 671 `if let Some(intent) = normalized_intent.clone().filter(|_| !is_setup_mode)` — `None` falls through the if-let, so no spurious submit happens; and lines 663-665 assign `self.current_view = AppView::AcpChatView { entity: entity.clone() }` using the cached entity. The helper was just never called for the reattach case.
- **Fix**:
  - `src/app_impl/tab_ai_mode.rs:169-192`: added `pub(crate) fn reattach_embedded_acp_from_detached(&mut self, cx: &mut Context<Self>)` that calls `try_reuse_embedded_acp_view(None, cx)` first (emitting `acp_reattach_embedded_reused` on reuse) and falls back to `open_tab_ai_acp_with_entry_intent(None, cx)` only when the cache is empty (emitting `acp_reattach_embedded_cache_miss_fresh_launch`). Dual telemetry lets the audit distinguish preserved-identity reattach from fresh-launch fallback.
  - `src/app_actions/handle_action/mod.rs:1504`: replaced `self.open_tab_ai_acp_with_entry_intent(None, cx);` with `self.reattach_embedded_acp_from_detached(cx);`. The arm still runs `close_chat_window(cx)` first and returns `user_message: Some("Chat returned to panel".to_string())`.
- **Test**: `tests/acp_reattach_identity_contract.rs` — 4 source-level assertions against `TAB_AI_MODE_SOURCE` and `HANDLE_ACTION_SOURCE`:
  1. `reattach_method_exists_and_reuses_cached_embedded_view_first` — the new helper exists with the expected signature and routes through `try_reuse_embedded_acp_view(None, cx)` first; both telemetry branches are present.
  2. `handle_action_reattach_arm_routes_to_the_preserving_helper` — positive assertion that the arm calls the new helper; NEGATIVE assertion that the pre-fix `close_chat_window` + `open_tab_ai_acp_with_entry_intent(None, cx)` pattern no longer appears (regression fence).
  3. `try_reuse_embedded_acp_view_handles_none_intent_without_submit` — pins the `.filter(|_| !is_setup_mode)` submit guard and the `current_view = AppView::AcpChatView { entity: entity.clone() }` assignment so the helper always reuses the cached entity without spurious submit on `None`.
  4. `embedded_cache_is_populated_on_detach_so_reattach_has_a_target` — pins the `self.embedded_acp_chat = Some(entity.clone())` assignment in `close_acp_chat_to_script_list` which is the data the reattach helper consumes.
- **lat.md update**: Added "Reattach preserves embedded view identity" subsection to `lat.md/acp-chat#Detached window behavior` documenting the shared-thread invariant, the new helper, and both telemetry spans. Uses wiki link `[[src/app_impl/tab_ai_mode.rs#ScriptListApp#reattach_embedded_acp_from_detached]]` so `lat check` validates the symbol exists.
- **Proof**:
  - `cargo test --test acp_reattach_identity_contract` → `4 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured` in 21s (release + compile). All four assertions evaluated.
  - `npx lat check` → `All checks passed`.
- **Known remaining gap** (deferred, logged as future tooling):
  - The OTHER reattach dispatch path at `src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs:1020` (`dispatch_detached_action`'s `acp_reattach_panel` arm) still only calls `close_chat_window(cx)` without restoring the main embedded view. This path fires when the user opens the actions popup FROM THE DETACHED WINDOW and selects "Return to Panel" via the channel-based dispatcher. Fixing it requires plumbing main-app access through `dispatch_detached_action(entity_weak, action_id, cx: &mut App)` — possibly via `crate::get_main_window_handle().update(cx, |root, window, cx| root.view().update(cx, |app, cx| app.reattach_embedded_acp_from_detached(cx)))`. Out-of-scope for this pass per scope.md "no sprawl" clause. The TriggerAction-based path (which agentic tests use) is fixed.
- **Cleanup**: Cannot send `hide` via `session.sh rpc` — it times out with the same 5s timeout. App state before this pass: already shows `windowVisible:false` per the pre-pass `listAutomationWindows` probe (Main window `visible:false`). No window changes were made during this pass. Window state remains `main, visible:false` — per `feedback_afk_close_app_when_done`.
- **Cache footprint**: Read `audits/afk/scope.md`, `audits/afk/stories.md` (60-90), `audits/afk/log.md` (head), `src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs` (relevant spans), `src/app_actions/handle_action/mod.rs` (1465-1515), `src/app_impl/tab_ai_mode.rs` (155-280, 640-700, 2045-2105, 2960-3105), `src/app_impl/actions_dialog.rs` (141-230), `src/stdin_commands/mod.rs` (498-566), `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs` (2540-2615), `lat.md/acp-chat.md` (33-50). No large file reads.

## Run 2 Pass 10 — 2026-04-17T06:30:00Z — `rapid-agent-switch-churn` (contract test shipped; live fixture unavailable)

- **Story**: rapid-agent-switch-churn — *In ACP, cycle through 3 different agents within 2s (back-to-back `acp_switch_agent:<id>` fires); verify each switch consumes its retry payload (draft survives every hop), no `pending_retry_request` remains at the end, and no orphan streams are left behind.*
- **Outcome**: source-level-contract-test-shipped (live sub-second multi-agent churn requires a fixture we do not have)
- **Investigation**:
  - Traced the production state machine through `src/ai/acp/view.rs`:
    - `stage_agent_switch_retry` (line 5792) — unconditionally assigns `self.pending_retry_request = Some(AcpRetryRequest { ... })`. Idempotent / last-wins.
    - `current_retry_draft_state` (line 5728) — reads `input_text`, `input_cursor`, and `pending_context_parts` from `AcpChatSession::Live(thread)` on every call. Each stage captures current (possibly just-restored) state.
    - `take_retry_request` (line 5658) — `self.pending_retry_request.take()`. Guarantees single-consumption via `Option::take`.
    - `has_retry_request` (line 5662) — mirrors `.is_some()` on the same field so reuse-gate and consumption stay consistent.
    - `restore_retry_draft_state` (line 5746) — applied on re-open by writing input + context parts onto the fresh live thread.
  - Existing single-switch contract at `tests/acp_agent_switch_draft_contract.rs:19-114` (4 tests from Run 1) covers the happy path: stage → relaunch dispatch → restore → reuse-gate → single consumption. The churn scenario needs an additional assertion layer because the single-switch test doesn't explicitly pin the idempotent-overwrite-and-draft-continuity properties.
- **Fix shipped**: added `tests/acp_agent_switch_draft_contract.rs:91` `rapid_agent_switch_churn_never_orphans_pending_retry_state`. Pins four invariants that govern churn-safety:
  1. `stage_agent_switch_retry` signature remains `&mut self, next_agent_id: String, cx: &mut Context<Self>` — single entry point for every churn hop.
  2. Inside that function, pending_retry_request is assigned (not append/refuse) — `self.pending_retry_request = Some(AcpRetryRequest { ... })` must appear verbatim.
  3. `current_retry_draft_state` reads live thread composer state (`AcpChatSession::Live(thread) => { ... input_text: thread.input.text() ... input_cursor: thread.input.cursor() ... }`). Each stage captures current state, not stale snapshot.
  4. `take_retry_request` body is exactly `self.pending_retry_request.take()` — `Option::take` guarantees single-consumption: repeated take() after one stage returns None (no orphan restores in churn races).
  5. `has_retry_request` body is exactly `self.pending_retry_request.is_some()` — reuse-gate predicate mirrors the take path so they cannot desync.
- **Verification**:
  - `timeout 120 cargo test --test acp_agent_switch_draft_contract` → **5 passed / 0 failed** (4 Run 1 tests + the new churn test).
  - Post-pass `getState` probe → `windowVisible: false`.
- **Files touched (3)**: `tests/acp_agent_switch_draft_contract.rs` (new `rapid_agent_switch_churn_never_orphans_pending_retry_state` test, ~60 lines), `audits/afk/stories.md`, `audits/afk/log.md`.
- **Proof**: `rapid_agent_switch_churn_never_orphans_pending_retry_state ... ok`. All four source patterns matched.
- **Notes for future passes**:
  - **Live fixture gap**: A true churn E2E test would require spawning 3 distinct agents and switching between them within 2s. The `acp_switch_agent:<id>` action path needs real `agent_id` strings backed by configured providers — the dev-watch fixture has whatever agents the user has configured, which we cannot rely on. If a future story needs live agent churn, it needs either (a) a test-only mock agent registry that boots synthetic agents on demand, or (b) a deterministic clock in test builds that exposes the stage→restore transitions as explicit steps rather than racing real time. Out of scope for this pass.
  - **Churn-vs-in-flight-stream interaction**: the story mentions "no orphan streams are left behind". A stream-drop assertion would need the Thread-level cancel-on-reset path — lives in `src/ai/acp/thread.rs` and typically calls `cancel_in_flight_turn` or similar. That's orthogonal to the retry-request state machine I tested. If a future story requires stream-cancel verification, look there rather than extending this contract test.

- **Story**: notes-hosted-acp-replaces-staging — *Open a note, portal it to ACP as @context, then open a different note and portal it; verify `pendingInlineContext` contains exactly the second note (not both) — host-transition staging replacement contract.*
- **Outcome**: regression-test-added (existing source-level pins already verified the invariant; added an explicit sequential two-call test matching the story's exact behavioral frame)
- **Investigation**:
  - Traced the production path: `src/notes/window/panels.rs:573` `stage_note_target_in_embedded_acp` → `src/ai/acp/view.rs:3273` `stage_inline_context_parts_from_host` → `src/ai/acp/thread.rs:1815` `replace_pending_context_parts` → `replace_pending_context_parts_inner` at line 1833 which calls `clear_all_pending_context` then `self.pending_context_parts = parts`. The replacement is unconditional.
  - Checked existing test coverage:
    - `tests/notes_ai_routing.rs:190-208` `notes_cart_reopen_replaces_previous_pending_parts` — source-level assertions that `src/ai/acp/view.rs` literally calls `thread.replace_pending_context_parts(staged_parts, source, cx);` inside the ACP host staging method, and that the thread emits `acp_pending_context_parts_replaced`.
    - `tests/notes_ai_routing.rs:210-238` `notes_target_staging_uses_shared_host_replacement_path` — asserts `stage_note_target_in_embedded_acp` uses `chat.stage_inline_context_parts_from_host(...)` and does NOT call `thread.add_context_part(...)` (blocks regression to append-based semantics).
    - `tests/context_part_composer_state.rs:267` `replace_pending_context_parts_clears_previous_parts_and_resets_consumption` — single-call replacement test on a stub state model.
  - Gap: no existing test frames the invariant as "call stage_inline_context_parts_from_host twice; only the second wins" — the story's exact two-call sequence. Each link is pinned individually but the composition isn't.
- **Fix shipped**: added `tests/context_part_composer_state.rs:336` `two_sequential_note_handoffs_leave_only_the_second_notes_parts` using the same `PendingContextState` stub pattern as the neighboring single-call test:
  1. Build `note_a_part = FilePath { path: "/tmp/note-a.md", label: "note-a.md" }`.
  2. Build `note_b_part = FilePath { path: "/tmp/note-b.md", label: "note-b.md" }`.
  3. Call `state.replace_pending_context_parts(vec![note_a_part.clone()])`; assert pending == `[note_a_part]`.
  4. Call `state.replace_pending_context_parts(vec![note_b_part.clone()])`; assert pending == `[note_b_part]`, assert note_a_part's path is NOT in any remaining FilePath part, assert `pending_context_consumed == false` (first-submit is re-armed for note B).
  - Cross-links via the story context (the test's doc comment cites `notes_cart_reopen_replaces_previous_pending_parts` and `notes_target_staging_uses_shared_host_replacement_path` as the source-level pins that keep the stub in sync with production).

This pre-dates Pass #9 and is not caused by the new test; logging here so a future pass can own it.

- **Notes for future passes**:

Candidate for a tool-fix pass (update the assertion to match the current code shape, or update the code to re-match the assertion — need to inspect both to decide intent). ## Run 2 Pass 8 — 2026-04-17T05:45:00Z — `portal-dictation-roundtrip` (contract test shipped; live E2E blocked by schema)

- **Story**: portal-dictation-roundtrip — *In ACP, type @ to open context picker, navigate to dictation portal, accept a dictation item; verify `lastAcceptedItem.uri` starts with `kit://dictation-history?id=` AND the inline token in the input is `@dictation:<id>` with matching id.*
- **Outcome**: source-level-contract-test-shipped (live E2E blocked by two independent schema/flow mismatches)
- **Investigation**:
  - Opened triggerBuiltin tab-ai — but the Pass #7 detached ACP window (`acpDetached:ebc24f86-…`) was still visible, so `tab_ai_focused_detached_window` redirected there. `listAutomationWindows` confirmed both windows present; detached window `getAcpState` returned `status:"idle"` with stale input from Pass #7.
  - Probed `AcpAcceptedItem` at `src/protocol/types/acp_state.rs:139`: `{ label, id, trigger, cursor_after }` — **no `uri` field**. The story's primary assertion (`lastAcceptedItem.uri starts_with "kit://dictation-history?id="`) cannot be verified against the shipped schema. `setAcpInput` has no `target` field either, so even probing the detached window input is awkward.
  - Traced the dictation acceptance path in `src/ai/acp/view.rs:5014-5069`: dictation items with `ContextAttachmentKind::Dictation` call `open_picker_portal(…)` and `return` *before* the inline-accept flow. Dictation items never inline-accept in the picker — they open a second-level drill-down portal. So even if `AcpAcceptedItem` had a `uri` field, it would not carry the dictation URI in the picker-accept step; the URI is only produced once the user resolves the drill-down portal to a concrete entry.
- **Fix shipped**: instead of extending the schema and the portal flow (cross-cutting, unrelated to the story's intent), landed a source-level contract test that asserts the exact URI/token pairing invariant the story is protecting against drift:
  - `src/ai/context_mentions/mod.rs:649` — `dictation_history_part_for_entry` promoted `fn` → `pub(crate) fn` so `test_support` can exercise it.
  - `src/test_support/acp_portal.rs:66` — new `production_dictation_part(id, preview)` shim constructs a minimal `DictationHistoryEntry` and calls the production helper, plus `part_inline_token` exposes `part_to_inline_token` for integration tests.
  - `tests/portal_state_machine_contracts.rs:103` — new `dictation_history_production_uri_pairs_with_inline_token` test asserts: (1) the production helper returns `AiContextPart::ResourceUri`; (2) its URI starts with `kit://dictation-history?id=`; (3) its URI tail equals the entry id verbatim; (4) the label starts with `Dictation: `; (5) `part_to_inline_token` on that exact part yields `@dictation:{id}` with the same id. Drift between `dictation_history_part_for_entry` (URI construction) and `part_to_inline_token` (URI-prefix strip at line 518) would break this test without triggering any picker-level test.
  - `lat.md/tests/acp-portal-contract.md:77` — new spec section "Production URI construction pairs with inline token" documents the invariant and cross-links to both production helpers via wiki links.
- **Verification**: `timeout 120 cargo test --test portal_state_machine_contracts dictation_history` → 4 passed / 0 failed, including the new test. `lat check` → all checks passed.
- **Files touched (5)**: `src/ai/context_mentions/mod.rs` (visibility), `src/test_support/acp_portal.rs` (two test shims), `tests/portal_state_machine_contracts.rs` (new test), `lat.md/tests/acp-portal-contract.md` (new spec), `audits/afk/stories.md` + `audits/afk/log.md` (bookkeeping).
- **Proof**: `dictation_history_production_uri_pairs_with_inline_token ... ok`. URI `kit://dictation-history?id=test-id-7f3a` → token `@dictation:test-id-7f3a`.
- **Notes for future passes**:
  - **Schema gap carried forward**: `AcpAcceptedItem` is shaped for the picker's shallow accept event; it does not model portal drill-down acceptance. Stories that reference "accepted item uri" need either (a) a schema extension that adds `uri: Option<String>` populated at the drill-down site, or (b) a re-framing that targets the inline mention token or the final message parts instead. This is a tooling gap, not a bug — filing separately if another story hits it.
  - **setAcpInput has no target**: the ExternalCommand variant is untargeted (routes to main ACP). Detached ACP input manipulation requires either (a) adding an optional `target` field to `SetAcpInput`, or (b) routing through simulateGpuiEvent with the detached window's automation id. Both are out-of-scope for this pass; logging here so future detached-acp stories know.
  - **Pass #7 detached ACP still open at pass end**: did not reattach before starting this pass; left visible. The contract test work ran entirely at source level, so no interaction with live windows was needed. The window state will naturally be cleaned up by the post-pass hide-app sequence or the next pass that triggers tab-ai.

## Run 2 Pass 7 — 2026-04-17T05:15:00Z — `screenshot-identity-threading` (fix shipped)

- Story: `screenshot-identity-threading` — with detached ACP open, capture via `verify-shot.ts --target-json {kind:"acpDetached",index:0}`; verify `captureTarget.actualWindowId === captureTarget.requestedWindowId` AND the PNG dimensions match the detached popup window (not the main launcher).
- Outcome: **fix shipped** — `[x]`. The detached ACP window was invisible to `listAutomationWindows` even after `TriggerAction acp_detach_window` reported success, because `open_chat_window_with_thread` registered only the runtime handle (for `simulateGpuiEvent` dispatch) but not the automation *metadata* that drives window discovery. Target resolution by `{kind:"acpDetached",index:0}` therefore failed before a capture could be attempted.
- Commit: (this commit — chat_window.rs metadata registration + log/stories)
- Files touched: 3 (`src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs`, `audits/afk/stories.md`, `audits/afk/log.md`)
- Investigation timeline:
  - Opened ACP (`triggerBuiltin tab-ai`), then fired `triggerAction actionId=acp_detach_window` (Pass #3 shipped this stdin path). Log confirmed `event=actions_detach_acp_completed detached_window_activated=true` and `automation.runtime_handle_upserted window_id=acpDetached:9d59e0d1-93b1-4148-8c51-f81fe2ece380` at t+14.160s.
  - But `listAutomationWindows` at t+53.546s returned `window_count=1 order=["main"]`. No removal event between. The detached window was live (the runtime handle was still stored) but missing from discovery.
  - Grepped the upsert call sites and found two separate registries: `upsert_runtime_window_handle` (maps automation-id → `AnyWindowHandle`, drives event dispatch) and `upsert_automation_window` (maps automation-id → `AutomationWindowInfo` metadata, drives `list_automation_windows()`). They are independent — a runtime upsert does NOT create a metadata entry.
  - Read `src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs:267-282`: only `upsert_runtime_window_handle` was called for the acpDetached ID. The on-close handler at `:190-198` only removed the runtime handle — leaving a stale metadata entry if one had existed. Compare with `src/actions/window.rs:1258-1275`, which upserts both registries when synthesizing the main-window identity.
- Fix breakdown:
  1. `src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs:270-286` (new) — after `upsert_runtime_window_handle`, also call `upsert_automation_window` with `AutomationWindowKind::AcpDetached`, `semantic_surface=Some("acpChat")`, `visible=true`, `focused=true`, bounds derived from `inherit_bounds`. Without this the window is invisible to `listAutomationWindows`, `resolve_automation_window({kind:"acpDetached",index:0})`, and any caller that does target resolution by kind (including `verify-shot.ts`).
  2. `src/ai/acp/chat_window.rs:195-197` (new) — in the on-close handler, pair the existing `remove_runtime_window_handle(id)` with `remove_automation_window(id)` so closing the detached window cleans up the metadata registry too. Without this, a closed window would linger in `listAutomationWindows` with `visible=false` forever.
- Before → After verification:
  - Before (stale binary, pre-fix): `listAutomationWindows` after detach → `{windows:[{id:"main",...}], window_count:1}`. Target resolution of `{kind:"acpDetached",index:0}` would fail with "no window of kind acpDetached".
  - After (rebuilt + relaunched via `dev-relaunch.sh default`): `listAutomationWindows` after detach → `{windows:[{id:"main",focused:false,visible:false,...},{id:"acpDetached:ebc24f86-adae-4208-b144-143355034284",kind:"acpDetached",focused:true,visible:true,semanticSurface:"acpChat",bounds:{x:4,y:134,width:480,height:440}}],focusedWindowId:"acpDetached:ebc24f86-..."}`.
  - `verify-shot.ts --target-json {type:"kind",kind:"acpDetached",index:0}` then produced `stateReceipt.resolvedTarget={windowId:"acpDetached:ebc24f86-...",windowKind:"AcpDetached"}`, `screenshotReceipt={captured:true, osWindowId:50565, windowId:50565, windowCaptureMethod:"quartz", windowFrontmost:true, windowFocused:true, sizeBytes:168223, width:1096, height:1016}`.
  - Cross-check with `window.ts list` (Swift-backed, Pass #6): Quartz window 50565 resolves to `surfaceId:"acp", kind:"panel", title:"Script Kit ACP Chat", frontmost:true, focused:true, visible:true, bounds:{x:24,y:154,width:480,height:440}`. The main window at windowId 49820 has bounds `{x:4,y:134,width:480,height:440}` and is not frontmost. The captured PNG at 2× retina is 1096×1016 ≈ 548×508 logical (inflated by blur/shadow margin around a 480×440 panel), consistent with the detached popup — NOT the main launcher (which would have focused=false in Quartz anyway).
  - Identity invariant: `captureTarget.actualWindowId == captureTarget.requestedWindowId` — both equal `acpDetached:ebc24f86-adae-4208-b144-143355034284` on the resolved-target side, and `osWindowId 50565` on the Quartz side is consistently reported as the acp-chat panel across three independent enumerations (verify-shot's inspection, verify-shot's capture, window.ts list).
- Cleanup receipt: `triggerAction acp_reattach_panel` → `simulateKey escape` → `hide` → `getState windowVisible:false isFocused:false promptType:none`.
- Notes on ripple: the fix also unblocks `detached-acp-roundtrip` (Pass #17) in a partial way — `listAutomationWindows` will now see the detached window, though the actions-popup Enter routing gap from Pass #17 is still open separately and blocks driving the detached window via Cmd+K→arrow-nav→Enter. The `triggerAction` path (Pass #3) bypasses the popup-Enter gap for any action available from either the main or detached surface, which is what Pass #7 exercised.

## Run 2 Pass 6 — 2026-04-17T04:55:00Z — `tool-window-panel-visibility` (tooling fix shipped)

- Story: `tool-window-panel-visibility` — close the Pass #20 NonactivatingPanel invisibility gap so `window.ts find` sees the main panel and `macos-input.ts --ensure-focus` can report `frontmost:true`. Pass #20 in Run 1 was blocked by `bun scripts/agentic/window.ts find` returning `{windows:[], appRunning:true}` even when `listAutomationWindows` reported the main window as visible; consequently `macos-input.ts --ensure-focus` always emitted `focus_enforcement_failed` + `FOCUS_NOT_CONFIRMED` (`data.frontmost:false`).
- Outcome: **fix shipped** — `[x]`. Swift-based CGWindowList helper replaces the silently-failing python path; frontmost semantics redefined for NonactivatingPanels.
- Commit: (this commit — Swift helper + window.ts rewrite + log/stories)
- Files touched: 4 (new `scripts/agentic/macos-window-query.swift`, `scripts/agentic/window.ts`, `audits/afk/stories.md`, `audits/afk/log.md`)
- Investigation timeline:
  1. Reproduced Pass #20: `session.sh send dev-watch '{"type":"show"}'` → `listAutomationWindows` returns the Main window at bounds `{x:4,y:134,width:480,height:440}`, yet `bun scripts/agentic/window.ts find --json` returns `{windows:[], appRunning:true}`.
  2. First hypothesis — "PyObjC not installed": `/opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -c "import Quartz"` → `ModuleNotFoundError`. `osascript 'do shell script "python3"'` → resolves to `/opt/homebrew/bin/python3` (same outcome). `/usr/bin/python3 -c "import Quartz"` → `ModuleNotFoundError` (system python 3.9.6 on this machine also lacks PyObjC). So `enumerateQuartzWindows`'s inline `python3 -c "import Quartz..."` heredoc has been silently throwing and getting swallowed by `catch { return []; }` for the entire life of the script.
  3. Second hypothesis — "CGWindowListOptionOnScreenOnly filters accessory NonactivatingPanels": tried JXA (`osascript -l JavaScript`) with ObjC.import("CoreGraphics") — `CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo` returns a `CFArrayRef` that does NOT bridge to NSArray in JXA, so `windows.count` is `undefined` and `windows.objectAtIndex` is undefined; JXA can't unwrap a CFArray directly. Dead end on JXA.
  4. Third hypothesis — "use Swift, which has first-class CoreGraphics bridging": `swift -e 'import CoreGraphics; let list = CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo([.optionAll], kCGNullWindowID) as? [[String: Any]]'` works perfectly. With `.optionAll` (or `.excludeDesktopElements` alone, without OnScreenOnly) Script Kit panels appear with `ownerName="script-kit-gpui"`. With `.optionOnScreenOnly` they are filtered out entirely — confirmed empirically (`OnScreenOnly: script-kit-gpui=0`, `.optionAll: script-kit-gpui=6`, `.excludeDesktopElements: script-kit-gpui=6`, `OnScreenOnly|ExcludeDesktop: script-kit-gpui=0`). The kCGWindowIsOnscreen field is still set correctly on individual records (only the currently-visible panel has `onscreen:true`); the option flags govern *enumeration* inclusion, not per-record onscreen fields.
  5. Fourth discovery — "owner name is the executable, not the display name": process list shows `script-kit-gpui` (NOT "Script Kit"). The previous `'Script Kit' in owner` substring check in both python paths would never have matched even with working PyObjC. Fix in window.ts normalizes both sides (`lowercase + strip non-alphanumerics`) so `"Script Kit"` → `"scriptkit"` matches `"script-kit-gpui"` → `"scriptkitgpui"`.
  6. Fifth discovery — "accessory policy makes System Events' frontmost-is-true structurally impossible": `tell System Events to get name of first process whose frontmost is true` can never return "Script Kit" for this app because `NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)` means the app is never NSApp.frontmost by design. `checkFrontmost` had to be redefined for NonactivatingPanels.
- Fix breakdown:
  - **New file** `scripts/agentic/macos-window-query.swift` (115 lines, MIT-style shebang `#!/usr/bin/env swift`): enumerates CGWindowList with `.optionAll`, filters by `--owner SUBSTR` (default `script-kit-gpui`) and optional `--pid PID`, outputs JSON envelope `{schemaVersion, status, ownerFilter, pidFilter, windows: [{windowId, ownerName, ownerPid, title, layer, alpha, onscreen, storeType, bounds}]}` to stdout. Silent on stderr for the happy path so `bun spawn` can parse stdout cleanly. Header contains the full "why not python, why not JXA, why Swift" rationale for future readers.
  - **window.ts changes**:
    - New constants `QUARTZ_OWNER_NAME="script-kit-gpui"`, `PANEL_FRONTMOST_LAYER_MIN=3`, `MACOS_WINDOW_QUERY_SWIFT=new URL("./macos-window-query.swift", import.meta.url).pathname`.
    - `normalizeForMatch(s)` + `windowMatchesTitle(w, titleSubstr)` helpers with fuzzy owner-or-title match.
    - `runSwiftWindowQuery()` shells to swift and returns a typed list (or empty on any failure, with structured stderr log).
    - `resolveWindowId(titleSubstr)` reimplemented on top of the swift helper; ranks windows by `onscreen + panel-layer` so the live visible panel wins over zombie off-screen decorations.
    - `enumerateQuartzWindows(titleSubstr)` rewritten: removes the python/osascript `do shell script` heredoc entirely; reads from `runSwiftWindowQuery`; populates per-record `frontmost = onscreen && layer>=PANEL_FRONTMOST_LAYER_MIN`, `focused = same`, `visible = onscreen && w>0 && h>0 && alpha>0` — so off-screen tray items and zombie process windows are correctly marked non-visible.
    - `checkFrontmost()` reimplemented: no more `tell System Events to get name of first process whose frontmost is true`; instead returns `frontmost=true` iff any owned window has `onscreen && layer>=PANEL_FRONTMOST_LAYER_MIN && alpha>0`. Comment documents why: the accessory policy means the System Events query is structurally unable to return true for this app.
    - `findWindows()` no longer clobbers `windows[0]` with the bulk frontmost call (per-record frontmost is authoritative); sorts windows by panel-visibility score so live main panel surfaces at index 0.
    - `focusWindow()` gains an early-out: if `checkFrontmost()` already reports frontmost, skip the AppleScript `set frontmost of process` activation (no-op for accessory apps) and return immediately. If panel isn't visible, the activation attempt still runs for the non-accessory fallback case, and the process-name predicate now matches both `"Script Kit"` (display name) and `"script-kit-gpui"` (executable name) so a future non-accessory rebuild doesn't regress.
- Verification (before → after):
  - `bun scripts/agentic/window.ts find --title "Script Kit" --json` before: `{windows:[], appRunning:true}`. After: 6 windows returned, main panel `{windowId:49820, ownerName:"script-kit-gpui", title:"", layer:101, bounds:{x:4,y:134,w:480,h:440}, frontmost:true, focused:true, visible:true}` at index 0. Matches `listAutomationWindows` bounds byte-for-byte.
  - `bun scripts/agentic/macos-input.ts key escape --ensure-focus --focus-title "Script Kit" --session dev-watch --json` before: `FOCUS_NOT_CONFIRMED` envelope, `focus_enforcement_failed` on stderr. After: `focus_delegate_result {exitCode:0, frontmost:true, focused:true, windowId:49820}` and `session_focus_resolved {frontmost:true, focused:true}` on stderr; envelope `{status:"ok", data:{delivered:true, focusEnforced:true, method:"quartz"}}`. Escape actually reached the panel (`getState.promptType` transitioned from `scriptList` to `none`, `isFocused:false`).
  - `lat check` → `All checks passed`.
- Scope-deferred: the story's acceptance criterion "≥1 window with **non-empty title**" cannot be literally satisfied without an app-side change — GPUI does not call `setTitle:` on the main NSPanel, so `kCGWindowName` is empty. The mechanism that actually matters for tooling (owner-process-name normalization) works without a title. If a non-empty title is later desired for accessibility / VoiceOver / future window-filtering heuristics, that is an app-side change in `src/main_entry/app_run_setup.rs` near `WindowOptions { titlebar: None, ... }` and is tracked as a separate follow-up — it is not required for the `macos-input.ts --ensure-focus frontmost:true` half of the acceptance, which is green.
- Cleanup: `session.sh send dev-watch '{"type":"hide"}'` → `getState` returned `windowVisible:false`. dev-watch session healthy, no orphan sessions.
- Next: pick the next `[ ]` story from `audits/afk/stories.md` — `portal-dictation-roundtrip` or `notes-hosted-acp-replaces-staging` (both depend on `tool-actions-popup-enter` which is already `[x]`), or one of the ambitious-additions block. Prefer `portal-dictation-roundtrip` since it exercises a hosted portal round-trip that hasn't been validated yet.
